items.title: JUB : confirmation de l’approche COMVIK pour l’activité inventive
items.title_zh: UPC：确认评估创造性时适用COMVIK方法
items.title_en: JUB : confirmation de l’approche COMVIK pour l’activité inventive
items.summary: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) has issued a significant ruling confirming the application of the COMVIK approach for assessing inventive step in patent cases. This decision provides crucial guidance on how technical and non-technical features are evaluated in determining patent validity. The ruling has important implications for patent practitioners and rights holders operating under the UPC system.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉法院于2026年4月17日作出裁决（UPC_COA_901/2025），确认在评估专利创造性时适用COMVIK方法。裁决指出，尽管用户界面特征可能属于信息呈现，但若改善人机交互可产生技术效果并贡献创造性。该决定明确了技术与非技术特征在专利有效性判定中的评估方式。
items.ai_insight_zh: 此裁决为UPC体系下软件及人机交互类专利的创造性评估提供关键指引。实务中需关注非技术特征与技术特征结合产生的额外技术效果，这对涉及GUI或医疗设备的专利有效性抗辩及撰写策略具有直接参考意义，建议持续追踪后续类似案件的法律适用一致性。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC上诉法院确认COMVIK方法用于评估包含非技术特征的专利创造性", "改善人机交互的用户界面特征可产生技术效果，从而支持创造性认定", "涉案专利涉及血糖仪，其特定UI交互被认定具有技术贡献而非单纯信息呈现"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确COMVIK方法在UPC诉讼中的主导地位，影响技术/非技术特征划分", "GUI特征若改善人机交互效率，可能被认定为具有技术贡献而非排除对象", "需观察UPC后续案件中对“额外技术效果”认定的具体尺度与证据要求"]

items.title: JUB : l’objection préliminaire strictement cantonnée à la compétence
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院：初步异议严格限于管辖权问题
items.title_en: JUB : l’objection préliminaire strictement cantonnée à la compétence
items.summary: This article examines a recent ruling from the Unified Patent Court regarding the scope and application of preliminary objections in patent litigation proceedings.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院中央分院在REEL International诉Fives ECL案中裁定，初步异议仅适用于管辖权、分庭权限及程序语言三类情形。被告试图以缺乏诉讼利益及既判力为由提出初步异议被驳回，法院强调此类实体问题需经完整审理，不得通过初步异议快速过滤，以保障程序效率。
items.ai_insight_zh: 该裁决明确了UPC程序中初步异议的狭窄适用范围，防止被告利用程序性异议拖延诉讼。实务中需区分管辖权异议与实体抗辩，后者将进入全面审理阶段。当事人应准备充分的实体证据而非依赖程序过滤，关注UPC对程序效率的严格要求及既有判例的一致性。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["初步异议仅限管辖权、分庭权限及程序语言，列表封闭不可扩展", "诉讼利益与既判力不涉及管辖权，须通过完整事实与法律审查解决", "法院拒绝分离辩论或提前裁决，主张合并审理以提升程序经济与速度"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确初步异议仅限于程序管辖类事项，实体抗辩无法在此阶段解决", "法院倾向合并审理以符合一年结案目标，避免程序碎片化", "需持续观察UPC后续案件中对程序效率与当事人权利平衡的司法尺度"]

items.title: 'Touch your boobs' not contrary to accepted principles of morality
items.title_zh: “Touch your boobs”不违反公序良俗原则
items.title_en: 'Touch your boobs' not contrary to accepted principles of morality
items.summary: On 7 May, the Fifth Board of Appeal (BoA) of the EUIPO held that the word mark “TOUCH YOUR BOOBS” is not contrary to public policy or to accepted principles of morality under Article 7(1)(f) of the EU Trade Mark Regulation (EUTMR). Contrary to the examiner, the BoA found that the mark would not be perceived as an unduly sexual or offensive phrase.BackgroundIn July 2025, Katjes Fassin GmbH and Co.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局第五上诉委员会于5月7日裁定，文字商标“TOUCH YOUR BOOBS”不违反《欧盟商标条例》第7(1)(f)条规定的公共政策或公认道德准则。上诉委员会认为该标志不会被感知为过度色情或冒犯性短语，推翻了审查员的反对意见。申请人背景涉及Katjes Fassin公司。
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案展示了欧盟商标审查中对于“道德违背”认定的具体尺度，即需结合社会语境判断是否构成“过度”冒犯。实务中需关注上诉机构对俚语或双关语在当代社会接受度的动态评估标准，以及此类非传统表达在注册阶段的合规边界。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟知识产权局第五上诉委员会裁定该商标不违反公序良俗条款", "委员会认定该短语不会被相关公众视为过度色情或具有冒犯性", "该裁决推翻了审查员此前基于道德理由提出的驳回意见"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["欧盟商标审查中对“公序良俗”的认定倾向于结合当下社会语境进行动态评估", "需持续观察上诉委员会对带有性暗示但非直接淫秽词汇的容忍度变化趋势", "申请含争议性词汇商标时，应准备关于社会接受度及非冒犯性感知的论证材料"]

items.title: EPO successfully completes ISO surveillance audit
items.title_zh: EPO成功完成ISO监督审核
items.title_en: EPO Maintains ISO Certifications Following Successful Surveillance Audit
items.summary: Update on ISO certifications for quality, information security, occupational health & safety, and environment management systems. ISO certifications for quality, information security, environmental management systems.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年4月，欧洲专利局（EPO）顺利通过外部审计机构的管理体系ISO监督审核。此次审核确认了EPO在质量管理、职业健康安全、信息安全和环境管理方面的稳健性，维持了其ISO 9001、ISO 45001、ISO 27001及ISO 14001认证。这体现了EPO对国际标准及合规性的承诺，并通过综合管理体系支持高质量服务交付。
items.ai_summary_en: In April 2026, the European Patent Office (EPO) successfully completed its periodic ISO surveillance audit conducted by external auditors. The review confirmed the continued robustness and maturity of the EPO’s integrated management systems, allowing the organization to maintain its certifications for quality management (ISO 9001), occupational health and safety (ISO 45001), information security (ISO 27001), and environmental management (ISO 14001). This outcome underscores the EPO’s adherence to international standards and regulatory compliance. By aligning policies, processes, and controls within a unified framework, the EPO aims to ensure the consistent delivery of high-quality services and support continuous improvement across all operational domains.
items.ai_insight_zh: 此新闻属于EPO内部行政与合规动态，不直接改变专利实体法或审查标准。实务中需关注其信息安全（ISO 27001）与质量管理（ISO 9001）的持续合规，这间接保障了电子提交系统及审查流程的稳定性与数据安全性，是评估EPO服务可靠性的基础指标。
items.ai_insight_en: This update confirms the administrative and operational stability of the EPO rather than introducing changes to substantive patent law or examination guidelines. For rights holders and legal practitioners, the maintenance of ISO 27001 and ISO 9001 certifications serves as a key indicator of the reliability and data security standards underpinning the EPO’s electronic filing systems and procedural workflows. While this does not alter immediate filing strategies or enforcement options, it reinforces confidence in the continuity and integrity of EPO services.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EPO于2026年4月通过外部审计机构的ISO管理体系监督审核", "维持ISO 9001、45001、27001及14001四项国际认证，确认体系成熟稳健", "通过综合管理体系整合政策流程，确保持续改进及服务交付质量"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EPO passed its April 2026 external ISO surveillance audit, confirming ongoing adherence to international management standards.", "Certifications maintained include ISO 9001 for quality, ISO 27001 for information security, ISO 45001 for safety, and ISO 14001 for environment.", "The EPO utilizes an Integrated Management System to unify policies and controls, supporting service quality and continuous operational improvement."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["Client relevance/客户相关性: EPO运营稳定性与数据安全合规性保障", "Practice note/实务观察: 关注EPO管理体系认证状态对服务连续性的影响", "What to monitor/后续观察: 无具体法律变动，仅需知悉机构合规状态更新"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Confirms the stability and data security compliance of EPO operations, indirectly supporting the reliability of electronic submission channels and case processin…", "Practice note: Monitor the status of EPO management system certifications as baseline indicators for service continuity and operational maturity.", "What to monitor: No immediate legal or procedural changes; treat as routine compliance confirmation regarding institutional operational standards."]
core_points_en: The EPO passed its April 2026 external ISO surveillance audit, confirming ongoing adherence to international management standards.
Certifications maintained include ISO 9001 for quality, ISO 27001 for information security, ISO 45001 for safety, and ISO 14001 for environment.
The EPO utilizes an Integrated Management System to unify policies and controls, supporting service quality and continuous operational improvement.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Confirms the stability and data security compliance of EPO operations, indirectly supporting the reliability of electronic submission channels and case processin…
Practice note: Monitor the status of EPO management system certifications as baseline indicators for service continuity and operational maturity.
What to monitor: No immediate legal or procedural changes; treat as routine compliance confirmation regarding institutional operational standards.

items.title: Highlights from the latest meeting of the SACEPO Working Party on Quality
items.title_zh: EPO SACEPO质量工作组会议亮点：2026年质量计划与AI应用
items.title_en: Highlights from the latest meeting of the SACEPO Working Party on Quality
items.summary: The EPO hosted a lively SACEPO Working Party on Quality meeting on 8 May 2026. Key topics included the Quality Action Plan for 2026, how AI can improve quality at the EPO, and how SACEPO members are currently employing AI.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局（EPO）于2026年5月8日举办第16届SACEPO质量工作组会议，71名代表参会。会议重点讨论了2026年质量行动计划、审查实践协调及AI在检索和文书辅助中的应用。EPO强调质量为核心优先事项，并介绍了AI工具在确保人类决策责任前提下的整合进展，同时预告了2026/2027年度用户满意度调查。
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务中需关注EPO在审查流程中引入AI工具的具体节奏及其对审查意见生成的影响。尽管EPO强调人类主导，但AI辅助可能改变交互效率。建议持续跟踪即将发布的用户满意度调查结果及后续关于AI风险缓解措施的具体指南，以评估其对专利申请策略的潜在影响。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EPO推进2026年质量行动计划，强化主动检索部门最佳实践及异议部门培训", "AI工具应用于预检索、口头程序记录及起草辅助，坚持人类对决策负责的原则", "会议确认了审查实践协调的进展，并预告将于近期开展2026/2027年度用户满意度调查"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["Client relevance/客户相关性: 关注EPO审查效率变化及AI介入程度对…", "Practice note/实务观察: 适应EPO基于AI工具的互动模式，注意保留人工…", "What to monitor/后续观察: 监测2026/2027用户满意度调查结果及…"]

items.title: Nokia withdraws action against Geely in connected cars dispute
items.title_zh: 诺基亚撤回对吉利联网汽车专利纠纷的起诉
items.title_en: Nokia withdraws action against Geely in connected cars dispute
items.summary: Since last summer, Finnish company Nokia has filed infringement claims in Munich and at the UPC over several patents essential to the 4G and 5G standards, which are used in connected cars. At Munich Regional Court Nokia sued Geely over EP 3 566 488 and EP 3 832 976. The oral hearing in the case of EP 488 […] The post Nokia withdraws action against Geely in connected cars dispute appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 芬兰公司诺基亚自去年夏季起，就4G和5G标准必要专利在德国慕尼黑地区法院及统一专利法院（UPC）对吉利提起侵权诉讼。近期，诺基亚已撤回针对吉利的部分诉讼行动，其中包括在慕尼黑法院涉及EP 3 566 488和EP 3 832 976号专利的案件。
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案显示SEP权利人在多法域并行诉讼中的策略调整。需关注撤诉是否伴随许可协议达成，或仅为程序性转移。UPC与德国法院在SEP案件中的互动及权利人诉讼路径选择仍是行业观察重点。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["诺基亚此前在慕尼黑地区法院及UPC就4G/5G SEP对吉利提起侵权诉讼", "慕尼黑诉讼涉及EP 3 566 488和EP 3 832 976号专利，现诺基亚已撤回…"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注SEP权利人在德国法院与UPC之间的诉讼策略协调与撤回动因", "监测撤诉后双方是否公布FRAND许可进展或后续在其他法域的诉讼动态"]

items.title: Switzerland introduces substantive examinations with Patent Act reform
items.title_zh: 瑞士通过专利法修订引入实质审查制度
items.title_en: Switzerland Reforms Patent Act with Mandatory Searches and Optional Substantive Examination
items.summary: Key changes in the Swiss Patent Act include a mandatory prior-art search, optional full substantive examination, the acceptance of English-language technical documents and a new appeal system. In March 2024, the Federal Assembly passed a partial revision of the Patent Act, introducing new features to the Swiss patent system. The Swiss government, as supported by […] The post Switzerland introduces substantive examinations with Patent Act reform appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 2024年3月，瑞士联邦议会通过《专利法》部分修订案。此次改革核心在于引入强制性的现有技术检索，并允许申请人选择是否进行全面的实质审查。此外，新规接受英文技术文件，并建立了新的上诉系统。这一变革标志着瑞士从形式审查向更严格的审查模式转变，旨在提升专利质量与法律确定性，对在该国进行专利布局的企业将产生深远影响。
items.ai_summary_en: In March 2024, the Swiss Federal Assembly passed a partial revision of the Patent Act, introducing significant procedural changes. Key amendments include the implementation of a mandatory prior-art search and an optional full substantive examination system. The reform also allows for the acceptance of English-language technical documents and establishes a new appeal mechanism. These measures mark a shift towards enhanced patent quality and legal certainty within the Swiss jurisdiction.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业赴瑞士申请专利需适应新流程，强制检索可能增加前期成本与时间。建议提前规划现有技术分析，利用英文文件便利降低翻译成本。同时，关注实质审查的可选性，根据商业价值权衡投入，以应对更高的授权门槛和潜在的驳回风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This legislative update alters the procedural landscape for patent registration in Switzerland. Rights holders should note the introduction of mandatory searches and the option for substantive review, which may impact prosecution timelines and grant probabilities. The acceptance of English documentation offers potential administrative efficiencies for international applicants managing European portfolios.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["瑞士联邦议会于2024年3月通过《专利法》部分修订案", "引入强制性现有技术检索及可选的全面实质审查机制", "接受英文技术文档并建立全新的专利上诉系统"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The revised Swiss Patent Act introduces a mandatory prior-art search requirement for all patent applications filed in Switzerland.", "Applicants may now opt for a full substantive examination, moving beyond the previous formal examination framework.", "The reform permits the submission of English-language technical documents and implements a new appellate structure."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["适应强制检索要求，优化前期现有技术分析与预算规划", "善用英文文件政策降低成本，审慎评估实质审查的商业必要性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor how the optional substantive examination affects grant rates and portfolio strength in the Swiss market.", "Practice note: Leverage the new allowance for English-language technical documents to streamline filing procedures where applicable.", "What to monitor: Track the implementation timeline and specific guidelines for the newly established appeal system."]
core_points_en: The revised Swiss Patent Act introduces a mandatory prior-art search requirement for all patent applications filed in Switzerland.
Applicants may now opt for a full substantive examination, moving beyond the previous formal examination framework.
The reform permits the submission of English-language technical documents and implements a new appellate structure.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor how the optional substantive examination affects grant rates and portfolio strength in the Swiss market.
Practice note: Leverage the new allowance for English-language technical documents to streamline filing procedures where applicable.
What to monitor: Track the implementation timeline and specific guidelines for the newly established appeal system.

items.title: Obelix conquers Luxembourg
items.title_zh: Obelix征服卢森堡：欧盟普通法院审理Obelix商标声誉案
items.title_en: Obelix conquers Luxembourg
items.summary: Asterix and Obelix conquered the entire Roman Empire with their wits and fists by mastering twelve Herculean tasks. Since this does not work outside films and comics, someone thought it would be a good idea to protect "Obelix" as a trade mark for weapons. In Les Éditions Albert René v EUIPO (case T‑24/25), the General Court dealt with the question whether the reputation of the "Obelix" trade mark
items.ai_summary_zh: 本案为Les Éditions Albert René诉EUIPO案（T‑24/25），由欧盟普通法院审理。争议焦点在于“Obelix”商标是否享有声誉，以及该声誉是否足以阻止第三方在“武器”商品上注册相同商标。尽管漫画角色以武力闻名，但法律上需判定其知名度跨类保护范围。该案涉及知名IP在非传统领域的商标防御策略及声誉认定标准。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应重视知名IP的跨类商标布局，尤其在非核心领域建立防御壁垒。面对EUIPO审查或异议时，需充分举证商标声誉及混淆可能性。建议监测类似案例，评估自身品牌在海外注册时的声誉证据链完整性，避免因缺乏知名度证明而丧失跨类保护机会。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟普通法院审理Obelix商标在武器类别的注册争议案件", "核心争议点为Obelix商标声誉及其跨类保护的适用性判定", "探讨知名虚构角色商标在非相关商品上的权利边界与限制"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重视知名IP跨类商标布局，建立非核心领域防御壁垒", "完善海外商标声誉证据链，应对EUIPO审查与异议挑战"]

items.title: Companies House and Intellectual Property Office (IPO) join forces to warn about unsolicited payment requests
items.title_zh: 英国公司注册署与知识产权局联合警示未olicited付款请求
items.title_en: Companies House and Intellectual Property Office (IPO) join forces to warn about unsolicited payment requests
items.summary: Companies House and IPO warn businesses about unsolicited payment requests and how to identify them.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国公司注册署（Companies House）与英国知识产权局（UKIPO）联合发布警告，提醒企业警惕针对其服务的未经请求的付款请求。此类诈骗通常模仿官方信函，诱导企业支付非必要的登记或续展费用。两机构详细说明了如何识别这些虚假通知，强调官方不会通过此类方式索取额外费用。此举旨在保护企业免受财务损失，确保知识产权及公司注册的正规流程不被干扰，维护市场秩序。
items.ai_insight_zh: 在英中国企业需高度警惕此类仿冒官方机构的诈骗邮件或信函，切勿轻信非官方渠道的缴费要求。建议建立内部IP管理审核机制，直接通过UKIPO官网核实状态。这不仅是财务风险防范，更是确保商标、专利等权利不因误操作而失效的关键合规步骤，避免因小失大影响品牌布局。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UKIPO与公司注册署联合打击模仿官方的付费诈骗行为", "指导企业识别未经请求的虚假注册及续展缴费通知", "明确官方渠道不收取此类非正式费用以保障企业权益"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立内部审核机制，直接通过官网核实官方缴费通知真伪", "警惕仿冒信函，防止因误付费用导致知识产权权利状态异常"]

items.title: Nokia settles global 5G patent dispute with Chinese automaker Geely
items.title_zh: 诺基亚与中国车企吉利达成全球5G专利和解
items.title_en: Nokia Settles Global 5G Patent Dispute With Geely As Avanci Adds New Licensee
items.summary: UPDATE: Since we published our report, Avanci has also announced that it has signed 4G and 5G licences with one new unnamed Chinese car company, and Avanci licensor Longhorn IP has revealed that it has terminated a recently-launched German lawsuit against Geely.
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文摘要显示，Avanci宣布与一家未具名中国车企签署4G和5G许可协议。同时，Avanci被许可人Longhorn IP透露，其已终止近期针对吉利在德国提起的诉讼。标题指出诺基亚已与吉利达成全球5G专利纠纷和解。
items.ai_summary_en: Nokia has settled its global 5G patent dispute with Chinese automaker Geely. Concurrently, the Avanci licensing platform announced new 4G and 5G licenses with an unnamed Chinese car manufacturer. Additionally, Avanci licensor Longhorn IP confirmed it terminated a recently filed lawsuit against Geely in Germany, signaling further resolution in ongoing automotive SEP licensing negotiations.
items.ai_insight_zh: Practice note: 需持续观察德国法院对已终止诉讼的最终程序裁定，以及Avanci平台新增许可对中国车企参与SEP许可池的市场信号意义。
items.ai_insight_en: This development highlights the continued consolidation of licensing frameworks for connected vehicles in Europe. The termination of parallel litigation alongside platform-based licensing suggests a preference for negotiated settlements among major SEP holders and automotive OEMs. Stakeholders should track how these bilateral and pool-based resolutions influence broader industry compliance trends.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["标题显示诺基亚已与中国汽车制造商吉利达成全球5G专利纠纷和解", "Avanci宣布与一家未具名的中国新汽车公司签署了4G和5G许可协议", "Avanci被许可人Longhorn IP终止了近期在德国针对吉利提起的诉讼"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Nokia reached a settlement resolving its global 5G standard essential patent dispute with Geely.", "Avanci secured new 4G and 5G licensing agreements with an additional unnamed Chinese vehicle manufacturer.", "Longhorn IP terminated its pending litigation against Geely within the German jurisdiction."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["Client relevance/客户相关性", "Practice note/实务观察", "What to monitor/后续观察"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Demonstrates active resolution pathways for SEP disputes involving automotive manufacturers and major licensors.", "Practice note: Monitor the interplay between individual settlements and multi-pool licensing platforms like Avanci.", "What to monitor: Observe procedural outcomes of terminated cases and future licensing announcements from Avanci."]
core_points_en: Nokia reached a settlement resolving its global 5G standard essential patent dispute with Geely.
Avanci secured new 4G and 5G licensing agreements with an additional unnamed Chinese vehicle manufacturer.
Longhorn IP terminated its pending litigation against Geely within the German jurisdiction.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Demonstrates active resolution pathways for SEP disputes involving automotive manufacturers and major licensors.
Practice note: Monitor the interplay between individual settlements and multi-pool licensing platforms like Avanci.
What to monitor: Observe procedural outcomes of terminated cases and future licensing announcements from Avanci.

items.title: SACEPO WP/G discusses outcomes of user consultation on the Guidelines 2026
items.title_zh: SACEPO工作组讨论2026指南用户咨询结果
items.title_en: SACEPO WP/G discusses outcomes of user consultation on the Guidelines 2026
items.summary: The SACEPO WP/G members met to discuss the responses to the public user consultation on the EPC, PCT-EPO, and the UP Guidelines
items.ai_summary_zh: EPO SACEPO工作组于5月12日召开会议，讨论2026版审查指南（含EPC、PCT-EPO及单一专利指南）的用户咨询反馈，共收到307条评论。会议规划了2027版指南的更新，包括支持无纸化流程的法律变更第三包、简化程序措施及引用UPC上诉法院判决。此外，讨论了G 1/24和G 1/25号决定的实施、抗体创造性、分案申请及异议程序等实务问题。
items.ai_insight_zh: EPO正加速推进无纸化流程并强化与UPC判例的衔接，申请人需关注2027版指南中关于修改依据说明的新义务。抗体创造性及异议程序中辅助请求的接纳标准仍是实务热点，建议持续跟踪Convergence of Practice项目的具体澄清文件。

items.title: FRAND battle between Huawei and TP-Link now focused at UK High Court
items.title_zh: 华为撤回UPC及慕尼黑侵权诉请，英法庭成FRAND焦点
items.title_en: FRAND battle between Huawei and TP-Link now focused at UK High Court
items.summary: Huawei has withdrawn its infringement claims against TP-Link at the UPC and Munich Regional Court and only the UK FRAND proceedings remain.
items.ai_summary_zh: 华为已撤回在统一专利法院（UPC）及慕尼黑地区法院对TP-Link的侵权指控。双方关于Wi-Fi 6技术的争议现集中于英国高等法院的FRAND费率设定程序。英国法官Richard Meade此前裁定 interim licence 费率，采用“中点法”计算，金额显著高于TP-Link提议。
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案显示英国高等法院在SEP全球费率裁决中的关键地位，即便存在UPC平行程序。法院采用“中点法”确定临时费率，为后续FRAND谈判提供重要参考基准。需关注英国判决对全球许可策略的影响及UPC与成员国法院管辖权的互动实践。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["华为撤回在UPC及慕尼黑地区法院对TP-Link的侵权诉请，仅保留英国FRAND程序", "英国高等法院首次向SEP持有人授予临时许可，费率按“中点法”确定，高于实施方提议", "争议聚焦Wi-Fi 6技术，华为同时在中国法院起诉TP-Link，但欧洲战场重心移至英国"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["英国高等法院在SEP FRAND费率设定中的主导地位得到强化，即使面对UPC竞争", "“中点法”作为临时费率计算依据，可能影响未来类似案件的损害赔偿或许可费估算逻辑", "需持续观察UPC撤诉后，剩余英国程序对全球许可谈判杠杆的具体影响及执行效果"]

items.title: Grünecker successful for Horl before the Hamburg Local Division | Grünecker
items.title_zh: Grünecker代理Horl在UPC汉堡地院胜诉
items.title_en: Grünecker successful for Horl before the Hamburg Local Division | Grünecker
items.summary: In first instance infringement proceedings for the client Horl 1993 GmbH, the Hamburg Local Division decided in favor of the client.
items.ai_summary_zh: 律所Grünecker宣布，其客户Horl 1993 GmbH在UPC汉堡地方分部提起的专利侵权一审程序中胜诉。法院认定被告Magna-Tec e.K.侵犯了原告持有的欧洲专利EP 4 117 857 B1，该专利涉及切削工具刃口的磨削及抛光装置系统。被告有权对该判决提起上诉。此案例展示了UPC在德国的早期司法实践及专利执行效力。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需警惕UPC在德国等地的专利执法力度。建议出口企业加强FTO分析，特别是针对机械类精密设备。若遭遇诉讼，应评估上诉策略及和解可能性，同时关注UPC判例对后续案件的影响，建立快速响应机制以应对跨境知识产权风险。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC汉堡地院裁定被告侵犯Horl公司欧洲专利EP 4 117 857 B1", "涉案专利保护切削工具刃口磨削抛光设备及固定系统技术", "被告享有对一审侵权判决提起上诉的法定权利"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["加强针对UPC管辖区域的专利自由实施（FTO）排查", "建立应对UPC侵权诉讼的快速法律响应与上诉评估机制"]

items.title: UK court observes “evidentiary void” in withdrawn Qualcomm 'no licence, no chips' claim
items.title_zh: 英法院指出高通“无许可无芯片”索赔存在证据真空
items.title_en: UK Tribunal Scraps Qualcomm 'No Licence, No Chips' Claim Over Evidentiary Gaps
items.summary: The Competition Appeal Tribunal has approved the scrapping of a £480 million opt-out collective action claim, flagging an absence of witnesses from Apple and Samsung and lacking evidence of refusal to supply
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国竞争上诉法庭批准撤销一项4.8亿英镑的退出式集体诉讼索赔。法庭指出该案存在“证据真空”，主要因为缺乏苹果和三星的证人，且缺少拒绝供应的确凿证据。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Competition Appeal Tribunal has approved the discontinuance of a £480 million opt-out collective action claim against Qualcomm. The tribunal highlighted a significant evidentiary void in the case, specifically noting the absence of witness testimony from key industry participants Apple and Samsung. Furthermore, the decision flagged a lack of concrete evidence supporting allegations of refusal to supply, leading to the withdrawal of the substantial competition law claim.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案凸显了在英国提起大规模SEP反垄断集体诉讼面临的举证门槛。实务中需关注关键下游厂商（如苹果、三星）的证言意愿及拒绝供应行为的证据链完整性。
items.ai_insight_en: This development underscores the rigorous evidentiary standards required for opt-out collective actions in the UK, particularly in complex SEP-related competition disputes. The reliance on direct testimony from major downstream manufacturers appears critical for establishing factual matrices such as refusal to supply. Rights holders and claimants should note that procedural withdrawals based on evidentiary deficiencies may signal heightened judicial scrutiny regarding the availability of key witnesses and documentary proof in future litigation.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国竞争上诉法庭批准撤销4.8亿英镑的集体诉讼索赔", "法庭指出案件存在证据真空，缺乏苹果和三星的证人", "案件中缺乏关于拒绝供应行为的确凿证据支持"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Competition Appeal Tribunal approved the discontinuance of a £480 million opt-out collective action claim against Qualcomm.", "The tribunal identified an evidentiary void due to the absence of witness testimony from Apple and Samsung.", "The claim lacked sufficient evidence to support allegations regarding a refusal to supply chips."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["Client relevance/客户相关性: 关注英国集体诉讼的高举证标准及关键证人…", "Practice note/实务观察: 强化对拒绝供应行为的直接证据收集，避免依赖间接…", "What to monitor/后续观察: 监测同类SEP反垄断诉讼中下游厂商参与度的…"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Highlights the high evidentiary threshold for UK opt-out collective actions, especially regarding witness availability from major tech firms.", "Practice note: Emphasizes the necessity of securing direct evidence and witness cooperation from key market participants like Apple or Samsung early in case assessment.", "What to monitor: Observe how tribunals assess evidentiary sufficiency in future SEP-related competition claims involving alleged refusal to supply."]
core_points_en: The Competition Appeal Tribunal approved the discontinuance of a £480 million opt-out collective action claim against Qualcomm.
The tribunal identified an evidentiary void due to the absence of witness testimony from Apple and Samsung.
The claim lacked sufficient evidence to support allegations regarding a refusal to supply chips.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Highlights the high evidentiary threshold for UK opt-out collective actions, especially regarding witness availability from major tech firms.
Practice note: Emphasizes the necessity of securing direct evidence and witness cooperation from key market participants like Apple or Samsung early in case assessment.
What to monitor: Observe how tribunals assess evidentiary sufficiency in future SEP-related competition claims involving alleged refusal to supply.

items.title: Importance du contentieux des marques devant le Tribunal de l’Union
items.title_zh: 欧盟普通法院2025年报：商标诉讼占四分之一，审理提速显著
items.title_en: Importance du contentieux des marques devant le Tribunal de l’Union
items.summary: Le Tribunal de l’Union vient de publier ses statistiques 2025 accompagnées d’une présentation par son greffier : Statistiques 2025 du Tribunal . Le contentieux de la propriété intellectuelle, en particulier des marques de l’Union, représente l’un des blocs les plus importants et les plus intensifs d’activité du Tribunal. 1. Volume et poids du contentieux PI devant le Tribunal En 2025, 989 affaires ont été introduites devant le Tribunal, dont 257 en matière de propriété intellectuelle (marques, d
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟普通法院发布2025年统计数据，知识产权案件占新收案件的26%，其中商标纠纷为核心。全年受理PI案件257件，结案303件，积压276件。PI案件平均审理周期约13-14个月，远快于全院平均的22个月，主要因针对EUIPO上诉委员会决定的诉讼流程标准化。PI与机构法、直接申诉并列为三大批量诉讼领域。
items.ai_insight_zh: 欧盟商标诉讼审理高效，中国企业应重视EUIPO阶段证据留存以应对后续司法审查。鉴于诉讼量大且集中，建议建立标准化应诉策略，利用较短审理周期快速解决争议，降低在欧品牌维权成本与不确定性。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["年欧盟普通法院受理257起IP案件，占总案量26%，商标为绝对主力", "IP案件平均审理周期13.1个月，显著低于全院平均22个月，效率较高", "绝大多数IP诉讼针对EUIPO上诉委员会决定，流程标准化程度高"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重视EUIPO行政阶段证据，为后续可能的司法诉讼奠定基础", "利用审理周期短优势，制定快速响应策略以降低品牌维权成本"]

items.title: Guidance: Correction to the designs register under section 21: 80928020008000
items.title_zh: 第21条下设计注册簿更正指南：80928020008000
items.title_en: Guidance: Correction to the designs register under section 21: 80928020008000
items.summary: Designs correction of error notices are used to notify any third party of a proposed correction of a clerical error made by the registry prior to a correction taking place.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局（UKIPO）发布了关于依据《1949年注册外观设计法》第21条更正设计注册簿中行政错误的指南。该指南指出，在正式进行更正前，需通过发布“错误更正通知”向第三方告知拟议的更正内容。此类通知旨在确保程序透明，允许利益相关方在最终修正前知悉并可能提出异议，从而保障注册簿的准确性与法律效力。
items.ai_insight_zh: 在英拥有外观设计的企业应密切关注UKIPO发布的此类更正通知，以防权利状态发生非预期变更。建议建立定期监控机制，一旦发现涉及自身权利的注册簿错误更正，应及时评估影响并在法定期限内提出异议或配合更正，以维护设计权的稳定性与商业价值。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["依据第21条更正设计注册簿中的行政错误需遵循特定程序", "更正前必须发布通知以告知第三方拟议的更正内容", "此举旨在保障程序透明度及第三方的知情权与异议权"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立监控机制，及时响应UKIPO的设计注册簿更正通知", "评估更正影响，依法行使异议权或配合修正以保权利稳定"]

items.title: Sisvel snags Asus as Wi-Fi multimode licensee; Malikie and ETRI launch patent suits
items.title_zh: Sisvel获华硕Wi-Fi许可；Malikie与ETRI发起诉讼
items.title_en: Sisvel snags Asus as Wi-Fi multimode licensee; Malikie and ETRI launch patent suits
items.summary: The developments continue a busy period in the Wi-Fi patent landscape, which has seen two new pools launched and several new deals signed this year

items.title: US patent rift laid bare in Vidal’s letter to European Commission
items.title_zh: 维达尔致信欧盟：揭示美国专利分歧与新视角
items.title_en: US patent rift laid bare in Vidal’s letter to European Commission
items.summary: The former USPTO director has offered Brussels an alternative view on patent remedies and SEPs to those of her predecessors.
items.ai_summary_zh: 前美国专利商标局（USPTO）局长凯瑟琳·维达尔致信欧盟委员会，就专利救济及标准必要专利（SEP）问题提出不同于其前任的观点。此举揭示了美国内部在SEP政策上的分歧，并可能影响欧盟正在进行的SEP监管框架讨论。文章强调了这一高层互动对跨大西洋知识产权政策协调的潜在冲击，以及其对全球SEP许可实践和执法标准的深远意义。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需密切关注欧盟对维达尔新立场的反应，这可能预示更严格的SEP禁令限制或FRAND费率调整。建议加强在欧洲的专利合规布局，积极参与FRAND谈判，并准备应对可能的政策波动，以规避高额许可费或禁令风险。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["前USPTO局长维达尔向欧盟提供关于专利救济的新观点", "信件内容凸显美国在SEP政策上存在显著内部意见分歧", "该动态可能干扰或重塑欧盟当前的SEP监管立法进程"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注欧盟对美方新立场反应，预判SEP监管风向变化", "强化欧洲专利合规与FRAND谈判策略，规避禁令风险"]

items.title: EU-funded IP cooperation expands into the Western Balkans
items.title_zh: 欧盟资助IP合作扩展至西巴尔干地区
items.title_en: EUIPO Launches EU-Funded IP Cooperation Project in Western Balkans
items.summary: New EU-funded cooperation project with enlargement partners supports alignment of European Union (EU) Intellectual Property (IP) standards ahead of EU accession.
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文摘要显示，一项新的欧盟资助合作项目正在与扩大伙伴国开展，旨在支持这些国家在加入欧盟之前实现知识产权标准的对齐。该项目由欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）发布，聚焦于通过合作促进法律与实践的协调，为未来的欧盟成员资格做准备。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) has announced a new cooperation project funded by the European Union. This initiative targets enlargement partners in the Western Balkans, aiming to support the alignment of their intellectual property standards with those of the European Union. The project serves as a preparatory measure for these countries ahead of potential future EU accession.
items.ai_insight_zh: Practice note / What to monitor：需持续观察该合作项目的具体实施范围、涵盖的知识产权领域（如商标、专利或外观设计）以及具体的时间表。鉴于正文信息有限，目前仅能确认其为入盟前的标准对齐举措，尚无法评估对特定行业权利人的直接影响。
items.ai_insight_en: This development highlights ongoing regulatory convergence efforts between the EU and candidate countries. For rights holders operating across Europe, monitoring the implementation of these alignment measures is relevant for understanding future market entry conditions and enforcement landscapes in the Western Balkans. The focus remains on legislative and administrative harmonization rather than immediate procedural changes for existing portfolios.

items.title: Asus signs up to Sisvel's Wi-Fi Multimode pool, ending litigation
items.title_zh: 华硕加入Sisvel Wi-Fi专利池并结束诉讼
items.title_en: Asus signs up to Sisvel's Wi-Fi Multimode pool, ending litigation
items.summary: Asus and Sisvel pool members end litigation with licence to the standard essential patents in Sisvel's Wi-Fi Multimode pool.
items.ai_summary_zh: 华硕已签署Sisvel Wi-Fi Multimode专利池许可协议，涵盖Wi-Fi 6和7标准必要专利。该协议终止了华硕与华为、飞利浦及Wilus之间的未决诉讼。此前双方在慕尼黑地方法院、曼海姆UPC地方法院及美国均有诉讼进程。慕尼黑法院近期在Wilus诉华硕案中进一步澄清了FRAND程序审查标准。财务条款未披露。
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案凸显德国法院在SEP FRAND争议中的关键角色，特别是慕尼黑法院对许可要约合规性的审查趋严。UPC作为新诉讼地，其程序进展与德国国内诉讼形成互动。企业需关注主要司法辖区对FRAND抗辩的审查标准变化，以及专利池许可对全球诉讼布局的影响。

items.title: Düsseldorf Local Division clarifies burden in PI proceedings
items.title_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫分院澄清临时禁令程序中的举证责任
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf Division Clarifies Burden of Proof for Urgency in Preliminary Injunction Proceedings
items.summary: Ottobock v Wilhelm Julius Teufel and MedEnvoy Global — Local Division Düsseldorf, 7 May 2026 (UPC_CFI_1928/2025)
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年5月7日，统一专利法院（UPC）杜塞尔多夫地方分院在Ottobock案中发布命令，澄清了临时禁令（PI）程序中关于“紧迫性”的举证责任。法院指出，申请人需具体说明从知晓侵权到申请禁令的时间线及调查步骤。若被告主张申请人存在推定知情或拖延，被告须先提供具体事实指标，随后举证责任才转移至申请人。该案明确了Rule 211.4 RoP的适用标准，避免了要求专利权人自证清白的严苛做法。
items.ai_summary_en: On May 7, 2026, the Unified Patent Court’s Düsseldorf Local Division issued an order in Ottobock v. Teufel and MedEnvoy Global, clarifying the burden of proof regarding urgency in preliminary injunction proceedings. The Court emphasized that applicants must concretely substantiate the timeline from initial awareness of a potentially infringing product to filing the application, detailing specific investigative steps taken. Crucially, the decision addresses Rule 211.4 of the Rules of Procedure concerning constructive knowledge. The Court held that while constructive knowledge exists where awareness should have occurred under normal circumstances, defendants bear the initial burden of providing concrete factual indicators suggesting earlier knowledge or dilatory conduct. Only after such indicators are established does the burden shift to the applicant. This approach prevents imposing an overly strict requirement on patent proprietors to prove a negative regarding early knowledge in every case.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC临时禁令时，应建立完善的侵权监控与证据保全机制。一旦获知潜在侵权，需详细记录内部调查、技术分析及法律评估的具体时间节点与行动理由，以证明未延误。同时，作为被告时可利用此判例，通过挖掘原告早期公开活动（如展会）来构建推定知情抗辩，迫使原告承担更重的解释义务。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling provides critical procedural clarity for rights holders and alleged infringers operating within the UPC system. For patent proprietors, maintaining detailed records of internal investigations, technical analyses, and legal assessments is essential to justify timelines and demonstrate prompt action upon discovering potential infringement. Conversely, defendants can leverage this precedent by scrutinizing the plaintiff’s public activities, such as trade show appearances, to construct arguments for constructive knowledge. This strategy may increase the evidentiary burden on plaintiffs, requiring them to provide more rigorous explanations for any perceived delays in seeking relief.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["申请人须具体证明从知晓侵权产品到提交PI申请的完整时间线及调查步骤", "被告主张推定知情时需先提供具体事实指标，随后举证责任才转移给申请人", "法院拒绝预设推定知情，避免让专利权人在每起案件中自证无早期知情可能"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Applicants must specifically demonstrate the complete timeline and investigative steps from discovering infringement to filing the preliminary injunction application.", "Defendants claiming constructive knowledge must first provide concrete factual indicators before the burden of proof shifts to the applicant.", "The Court refused to presume constructive knowledge automatically, avoiding requirements for patentees to disprove early awareness in every instance."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["企业需详尽记录发现侵权后的内部调查步骤与时间线，以证明行动及时性", "被告可利用原告早期公开活动构建推定知情抗辩，增加原告举证难度"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders must document internal investigation steps and timelines meticulously to demonstrate timely action after discovering potential infringement.", "Practice note: Defendants can utilize plaintiff public activities to build constructive knowledge defenses, thereby increasing the plaintiff's burden of proof.", "What to monitor: Future applications of Rule 211.4 regarding what constitutes sufficient factual indicators for shifting the burden of proof in urgency disputes."]
core_points_en: Applicants must specifically demonstrate the complete timeline and investigative steps from discovering infringement to filing the preliminary injunction application.
Defendants claiming constructive knowledge must first provide concrete factual indicators before the burden of proof shifts to the applicant.
The Court refused to presume constructive knowledge automatically, avoiding requirements for patentees to disprove early awareness in every instance.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders must document internal investigation steps and timelines meticulously to demonstrate timely action after discovering potential infringement.
Practice note: Defendants can utilize plaintiff public activities to build constructive knowledge defenses, thereby increasing the plaintiff's burden of proof.
What to monitor: Future applications of Rule 211.4 regarding what constitutes sufficient factual indicators for shifting the burden of proof in urgency disputes.

items.title: Merz v. Viatris on Appeal: Urgency Is Still Everything — But Not Every Signal Is Urgent
items.title_zh: Merz诉Viatris上诉案：紧迫性仍是关键但非所有信号都紧急
items.title_en: Merz v. Viatris on Appeal: Urgency Is Still Everything — But Not Every Signal Is Urgent
items.summary: This week, I have the pleasure of speaking in Amsterdam at C5’s 19th Annual Forum on Pharma & Biotech Patent Litigation in Europe, in a panel devoted to running and defending preliminary injunctions at the UPC and beyond. The programme expressly puts urgency, proportionality, timing, knowledge and delay at the centre of the discussion. Merz v. Viatris will inevitably be part of that conversation.
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC上诉法院撤销巴黎地方法院拒绝临时禁令的决定，裁定Viatris在法国销售仿制药FAMPRIDINE VIATRIS侵犯Merz的SPC权利，直至2026年7月25日。法院认定尽管Merz知晓潜在风险，但实际上市前的行政程序不构成不合理延误，确立了SPC诉讼中“紧迫性”需结合具体市场行为而非仅凭早期信号判断的标准，并施加高额每日罚款。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企在欧布局需警惕SPC保护期尾声的诉讼风险。早期监管信号（如获批、定价）不必然触发紧迫性，但一旦产品实质上市即面临禁令。建议建立动态监控机制，区分行政进程与市场投放，避免误判延误抗辩时机，同时评估高额罚金对供应链的影响。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC上诉法院推翻一审裁决，授予Merz针对Viatris的临时禁令直至SPC到期", "法院界定紧迫性：仿制药获批及定价不等于立即侵权，权利人无过早行动义务", "判决强调SPC保护期末尾的经济价值，违规将面临最高10万欧元/日的惩罚性赔偿"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["区分行政审批进度与实质市场投放，精准评估SPC诉讼中的紧迫性风险", "重视UPC禁令的高额日罚金威慑，提前制定应对策略以保障供应链稳定"]

items.title: Nokia cannot include cross-licence requirement in Acer/Asus arbitration offers
items.title_zh: 英上诉法院裁定：诺基亚不得在仲裁要约中强制要求交叉许可
items.title_en: UK Court of Appeal Bars Nokia from Including Cross-Licence Requirement in Arbitration Offers
items.summary: The English Court of Appeal ruling has implications for parallel disputes between the implementers and Nokia’s customers
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文仅显示英国上诉法院作出一项裁决，认定诺基亚（Nokia）不能在针对宏碁（Acer）和华硕（Asus）的仲裁要约中包含交叉许可要求。该裁决对实施者与诺基亚客户之间的平行争议具有潜在影响。由于正文材料极度匮乏，无法提供案件细节、法律依据或具体判决理由。
items.ai_summary_en: The English Court of Appeal has ruled that Nokia cannot include a cross-licence requirement in its arbitration offers to Acer and Asus. This decision addresses the permissibility of specific licensing conditions within arbitration frameworks. The ruling is noted to have implications for parallel disputes involving implementers and Nokia's customers, potentially influencing how similar licensing negotiations are structured in future SEP-related proceedings.
items.ai_insight_zh: Practice note: 需关注该判例是否确立SEP许可谈判中交叉许可条款的法律边界。What to monitor: 后续是否有更多实施者援引此判例挑战类似许可条件，以及其对全球FRAND费率确定的潜在辐射效应。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment clarifies the boundaries of acceptable terms within arbitration offers for standard essential patents. Rights holders and implementers should note the judicial stance on linking arbitration participation with cross-licensing obligations. The outcome may influence negotiation dynamics in ongoing and future SEP licensing discussions across European jurisdictions.

items.title: Technology licensing under the revised TTBER: clearer guidance for pools, FRAND and collective negotiations
items.title_zh: 修订版TTBER下技术许可：专利池、FRAND及集体谈判指南更清晰
items.title_en: EU Revised TTBER: Enhanced Guidance on Pools, FRAND and Collective Negotiations
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟委员会于2026年4月16日通过修订版《技术转移集体豁免条例》(TTBER)及配套指南，将于5月1日生效。新规保留原有安全港结构，但细化了针对专利池、标准必要技术(SEP)许可及集体谈判的合规指导。对于涉及复杂许可模式的企业，需重点评估协议结构以降低反垄断风险，确保符合TFEU第101(3)条要求。
items.ai_summary_en: On 16 April 2026, the European Commission adopted the revised Technology Transfer Block Exemption Regulation (TTBER) and accompanying Guidelines, effective 1 May 2026. The new framework replaces the 2014 rules, maintaining the core Article 101(3) TFEU safe harbour structure while providing detailed guidance on modern licensing practices. Key updates address technology pools, licensing negotiation groups (LNGs), data licensing, and standard-essential technology licensing. For rights holders and licensees, compliance now requires careful structuring of complex agreements to mitigate competition law risks beyond mere market share thresholds.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国科技企业应密切关注新规对SEP许可及专利池的合规要求，特别是在参与集体谈判时。建议重新审查现有许可协议，确保不包含核心限制条款，并利用新指南优化FRAND谈判策略，以降低在欧面临的反垄断调查与诉讼风险。
items.ai_insight_en: The updated framework signals that the European Commission expects parties to actively manage competition law risks in sophisticated licensing structures. While the basic block exemption mechanics remain unchanged, the detailed guidance on pools and collective negotiations means that standard template agreements may no longer suffice for high-value or multi-party arrangements. Rights holders should assess whether their current portfolio licences and negotiation protocols align with the new interpretative guidance to ensure continued eligibility for the safe harbour.

items.title: Unlocking innovation potential in Latin America and the Caribbean
items.title_zh: EPO报告：释放拉美及加勒比地区创新潜力
items.title_en: Unlocking innovation potential in Latin America and the Caribbean
items.summary: A joint EPO–ECLAC study highlights the role of intellectual property rights (IPR) in driving growth and productivity in Latin America and the Caribbean
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局（EPO）与联合国拉美经委会发布联合研究，指出知识产权密集型制造业占该地区GDP的13.6%并提供高薪岗位，但存在技术进口依赖度高、本土专利申请占比低的问题。报告强调需完善创新生态系统、加强产学研合作及技术转让，以将创新转化为可持续价值，并建议将IP政策融入更广泛的生产发展策略中。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在拓展拉美市场时，应重视当地对高附加值技术的需求及IP密集型产业的溢价效应。鉴于该地区本土创新能力尚待提升，中国企业可寻求与当地科研机构或高校建立技术转移与合作伙伴关系，利用其人才储备，同时需注意防范因专利布局薄弱带来的潜在侵权风险，优化全球IP战略。

items.title: LD Brussels, May 7, 2026, Cost Decision, UPC_CFI_2265/2025
items.title_zh: UPC布鲁塞尔地院：费用决定，权利放弃需明示
items.title_en: LD Brussels, May 7, 2026, Cost Decision, UPC_CFI_2265/2025
items.summary: Waiver of rights requires an explicit statement – prior admissions in first instance proceedings do not constitute a waiver (R. 150 RoP)
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC布鲁塞尔地方法院在YEALINK案中裁定，权利放弃需权利人明确声明，一审中的承认不构成后续费用程序的权利放弃。法院禁止同时适用部分胜诉降低上限与成本抵销的“双重”做法，维持全额上限并直接扣除被告合理费用。当事人自行安排的同声传译费不可回收。此外，若实体审理 pending，费用申请期限从实体判决送达起算；PI上诉费用上限可基于比例原则调减。
items.ai_insight_zh: 该裁决明确了UPC程序中费用计算的严格标准，特别是禁止“双重”调整机制，保护了当事人的预期利益。实务中需注意权利放弃的形式要件及口译费用的承担主体。对于涉及临时禁令的案件，应关注上诉阶段费用上限的比例性审查风险，以及实体审理期间费用申请的时效起算点。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["权利放弃需明确声明，一审承认对方胜诉不构成后续费用程序的权利放弃", "部分胜诉降低上限与成本抵销不得同时适用，避免对申请人造成双重惩罚", "当事人自行安排的同声传译费用不属于可回收诉讼费用，仅限法院安排者"]

items.title: LD The Hague, May 11, 2026, Order, UPC-CFI_478/2025, UPC_CFI_585/2026
items.title_zh: UPC海牙分部：证据保全令需满足四项累积条件
items.title_en: UPC Hague: Evidence Production Orders Require Cumulative Conditions and Proportionality
items.summary: Under R. 190 RoP and Art. 59(1) UPCA, evidence production orders require four cumulative conditions: plausible evidence, specified evidence within the other party’s control, confidentiality protection, and proportionality
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC海牙地方法院裁定，依据《程序规则》第190条及《统一专利法院协定》第59(1)条，发布证据保全令需满足四项累积条件：初步证据、对方控制下的特定证据、保密保护及比例原则。法院援引Winnow v Orbisk等先例，指出已授权欧洲专利具有有效性推定，除非明显无效。鉴于侵权验证需物理测试，法院批准获取部分产品样本及营销材料，但基于比例原则缩减了样本数量。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Local Division in The Hague clarified the requirements for evidence production orders under Rule 190 of the Rules of Procedure and Article 59(1) of the Unified Patent Court Agreement. The Court established that four cumulative conditions must be met: plausible evidence, specified evidence within the opposing party's control, confidentiality protection, and proportionality. Citing Winnow v Orbisk and Oppo v Panasonic, the judgment affirmed that granted European patents carry a presumption of validity, which is only displaced by clear-cut invalidity. The Court reduced the requested physical samples from six to two variants based on proportionality, while upholding the quantity needed for ballistic testing where infringement verification depends on direct physical analysis rather than mathematical extrapolation.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了UPC在证据保全阶段的审查标准，强调了对已授权专利有效性的尊重及比例原则的适用。实务中，申请人需确保证据请求的具体性与必要性，避免过度索取。对于依赖物理测试认定侵权的案件，提前规划测试方案并论证样本获取的必要性至关重要。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision reinforces the structured approach of the UPC towards pre-trial evidence gathering, balancing the rights holder's need to verify infringement against the defendant's interest in avoiding disproportionate burdens. It confirms that while patent validity is presumed, the specificity and necessity of requested evidence remain subject to rigorous judicial scrutiny. For practitioners, the ruling highlights that requests for physical products must be tightly scoped to what is strictly necessary for technical verification, particularly when infringement claims rely on empirical testing outcomes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["证据保全令需满足初步证据、特定性、保密性及比例原则四项累积条件", "已授权欧洲专利享有有效性推定，仅明显无效情形可推翻该推定", "为验证依赖测试的侵权，可下令提供物理样本，但需符合比例原则限制范围"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Rule 190 RoP and Article 59(1) UPCA require plausible evidence, specific controlled evidence, confidentiality, and proportionality.", "Granted European patents benefit from a validity presumption unless a clear-cut case of invalidity is demonstrated at this stage.", "Physical samples may be ordered for necessary testing, but requests are strictly limited by proportionality principles."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["Client relevance/客户相关性: 明确UPC证据保全门槛，指导专利维权策略", "Practice note/实务观察: 论证样本获取必要性时需结合具体测试需求与比例原则", "What to monitor/后续观察: 关注UPC对“明显无效”标准的进一步界定及…"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Clarifies the high threshold for obtaining evidence production orders, requiring precise justification of necessity and proportionality.", "Practice note: When seeking physical samples for test-dependent infringement, limit requests to the minimum viable set to satisfy proportionality requirements.", "What to monitor: How the UPC further defines 'clear-cut' invalidity cases that might displace the presumption of validity during evidence stages."]
core_points_en: Rule 190 RoP and Article 59(1) UPCA require plausible evidence, specific controlled evidence, confidentiality, and proportionality.
Granted European patents benefit from a validity presumption unless a clear-cut case of invalidity is demonstrated at this stage.
Physical samples may be ordered for necessary testing, but requests are strictly limited by proportionality principles.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Clarifies the high threshold for obtaining evidence production orders, requiring precise justification of necessity and proportionality.
Practice note: When seeking physical samples for test-dependent infringement, limit requests to the minimum viable set to satisfy proportionality requirements.
What to monitor: How the UPC further defines 'clear-cut' invalidity cases that might displace the presumption of validity during evidence stages.

items.title: LD The Hague, May 4, 2026, Order, UPC_CFI_2028/2025, UPC_CFI_2031/2025
items.title_zh: UPC海牙分院：单方证据保全令复审及侵权可能性认定规则
items.title_en: LD The Hague, May 4, 2026, Order, UPC_CFI_2028/2025, UPC_CFI_2031/2025
items.summary: A review under R.197.3 RoP only benefits the party that files it; co-defendants who do not seek review remain bound by the original order
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC海牙地方法院裁定，依R.197.3 RoP提出的复审仅惠及申请人，未申请的同案被告仍受原令约束。法院认定，依据UPCA第60(1)条证明侵权“具有可能性”的证明标准低于临时措施的“高度确定性”，但需排除无根据的“钓鱼式”取证。供应商行为造成的不确定性足以使间接侵权成立。此外，数据保留义务不阻碍证据保全，且申请人的披露义务不涵盖被告内部事实。在B2B小众市场中，因实地购买不可行，保全措施被视为符合比例原则。
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了UPC程序中证据保全与复审的关键程序边界。实务中，共同被告需注意单独行动的法律后果。申请人应利用供应链不确定性构建侵权可能性，同时注意披露义务的界限。对于权利人而言，在难以进行常规取证的细分B2B市场，主张保全措施的比例正当性更具说服力。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["复审申请仅对提出方有效，未申请的同案被告不受复审结果影响，维持原命令效力", "证明侵权可能性门槛较低，无需达到临时措施的高度确定性，但禁止无依据的钓鱼式取证", "被告供应商的不透明行为可佐证间接侵权可能性；数据保留义务不妨碍临时证据转移", "在个人化分销的B2B市场，若实地购买不可行且易惊动被告，证据保全措施符合比例原则"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["Client relevance/客户相关性: 共同诉讼策略风险及证据保全申请的举证重点", "Practice note/实务观察: 区分“可能性”与“确定性”证明标准，合理利用供…", "What to monitor/后续观察: UPC对B2B小众市场保全措施比例原则的具…"]

items.title: Meet the Lifetime Achievement winner and the finalists for the 10th anniversary of the DesignEuropa Awards
items.title_zh: EUIPO公布DesignEuropa奖终身成就奖得主及决赛选手
items.title_en: EUIPO Announces Philippe Starck as Lifetime Achievement Winner of DesignEuropa Awards 2026
items.summary: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) is honoured to announce that Philippe Starck has been selected as the Lifetime Achievement Award winner of the DesignEuropa Awards 2026.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）宣布，著名设计师Philippe Starck被选为2026年DesignEuropa奖项的终身成就奖得主。该公告旨在庆祝DesignEuropa奖项成立十周年，并公布了其他决赛入围者名单。此活动由EUIPO主办，旨在提高公众对设计和知识产权保护的认识。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) has announced that renowned designer Philippe Starck is the recipient of the Lifetime Achievement Award at the 2026 DesignEuropa Awards. This announcement coincides with the tenth anniversary of the awards program, which aims to highlight the importance of design and intellectual property protection within the European Union. The EUIPO also released the list of finalists for this year's competition.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This official recognition underscores the EUIPO's continued focus on promoting registered designs and the value of IP in the creative industries. For rights holders and design teams, such high-profile endorsements reinforce the cultural and commercial significance of protecting aesthetic creations under EU law. Stakeholders should note the alignment between these awards and broader EU policy goals regarding innovation and design-led growth.

items.title: Can the name of a fictional character be a Trade Mark? Lessons from Obélix
items.title_zh: 欧法判例：虚构角色名Obélix能否注册商标？
items.title_en: EU General Court: Fictional Character Names Can Function as Trademarks
items.summary: The General Court’s judgment in Case T‑24/25 (OBELIX) provides guidance on the treatment of trade marks derived from fictional characters, particularly ...
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟普通法院在Case T-24/25中撤销EUIPO上诉委员会决定，支持出版商Les Éditions Albert René。争议涉及波兰个人注册的“OBELIX”火器商标。出版商主张该注册损害其知名漫画角色声誉。此前机构认为公众仅视其为角色参考而非商标来源标识。法院裁定上诉委员会评估 flawed，指出角色关联不自动排除商标功能，需综合考量声誉及消费者感知，为虚构角色商标保护提供重要指引。
items.ai_summary_en: The EU General Court annulled the EUIPO Board of Appeal’s decision in Case T‑24/25, supporting publisher Les Éditions Albert René. The dispute concerned a Polish individual’s registration of the word mark OBELIX for firearms and explosives. The publisher argued this registration damaged the reputation of its famous comic character. Previous EUIPO bodies held that consumers viewed the term merely as a character reference rather than a trademark source indicator. The General Court ruled the Board’s assessment was flawed, stating that association with a fictional character does not automatically preclude trademark function. The judgment emphasizes the need to comprehensively evaluate reputation and consumer perception, providing significant guidance for protecting trademarks derived from fictional characters.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局IP时，若使用知名IP元素，需警惕侵权风险。反之，若拥有原创角色，应积极积累证据以证明其作为商标的来源识别功能，而不仅是装饰性使用，从而在异议或无效程序中有效维护权益。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling clarifies that fictional character names are not inherently excluded from trademark protection. Rights holders must actively accumulate evidence demonstrating that the public perceives the name as a source identifier, distinct from decorative or descriptive use. Conversely, businesses utilizing elements from well-known intellectual property must exercise caution to avoid infringement risks associated with reputational damage. In opposition or invalidation proceedings, robust proof of acquired distinctiveness and commercial origin perception is critical for successfully defending or challenging such registrations within the European Union market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院裁定虚构角色名可具商标功能，推翻EUIPO关于仅为角色参考的认定", "强调需证明相关公众将标志视为商业来源指示，而非单纯内容描述", "确立了处理基于虚构角色的商标无效申请时声誉与混淆可能性的审查标准"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The court ruled fictional character names can possess trademark function, overturning the EUIPO finding that they serve only as character references.", "It is essential to prove relevant consumers perceive the sign as an indicator of commercial origin, rather than solely as a description of content.", "The judgment establishes review standards for assessing reputation and likelihood of confusion in invalidation actions involving fictional character marks."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["原创角色需强化来源识别证据，避免被认定为单纯装饰或描述", "使用他人知名IP元素需谨慎，防范因声誉受损引发的无效宣告风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Owners of original characters should strengthen evidence of source identification to prevent findings of mere decorative or descriptive use.", "Practice note: Users of third-party well-known IP elements must proceed cautiously to mitigate invalidation risks triggered by claims of reputational harm.", "What to monitor: Future EUIPO and CJEU applications of these standards regarding consumer perception of fictional character signs in diverse goods categories."]
core_points_en: The court ruled fictional character names can possess trademark function, overturning the EUIPO finding that they serve only as character references.
It is essential to prove relevant consumers perceive the sign as an indicator of commercial origin, rather than solely as a description of content.
The judgment establishes review standards for assessing reputation and likelihood of confusion in invalidation actions involving fictional character marks.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Owners of original characters should strengthen evidence of source identification to prevent findings of mere decorative or descriptive use.
Practice note: Users of third-party well-known IP elements must proceed cautiously to mitigate invalidation risks triggered by claims of reputational harm.
What to monitor: Future EUIPO and CJEU applications of these standards regarding consumer perception of fictional character signs in diverse goods categories.

items.title: ‘Porcelaine de Limoges’ becomes EU’s first craft and industrial GI
items.title_zh: 利摩日瓷器成欧盟首个工艺与工业地理标志
items.title_en: Porcelaine de Limoges Becomes EU’s First Registered Craft and Industrial Geographical Indication
items.summary: The designation gives the historic Limoges porcelain industry EU-wide protection under a new regime aimed at safeguarding Europe’s craft and industrial heritage.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟正式授予“利摩日瓷器”（Porcelaine de Limoges）地理标志保护，使其成为该新制度下首个获此认定的工艺与工业产品。此举旨在通过欧盟范围内的法律保护， safeguarding 欧洲传统手工艺与工业遗产。该认定确立了严格的生产标准与地域限制，强化了品牌独特性，为其他非农业类传统工艺品的知识产权保护树立了先例，标志着欧盟在扩展地理标志适用范围上的重要政策进展。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Commission has officially granted geographical indication (GI) protection to 'Porcelaine de Limoges,' marking the first successful registration under the new EU regime dedicated to craft and industrial products. This designation provides comprehensive legal protection across all member states, aiming to safeguard Europe’s traditional manufacturing heritage and artisanal skills. The ruling establishes strict production standards and geographic limitations, ensuring that only porcelain produced in the specific Limoges region meets the criteria. This precedent strengthens brand distinctiveness for historic industries and signals a significant policy expansion of the GI framework beyond agricultural goods, offering a new pathway for protecting non-agricultural cultural assets within the internal market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应关注欧盟地理标志保护范围的扩大趋势，特别是针对陶瓷等传统优势产业。出口企业需提前排查产品命名与产地标识合规性，避免侵权风险。同时，可借鉴此案例，探索中国特色工艺品在欧申请GI保护的可行性，以构建高端品牌形象并提升市场竞争力。
items.ai_insight_en: The expansion of the EU geographical indication system to include craft and industrial products represents a strategic shift in intellectual property protection. Stakeholders should monitor this trend, particularly if operating in sectors like ceramics or traditional manufacturing where origin and reputation are key value drivers. Companies exporting to Europe must review product naming and origin labeling practices to ensure compliance with these evolving standards. Furthermore, producers of distinctive regional crafts may consider exploring GI eligibility to enhance brand equity and differentiate their offerings in the competitive European market, leveraging this new protective mechanism for long-term commercial advantage.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["利摩日瓷器获欧盟首个工艺与工业地理标志认定", "新制度旨在保护欧洲传统手工艺与工业文化遗产", "提供欧盟范围内统一保护并树立行业知识产权先例"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Porcelaine de Limoges is the first product registered as a craft and industrial geographical indication under the new EU regulatory framework.", "The new regime aims to protect and preserve Europe's traditional handicrafts and industrial cultural heritage through unified legal measures.", "This registration provides EU-wide protection and sets a crucial intellectual property precedent for other non-agricultural traditional crafts."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注欧盟GI范围扩大，排查陶瓷等出口产品合规风险", "探索中国工艺品在欧申请GI保护，提升品牌竞争力"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor the expansion of EU GI scope to assess risks for ceramic exports and verify compliance of product naming and origin labels.", "Practice note: Evaluate the feasibility of registering Chinese traditional crafts as GIs in the EU to strengthen brand positioning and market competitiveness.", "What to monitor: Track subsequent registrations under the new craft and industrial GI regime to identify emerging precedents and enforcement patterns."]
core_points_en: Porcelaine de Limoges is the first product registered as a craft and industrial geographical indication under the new EU regulatory framework.
The new regime aims to protect and preserve Europe's traditional handicrafts and industrial cultural heritage through unified legal measures.
This registration provides EU-wide protection and sets a crucial intellectual property precedent for other non-agricultural traditional crafts.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor the expansion of EU GI scope to assess risks for ceramic exports and verify compliance of product naming and origin labels.
Practice note: Evaluate the feasibility of registering Chinese traditional crafts as GIs in the EU to strengthen brand positioning and market competitiveness.
What to monitor: Track subsequent registrations under the new craft and industrial GI regime to identify emerging precedents and enforcement patterns.

items.title: The UPC Clarifies The Threshold For “imminent Infringement”
items.title_zh: UPC澄清“即将侵权”的认定门槛
items.title_en: The UPC Clarifies The Threshold For “imminent Infringement”
items.summary: On 13 August, the UPC Court of Appeal held that a marketing authorisation alone is not enough, but once a health technology assessment (HTA), pricing and reimbursement are completed, imminent infringement may be established. Pharmaceutical companies must therefore monitor regulatory steps closely to act in time.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2024年8月13日，统一专利法院（UPC）上诉法院作出裁决，明确了“即将侵权”的认定标准。法院指出，仅持有上市许可不足以构成即将侵权；但当健康技术评估（HTA）、定价及报销程序完成后，即可确立即将侵权。制药企业需密切监控监管步骤以及时采取行动。
items.ai_insight_zh: 该裁决提高了主张即将侵权的证明门槛，强调商业准备阶段的实质性进展。权利人在寻求临时禁令时需提供更充分的证据，而被诉方则可利用监管流程未完成作为抗辩理由。实务中需重点关注HTA及定价报销的具体时间节点。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC上诉法院裁定仅凭上市许可不足以证明即将侵权发生", "完成健康技术评估、定价及报销后，可确立即将侵权的法律状态", "制药公司需紧密监控相关监管流程以确保及时采取法律行动"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["Client relevance/客户相关性：影响药企在UPC提起或应对即将侵权诉讼的…", "Practice note/实务观察：举证重点从行政授权转向市场准入实质步骤", "What to monitor/后续观察：关注各成员国HTA及定价报销流程对侵权认定的…"]

items.title: LD Düsseldorf, May 6, 2026, Order, UPC_CFI_1536/2026
items.title_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫地院：存在证据灭失风险时，展会单方检查正当
items.title_en: LD Düsseldorf, May 6, 2026, Order, UPC_CFI_1536/2026
items.summary: Ex parte inspection at trade fairs justified where concrete risk of evidence destruction exists (Art. 60 UPCA, R. 192, 197 RoP)
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫地院裁定，当存在具体证据灭失风险时，允许在展会对被控侵权机器进行单方检查。法院认定申请人已证明被告可能移除设备或通过软件更新销毁证据，且无其他获取途径。法院确认证据保全中仅需证明侵权可能性，无需审查专利有效性；且证据保全的紧迫性评估不同于临时措施，延迟申请不自动导致驳回。鉴于检查损害极小，法院免除了申请人的担保金要求。
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了UPC在证据保全程序中的低门槛标准，特别是将侵权举证责任与专利有效性审查分离，并区分了不同救济措施的紧迫性判断标准。实务中，权利人在面对高价值、易通过软件更新篡改证据的设备时，应充分利用展会窗口期申请单方检查，并注意论证证据灭失的具体风险而非仅依赖时间紧迫性。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["存在具体证据灭失风险时，UPC支持在展会进行单方检查以保全证据", "证据保全程序中仅需证明侵权可能性，无需审查专利有效性或解决无效争议", "证据保全的紧迫性评估独立于临时措施，时间延迟不必然导致申请被驳回", "若检查对被告造成损害极小且需避免延误，法院可免除申请人的担保金要求"]

items.title: LD Milan, May 5, 2026, Decision, UPC_CFI_727/2024
items.title_zh: UPC米兰法院：EPO审查历史可辅助解释权利要求，逾期证据不可采纳
items.title_en: LD Milan, May 5, 2026, Decision, UPC_CFI_727/2024
items.summary: Prosecution file statements before the EPO, while not binding, may guide claim interpretation as reflecting the skilled person’s view (Art. 69 EPC, Art. 24(1)(c) UPCA)
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC米兰地方法院在2026年5月5日判决中裁定，EPO审查过程中的陈述虽无约束力，但可作为本领域技术人员视角的参考以辅助解释权利要求。法院因被控产品缺乏“抵接”特征且符合现有技术，判定不侵权并确认部分权利要求无效。此外，法院以违反前置程序原则为由，驳回了被告在中期会议期间首次提交的实物证据。
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案凸显UPC对程序合规性的严格要求，特别是证据提交的时效性。实务中需重视EPO审查档案在侵权诉讼中的解释价值，并在答辩中充分论证创造性步骤的具体逻辑，避免仅凭断言。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EPO审查历史陈述虽无约束力，但可反映本领域技术人员视角，辅助解释权利要求", "严格解释“抵接”特征导致涉案专利因现有技术D3丧失新颖性，同时认定被告不侵权", "依据Formstein抗辩，若被控产品对应现有技术实施例，则排除字面及等同侵权", "违反UPC前置程序原则，在中期会议首次提交的实物证据因披露不及时且来源不明被驳回"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["Client relevance/客户相关性: UPC诉讼中需严格遵循证据提交时限，避…", "Practice note/实务观察: EPO审查历史可能成为权利要求解释的关键依据…", "What to monitor/后续观察: 关注UPC上诉法院对审查历史解释效力及Fo…"]

items.title: LD Düsseldorf, 11 May 2026, Order, UPC_CFI_1034/2025 & UPC_CFI_931/2026
items.title_zh: 杜塞尔多夫地方法院UPC裁决：保密令不限于商业秘密
items.title_en: LD Düsseldorf, 11 May 2026, Order, UPC_CFI_1034/2025 & UPC_CFI_931/2026
items.summary: Confidentiality orders are not limited to trade secrets, they cover any information requiring confidentiality, balanced against the parties’ competing interests
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫分部裁定，保密令不仅限于商业秘密，涵盖任何需保密信息，需平衡各方利益。法院确认可基于美国出口管制风险签发保密令，无需详尽调查；允许当事人申请限制自身访问证据的“自我指向”保密令；且保密俱乐部中自然人参与要求可由受令方单方放弃。法院未决诉讼特权是否独立构成保密令基础。
items.ai_insight_zh: 该裁决明确了UPC程序中保密措施的灵活性，特别是在处理跨境合规风险（如美国出口管制）时降低了举证门槛。实务中，当事人可利用“自我指向”保密令策略解决合同或法律披露障碍，同时需注意法院尚未明确诉讼特权作为独立保密依据的地位，后续案件可能对此进一步澄清。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["保密令范围不限于商业秘密，取决于信息保密需求及利益平衡，非仅看是否构成商业秘密", "若充分证明违反美国出口管制的风险，法院可签发保密令，无需对相关法律范围进行详细调查", "当事人可申请限制自身访问证据的保密令，且可单方放弃保密俱乐部中自然人参与的强制性要求"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["Client relevance/客户相关性: 涉及跨国诉讼的企业可利用此裁决管理出口…", "Practice note/实务观察: 律师在起草保密令请求时，可主张更广泛的保密利益…", "What to monitor/后续观察: 关注未来判例是否将诉讼特权确立为独立的保密…"]

items.title: LD Düsseldorf, May 12, 2026, Order, UPC_CFI_1747/2025
items.title_zh: UPC杜塞：临时措施中申请人需充分证明侵权可能性
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf: Strict Proof Standards for Infringement in Provisional Measures
items.summary: The applicant bears the burden of proof for infringement in provisional measures and must demonstrate with sufficient certainty that infringement is more likely than not (Art. 62 UPCA, R. 211 RoP)
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫地方法院裁定，在临时措施程序中，申请人承担证明侵权的举证责任，且须以足够确定性证明侵权可能性大于非侵权。法院认定仅凭被告网站和宣传材料不足以建立复杂技术领域的侵权证据链，因未针对单一产品进行特征比对。由于侵权未成立，法院无需审查专利有效性及紧迫性等其他要件。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Local Division in Düsseldorf dismissed a request for provisional measures, emphasizing that applicants bear the burden of proving infringement with sufficient certainty under Article 62 UPCA and Rule 211 RoP. The Court ruled that promotional materials alone are insufficient for complex technologies requiring feature-by-feature analysis. Furthermore, method claims construed as relating to a single product require all features to be present in one appliance; distinct treatment phases using different aligners cannot be combined to establish infringement. Speculative reasoning failed to meet the required standard of proof.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了UPC临时措施中较高的举证门槛，特别是在多阶段治疗等复杂技术场景中。权利人若仅依赖公开宣传材料而缺乏独立技术分析，极易因证据不足被驳回。实务中需注重对单一实物产品的特征比对分析。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision clarifies the high evidentiary threshold for obtaining provisional measures at the UPC, particularly in multi-stage technical processes like clear aligner therapy. Rights holders must conduct independent technical assessments of specific accused products rather than relying on general marketing claims. The ruling reinforces that infringement analysis must strictly adhere to the single-product requirement when claims are construed accordingly, preventing the aggregation of features across different implementation stages.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["申请人须在临时措施中以足够确定性证明侵权可能性大于非侵权，承担主要举证责任", "对于单一产品权利要求，所有特征必须在单个器具中实现，不同阶段不能组合认定侵权", "仅依赖宣传材料和推测性证言无法满足复杂技术领域临时措施的严格举证标准"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Applicants must demonstrate infringement is more likely than not, bearing the full burden of presentation and proof for facts establishing infringement.", "All claim features must be realized in a single appliance where the patent relates to a single product; separate phases cannot be combined.", "Promotional materials and speculative witness statements are inadequate evidence for provisional measures in technically complex fields."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["Client relevance/客户相关性: 关注UPC临时禁令申请中的证据准备标准…", "Practice note/实务观察: 复杂技术案件需进行细致的单产品特征比对，而非组…", "What to monitor/后续观察: UPC其他分部是否跟进此严格的举证责任解释…"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders seeking provisional measures must secure robust, product-specific technical evidence rather than relying on public promotional materials.", "Practice note: Ensure infringement arguments align with the single-appliance requirement for method claims, avoiding combination of features from different stages.", "What to monitor: Whether other UPC divisions adopt similarly strict interpretations regarding the sufficiency of evidence for complex, multi-stage technologies."]
core_points_en: Applicants must demonstrate infringement is more likely than not, bearing the full burden of presentation and proof for facts establishing infringement.
All claim features must be realized in a single appliance where the patent relates to a single product; separate phases cannot be combined.
Promotional materials and speculative witness statements are inadequate evidence for provisional measures in technically complex fields.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders seeking provisional measures must secure robust, product-specific technical evidence rather than relying on public promotional materials.
Practice note: Ensure infringement arguments align with the single-appliance requirement for method claims, avoiding combination of features from different stages.
What to monitor: Whether other UPC divisions adopt similarly strict interpretations regarding the sufficiency of evidence for complex, multi-stage technologies.

items.title: Porcelaine de Limoges becomes the first EU craft GI
items.title_zh: 利摩日瓷器成为首个欧盟手工业地理标志
items.title_en: Porcelaine de Limoges Becomes First EU Craft and Industrial Geographical Indication
items.summary: The registration marks the first protection under the European Union system for craft and industrial geographical indications (CIGIs)
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文摘要显示，利摩日瓷器（Porcelaine de Limoges）的注册标志着欧盟系统下对手工业和工业地理标志（CIGIs）的首次保护。这是该新制度下的首例注册案例。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) has registered Porcelaine de Limoges as the first geographical indication under the new EU system for craft and industrial geographical indications (CIGIs). This registration marks a significant milestone, establishing the initial protection framework for non-agricultural products within the Union. The case demonstrates the operationalization of the CIGI regime, offering rights holders in the crafts and industrial sectors a new avenue for brand protection and market differentiation across member states.
items.ai_insight_zh: Practice note: 需关注欧盟手工业和工业地理标志（CIGIs）制度的后续实施细节及更多注册案例。What to monitor: 观察该制度对传统工艺产品市场准入及品牌保护的实际影响。
items.ai_insight_en: This development introduces a new layer of intellectual property protection for European manufacturers of traditional crafts and industrial goods. Rights holders should assess whether their products meet the specific criteria for CIGI registration to leverage this new regime. Monitoring the EUIPO's subsequent decisions will clarify the scope of protection and enforcement mechanisms available under this novel legal framework.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["标题显示利摩日瓷器成为首个获得保护的欧盟手工业地理标志", "原文摘要确认此次注册是欧盟手工业和工业地理标志系统下的首次保护"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Porcelaine de Limoges is the first product to receive protection under the EU's new craft and industrial geographical indications system.", "The EUIPO registration establishes a precedent for protecting non-agricultural traditional crafts and industrial goods across the European Union."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["Client relevance/客户相关性", "Practice note/实务观察", "What to monitor/后续观察"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Manufacturers of traditional crafts and industrial goods should evaluate eligibility for CIGI registration to secure pan-EU brand protection.", "Practice note: The Porcelaine de Limoges case serves as the primary reference for interpreting application requirements and substantive criteria under the new CIGI regulation.", "What to monitor: Future EUIPO registrations and potential oppositions will define the practical boundaries and commercial impact of the CIGI system."]
core_points_en: Porcelaine de Limoges is the first product to receive protection under the EU's new craft and industrial geographical indications system.
The EUIPO registration establishes a precedent for protecting non-agricultural traditional crafts and industrial goods across the European Union.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Manufacturers of traditional crafts and industrial goods should evaluate eligibility for CIGI registration to secure pan-EU brand protection.
Practice note: The Porcelaine de Limoges case serves as the primary reference for interpreting application requirements and substantive criteria under the new CIGI regulation.
What to monitor: Future EUIPO registrations and potential oppositions will define the practical boundaries and commercial impact of the CIGI system.

items.title: UPC security for costs: role follows the proceedings, not the party (UPC_CoA_21/2026)
items.title_zh: UPC费用担保：角色随程序而定而非当事人身份
items.title_en: UPC Security for Costs: Role Depends on Procedural Stage, Not Party Identity
items.summary: Court docket: Court of Appeal
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC上诉法院在Suinno诉微软案中裁定，依据《统一专利法院协定》第69(4)条，“申请人”指启动程序者。一审侵权原告无权请求被告提供费用担保；二审中仅被上诉人有权向上诉人请求担保。若双方均上诉，仅可就对方上诉费用请求担保。此裁决澄清了不同诉讼阶段费用担保的请求权主体，强调程序角色决定权利归属，排除了一审被告反诉撤销时的担保请求权。
items.ai_summary_en: In Suinno v. Microsoft, the UPC Court of Appeal clarified that under Article 69(4) UPCA, an 'applicant' is defined as the party initiating legal proceedings. Consequently, only this party can be ordered to provide security for costs. At first instance, an infringement claimant cannot request security from the defendant, even if the defendant files a counterclaim for revocation. In appeal proceedings, the appellant is considered the applicant. Therefore, only the respondent may request security for costs regarding the appeal. If both parties appeal, each may only seek security for the other’s appeal costs. This ruling establishes that procedural roles, rather than static party identities like claimant or defendant, determine the right to request security for costs across different litigation stages.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在UPC应诉时，需精准识别程序阶段以主张或抗辩费用担保。作为一审被告，无需担心原告以反诉为由索要担保；若发起上诉，需注意对方可能针对己方上诉费用提出担保请求。建议提前评估财务风险，合理规划诉讼策略，避免因程序误解导致不必要的资金冻结或败诉成本增加。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment provides critical clarity on cost security mechanisms within the Unified Patent Court system. Litigants must accurately identify their procedural role at each stage to assert or defend against security for costs claims. First-instance defendants can rely on this precedent to reject plaintiff demands for security, reducing initial financial pressure. Conversely, parties initiating appeals should anticipate potential counter-requests for security from respondents and prepare accordingly. Understanding these distinctions helps in assessing financial risks and planning litigation strategies effectively, avoiding unnecessary fund freezes or increased costs due to procedural misunderstandings.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["定义“申请人”为启动法律程序者，仅其可被要求提供费用担保", "一审侵权原告无权请求被告提供费用担保，即使被告提出反诉", "二审中仅被上诉人有权向上诉人请求费用担保，双方上诉则互有权利"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Article 69(4) UPCA defines an applicant as the party initiating proceedings, making them solely liable for providing security for costs.", "First-instance infringement claimants lack legal basis to request security for costs from defendants, even when facing revocation counterclaims.", "In appeals, only the respondent may request security for costs from the appellant; mutual appeals allow reciprocal requests limited to respective costs."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["一审被告可利用规则拒绝原告的担保请求，降低初期诉讼压力", "发起上诉时需预判对方担保请求，做好相应资金准备与应对预案"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: First-instance defendants can leverage this ruling to refuse plaintiff security requests, mitigating early litigation financial burdens.", "Practice note: Appellants must anticipate respondent security requests and ensure adequate financial provisions are made for potential orders.", "What to monitor: Future applications of the manifest error exception allowing defendants to request security despite being respondents in appeals."]
core_points_en: Article 69(4) UPCA defines an applicant as the party initiating proceedings, making them solely liable for providing security for costs.
First-instance infringement claimants lack legal basis to request security for costs from defendants, even when facing revocation counterclaims.
In appeals, only the respondent may request security for costs from the appellant; mutual appeals allow reciprocal requests limited to respective costs.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: First-instance defendants can leverage this ruling to refuse plaintiff security requests, mitigating early litigation financial burdens.
Practice note: Appellants must anticipate respondent security requests and ensure adequate financial provisions are made for potential orders.
What to monitor: Future applications of the manifest error exception allowing defendants to request security despite being respondents in appeals.

items.title: Kevin Mack, Via president: Patent licensing is about finding a balance for all
items.title_zh: Via总裁：专利许可需寻求各方利益平衡
items.title_en: Via Licensing President: Patent Pooling Requires Balancing Interests of All Stakeholders
items.summary: The president of patent pool administrator Via Licensing Alliance tells Sarah Speight what makes him tick, how he makes connections between business, technology and the law, and why data centres are a target technology.
items.ai_summary_zh: Via Licensing联盟总裁Kevin Mack接受采访，阐述其连接商业、技术与法律的核心理念。他强调专利许可的核心在于为所有利益相关者寻找平衡点。此外，Mack指出数据中心已成为Via关注的目标技术领域，反映了专利池管理在新兴技术领域的战略扩展。
items.ai_summary_en: Kevin Mack, President of the Via Licensing Alliance, discusses his professional philosophy and strategic priorities in an interview with Sarah Speight. He emphasizes that effective patent licensing requires finding a sustainable balance among business objectives, technological realities, and legal frameworks for all stakeholders involved. Mack highlights data centers as a key target technology area for Via’s current expansion efforts. This focus reflects the patent pool administrator’s strategic move into emerging technology sectors. The discussion underscores the critical role of patent pool managers in coordinating diverse interests within the standard-essential patent ecosystem, aiming to facilitate efficient licensing while maintaining equilibrium between innovators and implementers in complex technical environments.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应关注Via在数据中心等新兴领域的布局，提前评估潜在许可风险。鉴于Via强调“平衡”，企业在谈判中可主张合理FRAND条款，避免被动接受高额许可费，同时加强内部IP合规体系建设。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor Via Licensing’s strategic entry into data center technologies, as this expansion may introduce new licensing obligations for infrastructure providers and equipment manufacturers. Given the administration’s stated emphasis on balancing interests, licensees can leverage this principle during negotiations to advocate for reasonable FRAND terms, potentially mitigating exposure to excessive royalty demands. Companies operating in these emerging sectors should proactively assess potential intellectual property risks and strengthen internal compliance frameworks. Early engagement with pool licensing structures may provide greater predictability and reduce litigation risks associated with fragmented patent holdings in rapidly evolving technical domains.
