items.title: Game over: Subsonic prevails against Sony in infringement case over PlayStation controllers
items.title_zh: Subsonic诉索尼PS手柄专利侵权案：Subsonic胜诉
items.title_en: Subsonic Prevails Against Sony in PlayStation Controller Patent Infringement Dispute
items.summary: Since 2014 the parties have been locked in a fierce battle over three patents, EP 0 867 212, EP 08 34 338 and EP 13 31 974. They cover controllers for the popular Sony PlayStation games console. All three patents have long expired: EP 212 and EP 338 in 2017, with EP 974 expiring in 2020. In late 2016, […] The post Game over: Subsonic prevails against Sony in infringement case over PlayStation controllers appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 自2014年起，Subsonic与索尼就三项涉及PlayStation控制器的欧洲专利（EP 0 867 212等）展开激烈诉讼。尽管涉案专利已分别于2017年和2020年过期，但双方围绕侵权赔偿及统一专利法院（UPC）的管辖权进行了长期法律博弈。最终，Subsonic在侵权案件中取得胜诉，确立了其在相关专利纠纷中的有利地位，该判决对处理过期专利的追溯性侵权责任具有参考意义。
items.ai_summary_en: Since 2014, Subsonic and Sony have engaged in protracted litigation concerning three European patents covering PlayStation controllers: EP 0 867 212, EP 0 834 338, and EP 1 331 974. Although these patents expired in 2017 and 2020 respectively, the parties continued to dispute infringement damages and the jurisdiction of the Unified Patent Court (UPC). The recent judgment confirms Subsonic’s victory in the infringement action, establishing a favorable precedent for rights holders. This outcome underscores the legal complexities surrounding post-expiration liability and demonstrates that expired patents can still generate significant financial exposure through retrospective infringement claims within the evolving European patent litigation landscape.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需警惕欧洲专利诉讼的长周期特性，即使专利过期仍可能面临追溯索赔。建议出口企业建立完善的FTO监控机制，特别关注UPC管辖下的新型诉讼策略。同时，应重视专利到期后的权利边界评估，避免因历史侵权风险导致重大经济损失。
items.ai_insight_en: This case highlights the strategic importance of monitoring expired patents for potential retrospective infringement liabilities. Companies operating in Europe should maintain robust Freedom to Operate (FTO) frameworks that account for historical usage risks, not just current validity status. With the Unified Patent Court introducing new procedural dynamics, stakeholders must adapt their compliance strategies to address cross-border enforcement challenges. Regular audits of legacy IP exposures are essential to mitigate unexpected financial burdens arising from prolonged litigation cycles involving older patent portfolios.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Subsonic在与索尼关于PS控制器专利的长期诉讼中最终获胜", "涉案三项欧洲专利虽已过期，但仍引发了复杂的侵权与管辖权争议", "案件涉及统一专利法院（UPC）管辖权问题，凸显欧洲专利诉讼复杂性"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Subsonic secured a final victory against Sony in a long-running patent infringement dispute regarding PlayStation controller technology.", "The case involved three expired European patents, highlighting complex legal battles over infringement damages and UPC jurisdictional scope.", "The ruling illustrates the enduring risks of retrospective infringement claims even after the formal expiration of patent rights."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕过期专利引发的追溯性侵权索赔风险", "加强UPC管辖下欧洲专利诉讼的合规与应对能力"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Expired patents may still trigger substantial retrospective infringement claims, requiring continuous risk assessment beyond standard validity checks.", "Practice note: Strengthen FTO monitoring mechanisms to include historical usage analysis and prepare for UPC-specific procedural strategies.", "What to monitor: Evolving UPC jurisprudence on jurisdiction and damages calculation for patents that have already lapsed during litigation."]
core_points_en: Subsonic secured a final victory against Sony in a long-running patent infringement dispute regarding PlayStation controller technology.
The case involved three expired European patents, highlighting complex legal battles over infringement damages and UPC jurisdictional scope.
The ruling illustrates the enduring risks of retrospective infringement claims even after the formal expiration of patent rights.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Expired patents may still trigger substantial retrospective infringement claims, requiring continuous risk assessment beyond standard validity checks.
Practice note: Strengthen FTO monitoring mechanisms to include historical usage analysis and prepare for UPC-specific procedural strategies.
What to monitor: Evolving UPC jurisprudence on jurisdiction and damages calculation for patents that have already lapsed during litigation.

items.title: WIPO survey: Sequence search for PATENTSCOPE
items.title_zh: WIPO启动PATENTSCOPE序列搜索重构用户调研
items.title_en: WIPO Launches User Survey to Redesign PATENTSCOPE Biological Sequence Search Functionality
items.summary: WIPO is in the process of redesigning sequence search for PATENTSCOPE and needs your help.WIPO has launched a short survey (email login required) for users who search biological sequences (DNA, RNA, proteins) in patent databases. The 2-minute survey seeks to collate user feedback on not just what users want to find, but the process that users want to follow, as well as what works well and
items.ai_summary_zh: 世界知识产权组织（WIPO）正在重新设计其全球专利数据库PATENTSCOPE的生物序列搜索功能。为优化用户体验，WIPO发起了一项简短的用户调查，面向在专利数据库中搜索DNA、RNA及蛋白质等生物序列的专业人士。该调查旨在收集用户对搜索目标、操作流程偏好及现有功能有效性的反馈。此举意在提升生物制药等领域的专利信息获取效率，确保新工具更符合实际研发与检索需求。
items.ai_summary_en: The World Intellectual Property Organization is currently redesigning the biological sequence search capabilities within its global patent database, PATENTSCOPE. To enhance user experience and ensure the new tools align with practical research and retrieval needs, WIPO has initiated a brief user survey targeting professionals who search for DNA, RNA, and protein sequences in patent databases. This initiative aims to gather comprehensive feedback on users' specific search objectives, preferred operational workflows, and the effectiveness of existing features. By incorporating this input, WIPO intends to improve the precision and efficiency of patent information access, particularly for sectors such as biopharmaceuticals. The survey seeks to understand not only what users wish to find but also how they prefer to navigate the search process, ensuring that the redesigned interface better supports complex biological data queries.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国生物医药企业应积极参与此次调研，确保新检索工具符合本土研发习惯。优化后的序列搜索将提升FTO分析效率，降低侵权风险。建议IP团队关注更新动态，及时调整内部检索策略，利用更精准的工具加速创新成果的全球专利布局与保护。
items.ai_insight_en: Stakeholders involved in biotechnology and pharmaceutical research should actively participate in this consultation to ensure the updated search tools accommodate diverse international research methodologies. Improved sequence search functionality will likely streamline freedom-to-operate analyses, thereby reducing potential infringement risks associated with complex biological patents. Intellectual property teams are advised to monitor these developments closely and adjust internal search strategies accordingly. Leveraging more precise retrieval tools can accelerate the global patent layout and protection of innovative results, ensuring that competitive intelligence gathering remains robust and efficient in an evolving digital landscape.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["WIPO重构PATENTSCOPE生物序列搜索功能以优化体验", "面向DNA/RNA/蛋白质搜索用户开展流程与需求调研", "旨在提升生物领域专利信息检索的精准度与效率"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["WIPO is restructuring PATENTSCOPE's biological sequence search functions to significantly optimize overall user experience.", "A targeted survey collects detailed feedback on workflows and requirements from users searching for DNA, RNA, and proteins.", "The initiative aims to enhance the precision and efficiency of patent information retrieval within the biological sciences sector."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["积极参与调研以确保检索工具适配中国研发习惯", "利用优化工具提升FTO分析效率并优化全球布局"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Participate in the survey to ensure search tools align with diverse international R&D habits and technical requirements.", "Practice note: Utilize optimized sequence search capabilities to enhance freedom-to-operate analysis efficiency and support global patent strategies.", "What to monitor: Track updates to PATENTSCOPE's search algorithms and adjust internal retrieval protocols to maintain competitive intelligence accuracy."]
core_points_en: WIPO is restructuring PATENTSCOPE's biological sequence search functions to significantly optimize overall user experience.
A targeted survey collects detailed feedback on workflows and requirements from users searching for DNA, RNA, and proteins.
The initiative aims to enhance the precision and efficiency of patent information retrieval within the biological sciences sector.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Participate in the survey to ensure search tools align with diverse international R&D habits and technical requirements.
Practice note: Utilize optimized sequence search capabilities to enhance freedom-to-operate analysis efficiency and support global patent strategies.
What to monitor: Track updates to PATENTSCOPE's search algorithms and adjust internal retrieval protocols to maintain competitive intelligence accuracy.

items.title: LD Düsseldorf, May 7, 2026, Refusal Of Provisional Measures, UPC_CFI_1927/2025, 1928/2025
items.title_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫地院驳回临时措施：明确紧迫性举证责任
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf Refuses Provisional Measures: Clarifying Urgency Burden of Proof
items.summary: Re urgency requirement in case of provisional measures:– Applicant’s burden to present/prove: the gaining of knowledge of the infringing embodiment, the potential infringement, and its prompt verification. – Defendant’s burden to present/prove: the evidence from which it can be inferred that the applicant must have had prior knowledge and from which hesitant conduct can be inferred.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年5月7日，UPC杜塞尔多夫地方法院在Ottobock诉BrainPortfolio案中驳回临时措施申请。法院裁定申请人须证明其获知侵权产品后迅速核实并行动；若被告证明申请人应早已知情且行为迟疑，则推定缺乏紧迫性。该案明确了临时措施中双方关于“知晓时间”与“及时行动”的举证责任分配及推定规则，对UPC诉讼策略具有指导意义。
items.ai_summary_en: On May 7, 2026, the UPC Local Division in Düsseldorf rejected a request for provisional measures in Ottobock v. BrainPortfolio. The court clarified that applicants must demonstrate prompt verification and action upon gaining knowledge of potentially infringing products. If defendants provide evidence suggesting the applicant had prior knowledge and exhibited hesitant conduct, urgency is presumed absent. This ruling establishes clear standards for allocating the burden of proof regarding 'time of knowledge' and 'prompt action,' offering significant guidance for litigation strategy before the Unified Patent Court.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC临时禁令时，需建立快速侵权评估机制，确保从获知风险到起诉的时间间隔合理。同时，应注重保存内部沟通记录，以反驳对方关于“早已知情却故意拖延”的主张，避免因程序瑕疵导致禁令被驳回或面临不利后果。
items.ai_insight_en: Parties seeking provisional measures at the UPC must establish robust internal mechanisms for rapid IP risk assessment. It is critical to minimize the time gap between identifying potential infringement and initiating legal proceedings. Maintaining detailed records of internal communications and decision-making processes is essential to refute allegations of strategic delay or bad faith. Failure to demonstrate sufficient promptness may result in the refusal of interim relief, undermining enforcement efforts and exposing rights holders to procedural disadvantages in cross-border disputes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["申请人需证明获知侵权事实后迅速核实并采取法律行动以符合紧迫性要求", "被告可举证申请人应已知晓侵权且行为迟疑，从而推翻紧迫性推定", "法院依据常规认知推定申请人应知悉潜在侵权特征，除非有反证"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Applicants must prove they verified facts and acted promptly after discovering potential infringement to satisfy urgency requirements.", "Defendants can rebut urgency by proving applicants likely knew earlier and delayed action, shifting the evidentiary burden back.", "Courts presume awareness based on conventional understanding unless applicants provide counter-evidence explaining the delay."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立快速响应机制，缩短从发现侵权到提起诉讼的时间差", "完善内部合规记录，防范对方主张权利人存在恶意拖延行为"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Establish rapid response protocols to shorten the interval between infringement discovery and filing.", "Practice note: Preserve comprehensive internal records to counter claims of deliberate delay or prior knowledge.", "What to monitor: How other UPC divisions apply these urgency presumptions and evidentiary standards in future cases."]
core_points_en: Applicants must prove they verified facts and acted promptly after discovering potential infringement to satisfy urgency requirements.
Defendants can rebut urgency by proving applicants likely knew earlier and delayed action, shifting the evidentiary burden back.
Courts presume awareness based on conventional understanding unless applicants provide counter-evidence explaining the delay.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Establish rapid response protocols to shorten the interval between infringement discovery and filing.
Practice note: Preserve comprehensive internal records to counter claims of deliberate delay or prior knowledge.
What to monitor: How other UPC divisions apply these urgency presumptions and evidentiary standards in future cases.

items.title: LD Düsseldorf, April 30, 2026, Dismissal Of Confidentiality Order, UPC_CFI_351/2024, 595/2024
items.title_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫分院驳回执行阶段保密令申请
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf Dismisses Confidentiality Order Application Filed During Enforcement Proceedings
items.summary: A party may make an application for a confidentiality order pursuant to R. 262A.1 RoP if it is seeking protection for information that it is required to disclose under the operative part of a court decision. However, the application must be made during the proceedings on the merits if it can be reasonably foreseen that confidentiality issues will arise.
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫地方法院在Canon诉Katun案中，驳回了被告在执行阶段提出的保密令申请。法院依据《程序规则》第262A.1条指出，若可合理预见需披露机密信息，当事人必须在实体审理期间提出申请。在执行程序中，无法再限制基于判决获取信息的使用方式。此裁决强调保密请求的时效性，逾期提出通常不中止强化处罚令的合规期限。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Local Division in Düsseldorf dismissed a defendant’s application for a confidentiality order submitted during enforcement proceedings in Canon v. Katun. Relying on Rule 262A.1 of the Rules of Procedure, the Court held that if the need to disclose confidential information is reasonably foreseeable, the application must be filed during the proceedings on the merits. The judgment clarifies that enforcement stages do not permit restricting how information obtained via a court decision is subsequently used or disclosed. This ruling underscores the strict timeliness required for confidentiality requests. Filing such requests after the merits phase generally does not suspend compliance deadlines for penalty-reinforced orders, thereby limiting procedural remedies available once enforcement begins.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC专利诉讼时，必须建立全流程保密管理意识。一旦预判可能涉及商业秘密披露，务必在实体审理阶段主动申请保密令，切勿寄希望于执行阶段的补救。忽视程序时效将导致敏感信息在强制执行中失去保护，增加商业泄露风险。建议法务团队严格遵循R.262A规则，提前规划证据披露策略。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision highlights critical procedural timing requirements under the Unified Patent Court framework. Parties anticipating the disclosure of sensitive business information must proactively seek confidentiality protections during the main proceedings rather than relying on post-judgment remedies. Failure to act during the merits phase may result in the loss of control over how disclosed information is handled during enforcement. Legal teams should integrate confidentiality risk assessments into early case strategy, ensuring compliance with Rule 262A.1 to prevent unintended exposure of trade secrets when complying with court-ordered information production or evidence disclosure mandates.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["保密令申请须在实体审理期间提出，不可拖延至执行阶段", "若可预见披露义务，被告应在审理中及时主张以获保护措施", "执行程序无法限制判决所涉信息的后续使用或披露范围"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Confidentiality order applications must be filed during merits proceedings and cannot be deferred to the enforcement stage.", "Defendants must raise confidentiality concerns early if disclosure obligations are reasonably foreseeable to secure protective measures.", "Enforcement proceedings cannot restrict subsequent use or external disclosure of information derived from the court's decision."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["严守实体审理期申请保密令，避免执行阶段权利丧失", "预判披露风险需即时行动，防止商业秘密在强制中泄露"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Early identification of confidential data risks is essential to maintain protection throughout litigation and enforcement phases.", "Practice note: File confidentiality applications during merits proceedings; do not wait for enforcement to address disclosure concerns.", "What to monitor: Compliance deadlines for penalty-reinforced orders remain unaffected by late-filed confidentiality requests."]
core_points_en: Confidentiality order applications must be filed during merits proceedings and cannot be deferred to the enforcement stage.
Defendants must raise confidentiality concerns early if disclosure obligations are reasonably foreseeable to secure protective measures.
Enforcement proceedings cannot restrict subsequent use or external disclosure of information derived from the court's decision.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Early identification of confidential data risks is essential to maintain protection throughout litigation and enforcement phases.
Practice note: File confidentiality applications during merits proceedings; do not wait for enforcement to address disclosure concerns.
What to monitor: Compliance deadlines for penalty-reinforced orders remain unaffected by late-filed confidentiality requests.

items.title: [Blog] EPO Technology Dashboard 2025 : Croissance Modérée Des Brevets Et Essor Des Technologies Numériques
items.title_zh: EPO 2025技术仪表盘：专利温和增长，数字技术崛起
items.title_en: EPO Technology Dashboard 2025: Moderate Patent Growth and Rise of Digital Technologies
items.summary: An unexpected error has occurred on this page. The technical team has been notified and is working to resolve the issue. Users are encouraged to retry their request.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局发布2025年数据，申请量微增1.4%至20.2万件，非欧国家贡献主要增量。AI、量子计算及通信技术驱动数字领域增长。德国居首，法国次之，芬兰增速显著。亚洲企业领跑，欧洲单一专利占比升至29%。女性发明人比例仅26%，显示结构性挑战。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office released its 2025 Technology Dashboard, reporting 201,974 patent applications, a 1.4% increase from 2024. This moderate growth was primarily driven by filings from non-European countries (+2.1%) rather than European states (+0.4%). Digitally focused sectors led the expansion, with significant rises in artificial intelligence, quantum technologies, and digital communications. Semiconductor and energy battery technologies also continued to grow, while medical technologies remained high despite declines in biotechnology and pharmaceuticals. Geographically, Europe retained dominance with 43% of filings, followed by the United States and China, which showed significant growth. Among top applicants, Asian corporations like Samsung, Huawei, and LG maintained leading positions. Structurally, the unitary patent’s adoption reached 29%, yet female inventor representation remained low at 26%, highlighting ongoing diversity challenges within the European innovation landscape.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在全球专利布局中保持强势，应重点关注AI与量子等新兴领域的竞争态势。鉴于欧洲单一专利采纳率提升，建议优化成本结构，优先通过单一专利覆盖主要市场。同时，需关注性别多样性指标，完善内部创新激励机制以符合ESG趋势。
items.ai_insight_en: Stakeholders should monitor the accelerating competition in emerging digital fields, particularly artificial intelligence and quantum technologies, where filing volumes are surging. The rising adoption rate of the unitary patent suggests that optimizing portfolio costs through this mechanism is increasingly viable for covering key European markets efficiently. Additionally, the persistent gap in female inventor representation presents both a compliance consideration and an opportunity for organizations to strengthen internal innovation incentives. Aligning IP strategies with broader ESG objectives by supporting diverse inventorship may enhance corporate reputation and ensure alignment with evolving regulatory expectations in the European market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["年欧洲专利申请量达201,974件，同比增长1.4%，非欧洲国家申请增幅高于欧洲本土", "人工智能、量子技术及通信技术领域增长显著，半导体与能源电池技术持续扩张", "欧洲单一专利使用率升至29%，但女性发明人占比仍低，仅占申请总量的26%"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["European patent applications rose 1.4% to 201,974, with non-European filings driving the majority of this annual growth.", "Digital sectors including AI, quantum computing, and communications saw significant increases alongside steady semiconductor expansion.", "Unitary patent usage reached 29%, while female inventor participation remained limited at 26% of total European filings."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["聚焦AI与量子计算等高增长领域，强化核心技术专利壁垒以应对国际竞争", "利用欧洲单一专利机制优化布局成本，并加强女性发明人培养以提升合规形象"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor competitive dynamics in high-growth digital sectors like AI and quantum computing to strengthen core technology patent barriers.", "Practice note: Leverage the increasing unitary patent adoption rate to optimize geographic coverage and reduce overall maintenance costs.", "What to monitor: Track gender diversity metrics in patent filings to align internal innovation incentives with emerging ESG compliance trends."]
core_points_en: European patent applications rose 1.4% to 201,974, with non-European filings driving the majority of this annual growth.
Digital sectors including AI, quantum computing, and communications saw significant increases alongside steady semiconductor expansion.
Unitary patent usage reached 29%, while female inventor participation remained limited at 26% of total European filings.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor competitive dynamics in high-growth digital sectors like AI and quantum computing to strengthen core technology patent barriers.
Practice note: Leverage the increasing unitary patent adoption rate to optimize geographic coverage and reduce overall maintenance costs.
What to monitor: Track gender diversity metrics in patent filings to align internal innovation incentives with emerging ESG compliance trends.

items.title: LD Düsseldorf, May 4, 2026, Revocation Of An Order On Inspection And Evidence Preservation, UPC_CFI_885/2025
items.title_zh: 杜塞尔多夫地方法院撤销UPC证据保全令案析
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf Revokes Evidence Preservation Order for Failure to Initiate Merits Proceedings
items.summary: This page appears to be a security verification checkpoint that temporarily blocks access while validating the incoming request. The system implements protective measures to distinguish legitimate users from automated traffic or potential security threats.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本案系UPC杜塞尔多夫地方法院审理的OTEC诉STEROS专利纠纷。法院依据《统一专利法院协议》第60条及规则，裁定若申请人在专家报告指定日期后31个自然日或20个工作日内未启动实体诉讼，应撤销证据保全令。法院可责令销毁描述材料并支持被告索赔。律师费需通过单独的费用决定程序主张，不直接作为损害赔偿处理。
items.ai_summary_en: In the patent dispute between OTEC and STEROS, the Local Division of the Unified Patent Court in Düsseldorf issued a significant ruling regarding evidence preservation orders. The court determined that such an order must be revoked if the applicant fails to initiate proceedings on the merits within thirty-one calendar days or twenty working days from the date specified in the order for the expert report presentation. This timeframe aligns with Article 60(8) of the Unified Patent Court Agreement and relevant Rules of Procedure. Furthermore, the court exercised its authority to order the return, destruction, or deletion of detailed descriptions and brochures to remedy the consequences of the revoked measure. Regarding financial remedies, the judgment clarified that while defendants may claim damages for losses suffered due to the preservation measures, attorney costs incurred during these specific proceedings are not considered damages. Instead, such legal fees must be asserted separately through distinct cost decision proceedings under the established procedural rules.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧涉诉企业须严格监控UPC证据保全后的起诉期限，避免因逾期导致保全失效及承担赔偿责任。建议建立案件流程预警机制，妥善应对对方可能的撤销请求及后续费用追偿，确保程序合规以降低法律风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Parties involved in UPC litigation must strictly monitor statutory deadlines following the execution of evidence preservation measures. Failure to initiate substantive proceedings within the prescribed period results in the automatic revocation of the order and potential liability for damages. Legal practitioners should establish robust internal workflow alerts to track these critical timelines effectively. Additionally, when responding to a defendant's request for revocation, applicants must be prepared for potential compensation claims. It is crucial to distinguish between compensable damages and recoverable attorney costs, as the latter requires a separate procedural avenue. Proactive case management ensures procedural compliance and mitigates the risk of adverse financial consequences arising from missed deadlines or improper handling of preserved evidence materials.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["未在法定期限内启动实体诉讼，证据保全令将被撤销", "法院可责令返还或销毁详细记录以消除后果", "律师费需通过费用决定程序主张，非直接损害赔偿"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Evidence preservation orders are revoked if merits proceedings are not initiated within thirty-one calendar days or twenty working days.", "Courts may order the return, destruction, or deletion of detailed descriptions and brochures to remedy consequences.", "Attorney costs from preservation proceedings must be claimed via separate cost decisions, not as direct damages."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["严格监控UPC证据保全后的起诉期限", "建立流程预警以应对撤销请求及费用追偿"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strictly monitor UPC evidence preservation deadlines to prevent order revocation and liability exposure.", "Practice note: Establish workflow alerts for merits filing deadlines and distinguish damages from attorney cost recovery procedures.", "What to monitor: Defendant requests for revocation and subsequent claims for compensation or cost decisions."]
core_points_en: Evidence preservation orders are revoked if merits proceedings are not initiated within thirty-one calendar days or twenty working days.
Courts may order the return, destruction, or deletion of detailed descriptions and brochures to remedy consequences.
Attorney costs from preservation proceedings must be claimed via separate cost decisions, not as direct damages.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strictly monitor UPC evidence preservation deadlines to prevent order revocation and liability exposure.
Practice note: Establish workflow alerts for merits filing deadlines and distinguish damages from attorney cost recovery procedures.
What to monitor: Defendant requests for revocation and subsequent claims for compensation or cost decisions.

items.title: Court Of Appeal, April 30, 2026, Order Rejecting Appeal As Inadmissible, UPC_CoA_1/2026
items.title_zh: UPC上诉法院裁定驳回上诉：程序合规与费用担保标准
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Rejects Adobe Appeal on Cost Security, Upholds Strict Procedural Compliance
items.summary: The Court of Appeal verifies ex officio compliance with the time limits and rules governing the appeal. When deciding on the admissibility of an appeal, an order rejecting the appeal as...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉法院在Adobe诉Keeex案中，裁定驳回上诉并确认程序规则需严格适用以保障法律确定性。法院依职权审查上诉期限，认定驳回裁定未超出请求范围。此外，法院重申原告与被告的相对财务状况不构成Rule 158下的费用担保考量因素。该判决强化了UPC程序规则的刚性，强调避免司法任意性，对后续涉及费用担保及程序合规的案件具有先例约束力。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal issued an order rejecting an appeal as inadmissible in proceedings between Adobe Inc. and Keeex SAS concerning cost security. The Court confirmed it verifies compliance with time limits and procedural rules ex officio. It held that rejecting an appeal as inadmissible does not exceed the scope of the claims. Furthermore, the Court reaffirmed that strict application of procedural rules ensures legal certainty and prevents arbitrary treatment. Crucially, the relative financial situation of the claimant compared to the defendant is not a valid criterion for assessing cost security under Rule 158 of the Rules of Procedure.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC诉讼时，须严守程序期限，避免因技术性违规导致权利丧失。针对费用担保，不能仅凭双方财力差距抗辩，需准备实质性的胜诉可能性证据。建议加强内部IP合规流程，特别是跨境诉讼中的程序管理，以降低因程序失误带来的高额成本风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision reinforces the UPC's commitment to rigid procedural adherence to maintain legal certainty and equal treatment. For rights holders and defendants, it clarifies that financial arguments alone cannot override standard cost security requirements. Practitioners must ensure precise compliance with filing deadlines and procedural formalities, as the Court will actively enforce these rules without leniency based on party status or financial standing.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院依职权严格审查上诉期限与程序规则，确保法律确定性及非歧视原则", "驳回上诉作为不可受理裁定，不视为超出当事人诉讼请求的范围", "当事人相对财务状况不构成申请费用担保的法定依据，维持既往判例立场"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Court of Appeal conducts an ex officio assessment of admissibility, verifying strict compliance with time limits and procedural rules governing appeals.", "An order rejecting an appeal as inadmissible is deemed within the scope of the claims and does not violate procedural boundaries under the Rules of Procedure.", "The relative financial disparity between claimant and defendant does not constitute a valid criterion for cost security assessments under Rule 158."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["严守UPC程序期限，规避因技术性违规导致的权利丧失风险", "费用担保抗辩需侧重胜诉可能性，而非单纯依赖财力对比"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Financial disparity between parties is insufficient to challenge cost security orders; substantive procedural compliance is paramount.", "Practice note: Ensure rigorous internal monitoring of appeal deadlines and procedural steps, as the Court reviews admissibility ex officio.", "What to monitor: Future applications of Rule 158 regarding cost security criteria and any potential shifts in the Court's strict interpretation of procedural rules."]
core_points_en: The Court of Appeal conducts an ex officio assessment of admissibility, verifying strict compliance with time limits and procedural rules governing appeals.
An order rejecting an appeal as inadmissible is deemed within the scope of the claims and does not violate procedural boundaries under the Rules of Procedure.
The relative financial disparity between claimant and defendant does not constitute a valid criterion for cost security assessments under Rule 158.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Financial disparity between parties is insufficient to challenge cost security orders; substantive procedural compliance is paramount.
Practice note: Ensure rigorous internal monitoring of appeal deadlines and procedural steps, as the Court reviews admissibility ex officio.
What to monitor: Future applications of Rule 158 regarding cost security criteria and any potential shifts in the Court's strict interpretation of procedural rules.

items.title: Réplique de Ferrari F355 : une contrefaçon sanctionnée par la justice néerlandaise
items.title_zh: 荷兰法院裁定法拉利F355仿制车侵犯版权及商标
items.title_en: Dutch Court Rules Ferrari F355 Replica Infringes Copyright and Trademark Rights
items.summary: Le tribunal de La Haye a sanctionné la commercialisation d'une réplique de Ferrari F355 pour contrefaçon de marque et atteinte au droit d'auteur. Cette décision du 8 avril 2026 illustre la protection cumulative offerte...
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年4月8日，海牙地方法院裁定一荷兰车商销售的丰田改装法拉利F355仿制车侵权。法院认定该车型整体设计具独创性，受版权保护；且使用法拉利Logo构成商标侵权。被告需停止销售、销毁车辆并赔偿。此案凸显了欧盟法域内版权与商标权的累积保护机制，即使存在细微差异，若整体印象相似仍可能被判侵权。
items.ai_summary_en: The District Court of The Hague ruled on April 8, 2026, that a Dutch car dealer infringed Ferrari's copyright and trademark rights by selling a Toyota-based replica marketed as a Ferrari F355. The court found the vehicle's overall design sufficiently original for copyright protection, dismissing minor technical differences as negligible given the identical overall impression. Additionally, the unauthorized use of Ferrari logos constituted EU trademark infringement. The defendant was ordered to cease sales, destroy the replica, pay legal costs exceeding €22,000, and provide further compensation. This judgment highlights the cumulative protection available under EU law for automotive designs.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国车企出口欧洲需警惕外观设计的版权风险，不仅限于专利。改装或复刻经典车型时，即便更换核心部件，若整体视觉印象相似且使用原品牌标识，将面临高额赔偿及销毁风险。建议提前进行FTO分析，避免在营销中使用易混淆的品牌元素。
items.ai_insight_en: This case illustrates the robust cumulative protection afforded to automotive designs in the Netherlands and broader EU jurisdiction. Rights holders can leverage both copyright and trademark laws to combat replicas that mimic the overall aesthetic of protected models, even if internal components differ. For market participants dealing in modified or classic cars, reliance on technical differences alone is insufficient to avoid liability if the visual identity remains confusingly similar to the original brand.

items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) has published an official news item titled 'Bridging law and practice: how case law guides IP decision making.' The available source material is currently limited to the headline, indicating a focus on the intersection of judicial precedents and administrative or legal practices within the EU intellectual property framework. No specific case details or full text are provided in the current excerpt.

items.ai_insight_en: This item signals EUIPO's continued emphasis on the influence of jurisprudence on office practices. For rights holders and practitioners, understanding this dynamic is essential for anticipating how legal interpretations may affect examination outcomes and procedural decisions within the EU system.

items.title: Pro-arbitration turn? Appeals court stays FRAND case against Nokia
items.title_zh: 倾向仲裁？英上诉法院中止对诺基亚的FRAND诉讼
items.title_en: UK Court of Appeal Stays FRAND Litigation Against Nokia, Signaling Shift Toward Arbitration
items.summary: Nokia, represented by a team from Bird & Bird, adjudged to have made fair offer to Asus and Acer in UK SEP dispute
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国上诉法院在一宗涉及诺基亚、华硕和宏碁的标准必要专利（SEP）纠纷中，裁定暂停诉讼程序。法院认定诺基亚已向被诉方提供了符合公平、合理和无歧视（FRAND）原则的许可要约。此举被视为司法系统倾向于通过仲裁解决FRAND争议的信号，强调了当事方协议在确定许可条款中的优先地位，可能影响未来欧洲SEP诉讼的策略走向。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Court of Appeal has ordered a stay of proceedings in the standard essential patent (SEP) dispute involving Nokia, Asus, and Acer. The court determined that Nokia had already extended a licensing offer to the defendants that satisfied fair, reasonable, and non-discriminatory (FRAND) obligations. This ruling effectively pauses the litigation, prioritizing the enforcement of existing contractual agreements over judicial determination of license terms. The decision is widely interpreted as a significant signal from the English judiciary favoring arbitration for resolving FRAND disputes. By emphasizing the primacy of party autonomy and contractual commitments, this judgment may substantially influence future strategic approaches to SEP enforcement and defense across European jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国科技企业应重视合同中的仲裁条款，利用此判例争取将FRAND争议导向更私密、高效的仲裁程序，避免高昂且公开的英国诉讼。同时需确保自身许可报价符合FRAND标准，以在潜在的法律对抗中占据主动，降低合规风险与诉讼成本。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment underscores the critical importance of robust arbitration clauses within SEP licensing frameworks. Parties should strategically leverage such provisions to divert disputes away from costly, public national court proceedings toward more private and efficient arbitral forums. Furthermore, licensors must ensure their initial offers strictly adhere to FRAND standards to maintain legal leverage and mitigate compliance risks. Defense teams should similarly scrutinize offer validity early in proceedings. Monitoring how lower courts interpret this preference for arbitration will be essential for shaping future litigation strategies and settlement negotiations in the evolving European SEP landscape.

items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UK Court of Appeal stayed the SEP FRAND litigation against Nokia, prioritizing contractual arbitration clauses.", "Nokia was adjudged to have made a valid FRAND-compliant licensing offer to plaintiffs Asus and Acer.", "The ruling indicates a judicial trend supporting arbitration for resolving complex FRAND licensing disputes."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["善用仲裁条款规避高昂公开的英国SEP诉讼风险", "确保许可报价合规以在FRAND争议中占据法律主动"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage arbitration clauses to avoid high-cost, public UK SEP litigation and ensure privacy.", "Practice note: Verify that all licensing offers strictly meet FRAND criteria to secure procedural advantages.", "What to monitor: How subsequent rulings apply this pro-arbitration stance to other European SEP cases."]
core_points_en: The UK Court of Appeal stayed the SEP FRAND litigation against Nokia, prioritizing contractual arbitration clauses.
Nokia was adjudged to have made a valid FRAND-compliant licensing offer to plaintiffs Asus and Acer.
The ruling indicates a judicial trend supporting arbitration for resolving complex FRAND licensing disputes.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage arbitration clauses to avoid high-cost, public UK SEP litigation and ensure privacy.
Practice note: Verify that all licensing offers strictly meet FRAND criteria to secure procedural advantages.
What to monitor: How subsequent rulings apply this pro-arbitration stance to other European SEP cases.

items.title: “The UPC wants to bring ZTE and Samsung to the negotiating table”
items.title_zh: UPC建议ZTE与三星在PMAC进行调解以达成和解
items.title_en: UPC Recommends ZTE and Samsung Pursue PMAC Mediation Over Continuing Litigation
items.summary: In its proposal, the UPC recommends that, rather than continuing the patent proceedings, the parties convene at the PMAC for mediation on the very day it begins operations. The UPC is therefore strongly encouraging the parties to come to what it describes as an “amicable solution”. Although the order is merely a recommendation and not […] The post “The UPC wants to bring ZTE and Samsung to the negotiating table” appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）向中兴通讯（ZTE）与三星电子提出建议，主张双方应在UPC正式运作首日前往专利调解与仲裁中心（PMAC）进行调解，而非继续推进专利诉讼程序。UPC强烈鼓励双方寻求其所谓的“友好解决方案”。尽管该提议仅为建议性质而非强制命令，但此举反映了UPC在处理涉及标准必要专利（SEP）等复杂商业纠纷时，倾向于通过替代性争议解决机制促进当事人和解，以降低诉讼成本并加速纠纷解决进程。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) has formally recommended that ZTE and Samsung engage in mediation at the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Center (PMAC) rather than continuing their ongoing patent litigation. This proposal suggests convening for mediation on the very day the PMAC begins its operations, signaling a strong judicial preference for resolving disputes through alternative mechanisms. The UPC explicitly encourages both parties to seek an amicable solution to their standard essential patent (SEP) conflict. Although this recommendation lacks binding legal force, it reflects the court’s strategic orientation toward facilitating settlements in complex commercial disputes. By promoting early mediation, the UPC aims to reduce litigation costs and accelerate resolution timelines, offering a pragmatic pathway for parties involved in high-stakes SEP disagreements to reach mutually acceptable outcomes outside of traditional courtroom proceedings.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对欧洲SEP诉讼时，应重视UPC推动调解的趋势。面对此类建议，企业需评估诉讼成本与和解可能性，利用PMAC机制争取更有利的FRAND许可条件。同时，应密切关注UPC后续类似案例，调整在欧洲的知识产权维权与应诉策略，避免陷入长期高成本的司法拉锯战。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor the UPC's increasing emphasis on mediation via the PMAC as a preferred method for resolving standard essential patent disputes. Companies involved in similar litigation should carefully evaluate the cost-benefit ratio of continuing protracted legal battles versus engaging in early-stage mediation. Utilizing the PMAC framework may provide opportunities to negotiate more favorable FRAND licensing terms while avoiding the financial and operational burdens of extended court proceedings. Stakeholders must remain agile, adjusting their European IP enforcement and defense strategies to align with this judicial trend toward consensual resolution, thereby mitigating risks associated with prolonged judicial uncertainty and escalating legal expenses.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC建议ZTE与三星在PMAC进行调解而非继续诉讼", "UPC强烈鼓励双方达成被描述为友好的解决方案", "该提议虽无强制力但体现UPC推动和解的政策导向"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC recommends that ZTE and Samsung utilize the PMAC for mediation instead of continuing with current patent infringement proceedings.", "The court strongly encourages both parties to achieve an amicable solution, highlighting a shift toward alternative dispute resolution methods.", "While non-binding, the recommendation underscores the UPC's policy preference for settlement to manage complex SEP disputes efficiently."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重视UPC调解趋势，评估诉讼成本与和解机会", "利用PMAC机制争取有利FRAND条件，调整应诉策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Evaluate the strategic advantage of early PMAC mediation over continued litigation to potentially secure better FRAND terms.", "Practice note: Assess litigation costs against settlement probabilities, leveraging the UPC's preference for amicable solutions in SEP cases.", "What to monitor: Track subsequent UPC decisions and PMAC outcomes to refine European IP enforcement and defense strategies."]
core_points_en: The UPC recommends that ZTE and Samsung utilize the PMAC for mediation instead of continuing with current patent infringement proceedings.
The court strongly encourages both parties to achieve an amicable solution, highlighting a shift toward alternative dispute resolution methods.
While non-binding, the recommendation underscores the UPC's policy preference for settlement to manage complex SEP disputes efficiently.

Practice note: Assess litigation costs against settlement probabilities, leveraging the UPC's preference for amicable solutions in SEP cases.

items.title: Nokia wins landmark English Court of Appeal decision against Acer/Asus
items.title_zh: 诺基亚胜诉：英格兰上诉法院永久中止针对宏碁/华硕的RAND索赔
items.title_en: Nokia Secures Landmark Court of Appeal Stay Against Acer and Asus RAND Claims
items.summary: Appellate judges permanently stay final and interim RAND claims based on Nokia arbitration-based adjustable licence offer.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英格兰上诉法院作出里程碑式裁决，基于诺基亚提供的以仲裁为基础的可调整许可要约，永久中止了针对宏碁和华硕的最终及临时RAND（合理和非歧视）索赔。该判决确立了在存在有效仲裁机制时，法院对FRAND费率争议的管辖权界限，强调了当事人通过仲裁解决许可纠纷的优先性，对标准必要专利许可谈判具有深远影响。
items.ai_summary_en: The English Court of Appeal has issued a landmark judgment permanently staying both final and interim claims regarding Fair, Reasonable, and Non-Discriminatory (FRAND) licensing rates against Acer and Asus. This decision rests on Nokia’s provision of an adjustable license offer grounded in binding arbitration. The appellate judges determined that where a valid and effective arbitration mechanism is available to resolve rate disputes, the courts should not intervene to determine FRAND terms judicially. This ruling significantly clarifies the jurisdictional boundaries of English courts in SEP litigation, establishing that party autonomy and agreed-upon arbitration mechanisms take precedence over judicial determination of royalty rates when such options are properly presented by the patent holder.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出口企业应重视合同中的仲裁条款，将其作为应对SEP诉讼的有效防御手段。面对诺基亚等权利人的许可要约，若包含仲裁选项，可积极主张中止法院诉讼，以降低高额禁令风险并争取更公平的费率裁定，避免陷入漫长的跨国司法拉锯战。
items.ai_insight_en: For multinational technology firms and implementers operating in Europe, this judgment underscores the critical strategic value of robust arbitration clauses within licensing frameworks. When faced with SEP enforcement actions, parties should carefully evaluate whether the patentee has offered a viable arbitration route. Successfully demonstrating the existence of such a mechanism can serve as a powerful procedural defense, potentially securing a stay of court proceedings. This approach allows companies to mitigate the immediate risk of injunctive relief while pursuing a potentially more balanced and efficient resolution of royalty disputes outside of prolonged, high-stakes national litigation.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英格兰上诉法院基于仲裁许可要约，永久中止针对宏碁和华硕的RAND索赔", "裁决确认在提供有效仲裁机制时，法院不再干预FRAND费率的司法确定", "此判例强化了仲裁作为解决SEP许可争议优先途径的法律地位"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Court of Appeal permanently stayed FRAND rate claims based on Nokia's arbitration-based adjustable license offer.", "Judicial intervention in FRAND rate determination is barred when an effective arbitration mechanism is available.", "This precedent reinforces arbitration as the primary and preferred pathway for resolving standard essential patent disputes."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["善用合同仲裁条款，主张中止法院诉讼以规避高额禁令风险", "将仲裁作为对抗SEP权利人激进诉讼策略的核心防御工具"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage arbitration offers to seek stays of court proceedings and reduce exposure to injunctions in SEP disputes.", "Practice note: Scrutinize licensing offers for binding arbitration clauses to mount effective procedural defenses against FRAND claims.", "What to monitor: How other European jurisdictions interpret the priority of arbitration over judicial FRAND determinations following this UK precedent."]
core_points_en: The Court of Appeal permanently stayed FRAND rate claims based on Nokia's arbitration-based adjustable license offer.
Judicial intervention in FRAND rate determination is barred when an effective arbitration mechanism is available.
This precedent reinforces arbitration as the primary and preferred pathway for resolving standard essential patent disputes.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage arbitration offers to seek stays of court proceedings and reduce exposure to injunctions in SEP disputes.
Practice note: Scrutinize licensing offers for binding arbitration clauses to mount effective procedural defenses against FRAND claims.
What to monitor: How other European jurisdictions interpret the priority of arbitration over judicial FRAND determinations following this UK precedent.

items.title: The General Court of the EU refuses Klarna’s application to register the ‘K’ trade mark in relation to financial services
items.title_zh: 欧盟普通法院驳回Klarna金融服务“K”商标申请
items.title_en: The General Court of the EU refuses Klarna’s application to register the ‘K’ trade mark in relation to financial services
items.summary: Today’s judgment by the General Court of the European Union (GCEU) upholds the refusal to register Karna’s ‘K’ trade mark in relation to financial, monetary, banking, insurance and real estate services, primarily confirming its incompatibility with the trade mark protected by Kutxabank in Class 36, a position the Spanish bank has maintained for years. The ... Read more La entrada The General Court of the EU refuses Klarna’s application to register the ‘K’ trade mark in relation to financial serv
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟普通法院维持决定，驳回瑞典金融科技公司Klarna在欧盟注册“K”商标的申请，涵盖金融、银行及保险等服务。法院认定该标志与西班牙银行Kutxabank在第36类拥有的在先商标构成混淆性相似。此判决确认了Kutxabank长期主张的权利基础，强调在后申请不得侵犯他人在先注册商标权，即便申请人知名度较高，若存在视觉或概念上的近似且服务类别重合，仍无法获准注册。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧拓展金融或科技业务时，需提前进行全面的欧盟商标检索，避免使用单一字母等显著性弱但易与他人在先权利冲突的标志。应重视在先权利人的异议风险，建立完善的全球品牌布局策略，确保新品牌在主要市场的可注册性与稳定性，规避高昂的诉讼成本。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟普通法院维持驳回Klarna“K”商标申请的决定", "裁定其与西班牙Kutxabank银行在先第36类商标构成冲突", "确认在先商标权优先，防止市场混淆和消费者误认"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["强化欧盟商标检索，规避单一字母等高冲突风险标志", "重视在先权利审查，建立稳健的全球品牌防御体系"]

items.title: Come Together? Acer v Nokia and the Contractual Turn of FRAND Arbitration
items.title_zh: Acer诉诺基亚案与FRAND仲裁的合同化转向
items.title_en: Come Together? Acer v Nokia and the Contractual Turn of FRAND Arbitration
items.summary: "The readiness is all." - HamletSometimes a judgment does not change the melody of FRAND law. It changes the instrument through which that melody may be played. Not every player reads the same score, and not every performance is equally faithful to the underlying composition. What the UK Court of Appeal has done in Acer v Nokia is reassign one voice in the ensemble - without rewriting the work.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国上诉法院在Acer诉诺基亚案中裁定，诺基亚提出的“可调整许可要约”（即立即授予 interim 许可，最终条款由ICC仲裁确定）构成客观上的RAND要约。法院虽维持管辖权，但批准中止诉讼。这意味着实施者若拒绝此类包含仲裁机制的要约，可能丧失在英国法院寻求declaratory judgment的权利，凸显仲裁在FRAND履约中的新角色。
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案标志着FRAND争议解决从单纯法院裁决向“法院+仲裁”混合模式转变。权利人可通过单方要约引入仲裁机制，从而改变管辖权博弈格局。实施者需警惕拒绝此类要约导致的程序性不利后果，仲裁正成为SEP许可谈判与诉讼中的关键变量。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国上诉法院认定含ICC仲裁条款的即时许可要约可构成客观RAND要约", "法院批准中止诉讼，因实施者拒绝该要约可能使其失去英法院declaratory管辖权", "判决未改写FRAND法律实质，但改变了履约形式，赋予仲裁更重程序地位"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["FRAND履约形式多元化，仲裁机制被纳入合规要约范畴", "拒绝含仲裁条款的要约可能导致实施者丧失特定法院的宣告性判决管辖权", "需关注后续案件中法院对单方仲裁要约客观合理性的审查标准变化"]

items.title: UK appeals court overturns key ruling on trademark licensee damages
items.title_zh: 英上诉法院推翻商标被许可人损害赔偿关键裁决
items.title_en: UK Court of Appeal Rules Unregistered Trademark Licensees Cannot Claim Damages
items.summary: Frasers Group—formerly Sports Direct—secures a win against Lifestyle Equities, which has significant implications for brands with unregistered licensees for their marks.
items.ai_summary_zh: Frasers Group（原Sports Direct）在针对Lifestyle Equities的诉讼中获胜。英国上诉法院推翻了下级法院关于未注册商标被许可人有权获得损害赔偿的裁决。这一判决明确了在未注册商标侵权案件中，被许可人主张损害赔偿的法律门槛显著提高，对品牌方管理未注册商标许可及维权策略产生深远影响，强调了完善商标注册与许可协议条款的重要性。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Court of Appeal has overturned a significant lower court ruling in the dispute between Frasers Group and Lifestyle Equities, determining that unregistered trademark licensees generally lack standing to claim damages for infringement. This decision reverses previous findings that favored the trademark owner, establishing a higher legal threshold for such claims. The judgment clarifies that without registration, licensees face substantial hurdles in seeking compensation independently. This outcome carries profound implications for brand owners managing portfolios with unregistered marks, highlighting the critical importance of securing formal trademark registrations and carefully structuring licensing agreements to mitigate enforcement risks and potential financial losses.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在英欧市场应加速核心商标的本地注册，避免依赖未注册商标许可。在签署许可协议时，需明确约定侵权索赔权的归属及赔偿分配机制，以规避因法律变动导致的维权困境和经济损失。
items.ai_insight_en: Brand owners operating in the UK and EU markets should prioritize the local registration of core trademarks to avoid reliance on unregistered rights, which offer limited enforcement capabilities. When drafting licensing agreements, parties must explicitly define the allocation of infringement claim rights and mechanisms for damage distribution. Clear contractual terms are essential to navigate potential legal uncertainties and prevent disputes over compensation entitlements. Companies should review existing licenses to ensure they align with this stricter judicial interpretation, thereby safeguarding their ability to enforce rights effectively and minimizing exposure to uncompensated infringement.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国上诉法院裁定未注册商标被许可人通常无权单独主张损害赔偿", "Frasers Group胜诉，推翻了此前有利于商标权人的下级法院判决", "该判例显著提高了未注册商标被许可人在侵权诉讼中的获赔难度"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Unregistered trademark licensees generally cannot independently claim damages for infringement under current UK law.", "Frasers Group successfully appealed, overturning a prior lower court judgment that had favored the trademark licensee.", "This precedent significantly raises the difficulty for unregistered licensees to obtain compensation in infringement litigation."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["加速核心商标在英欧注册，降低对未注册商标的依赖风险", "细化许可协议条款，明确侵权索赔权归属及赔偿分配机制"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Accelerate local trademark registration in the UK and EU to reduce dependency on unregistered mark protections.", "Practice note: Refine licensing agreement clauses to clearly specify ownership of infringement claims and damage distribution mechanisms.", "What to monitor: Monitor further judicial interpretations regarding standing and damages for unregistered intellectual property rights."]
core_points_en: Unregistered trademark licensees generally cannot independently claim damages for infringement under current UK law.
Frasers Group successfully appealed, overturning a prior lower court judgment that had favored the trademark licensee.
This precedent significantly raises the difficulty for unregistered licensees to obtain compensation in infringement litigation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Accelerate local trademark registration in the UK and EU to reduce dependency on unregistered mark protections.
Practice note: Refine licensing agreement clauses to clearly specify ownership of infringement claims and damage distribution mechanisms.
What to monitor: Monitor further judicial interpretations regarding standing and damages for unregistered intellectual property rights.

items.title: EPO Enlarged Board Clarifies: Publicly Available Products Are Prior Art, Even If Irreproducible
items.title_zh: EPO扩大上诉委员会：公开产品即便不可再现亦属现有技术
items.title_en: EPO Enlarged Board: Publicly Available Products Are Prior Art Even If Irreproducible
items.summary: The European Patent Office's Enlarged Board has fundamentally altered the landscape of prior art assessment, establishing that products made publicly available before a patent filing date constitute prior art regardless of whether they can be analyzed or reproduced. This landmark decision removes the long-standing reproducibility requirement, making market availability alone sufficient to destroy novelty and significantly raising the stakes for timely patent filings.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2025年7月2日，EPO扩大上诉委员会在G1/23案中裁定，申请日前公开的产品构成现有技术，无论其成分是否可被分析或再现。该决定推翻了G1/92案确立的“可再现性”要求，认为仅市场可获得性即足以破坏新颖性。此举消除了长期存在的法律不确定性，显著提高了及时提交专利申请的重要性，任何上市前公开均可能使后续专利无效。
items.ai_summary_en: In decision G 1/23, the EPO Enlarged Board of Appeal ruled that products made publicly available before a patent filing date constitute prior art under Article 54(2) EPC, regardless of whether their composition or internal structure can be analyzed or reproduced. This judgment departs from the earlier G 1/92 precedent, which required both public availability and reproducibility for a product to form part of the state of the art. The Enlarged Board found that maintaining a reproducibility requirement would lead to absurd results, as many materials rely on irreproducible starting elements. Consequently, market availability alone is now sufficient to destroy novelty, significantly expanding the scope of prior art.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需警惕产品上市前的任何公开行为，包括展会展示或销售，因即使技术细节未完全披露，产品本身也可作为现有技术驳回专利。建议建立严格的保密与申请时间管理机制，确保在申请日前不公开核心技术，避免在新规下丧失新颖性。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling fundamentally alters the landscape of prior art assessment in Europe. Rights holders must recognize that any public disclosure, including sales or exhibitions, may invalidate subsequent patent applications if the product itself is considered prior art, irrespective of reverse-engineering capabilities. This heightens the importance of strict confidentiality measures and timely filing strategies to prevent inadvertent loss of novelty due to pre-filing market activities.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["推翻G1/92先例，取消现有技术认定中的“可再现性”硬性门槛", "确立市场公开即构成现有技术原则，无论内部结构能否被反向工程", "极大扩展现有技术范围，对专利新颖性审查及申请策略产生深远影响"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["G 1/23 establishes that public availability alone suffices for prior art status, removing the reproducibility requirement previously mandated by G 1/92.", "Products placed on the market before the filing date are prior art even if their internal structure or composition cannot be analyzed without undue burden.", "The decision expands the state of the art to include all publicly accessible products, impacting novelty assessments for chemical and material compositions."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["强化申请前保密管理，严防产品上市导致新颖性丧失风险", "调整全球布局策略，优先在欧洲完成申请后再进行市场推广"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Market availability now definitively destroys novelty, requiring rigorous control over pre-filing disclosures and product launches.", "Practice note: Assess prior art risks based on physical availability rather than analytical accessibility when evaluating novelty against existing products.", "What to monitor: Future case law applying G 1/23 to specific technical fields and potential impacts on opposition and invalidation proceedings."]
core_points_en: G 1/23 establishes that public availability alone suffices for prior art status, removing the reproducibility requirement previously mandated by G 1/92.
Products placed on the market before the filing date are prior art even if their internal structure or composition cannot be analyzed without undue burden.
The decision expands the state of the art to include all publicly accessible products, impacting novelty assessments for chemical and material compositions.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Market availability now definitively destroys novelty, requiring rigorous control over pre-filing disclosures and product launches.
Practice note: Assess prior art risks based on physical availability rather than analytical accessibility when evaluating novelty against existing products.
What to monitor: Future case law applying G 1/23 to specific technical fields and potential impacts on opposition and invalidation proceedings.

items.title: Why Tesla is ‘forum shopping’ using InterDigital’s UK assets
items.title_zh: 特斯拉如何利用InterDigital英国资产进行“挑选法院”
items.title_en: Tesla's Strategic Use of InterDigital UK Assets Raises FRAND Jurisdiction Questions
items.summary: A major patent hearing has concluded at the UK Supreme Court. Eeva Hakoranta—formerly chief licensing officer at InterDigital and senior vice president at Nokia—argues that no national court should act as arbiter of global FRAND disputes.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国最高法院已就一起重大专利听证结束审理。前InterDigital首席许可官Eeva Hakoranta指出，任何国家法院均不应充当全球FRAND争议的仲裁者。文章探讨特斯拉利用InterDigital在英国资产的诉讼策略，引发对英国法院在全球标准必要专利许可费率裁定中管辖权边界的关注，反映了当前SEP许可谈判中的法律博弈焦点。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Supreme Court has concluded a significant patent hearing, bringing renewed attention to the boundaries of national courts in adjudicating global standard-essential patent disputes. Eeva Hakoranta, former chief licensing officer at InterDigital and senior vice president at Nokia, argues that no single national court should serve as the arbiter for global fair, reasonable, and non-discriminatory terms. The discussion highlights Tesla's litigation strategy involving InterDigital's UK assets, which some characterize as forum shopping. This case underscores the intense legal maneuvering currently defining SEP licensing negotiations. It raises critical questions about whether UK courts are overreaching their jurisdiction by setting global royalty rates, a trend that impacts multinational technology companies navigating complex intellectual property landscapes across multiple jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临SEP诉讼时，需密切关注英国法院对全球FRAND费率的裁决倾向。建议提前评估“挑选法院”风险，制定多国并行应对策略，避免被动接受不利管辖判决，同时加强合规与许可谈判准备。
items.ai_insight_en: Multinational corporations operating in Europe must closely monitor the UK Supreme Court's evolving stance on global FRAND rate determinations. The potential expansion of UK judicial authority in SEP cases creates significant forum-shopping risks for defendants. Companies should proactively assess these jurisdictional vulnerabilities and develop coordinated, multi-jurisdictional defense strategies rather than reacting passively to adverse rulings. Strengthening internal compliance frameworks and preparing robust negotiation positions are essential to mitigate exposure. Stakeholders should anticipate continued legal friction regarding the extraterritorial reach of national IP courts and align their global litigation management accordingly to avoid being bound by unfavorable unilateral decisions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国最高法院完成重大专利听证，聚焦全球FRAND争议管辖权", "专家主张单一国家法院无权裁定全球标准必要专利许可费率", "特斯拉被指利用InterDigital英国资产进行诉讼策略性选择"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UK Supreme Court concluded a major hearing focusing on the jurisdictional limits for resolving global FRAND disputes.", "Industry experts argue that individual national courts lack the authority to determine global standard-essential patent licensing rates.", "Tesla is accused of leveraging InterDigital's UK assets to strategically select favorable forums for patent litigation."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕英国法院在SEP全球费率裁定中的扩张倾向", "构建多国司法辖区协同的SEP诉讼防御体系"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor UK court tendencies in expanding jurisdiction over global SEP royalty rates to assess litigation exposure.", "Practice note: Develop coordinated multi-jurisdictional defense strategies to counter potential forum-shopping tactics effectively.", "What to monitor: Shifts in judicial reasoning regarding the extraterritorial application of national FRAND determinations."]
core_points_en: The UK Supreme Court concluded a major hearing focusing on the jurisdictional limits for resolving global FRAND disputes.
Industry experts argue that individual national courts lack the authority to determine global standard-essential patent licensing rates.
Tesla is accused of leveraging InterDigital's UK assets to strategically select favorable forums for patent litigation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor UK court tendencies in expanding jurisdiction over global SEP royalty rates to assess litigation exposure.
Practice note: Develop coordinated multi-jurisdictional defense strategies to counter potential forum-shopping tactics effectively.
What to monitor: Shifts in judicial reasoning regarding the extraterritorial application of national FRAND determinations.

items.title: LD Düsseldorf, May 4, 2026, Review Of An Order To Preserve Evidence, Request For The Expert’s Recusal, UPC_CFI_1696/2025
items.title_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫地院：证据保全令审查与专家回避申请裁决
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf: Review of Evidence Preservation Order and Expert Recusal Standards
items.summary: There is a basis for concern regarding an expert’s bias if – from the perspective of a knowledgeable and reasonable observer – certain circumstances give rise to justified doubts as to the expert’s impartiality or independence i.e., there is a likelihood that the expert’s decision will be influenced by factors other than the aforementioned duties.
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫地方法院于2026年5月4日作出裁决（UPC_CFI_1696/2025），审查单方证据保全令并驳回专家回避请求。法院确立两步测试法审查保全令是否存在明显错误，并明确专家偏见认定标准：仅凭意见内容或专业失误不足以证明偏见，需有客观态度不端的其他情形。该裁决遵循布鲁塞尔地方法院先例，强调专家独立性与公正性义务。
items.ai_summary_en: On May 4, 2026, the UPC Local Division in Düsseldorf issued a judgment (UPC_CFI_1696/2025) reviewing an ex parte order for the preservation of evidence and dismissing a request for expert recusal. The Court established a two-step test to examine whether the initial order contained manifest errors, focusing on the procedural validity rather than the execution or results. Regarding the expert’s impartiality, the judgment clarified that mere dissatisfaction with the opinion’s content or alleged technical errors does not constitute bias. Instead, justified doubts require additional circumstances suggesting an unobjective attitude from the perspective of a reasonable observer. This decision aligns with prior rulings from the Brussels Local Division, reinforcing the strict duties of independence and impartiality required of court-appointed experts under the Rules of Procedure.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC单方证据保全时，应聚焦程序合法性而非实体争议。若遇不利专家报告，需收集其存在利益冲突或主观偏见的客观证据，而非仅质疑技术结论。建议加强内部合规，确保在欧知识产权诉讼中的程序抗辩策略精准有效。
items.ai_insight_en: Parties facing ex parte evidence preservation orders at the UPC should prioritize challenging procedural legality over engaging in immediate substantive technical disputes. When contesting expert neutrality, litigants must gather concrete evidence of conflicts of interest or subjective bias, as mere disagreement with technical conclusions is insufficient for recusal. Effective defense strategies require distinguishing between legitimate professional differences and actual partiality. Companies operating in Europe should ensure their internal compliance frameworks support robust procedural defenses, allowing for precise and timely challenges to court-appointed experts based on statutory grounds rather than adversarial preferences.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["采用两步测试法审查单方证据保全令，重点在于签发命令时是否存在明显法律错误", "专家偏见认定需基于合理观察者视角，仅意见错误或缺乏专业性不构成回避理由", "专家负有协助法院的压倒性义务，必须保持独立、客观，不得充当任何一方代理人"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Court applies a two-step test to review ex parte preservation orders, examining only for manifest legal errors in issuance.", "Expert bias requires objective circumstances suggesting unobjective attitudes; technical errors alone do not justify recusal.", "Experts hold an overriding duty to assist the court impartially, maintaining strict independence and objectivity throughout proceedings."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["应对单方保全应侧重程序合法性审查，避免陷入实体技术争议的被动局面", "挑战专家中立性需举证客观偏见情形，单纯的技术分歧无法构成有效回避理由"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Focus defense efforts on procedural validity of preservation orders to avoid being drawn into premature substantive technical debates.", "Practice note: Challenge expert neutrality by presenting objective evidence of bias or conflict, recognizing that technical disagreements do not meet the threshold for recusal.", "What to monitor: Observe how other UPC Local Divisions apply the 'reasonable observer' standard for expert impartiality in future preservation and infringement cases."]
core_points_en: The Court applies a two-step test to review ex parte preservation orders, examining only for manifest legal errors in issuance.
Expert bias requires objective circumstances suggesting unobjective attitudes; technical errors alone do not justify recusal.
Experts hold an overriding duty to assist the court impartially, maintaining strict independence and objectivity throughout proceedings.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Focus defense efforts on procedural validity of preservation orders to avoid being drawn into premature substantive technical debates.
Practice note: Challenge expert neutrality by presenting objective evidence of bias or conflict, recognizing that technical disagreements do not meet the threshold for recusal.
What to monitor: Observe how other UPC Local Divisions apply the 'reasonable observer' standard for expert impartiality in future preservation and infringement cases.

items.title: LD Hamburg, May 5, 2026, Order On Penalty Payments, UPC_CFI_1881/2025
items.title_zh: UPC汉堡分院裁定：违反命令可处周期性罚款
items.title_en: UPC Hamburg: Mandatory Prior Threat and Caps for Recurring Penalty Payments
items.summary: Recurring penalty payments may be imposed for non-compliance with court orders under Rule 354(3) RoP
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC汉堡地方法院在Brita诉Ningbo Blue Pluser案中裁定，依据程序规则354(3)条，对不遵守法院命令者可施加周期性罚款。法院强调，实施罚款前必须预先发出威胁通知，且罚款金额不得超过此前威胁的最高额度。鉴于被告未履行信息提供义务且态度消极，法院判处每日1000欧元的最高限额罚款。此外，法院澄清律师终止委托后仍须负责与法院沟通。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Local Division in Hamburg ruled in Brita v. Ningbo Blue Pluser that recurring penalty payments may be imposed for non-compliance with court orders under Rule 354(3) of the Rules of Procedure. The Court emphasized that a prior threat of penalties is mandatory before imposition, even in provisional measures proceedings. Furthermore, the penalty amount cannot exceed the maximum previously threatened. Due to the Respondent’s failure to fulfill information obligations and uncooperative conduct, the Court imposed the maximum daily rate of EUR 1,000. The decision also clarified that legal representatives remain responsible for court communication even after notifying the Court of their mandate’s termination, ensuring procedural continuity until formal discharge.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC诉讼时，需高度重视临时措施及判决的执行合规性。务必建立严格的内部响应机制，避免因忽视信息披露义务导致高额日罚金。同时，应明确代理律师在案件结束后的沟通责任边界，防止因程序疏漏引发不必要的经济损失及法律风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Parties involved in UPC litigation must prioritize strict compliance with enforcement orders, particularly regarding information and accounting obligations. Ignoring these duties can lead to significant daily financial penalties, capped only by the previously threatened maximum. It is crucial to establish robust internal mechanisms to respond promptly to court orders. Additionally, litigants should clarify the scope of their legal counsel's responsibilities post-termination to prevent communication gaps. Proactive management of these procedural aspects helps mitigate the risk of accumulating substantial penalty payments and ensures smoother enforcement proceedings.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["实施周期性罚款前必须预先发出威胁通知，此为强制性程序要求", "罚款金额受限于先前威胁的最高额度，不得超出既定上限进行追加", "律师终止委托后仍需承担与法院沟通的责任，直至正式解除"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["A prior threat of coercive measures is mandatory before imposing recurring penalty payments, including in provisional measures proceedings.", "Penalty amounts are strictly limited to the maximum figure previously threatened; courts cannot impose higher escalating fines retroactively.", "Legal representatives retain responsibility for court communication even after notifying the Court of their mandate's termination."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重视UPC执行阶段合规，避免忽视披露义务引发高额日罚金", "明确代理律师后续沟通责任，防范程序疏漏导致的额外损失"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strict adherence to disclosure obligations is critical to avoid high daily penalty rates during enforcement.", "Practice note: Ensure all court orders explicitly state the maximum threatened penalty amount to allow for effective future enforcement.", "What to monitor: Clarify communication protocols with legal counsel upon mandate termination to prevent procedural delays or liabilities."]
core_points_en: A prior threat of coercive measures is mandatory before imposing recurring penalty payments, including in provisional measures proceedings.
Penalty amounts are strictly limited to the maximum figure previously threatened; courts cannot impose higher escalating fines retroactively.
Legal representatives retain responsibility for court communication even after notifying the Court of their mandate's termination.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strict adherence to disclosure obligations is critical to avoid high daily penalty rates during enforcement.
Practice note: Ensure all court orders explicitly state the maximum threatened penalty amount to allow for effective future enforcement.
What to monitor: Clarify communication protocols with legal counsel upon mandate termination to prevent procedural delays or liabilities.

items.title: PMAC Advances Operations with New Procedural Rules and Launch of Case Management System
items.title_zh: UPC专利调解仲裁中心发布新规并上线案件管理系统
items.title_en: UPC Patent Mediation Centre Adopts Rules and Launches Case Management System
items.summary: The Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC) of the Unified Patent Court (UPC) has taken significant steps toward full operational readiness,
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）行政委员会于2026年4月正式通过专利调解和仲裁中心（PMAC）的程序规则，并于5月12日上线专用案件管理系统（CCMS）。PMAC旨在提供调解、仲裁等替代性争议解决服务，涵盖欧洲专利及标准必要专利（SEP）纠纷。此举标志着PMAC具备全面运营能力，为权利人提供了高效灵活的专利争议解决新途径，进一步完善了UPC生态系统。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC) has achieved key operational milestones with the adoption of its procedural rules and the launch of its Case Management System. The Administrative Committee formally approved the framework on 24 April 2026, establishing the legal basis for offering mediation, arbitration, and expert determination services. These mechanisms cover European patents, Unitary Patents, and Standard Essential Patent disputes. The electronic Case Management System went live on 12 May 2026, enabling digital submission of ADR requests. Mediation services are scheduled to begin in late May 2026, with other services following later in the year.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧涉及专利纠纷时，可关注UPC-PMAC提供的调解与仲裁渠道。相比传统诉讼，ADR程序更具灵活性与保密性，有助于降低维权成本并加速SEP许可谈判进程。建议企业评估将部分非核心或复杂跨国争议转入PMAC处理，以优化全球知识产权战略布局。
items.ai_insight_en: The operational launch of the UPC’s Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre provides rights holders with structured alternative dispute resolution options alongside traditional litigation. This development is particularly relevant for managing complex patent portfolios and resolving high-stakes technical or licensing disputes efficiently. Parties should review the newly published procedural rules and fee schedules to understand the scope and requirements of these non-litigation pathways within the unified patent system.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC行政委员会正式批准PMAC程序规则，奠定法律与运营基础", "PMAC案件管理系统正式上线，支持电子提交替代性争议解决请求", "服务范围涵盖欧洲专利及SEP/FRAND案件，提供多元ADR选项"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Administrative Committee adopted PMAC's procedural rules on 24 April 2026, creating the legal foundation for alternative dispute resolution services within the court ecosy…", "PMAC launched its dedicated Case Management System on 12 May 2026, allowing parties to electronically submit requests for mediation, arbitration, and expert determination proceedi…", "The Centre will handle disputes involving European and Unitary Patents, including cross-border conflicts and Standard Essential Patent matters, with mediation services commencing…"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用PMAC的ADR机制可降低在欧专利诉讼成本并提升解决效率", "针对SEP/FRAND纠纷，可探索通过调解仲裁达成更优许可条件"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Offers a new avenue for resolving SEP/FRAND and cross-border patent disputes outside of full litigation, potentially enhancing flexibility in portfolio managemen…", "Practice note: Review the adopted procedural rules and CCMS guidance to prepare for electronic case submissions as mediation services commence in late May 2026.", "What to monitor: Track the rollout schedule for arbitration and expert determination services later in the year and any updates to applicable fee structures."]

PMAC launched its dedicated Case Management System on 12 May 2026, allowing parties to electronically submit requests for mediation, arbitration, and expert determination proceedi…
The Centre will handle disputes involving European and Unitary Patents, including cross-border conflicts and Standard Essential Patent matters, with mediation services commencing…

items.title: Judgment of the General Court in Case T-24/25 Les Éditions Albert René v EUIPO - Works 11 Michał Lubiński (Obelix)
items.title_zh: 普通法院撤销EUIPO决定：武器类“Obelix”商标应无效
items.title_en: General Court Annuls EUIPO Refusal to Invalidate Obelix Mark for Weapons and Explosives
items.summary: The General Court nullifies EUIPO’s refusal to invalidate the word mark Obelix for weapons, ammunition and explosives
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟普通法院在T-24/25案中撤销EUIPO拒绝宣告“Obelix”武器商标无效的决定。出版商Les Éditions Albert René依据在先OBELIX商标及声誉受损申请无效，EUIPO曾以声誉证据不足为由驳回。法院指出EUIPO分析有误，未综合评估商标声誉及两商标间的联系。法院强调需全局考察在先商标显著性及公众联想，认定存在损害在先商标声誉的风险，支持了权利人的主张。
items.ai_summary_en: The General Court annulled an EUIPO decision refusing to invalidate the word mark Obelix for firearms, ammunition, and explosives. The applicant, Les Éditions Albert René, relied on its earlier OBELIX mark and alleged damage to its reputation. The Court found EUIPO’s assessment of the earlier mark’s reputation incomplete, noting it improperly disregarded evidence of use with the registered symbol and in association with Asterix. Furthermore, EUIPO failed to adequately assess the link between the marks and the risk of detriment to the earlier mark’s distinctive character or repute, despite differences in goods.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment reinforces that EU trademark offices must conduct a holistic assessment of reputation, considering cumulative evidence rather than isolated deficiencies. Rights holders asserting cross-class protection based on reputation should ensure their submissions clearly demonstrate how distinct elements contribute to overall market recognition. The case highlights the importance of properly contextualizing co-branded or associated signs when establishing independent distinctiveness and repute for specific terms within a larger franchise.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院认定EUIPO对在先商标声誉评估不完整，忽视关键使用证据", "强调需全局评估商标间联系，不能仅因商品差异大而否定关联", "撤销EUIPO决定，支持基于声誉受损宣告争议商标无效的诉求"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["EUIPO erred by failing to comprehensively assess all relevant factors demonstrating the reputation of the earlier OBELIX mark.", "Evidence of use alongside the registered symbol and in combination with Asterix was unjustifiably disregarded in the reputation analysis.", "The assessment of the link between marks and potential detriment to reputation cannot rely solely on wide differences between goods."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["避免注册知名文化符号，防范跨类声誉侵权法律风险", "重视商标使用证据留存，积极应对欧盟商标无效宣告挑战"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders must present comprehensive, cumulative evidence of reputation to withstand scrutiny in invalidity proceedings involving well-known cultural icons.", "Practice note: When assessing reputation, authorities must consider all relevant factors collectively; isolated insufficiencies do not negate established market recognition.", "What to monitor: Future applications of this holistic reputation assessment standard in cases involving associated signs or franchise-related trademarks."]
core_points_en: EUIPO erred by failing to comprehensively assess all relevant factors demonstrating the reputation of the earlier OBELIX mark.
Evidence of use alongside the registered symbol and in combination with Asterix was unjustifiably disregarded in the reputation analysis.
The assessment of the link between marks and potential detriment to reputation cannot rely solely on wide differences between goods.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders must present comprehensive, cumulative evidence of reputation to withstand scrutiny in invalidity proceedings involving well-known cultural icons.
Practice note: When assessing reputation, authorities must consider all relevant factors collectively; isolated insufficiencies do not negate established market recognition.
What to monitor: Future applications of this holistic reputation assessment standard in cases involving associated signs or franchise-related trademarks.

items.title: Judgment of the Court in Case C-286/25 BRANDL
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院判决：匈牙利农地用益物权赔偿不得仅依删除时市值计算
items.title_en: Judgment of the Court in Case C-286/25 BRANDL
items.summary: Rights of usufruct over agricultural land in Hungary: EU law precludes financial compensation calculated solely on the basis of the market value of the land at the time of deletion of those rights from the land register
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院在BRANDL案中裁定，欧盟法禁止成员国将农地用益物权被注销后的赔偿金，仅基于注销时的土地市场价值计算。此前匈牙利立法废除非亲属用益物权被认定违反资本自由流动与财产权。新法允许恢复权利并获赔，但规定按注销时市值的1/20乘以年限计算。法院认为该公式未涵盖利润损失等损害，无法确保有效权利保护与充分赔偿，故不符合欧盟法要求。
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案确立了欧盟法下国家赔偿机制的底线：标准化计算公式不得系统性排除关键损害要素（如利润损失）。对于涉及跨国资产权利变动或历史遗留权利清理的企业，需关注成员国赔偿立法是否符合“充分性”与“有效性”原则，警惕形式合规但实质不足的补偿方案。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟法禁止仅以用益物权注销时的土地市场价值为唯一基准计算赔偿金", "完全排除可赔偿损害中的利润损失，可能导致实际上无法弥补所受损害", "成员国虽有权设定赔偿标准，但必须确保赔偿与所受损害相称且有效"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["标准化赔偿公式若排除利润损失，可能因违反有效性原则而被认定无效", "成员国在设定赔偿标准时仍享有裁量权，但须确保个案赔偿的充分性", "需持续观察匈牙利后续如何调整赔偿计算模型以符合欧盟法院判决要求"]
