items.title: Statutory guidance: Designs Practice Notice (DPN) 01/26: Practice in respect of graphic symbols/icons, graphical/web user interfaces and animated designs
items.title_zh: 法定指南：图形符号、用户界面及动画外观设计审查实务
items.title_en: UKIPO Guidance on Examining Graphic Symbols, User Interfaces, and Animated Designs
items.summary: This provides information on the examination of applications which consist of graphic symbols/icons, graphical web user interfaces and animated designs.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局发布第 01/26 号设计实务通知，明确针对图形符号、图标、图形化网页用户界面及动画外观设计的审查标准。该文件旨在为申请人和审查员提供具体操作指引，规范此类非传统静态设计在申请阶段的呈现方式与可注册性判断，确保审查实践的一致性。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office has issued Designs Practice Notice DPN 01/26, establishing specific examination standards for graphic symbols, icons, graphical web user interfaces, and animated designs. This statutory guidance aims to provide clear operational instructions for both applicants and examiners, standardizing the presentation methods and registrability assessments for these non-traditional static design types during the application phase. By clarifying procedural requirements, the notice ensures consistency in examination practices across the UK jurisdiction. It addresses the unique characteristics of digital and dynamic visual elements, offering a structured framework for evaluating their compliance with current design law provisions. This update is essential for stakeholders seeking to protect modern digital assets within the UK market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国科技与互联网企业应关注英国对 UI 及动画设计的新规，调整申请策略以符合呈现要求。建议在出口英国前优化界面设计文档，避免因形式问题被驳回，提升数字产品在英确权效率。
items.ai_insight_en: Stakeholders operating in the technology and internet sectors should closely monitor these new UK regulations concerning user interfaces and animated designs. Adjusting application strategies to meet updated presentation requirements is crucial for securing protection. Applicants are advised to optimize interface design documentation before filing to prevent rejections based on formal deficiencies. Proactive compliance with these guidelines will enhance the efficiency of rights confirmation for digital products in the UK market. Ensuring that animation sequences and icon sets adhere to the specified formats can significantly reduce examination obstacles and streamline the registration process for complex digital assets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["明确图形符号与图标类外观设计的审查具体要求", "规范图形化及网页用户界面的申请提交标准", "确立动画外观设计在审查中的实务处理原则"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Clarifies specific examination requirements for graphic symbol and icon-based design applications.", "Standardizes submission criteria for graphical and web-based user interface design registrations.", "Establishes practical handling principles for assessing animated design applications during examination."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["涉英数字产品需按新指南调整界面设计申请材料", "动画与图标设计应提前合规以避免审查障碍"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Digital product teams must align interface design materials with the new UK guidelines to ensure successful registration.", "Practice note: Prepare animation and icon design submissions carefully to avoid procedural objections during examination.", "What to monitor: Future updates from the UKIPO regarding the interpretation of 'animated' features in registered design claims."]
core_points_en: Clarifies specific examination requirements for graphic symbol and icon-based design applications.
Standardizes submission criteria for graphical and web-based user interface design registrations.
Establishes practical handling principles for assessing animated design applications during examination.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Digital product teams must align interface design materials with the new UK guidelines to ensure successful registration.
Practice note: Prepare animation and icon design submissions carefully to avoid procedural objections during examination.
What to monitor: Future updates from the UKIPO regarding the interpretation of 'animated' features in registered design claims.

items.title: Not so easy: easyGroup wins on confusion, but loses on non-use
items.title_zh: easyGroup 混淆胜诉但因未使用败诉
items.title_en: easyGroup Wins Confusion Finding but Loses Case Due to Non-Use Defence
items.summary: In easyGroup Limited v Jaybank Leisure Limited [2025] EWHC 3077 (IPEC), HHJ Hacon dealt with a trade mark claim with an unusual outcome. easyGroup succeeded on infringement in principle, but still lost the case. That is what makes the decision worth reading. Background and the issues
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权企业法院判决 easyGroup 虽证明被告使用 EASIHIRE 构成混淆侵权，但因无法举证涉案服务上的真实使用，被告援引未使用抗辩成功。该案凸显程序上未使用可作为实质抗辩，即便侵权成立，若商标面临撤销风险则无法执行。
items.ai_summary_en: In easyGroup Limited v Jaybank Leisure Limited [2025] EWHC 3077 (IPEC), the UK Intellectual Property Enterprise Court delivered a nuanced judgment. While HHJ Hacon found that Jaybank’s use of EASIHIRE created a likelihood of confusion with easyGroup’s registered easyHire mark, thereby establishing infringement in principle under section 10(2) TMA 1994, easyGroup ultimately lost the case. The defendant successfully invoked a section 11A non-use defence. The court determined that easyGroup failed to prove genuine use of the mark for the specific services relied upon at the relevant date. This decision underscores that procedural reliance on section 11A can operate as a substantive defence within infringement proceedings, effectively blocking enforcement even when similarity and confusion are proven, if the claimant cannot demonstrate qualifying use or proper reasons for non-use.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧维权时，须确保商标在指定商品服务上有连续真实使用证据。仅凭集团品牌声誉或网站链接难以满足法定要求，应提前梳理使用凭证以防被反制。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment highlights critical evidentiary requirements for trademark enforcement in the UK. Rights holders must maintain robust records of genuine commercial use for each specific good or service covered by their registrations. Relying on broader group brand reputation or website cross-linking is often inadequate to satisfy statutory use requirements. Practitioners should conduct thorough audits of use evidence before initiating infringement actions to mitigate the risk of successful non-use defences. Furthermore, defendants should consider pleading section 11A as a standalone defence within infringement proceedings, offering a strategic alternative to separate revocation actions when challenging the validity of asserted rights.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院认定标志相似且存在混淆可能性，原则上构成侵权", "被告成功援引第 11A 条未使用抗辩，阻断侵权责任", "广泛品牌架构宣传不足以替代具体服务的真实使用证据"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The court found sign similarity and likelihood of confusion, establishing that infringement occurred in principle under section 10(2).", "The defendant successfully raised a section 11A non-use defence, which blocked liability despite the finding of infringement.", "General brand architecture claims were insufficient; strict proof of genuine use for specific services was required."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["维权前需自查商标使用证据，避免因未使用被抗辩", "集团品牌策略不能替代具体类别的真实商业使用"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure continuous, documented genuine use for all registered goods and services to prevent enforcement failures.", "Practice note: Conduct pre-litigation audits of use evidence to counter potential section 11A non-use defections effectively.", "What to monitor: How courts assess the sufficiency of digital evidence and brand structure claims against specific use requirements."]
core_points_en: The court found sign similarity and likelihood of confusion, establishing that infringement occurred in principle under section 10(2).
The defendant successfully raised a section 11A non-use defence, which blocked liability despite the finding of infringement.
General brand architecture claims were insufficient; strict proof of genuine use for specific services was required.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure continuous, documented genuine use for all registered goods and services to prevent enforcement failures.
Practice note: Conduct pre-litigation audits of use evidence to counter potential section 11A non-use defections effectively.
What to monitor: How courts assess the sufficiency of digital evidence and brand structure claims against specific use requirements.

items.title: Ericsson proposes changes to ETSI IPR Guide to address perceived SEP licensing problems
items.title_zh: 爱立信提议修改ETSI知识产权指南以解决许可问题
items.title_en: Ericsson Proposes ETSI IPR Guide Revisions to Address SEP Licensing Challenges
items.summary: Changes intended to address policy concerns in market-savvy way, IPR policy chief Patricio Delgado tells IAM in exclusive interview
items.ai_summary_zh: 爱立信向ETSI提出修改知识产权指南的建议，旨在以市场导向方式解决标准必要专利（SEP）许可中的政策担忧。爱立信IPR政策主管Patricio Delgado在接受IAM独家采访时指出，当前许可实践存在争议，需通过规则调整平衡各方利益。此举反映了行业巨头试图重塑SEP许可框架的努力，可能对欧盟及全球FRAND谈判产生深远影响，引发对许可透明度与公平性的新一轮讨论。
items.ai_summary_en: Ericsson has submitted proposals to modify the ETSI Intellectual Property Rights Guide, aiming to resolve perceived issues within standard essential patent licensing frameworks. In an exclusive interview with IAM, Patricio Delgado, Ericsson’s IPR policy chief, explained that these changes are designed to address policy concerns through market-oriented solutions. The initiative highlights ongoing disputes regarding current licensing practices and seeks to balance the interests of various stakeholders. This move reflects a broader strategic effort by major industry players to reshape the SEP licensing landscape. Consequently, it may significantly influence FRAND negotiations across the EU and globally, sparking renewed debates concerning transparency and fairness in intellectual property licensing standards.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应密切关注ETSI指南修订动向，评估其对FRAND谈判立场的影响。建议提前梳理自身SEP组合，加强合规审查，避免在新规则下陷入被动。同时，积极参与行业标准讨论，争取在规则制定中维护自身合法权益，降低潜在诉讼风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely track the evolution of ETSI guidelines, as potential revisions may alter the baseline for FRAND determinations. Rights holders are advised to review their SEP portfolios and compliance protocols proactively to ensure alignment with emerging standards. Engaging actively in industry standard-setting discussions allows companies to safeguard their interests during rule formulation. This approach helps mitigate litigation risks and ensures that licensing positions remain robust against shifting regulatory expectations and industry norms.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["爱立信提议修改ETSI IPR指南以应对许可挑战", "旨在通过市场友好方式解决政策层面的许可担忧", "反映行业巨头重塑SEP许可框架的战略意图"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Ericsson proposes amendments to the ETSI IPR Guide to tackle persistent challenges in standard essential patent licensing.", "The proposed revisions aim to resolve policy-level licensing concerns using approaches that are friendly to market dynamics.", "This initiative reflects a strategic intent by industry leaders to fundamentally reshape the existing SEP licensing framework."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["密切跟踪ETSI指南修订以预判FRAND规则变化", "强化SEP合规管理并积极参与行业标准制定"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor ETSI guide revisions to anticipate shifts in FRAND negotiation baselines and legal standards.", "Practice note: Strengthen SEP compliance management and portfolio reviews to align with evolving licensing frameworks.", "What to monitor: Active participation in industry standard-setting processes to protect rights and reduce litigation exposure."]
core_points_en: Ericsson proposes amendments to the ETSI IPR Guide to tackle persistent challenges in standard essential patent licensing.
The proposed revisions aim to resolve policy-level licensing concerns using approaches that are friendly to market dynamics.
This initiative reflects a strategic intent by industry leaders to fundamentally reshape the existing SEP licensing framework.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor ETSI guide revisions to anticipate shifts in FRAND negotiation baselines and legal standards.
Practice note: Strengthen SEP compliance management and portfolio reviews to align with evolving licensing frameworks.
What to monitor: Active participation in industry standard-setting processes to protect rights and reduce litigation exposure.

items.title: LD Düsseldorf, April 10, 2026, Order, UPC_CFI_1110/2025 And UPC_CFI_1111/2025
items.title_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫分院：初审判决被推翻后已付费用可追回
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf: Costs Paid Under Overturned First-Instance Decision Are Recoverable
items.summary: Costs paid under a first-instance decision are recoverable if that decision is overturned on appeal. The appeal’s outcome invalidates the initial cost order (Rule 151 RoP).
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）杜塞尔多夫地方法院在Seoul Viosys案中裁定，若初审判决在上诉中被推翻，依据程序规则第151条，基于该判决产生的初始费用命令失效，已支付费用可在后续程序中追回。法院明确指出，初审阶段达成的费用协议不自动延伸至上诉阶段，因为上诉成本需单独评估其合理性与比例性。此裁决明确了UPC诉讼中费用承担的法律基础随主判决变动而变动的原则，对当事人管理诉讼预算及风险评估具有重要指导意义。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Local Division in Düsseldorf ruled that costs paid pursuant to a first-instance decision are recoverable if that decision is subsequently overturned on appeal. The Court held that the reversal of the main judgment removes the legal basis for the initial cost order under Rule 151 of the Rules of Procedure. Consequently, amounts previously paid become subject to recovery in subsequent cost proceedings. Additionally, the Court determined that a cost agreement concluded for the first instance does not automatically extend to the appeal stage. Appeal costs must be assessed separately based on reasonableness and proportionality under Article 69 of the UPCA, as the original agreement relied on first-instance estimates where an appeal was not foreseeable.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在参与UPC诉讼时，应严格区分初审与上诉阶段的费用预算，避免默认费用协议覆盖全程。鉴于败诉方可能通过上诉逆转费用责任，企业需动态调整诉讼策略，并在和解谈判中明确费用条款的适用范围，以控制跨境知识产权纠纷的整体财务风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision clarifies the temporal scope of cost orders and agreements within UPC litigation. Rights holders and defendants should recognize that financial obligations arising from first-instance rulings are contingent on the final outcome of any appeals. Cost settlements or agreements negotiated at the trial level do not inherently cover appellate stages, necessitating distinct budgeting and strategic planning for potential appeals to manage exposure effectively.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["初审判决被上诉推翻后，原费用命令失效，已付费用具备可追回性", "初审费用协议不自动涵盖上诉阶段，上诉费用需单独评估合理性", "法律依据为《统一专利法院协定》第69条及程序规则第151条"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Reversal of a first-instance decision invalidates the initial cost order, making previously paid costs recoverable under Rule 151 RoP.", "A cost agreement for the first instance does not automatically apply to appeal proceedings, which require separate assessment.", "Appeal costs are evaluated independently for reasonableness and proportionality pursuant to Article 69 UPCA."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["严格区分初审与上诉费用预算，避免协议范围误判", "动态调整策略，关注上诉对费用责任的逆转影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Initial cost payments may be reclaimed if the underlying judgment is reversed, affecting overall litigation financial risk.", "Practice note: Draft cost agreements specifically for the first instance; do not assume they cover subsequent appeal stages.", "What to monitor: How separate assessments of reasonableness and proportionality under Article 69 UPCA are applied to appeal costs."]
core_points_en: Reversal of a first-instance decision invalidates the initial cost order, making previously paid costs recoverable under Rule 151 RoP.
A cost agreement for the first instance does not automatically apply to appeal proceedings, which require separate assessment.
Appeal costs are evaluated independently for reasonableness and proportionality pursuant to Article 69 UPCA.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Initial cost payments may be reclaimed if the underlying judgment is reversed, affecting overall litigation financial risk.
Practice note: Draft cost agreements specifically for the first instance; do not assume they cover subsequent appeal stages.
What to monitor: How separate assessments of reasonableness and proportionality under Article 69 UPCA are applied to appeal costs.

items.title: LD Brussels, April 14, 2026, Procedural Order, UPC_CFI_1357/2025; UPC_CFI_629/2025
items.title_zh: UPC布鲁塞尔：证据开示需满足四项累积条件及保密保护
items.title_en: UPC Brussels: Evidence Production Requires Four Cumulative Conditions and Confidentiality Safeguards
items.summary: Evidence production applications (Art. 59 UPCA, R. 190 RoP) require four cumulative conditions: reasonably available evidence, specification and control, confidentiality protection, and proportionality.
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC布鲁塞尔地方法院在Establish Labs诉GC Aesthetics案中发布程序令，明确依据《统一专利法院协定》第59条申请证据开示需满足四项累积条件：合理可获得证据、具体指定且由对方控制、保密保护及比例原则。法院裁定无需穷尽其他救济途径，仅主张机密性不足以拒绝开示，需在提交专家信息后签发保密令以平衡双方利益。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Local Division in Brussels issued a procedural order clarifying that applications for evidence production under Article 59 of the UPCA and Rule 190 of the Rules of Procedure must satisfy four cumulative conditions. These include reasonably available evidence, specific identification and control by the opposing party, adequate confidentiality protection, and proportionality. The Court ruled that applicants are not required to exhaust other remedies before seeking disclosure, as demanding conclusive proof beforehand would create circular logic. Merely asserting that information is confidential or internal is insufficient to deny production requests. Instead, courts rely on Rule 262A to implement proportionate safeguards. In this case, the Court granted production conditional upon the defendants submitting details of independent experts and a designated natural person, followed by a formal confidentiality order to balance the interests of both parties effectively.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC诉讼时，应重视早期证据收集以满足“合理可获得”标准。面对对方证据开示请求，不可仅以内部机密为由抗辩，需主动提出符合R.262A的保密方案（如限制接触人员）。同时，利用无需穷尽其他途径的规则，积极申请关键证据以打破举证僵局。
items.ai_insight_en: Parties involved in UPC litigation should prioritize early evidence collection to meet the reasonably available standard for production orders. When facing requests from opponents, relying solely on internal confidentiality assertions is ineffective; instead, proactive proposals for specific protective measures under Rule 262A are essential. Litigants can leverage the rule that does not require exhausting other avenues to break evidentiary deadlocks. By securing key evidence through court orders with appropriate confidentiality safeguards, parties can strengthen their factual basis without compromising sensitive business information during the proceedings.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["证据开示须满足合理可得、指定可控、保密保护及比例原则四项累积条件", "申请人无需先穷尽其他取证手段， prima facie 门槛防止逻辑循环论证", "单纯主张机密性无效，需通过R.262A规则框架实施具体保密措施方可开示"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Evidence production requires reasonably available evidence, specific identification, control by the opponent, and proportionality.", "Applicants need not exhaust other remedies first, as the prima facie threshold prevents circular reasoning demands.", "Confidentiality claims alone do not block disclosure; specific protective measures under Rule 262A are mandatory."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["早期构建证据链以满足UPC证据开示的初步证明标准", "善用保密令机制对抗不当拒证，保障己方取证权利"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Early evidence gathering is critical to satisfy the prima facie standard for successful production orders.", "Practice note: Counter confidentiality objections by proposing specific access restrictions under Rule 262A rather than general refusals.", "What to monitor: Judicial application of proportionality tests when balancing disclosure needs against trade secret protections."]
core_points_en: Evidence production requires reasonably available evidence, specific identification, control by the opponent, and proportionality.
Applicants need not exhaust other remedies first, as the prima facie threshold prevents circular reasoning demands.
Confidentiality claims alone do not block disclosure; specific protective measures under Rule 262A are mandatory.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Early evidence gathering is critical to satisfy the prima facie standard for successful production orders.
Practice note: Counter confidentiality objections by proposing specific access restrictions under Rule 262A rather than general refusals.
What to monitor: Judicial application of proportionality tests when balancing disclosure needs against trade secret protections.

items.title: LD Paris, April 10, 2026, Procedural Order, UPC_CFI_301/2025
items.title_zh: UPC巴黎分部：FRAND抗辩中原告享有最后陈述权
items.title_en: UPC Paris: Claimant Entitled to Final Submission in FRAND Defence Proceedings
items.summary: Unlike in case of a counterclaim for revocation (R. 29 et seq. RoP) or an application to amend the patent (R. 30 et seq. RoP), where the number and content of the pleadings are precisely set out, the Rules of Procedure do not contain respective provisions with respect to a FRAND defence.
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC巴黎地方法院在Orange诉HMD案中发布程序令，指出《程序规则》未规定FRAND抗辩的具体答辩次数。法院依据公平与对抗原则，裁定作为FRAND抗辩反被告的原告有权进行最后陈述以确保公平审判，无需评估论点是否新颖。此举允许原告提交最终意见并驳回被告进一步回应的请求，填补了FRAND抗辩程序规则的空白。
items.ai_summary_en: In Orange SA v. HMD Global Oy, the UPC Local Division in Paris issued a procedural order addressing the absence of specific pleading rules for FRAND defences under the Rules of Procedure. Unlike provisions for revocation counterclaims or patent amendments, the Rules do not prescribe the number or content of pleadings for FRAND issues. Relying on general principles of fairness and adversarial proceedings from the Preamble, the Court held that the claimant, acting as counter-defendant to the FRAND defence, is entitled to the final word to ensure a fair trial. This ruling permits the claimant’s final submission while denying the defendant’s request for further response, without assessing whether the FRAND arguments were new. This decision clarifies procedural expectations and fills a gap in the current framework.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在UPC应对FRAND诉讼时，需重视程序策略。鉴于原告拥有最后陈述权，被告应在早期阶段充分、全面地提出FRAND抗辩理由及证据，避免依赖后续补充。建议提前规划答辩节奏，确保关键论点在首次或中期陈述中完整呈现，以抵消原告最后陈述带来的程序优势。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling establishes a significant procedural dynamic where the party asserting infringement retains the final say on FRAND issues. For defendants raising FRAND defences, this creates a strategic imperative to present comprehensive arguments and evidence early in the proceedings. Reliance on subsequent rebuttals is no longer viable. Legal teams must prioritize thorough preparation for initial and intermediate stages to mitigate the disadvantage of being unable to respond to the claimant’s final submissions. Proactive case management is essential to lock in key factual and legal positions before the claimant exercises their right to the last word.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["《程序规则》缺失FRAND抗辩具体答辩规定，法院依公平原则裁量", "原告作为FRAND抗辩反被告，依法享有确保公平审判的最后陈述权", "法院准许原告最终提交，驳回被告进一步回应请求，不审查论点新颖性"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Rules of Procedure lack specific provisions governing the number and content of pleadings for FRAND defences.", "The Court applied general fairness principles, granting the claimant the right to make the final submission in FRAND matters.", "The defendant's request for further response was denied, with no assessment made regarding the novelty of the arguments."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["被告应尽早全面提出FRAND抗辩理由，避免依赖后续补充机会", "利用早期陈述固化关键论点，以抵消原告最后陈述权的程序优势"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Defendants face a structural disadvantage as claimants hold the final word on FRAND defences, requiring robust early-case strategy.", "Practice note: Ensure all critical FRAND arguments and supporting evidence are fully presented in initial or intermediate pleadings.", "What to monitor: Future divisions may adopt similar approaches; track consistency in applying fairness principles to undefined procedural gaps."]
core_points_en: The Rules of Procedure lack specific provisions governing the number and content of pleadings for FRAND defences.
The Court applied general fairness principles, granting the claimant the right to make the final submission in FRAND matters.
The defendant's request for further response was denied, with no assessment made regarding the novelty of the arguments.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Defendants face a structural disadvantage as claimants hold the final word on FRAND defences, requiring robust early-case strategy.
Practice note: Ensure all critical FRAND arguments and supporting evidence are fully presented in initial or intermediate pleadings.
What to monitor: Future divisions may adopt similar approaches; track consistency in applying fairness principles to undefined procedural gaps.

items.title: UPC: Revocation of Independent Claims Does Not Invalidate Unchallenged Claims | Emporia v Seoul Viosys | EIP
items.title_zh: UPC：撤销独立权利要求不自动导致未受挑战的从属权利要求无效
items.title_en: UPC: Revocation of Independent Claim Does Not Automatically Invalidate Unchallenged Dependent Claims
items.summary: The UPC Central Division clarifies that revoking an independent patent claim does not automatically invalidate unchallenged dependent claims, highlighting important strategic considerations in revocation actions.
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC 中央分部在 Emporia 诉 Seoul Viosys 案中裁定，撤销专利的独立权利要求并不自动使未受挑战的从属权利要求无效。法院指出，从属权利要求的附加技术特征可能具备独立权利要求所缺乏的新颖性或创造性。即便独立权利要求因增加事项被撤销，专利权人仍可通过修改将从属权利要求提升为独立形式以维持专利部分有效。
items.ai_summary_en: In Emporia UK and Ireland Ltd. v Seoul Viosys Co., Ltd., the UPC Paris Central Division ruled that revoking an independent patent claim does not automatically invalidate unchallenged dependent claims. The court rejected a preliminary objection regarding competence, distinguishing the parties from parallel Court of Appeal proceedings where only specific claims were revoked. The Division held that dependent claims contain additional technical features that may independently satisfy novelty and inventive step requirements. Consequently, the invalidation of the sole independent claim did not extend to dependent claims outside the scope of the earlier dispute, allowing the patent to potentially remain valid in part.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision clarifies the structural independence of patent claims within UPC revocation actions. Rights holders can preserve partial patent validity by relying on the distinct technical contributions of dependent claims, even if the broadest independent claim is invalidated. This underscores the importance of precise claim construction and the strategic management of parallel proceedings across different jurisdictions to avoid unintended collateral effects on unchallenged claim sets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["独立权利要求撤销不自动波及未受挑战的从属权利要求", "从属权利要求的附加特征可单独满足新颖性与创造性要求", "专利权人需主动申请修改以将从属权利要求转为独立形式"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Paris Central Division confirmed that revocation of an independent claim does not automatically invalidate unchallenged dependent claims.", "Dependent claims possess additional technical features that may independently meet novelty and inventive step requirements separate from the independent claim.", "A preliminary objection based on parallel national proceedings was rejected because the parties involved were not considered identical under Article 33(4) UPCA."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["独立权利要求失效不等于专利整体无效，需重点防御从属权利要求", "应及时启动权利要求修改程序，将有效从属权利要求升格为独立权利要求"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Patent owners should assess the standalone validity of dependent claims when facing revocation of independent claims to maintain partial protection.", "Practice note: Ensure clear distinction between parties in parallel national and UPC proceedings to manage competence objections and scope of dispute effectively.", "What to monitor: How the UPC applies this principle to auxiliary requests and whether similar logic applies to infringement counterclaims involving partially revoked patents."]
core_points_en: The UPC Paris Central Division confirmed that revocation of an independent claim does not automatically invalidate unchallenged dependent claims.
Dependent claims possess additional technical features that may independently meet novelty and inventive step requirements separate from the independent claim.
A preliminary objection based on parallel national proceedings was rejected because the parties involved were not considered identical under Article 33(4) UPCA.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Patent owners should assess the standalone validity of dependent claims when facing revocation of independent claims to maintain partial protection.
Practice note: Ensure clear distinction between parties in parallel national and UPC proceedings to manage competence objections and scope of dispute effectively.
What to monitor: How the UPC applies this principle to auxiliary requests and whether similar logic applies to infringement counterclaims involving partially revoked patents.

items.title: President’s mission to Georgia
items.title_zh: EPO主席访问格鲁吉亚：强化专利验证与技术转移合作
items.title_en: EPO President Visits Georgia to Strengthen Patent Validation and Technology Transfer Cooperation
items.summary: During a mission to Georgia, the EPO President highlighted the role of patents, validation and skills in technology transfer, reinforcing cooperation with Sakpatenti and innovation stakeholders.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年4月，EPO主席António Campinos访问格鲁吉第比利斯，与Sakpatenti及教育部举行高层会议。双方重申长期伙伴关系，肯定专利验证协议成效（两年超300件请求），强调其在促进技术转移、吸引外资及构建创新生态中的作用。会议聚焦“从研究到影响”，探讨专利如何连接科研机构与市场，通过法律确定性支持创新成果商业化。
items.ai_summary_en: In April 2026, European Patent Office President António Campinos visited Tbilisi for high-level meetings with Sakpatenti and the Ministry of Education. The visit reaffirmed the long-standing partnership between the EPO and Georgia’s National Intellectual Property Center. Discussions highlighted the success of the validation agreement, which has generated over 300 requests in two years. This mechanism is recognized for enhancing the local innovation ecosystem by facilitating technology transfer, attracting foreign investment, and supporting economic development. The accompanying conference explored how patents bridge research institutions and markets, providing legal certainty that aids the commercialization of scientific results and fosters effective international collaboration within the innovation value chain.
items.ai_insight_zh: 格鲁吉亚作为EPO验证国，其创新生态日益活跃。中国企业应关注该国专利布局机会，利用验证程序低成本进入当地市场。同时，借鉴EPO在技术转移方面的经验，加强产学研合作，提升自身IP运营能力，以应对欧洲及周边市场的竞争与挑战。
items.ai_insight_en: Georgia’s active participation in the EPO validation system signals a maturing IP landscape in the Caucasus region. For rights holders, this offers a streamlined, cost-effective pathway to secure patent protection without establishing separate national filing procedures. Companies should evaluate the strategic value of validating European patents in Georgia to protect innovations and deter competitors in this emerging market. Furthermore, observing the EPO’s structured approach to linking academic research with industrial application provides valuable benchmarks for improving internal technology transfer frameworks and enhancing the commercial viability of R&D outputs across European jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EPO与格鲁吉亚Sakpatenti深化合作，验证协议两年受理超300件请求", "强调专利系统在促进技术转移、吸引外资及推动经济发展中的关键作用", "会议探讨利用专利法律确定性，助力科研成果向市场解决方案转化"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EPO and Sakpatenti deepened cooperation, with the validation agreement processing over 300 requests in its first two years.", "Patents are emphasized as critical drivers for technology transfer, foreign investment attraction, and broader economic development.", "Discussions focused on leveraging patent legal certainty to transform research outcomes into viable market solutions."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注格鲁吉亚验证专利布局，低成本拓展高加索地区市场", "借鉴EPO技术转移模式，强化产学研合作与IP商业化能力"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Utilize the EPO validation procedure for efficient, low-cost patent protection in Georgia and the wider Caucasus region.", "Practice note: Adopt EPO-inspired models to strengthen university-industry partnerships and improve IP commercialization capabilities.", "What to monitor: Track trends in validation filings and policy developments affecting technology transfer incentives in Georgia."]
core_points_en: The EPO and Sakpatenti deepened cooperation, with the validation agreement processing over 300 requests in its first two years.
Patents are emphasized as critical drivers for technology transfer, foreign investment attraction, and broader economic development.
Discussions focused on leveraging patent legal certainty to transform research outcomes into viable market solutions.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Utilize the EPO validation procedure for efficient, low-cost patent protection in Georgia and the wider Caucasus region.
Practice note: Adopt EPO-inspired models to strengthen university-industry partnerships and improve IP commercialization capabilities.
What to monitor: Track trends in validation filings and policy developments affecting technology transfer incentives in Georgia.

items.title: Modular IP Education Framework marks three years of growing impact
items.title_zh: EPO模块化IP教育框架实施三年影响显著
items.title_en: EPO Modular IP Education Framework Reaches Three-Year Milestone with Expanded University Network
items.summary: An exchange on insights, best practices and shared values to advance gender parity in innovation
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局（EPO）宣布其模块化知识产权教育框架（MIPEF）实施三年来成效显著。该框架旨在将IP教育融入STEM学科，已覆盖31个EPC缔约国的100多所大学，惠及6500多名学生并颁发2500多份证书。通过提供结构化、灵活的课程，MIPEF填补了高等教育中技术学科IP教育的空白，助力培养具备专利知识的下一代创新者，促进欧洲创新文化发展。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office (EPO) reports that its Modular IP Education Framework (MIPEF) has engaged over 6,500 students and issued more than 2,500 certifications since its 2023 launch. The programme now includes over 100 partner universities across 31 European Patent Convention contracting states. MIPEF integrates practical IP training into science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) curricula through ready-to-use courses developed with IP experts. Participants receive EPO certificates and, at the university's discretion, European Credit Transfer and Accumulation System (ECTS) credits. This initiative aims to democratize access to IP knowledge, equipping future inventors and entrepreneurs with essential skills for an innovation-driven economy while modernizing higher education offerings in technical disciplines.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: For European and US companies operating in R-intensive sectors, the expansion of MIPEF signals a shift toward early-stage IP literacy among technical talent. As universities increasingly integrate patent rights and IP management into STEM degrees, the baseline competency of new graduates entering the workforce is rising. This trend supports stronger internal IP cultures within organizations and may reduce the initial training burden for in-house counsel and IP teams. Rights holders should recognize this evolving educational landscape as a resource for building long-term innovation capacity and enhancing compliance with European IP standards.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["MIPEF覆盖31国百所高校，认证超2500人，深化STEM领域IP教育", "提供灵活课程填补高校技术学科IP教育空白，无需额外培训结构", "获证学生可得EPO证书及ECTS学分，赋能未来发明人与创业者"]

items.title: Judgment of the Court in Case C-769/22 Commission v Hungary (Values of the European Union)
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院裁定匈牙利LGBTI+相关法律违反欧盟法及GDPR
items.title_en: CJEU Rules Hungary Breaches EU Law and GDPR via LGBTI+ Content Restrictions
items.summary: Values of the European Union: by adopting a law which stigmatises and marginalises LGBTI+ persons, Hungary has acted in breach of EU law
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院在委员会诉匈牙利案中裁定，匈牙利2021年修订的《儿童保护法》因污名化LGBTI+群体而违反欧盟法。法院首次认定成员国侵犯《欧盟条约》第2条价值观，并指出该法违反内部市场服务自由、《欧盟基本权利宪章》及《通用数据保护条例》（GDPR）。判决要求匈牙利停止限制涉及性别认同或同性恋内容的传播，确立了欧盟价值观与数据权利在成员国立法中的优先地位。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the European Union ruled in Case C-769/22 that Hungary violated EU law by adopting legislation restricting content related to homosexuality and gender reassignment. The Court found infringements of internal market services rules, the Charter of Fundamental Rights, Article 2 TEU, and the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). This marks the first time the Court has established an infringement of Article 2 TEU values in an action against a Member State. The judgment underscores the primacy of EU fundamental rights and data protection standards over national legislative measures affecting content access and advertising sectors within the Union.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海企业需高度关注欧盟“价值观合规”趋势。除传统IP外，广告与内容营销若涉及性别议题，可能触发GDPR及基本权利诉讼风险。建议审查欧洲区内容策略，确保符合反歧视标准，避免因政治正确问题导致服务受限或品牌声誉受损。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment significantly expands the scope of EU value enforcement, linking fundamental rights directly to internal market regulations and data protection compliance. For rights holders and content providers operating in Europe, it signals heightened scrutiny of national laws that may restrict access to information or discriminate based on identity. Companies must ensure their content strategies and data practices align with non-discrimination principles and GDPR requirements to mitigate regulatory risks across member states.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟法院首次认定成员国立法侵犯《欧盟条约》第2条确立的欧盟价值观", "匈牙利法律因限制LGBTI+内容被裁定违反内部市场服务自由及GDPR规定", "判决强调欧盟基本权利宪章对成员国立法具有直接约束力，需保障非歧视原则"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The CJEU found Hungary breached Article 2 TEU for the first time, establishing that national laws stigmatizing LGBTI+ persons violate foundational EU values.", "The contested law restricting content on gender identity and homosexuality was ruled incompatible with internal market service freedoms and the EU Charter of Fundamental Rights.", "The Court determined that Hungary's legislative amendments also constituted violations of the General Data Protection Regulation regarding data processing and privacy rights."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕欧盟以基本权利和GDPR为由的内容监管风险，审查营销素材", "确保在欧洲市场的服务提供符合非歧视原则，避免触犯欧盟价值观底线"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Content providers and advertisers must review compliance with EU non-discrimination standards and GDPR when operating in member states with restrictive content l…", "Practice note: Monitor how Article 2 TEU violations are increasingly cited alongside secondary law breaches in enforcement actions affecting digital services and media.", "What to monitor: Future litigation challenging national measures that restrict access to content or services based on protected characteristics under EU fundamental rights framewo…"]
core_points_en: The CJEU found Hungary breached Article 2 TEU for the first time, establishing that national laws stigmatizing LGBTI+ persons violate foundational EU values.
The contested law restricting content on gender identity and homosexuality was ruled incompatible with internal market service freedoms and the EU Charter of Fundamental Rights.
The Court determined that Hungary's legislative amendments also constituted violations of the General Data Protection Regulation regarding data processing and privacy rights.

items.title: EU General Court rejects opposition to Irish Grass Fed Beef PGI
items.title_zh: 欧盟普通法院驳回对爱尔兰草饲牛肉地理标志的异议
items.title_en: EU General Court Dismisses Opposition to Irish Grass Fed Beef PGI Registration
items.summary: Last week, the EU General Court dismissed an opposition to the Irish Grass Fed Beef PGI in Case T-62/24. A group called the Independent Farmers Of Ireland (IFOI) opposed the Protected Geographical Indication (PGI) on the grounds that they were deprived of procedural rights to raise objection after the PGI application was substantially amended to extend the protected geographical area to
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟普通法院在 T-62/24 号案件中，驳回了爱尔兰独立农民组织（IFOI）对“爱尔兰草饲牛肉”受保护地理标志（PGI）提出的异议。原告主张在申请实质修改扩大保护区域后，其程序性权利被剥夺。法院最终裁定该异议不成立，维持了该地理标志的注册效力，确认了相关审查程序的合规性。
items.ai_summary_en: The EU General Court dismissed an opposition filed by the Independent Farmers Of Ireland (IFOI) against the registration of the 'Irish Grass Fed Beef' Protected Geographical Indication (PGI) in Case T-62/24. The applicant group argued that their procedural rights were violated because they were denied the opportunity to object after the PGI application underwent substantial amendments. These changes significantly extended the geographical area covered by the protection. The Court rejected these claims, ruling that the opposition was unfounded. Consequently, the registration of the geographical indication remains valid. The judgment confirms that the administrative review and examination procedures conducted by the relevant authorities complied with applicable legal standards and procedural requirements.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国涉农出口企业应关注欧盟地理标志壁垒，避免包装宣传侵权。同时可借鉴此案，在欧盟商标或地理标志异议程序中，需严格把握提出异义的法定时机与程序要求。
items.ai_insight_en: Stakeholders in agricultural exports should remain vigilant regarding EU geographical indication barriers to ensure compliance in product labeling and promotional materials. This case highlights the critical importance of adhering strictly to statutory timelines and procedural requirements when participating in EU intellectual property opposition proceedings. Failure to observe these formalities can result in the dismissal of substantive arguments. Market participants must monitor potential conflicts early and engage proactively within the designated legal windows to protect their commercial interests effectively against new GI registrations.

items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EU General Court dismissed the judicial opposition challenging the Irish Grass Fed Beef PGI registration.", "The claimant's argument regarding procedural rights infringement due to scope expansion was not upheld by the Court.", "The judgment confirms the legality of the geographical indication registration and its underlying examination procedures."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕欧盟地理标志壁垒，规范对欧农产品出口宣传", "参与欧盟 IP 程序须严守时限，避免因程序瑕疵败诉"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor EU geographical indication barriers to prevent infringement risks in packaging and promotional activities for agricultural exports.", "Practice note: Strictly adhere to statutory deadlines and procedural requirements during EU trademark or geographical indication opposition proceedings to avoid dismissal based on…", "What to monitor: Track new PGI applications and amendment notices to identify opportunities for timely objections before registration is finalized."]

The claimant's argument regarding procedural rights infringement due to scope expansion was not upheld by the Court.
The judgment confirms the legality of the geographical indication registration and its underlying examination procedures.

items.title: Afrique subsaharienne
items.title_zh: 法国INPI发布撒哈拉以南非洲知识产权服务指南
items.title_en: INPI Guidance on Sub-Saharan Africa IP Services: OAPI Coverage and Côte d’Ivoire Overview
items.summary: Afrique subsaharienne llegrand@inpi.fr 20 avril 2026 Pays couverts par la zone La zone Afrique Subsaharienne couvre les pays suivants : les pays membres de l’ Organisation africaine de la propriété intellectuelle (OAPI) couvrant 17 États membres à savoir : Bénin, Burkina Faso, Cameroun, Congo, Côte d’Ivoire, Gabon, Guinée, Guinée Bissau, Guinée équatoriale, Mali, Mauritanie, Niger, République centrafricaine, Sénégal, Tchad et Togo. La zone couvre également le Nigeria, le Ghana et le siège de l'O
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国国家工业产权局（INPI）更新撒哈拉以南非洲地区知识产权服务指引，明确该区域覆盖17个OAPI成员国及尼日利亚、加纳等。文章重点介绍科特迪瓦知识产权局（OIPI）职能，指出其作为OAPI联络机构负责接收申请并推广IP意识。数据显示2023年该国仅19件专利申请和1066件商标注册，全球创新指数排名靠后，但法律框架符合国际标准，涵盖专利、商标及版权保护。
items.ai_summary_en: The French National Institute of Industrial Property (INPI) has updated its guidance regarding intellectual property services in Sub-Saharan Africa. The defined region encompasses the seventeen member states of the African Intellectual Property Organization (OAPI), alongside Nigeria, Ghana, and the regional office of ARIPO. The document specifically highlights the role of the Ivorian Office of Industrial Property (OIPI), which serves as the national liaison for OAPI in Côte d’Ivoire. While local industrial property statistics remain modest—with only nineteen patent applications and 1,066 trademark filings recorded in 2023—the country’s legal framework aligns with international standards. This includes robust protection for patents, trademarks, designs, and copyright under the Bangui Agreement, offering a structured environment for rights holders despite lower current filing volumes.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应关注INPI提供的西非IP通道，利用OAPI单一注册覆盖多国的优势降低合规成本。鉴于科特迪瓦等地申请量少但增长潜力大，建议提前布局核心品牌与专利，防范抢注风险。同时需留意当地虽法律完善但执行细节差异，借助INPI专业支持优化出海策略。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders targeting West Africa should leverage the OAPI system to secure unified protection across seventeen jurisdictions through a single registration, significantly reducing administrative burdens and compliance costs. For markets like Côte d’Ivoire, where economic growth outpaces current IP filing activity, early defensive registration of core brands and technologies is advisable to mitigate squatting risks. Practitioners must also account for potential discrepancies between statutory frameworks and local enforcement practices. Utilizing specialized support channels, such as those provided by INPI, can help optimize market entry strategies and ensure effective rights management in this evolving regional landscape.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["INPI界定撒哈拉以南非洲IP服务范围，含17个OAPI成员国及尼日利亚等国", "科特迪瓦OIPI作为OAPI联络机构，负责接收申请及提升本土IP意识", "科特迪瓦IP申请量偏低，但立法符合国际公约，保障工业产权与版权"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["INPI defines the Sub-Saharan IP zone to include seventeen OAPI member states, plus Nigeria, Ghana, and ARIPO headquarters.", "Côte d’Ivoire’s OIPI acts as the OAPI liaison, handling application reception and promoting local intellectual property awareness.", "Although filing volumes are low, Côte d’Ivoire maintains international standard legislation protecting industrial property and copyright."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用OAPI机制实现多国统一注册，降低西非市场IP布局成本", "针对科特迪瓦等低申请量高增长市场，需提前进行品牌与专利防御性布局"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage OAPI's unitary registration system to efficiently cover seventeen West African states while minimizing individual filing costs.", "Practice note: Implement early defensive IP strategies in high-growth, low-filing markets like Côte d’Ivoire to prevent third-party squatting.", "What to monitor: Track differences between formal legal protections and actual enforcement practices within specific national jurisdictions."]
core_points_en: INPI defines the Sub-Saharan IP zone to include seventeen OAPI member states, plus Nigeria, Ghana, and ARIPO headquarters.
Côte d’Ivoire’s OIPI acts as the OAPI liaison, handling application reception and promoting local intellectual property awareness.
Although filing volumes are low, Côte d’Ivoire maintains international standard legislation protecting industrial property and copyright.

Practice note: Implement early defensive IP strategies in high-growth, low-filing markets like Côte d’Ivoire to prevent third-party squatting.
What to monitor: Track differences between formal legal protections and actual enforcement practices within specific national jurisdictions.

items.title: Another win for Abbott as Court of Appeal grants PI against Sinocare
items.title_zh: 雅培再胜：UPC 上诉法院批准针对三诺生物的临时禁令
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Upholds Preliminary Injunction Against Sinocare in Abbott Dispute
items.summary: Abbott thus wins outright at the UPC in the second instance. The company filed two PI applications with The Hague local division in June 2024. Abbott alleges Sinocare and its distribution partner Menarini infringe its EP 4 344 633 and EP 3 988 471 and seeks to halt sales of Sinocare’s GlucoMen iCan CGM system […] The post Another win for Abbott as Court of Appeal grants PI against Sinocare appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉法院作出二审裁决，维持海牙地方分院决定，批准雅培针对三诺生物及其分销商梅纳里尼的临时禁令申请。雅培指控被告侵犯其两项欧洲专利，成功阻止三诺生物 GlucoMen iCan 连续血糖监测系统在欧销售。此案标志着雅培在 UPC 二审中取得全面胜利，凸显该法院在快速制止侵权方面的执行力。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of Appeal has issued a second-instance decision affirming the ruling of The Hague Local Division. The court granted preliminary injunctions requested by Abbott against Sinocare and its distribution partner, Menarini. Abbott alleged that the defendants infringed European patents EP 4 344 633 and EP 3 988 471 through the sale of Sinocare’s GlucoMen iCan continuous glucose monitoring system. This judgment effectively halts the commercialization of the accused medical devices across participating member states. The outcome represents a comprehensive victory for Abbott at the appellate level, demonstrating the UPC’s capacity to enforce rapid, cross-border provisional measures to stop alleged patent infringement within the European market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国医疗器械企业需警惕 UPC 跨辖区禁令的高效杀伤力。建议在产品出海前进行严格的 FTO 分析，并针对核心市场提前布局无效宣告或抗辩策略，避免因单一诉讼导致整个欧洲市场停摆。
items.ai_insight_en: This case underscores the significant impact of UPC preliminary injunctions, which possess pan-European effect and can rapidly restrict market access across multiple jurisdictions. For companies operating in the medical technology sector, the speed and breadth of these provisional measures necessitate robust pre-launch freedom-to-operate analyses. Defenders must prepare emergency response mechanisms, including financial provisions for security deposits and rapid legal counter-strategies. Failure to anticipate such swift judicial actions may result in sudden supply chain disruptions and substantial commercial losses across the unified patent landscape.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 上诉法院二审维持原判，批准雅培针对三诺生物的临时禁令", "涉案专利为 EP4344633 与 EP3988471，直指连续血糖监测系统侵权", "裁决立即生效，强制停止三诺生物相关产品在欧洲市场的销售行为"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Court of Appeal upheld the preliminary injunction granted by The Hague Local Division against Sinocare and Menarini.", "Abbott successfully enforced patents EP 4 344 633 and EP 3 988 471 to block sales of the GlucoMen iCan CGM system.", "The immediate enforcement order mandates the cessation of Sinocare's product distribution throughout relevant European jurisdictions."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC 禁令具有泛欧效力，一旦获批将瞬间封锁多国市场准入", "应对二审突袭需储备快速反应机制，包括担保金准备与紧急抗辩"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Pan-European preliminary injunctions can instantly block market entry, requiring rigorous pre-launch FTO assessments.", "Practice note: Defendants should establish emergency response protocols, including liquidity for security deposits and rapid appeal strategies.", "What to monitor: The UPC's evolving standards for granting provisional relief and the practical enforcement timelines across local divisions."]
core_points_en: The UPC Court of Appeal upheld the preliminary injunction granted by The Hague Local Division against Sinocare and Menarini.
Abbott successfully enforced patents EP 4 344 633 and EP 3 988 471 to block sales of the GlucoMen iCan CGM system.
The immediate enforcement order mandates the cessation of Sinocare's product distribution throughout relevant European jurisdictions.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Pan-European preliminary injunctions can instantly block market entry, requiring rigorous pre-launch FTO assessments.
Practice note: Defendants should establish emergency response protocols, including liquidity for security deposits and rapid appeal strategies.
What to monitor: The UPC's evolving standards for granting provisional relief and the practical enforcement timelines across local divisions.

items.title: European Commission rows back on LNG “soft safe harbour” plans
items.title_zh: 欧委会撤回 LNG 软安全港计划并更新技术转让指南
items.title_en: European Commission Withdraws LNG Soft Safe Harbour Plans in Updated Technology Transfer Guidelines
items.summary: New technology transfer guidelines instead include a broad range of antitrust considerations that it considers relevant to the assessment of licensing negotiation groups
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟委员会在新技术转让指南中调整策略，撤回此前针对许可谈判组（LNG）的“软安全港”计划。新指南转而纳入广泛的反垄断考量因素，用于评估许可谈判组的合规性。此举意味着欧盟对标准必要专利集体许可行为的监管态度趋于审慎，不再提供简化的合规豁免路径，而是强调个案反垄断分析的重要性。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Commission has formally withdrawn its previously proposed 'soft safe harbour' plans for licensing negotiation groups (LNGs) within the new Technology Transfer Guidelines. Instead of offering a simplified compliance exemption pathway, the updated guidelines incorporate a broad range of antitrust considerations relevant to assessing the conduct of such groups. This strategic adjustment signals a more prudent regulatory stance by the EU regarding collective licensing behaviors involving standard-essential patents. The Commission emphasizes the necessity of rigorous, case-by-case antitrust analysis rather than providing blanket immunities. Consequently, entities engaging in joint licensing negotiations must navigate a complex landscape where general presumptions of legality are replaced by detailed competitive assessments tailored to specific market conditions and negotiation structures.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在参与或组建欧洲专利许可谈判组时，需警惕合规风险上升。建议重新评估现有联合理许可架构，避免触发反垄断调查，并准备应对更严格的个案竞争法分析，确保许可谈判符合欧盟最新监管导向。
items.ai_insight_en: Stakeholders participating in or establishing European patent licensing negotiation groups should anticipate heightened compliance risks under the revised framework. It is advisable to reassess existing joint licensing architectures to ensure they do not trigger antitrust investigations. Organizations must prepare for stricter, individualized competition law analyses rather than relying on generalized safe harbour protections. Ensuring that licensing negotiations align with the latest regulatory direction requires robust internal compliance mechanisms and readiness to defend specific practices against potential competition authority challenges.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧委会正式撤回针对许可谈判组的软安全港保护计划", "新指南引入广泛反垄断考量以评估许可谈判组行为", "欧盟强化对集体许可模式的竞争法审查而非简化豁免"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The European Commission officially withdrew the soft safe harbour protection plan previously designed for licensing negotiation groups.", "New guidelines introduce extensive antitrust factors to evaluate the compliance and competitive impact of licensing negotiation group activities.", "The EU strengthens competition law scrutiny over collective licensing models instead of providing simplified regulatory exemptions or safe harbors."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["联合理许可模式面临更严反垄断审查，需自查合规性", "企业应放弃依赖安全港心态，做好个案抗辩准备"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Joint licensing models face intensified antitrust scrutiny; entities must proactively audit their collaborative structures for compliance gaps.", "Practice note: Abandon reliance on presumed safe harbour status; develop comprehensive defenses for case-specific competition law assessments.", "What to monitor: Evolving EC enforcement priorities regarding collective SEP licensing and the practical application of new antitrust criteria."]
core_points_en: The European Commission officially withdrew the soft safe harbour protection plan previously designed for licensing negotiation groups.
New guidelines introduce extensive antitrust factors to evaluate the compliance and competitive impact of licensing negotiation group activities.
The EU strengthens competition law scrutiny over collective licensing models instead of providing simplified regulatory exemptions or safe harbors.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Joint licensing models face intensified antitrust scrutiny; entities must proactively audit their collaborative structures for compliance gaps.
Practice note: Abandon reliance on presumed safe harbour status; develop comprehensive defenses for case-specific competition law assessments.
What to monitor: Evolving EC enforcement priorities regarding collective SEP licensing and the practical application of new antitrust criteria.

items.title: SEP standardisation and AI exploration: Via president shares vision
items.title_zh: Via 新任总裁展望 SEP 标准化与 AI 探索
items.title_en: Via President Kevin Mack Highlights Collaborative SEP Licensing and AI-Driven Business Innovation
items.summary: Kevin Mack, Via’s new president, emphasises the importance of collaborative licensing structures and shares how AI tools can help create new lines of business
items.ai_summary_zh: Via 新任总裁凯文·麦克强调协作式许可架构在标准必要专利领域的重要性。他指出，通过建立合作机制可优化许可效率。同时，他分享了利用人工智能工具开发新业务线的愿景，旨在通过技术手段提升专利许可的精准度与商业价值，推动行业创新。
items.ai_summary_en: Kevin Mack, the newly appointed president of Via Licensing, underscores the critical role of collaborative licensing frameworks within the standard essential patent ecosystem. He argues that establishing robust cooperative mechanisms is essential for optimizing licensing efficiency and reducing transactional friction among stakeholders. Furthermore, Mack outlines a strategic vision for leveraging artificial intelligence tools to develop new business lines. By integrating advanced AI technologies into patent management processes, Via aims to enhance the precision of licensing assessments and unlock greater commercial value from intellectual property assets. This approach seeks to drive industry-wide innovation by modernizing how patent portfolios are evaluated and licensed, ensuring that both licensors and licensees benefit from more accurate, data-driven decision-making in an increasingly complex technological landscape.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局 SEP 时，应关注 Via 等平台的协作许可趋势。建议积极评估 AI 在专利盘点与许可谈判中的应用，主动适应新型许可模式，以降低合规成本并挖掘数据资产价值。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely observe the shift toward collaborative licensing models promoted by major aggregators like Via Licensing. Adopting these frameworks may help reduce the costs and risks associated with bilateral negotiations. Additionally, evaluating the practical application of AI in patent portfolio analysis and licensing strategy is becoming increasingly relevant. Companies should monitor how AI-driven insights impact valuation metrics and negotiation dynamics. Proactively adapting to these technological and structural changes can improve compliance efficiency and help organizations better leverage their data assets within the evolving standard essential patent landscape.

items.title: UPC Ruling Defines The Limits Of Unitary Patent Protection
items.title_zh: UPC 裁决界定单一专利保护的适用极限
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Clarifies Strict Territorial Scope for Unitary Patent Effect
items.summary: The Unified Patent Court system offers streamlined patent protection across multiple European jurisdictions, but understanding its mechanisms and strategic implications remains crucial for rights holders.
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC 上诉院确认，欧洲专利若要在所有参与成员国获得单一效力，必须在各国拥有完全相同的权利要求集。因马耳他于申请日后才加入欧洲专利公约，导致涉案专利无法覆盖该国，故被驳回单一效力请求。法院坚持严格解释法规，拒绝因地域缺失非权利人可控而给予灵活性。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of Appeal has confirmed that a European patent qualifies for unitary effect only if it covers all participating member states with an identical set of claims. In case UPC_CoA_8/2026, the Court upheld the European Patent Office's refusal to grant unitary effect where the patent did not cover Malta. Because Malta was not a party to the European Patent Convention at the application's filing date in 2005, it could not be designated. The Court rejected arguments for flexibility regarding circumstances beyond the proprietor's control, establishing that strict territorial coverage is a mandatory condition for unitary protection.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧申请专利时，需严格核查目标国在优先权日或申请日的公约成员状态。对于涉及新加入成员国的分案申请，应评估单一专利路径的可行性，避免因部分国家缺失导致整体单一效力失效，必要时考虑传统生效模式。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling establishes a rigid boundary for unitary patent eligibility, impacting portfolio strategies for rights holders with older priority dates or complex divisional histories. It underscores that the unitary system does not accommodate historical gaps in EPC membership, requiring precise alignment between the patent's territorial scope and the current list of participating states at the time of request.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["单一效力要求专利在所有参与国具有完全相同的权利要求集", "申请日前未加入公约的成员国（如马耳他）导致无法获得单一保护", "UPC 上诉院拒绝因客观情况排除领土而放宽单一效力的法定条件"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Unitary effect requires a European patent to cover all participating member states with an identical set of claims in each jurisdiction.", "Patents filed before a country's accession to the EPC cannot include that state, potentially disqualifying them from unitary protection.", "The UPC Court of Appeal refused to allow flexibility for missing territories caused by historical accession dates or procedural constraints."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["核查申请日各国公约状态，避免因地域缺失丧失单一效力", "针对特定国家缺口，提前规划传统验证与单一专利的组合策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders must verify whether all participating UPC states were EPC members on the relevant filing date to ensure unitary eligibility.", "Practice note: For divisional applications or patents with early priority dates, assess if historical non-membership of specific states precludes unitary effect.", "What to monitor: Future interpretations of 'identical claims' and potential legislative adjustments regarding transitional cases involving new EPC members."]
core_points_en: Unitary effect requires a European patent to cover all participating member states with an identical set of claims in each jurisdiction.
Patents filed before a country's accession to the EPC cannot include that state, potentially disqualifying them from unitary protection.
The UPC Court of Appeal refused to allow flexibility for missing territories caused by historical accession dates or procedural constraints.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders must verify whether all participating UPC states were EPC members on the relevant filing date to ensure unitary eligibility.
Practice note: For divisional applications or patents with early priority dates, assess if historical non-membership of specific states precludes unitary effect.
What to monitor: Future interpretations of 'identical claims' and potential legislative adjustments regarding transitional cases involving new EPC members.

items.title: LD Lisbon, April 10, 2026, Order, UPC_CFI_858/2025
items.title_zh: UPC 里斯本裁定：专利过期后撤诉的目的缺失评估与费用分担
items.title_en: UPC Lisbon: Dismissal After Expiry Implies No Infringement and Costs Shared
items.summary: The court assesses whether an action is devoid of purpose based on the claimant’s legitimate legal interest (R. 360 RoP). The defendant’s interest is not autonomously considered in this assessment.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）里斯本地方分部在勃林格殷格翰诉赞蒂瓦案中作出命令。法院依据程序规则第 360 条，裁定评估诉讼是否“目的缺失”仅基于原告的合法法律利益，被告利益不单独考量。若原告在专利过期后请求处置案件且未主张侵权声明，视为承认专利有效期内无侵权。因无法确定胜诉方，双方各自承担诉讼费用。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Local Division in Lisbon issued an order in Boehringer Ingelheim v. Zentiva Portugal regarding the disposal of a main action under Rule 360 of the Rules of Procedure. The Court clarified that assessing whether an action is devoid of purpose relies solely on the claimant’s legitimate legal interest, excluding autonomous consideration of the defendant’s interests. Crucially, requesting disposal after patent expiry without seeking a declaration of infringement implies an admission that no infringement occurred during the patent’s term. Regarding costs, the Court found it impossible to identify a successful party under Article 69 UPCA due to specific case circumstances. Consequently, applying the exception in Article 69(1) and (2), both parties were ordered to bear their own costs. This decision reinforces procedural boundaries between main actions and provisional measures.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应对专利诉讼时，需注意专利过期后的撤诉策略可能被视为对过往行为的自认。若试图通过拖延至专利过期来规避判决，可能面临无法转嫁诉讼成本的风险，应审慎评估程序时机。
items.ai_insight_en: Practitioners should recognize that withdrawing or seeking disposal of patent litigation after expiration carries significant evidentiary weight. Such procedural moves may be interpreted as an implicit admission that no infringement occurred while the patent was enforceable. Furthermore, strategic delays aiming to avoid a substantive judgment often complicate cost allocation. When courts cannot designate a prevailing party due to such procedural outcomes, each side typically bears its own legal expenses. Parties must carefully evaluate the timing of settlement or dismissal requests to manage both liability admissions and financial exposure effectively.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["诉讼目的缺失评估仅考量原告利益，被告利益非独立考量因素", "专利过期后撤诉且未求侵权声明，隐含承认期内未发生侵权", "无法判定胜诉方时，适用例外规则由双方当事人各自承担费用"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Rule 360 RoP assessments focus exclusively on the claimant's legitimate legal interest, not the defendant's position.", "Disposing of an action post-expiry without infringement claims implies admission of non-infringement during validity.", "Costs are shared when determining a successful party under Article 69 UPCA is impossible due to case specifics."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["专利过期后撤诉可能被推定为承认既往未侵权，需警惕法律后果", "程序策略不当可能导致无法认定胜诉方，从而被迫自行承担高额费用"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Post-expiry dismissal strategies may constitute admissions of non-infringement, impacting future enforcement positions.", "Practice note: Avoid delaying tactics that prevent successful party determination, as this triggers equal cost bearing under Article 69 UPCA.", "What to monitor: How UPC divisions interpret 'legitimate legal interest' under Rule 360 when balancing claimant versus defendant interests."]
core_points_en: Rule 360 RoP assessments focus exclusively on the claimant's legitimate legal interest, not the defendant's position.
Disposing of an action post-expiry without infringement claims implies admission of non-infringement during validity.
Costs are shared when determining a successful party under Article 69 UPCA is impossible due to case specifics.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Post-expiry dismissal strategies may constitute admissions of non-infringement, impacting future enforcement positions.
Practice note: Avoid delaying tactics that prevent successful party determination, as this triggers equal cost bearing under Article 69 UPCA.
What to monitor: How UPC divisions interpret 'legitimate legal interest' under Rule 360 when balancing claimant versus defendant interests.

items.title: LD The Hague, April 14, 2026, Procedural Order, UPC_CFI_1612/2025
items.title_zh: UPC 海牙分部程序令：确认保密性质与证据提交规则
items.title_en: UPC The Hague: Confidentiality Scope, Public Access, and Procedural Deadlines Clarified
items.summary: The Court acknowledges the confidential nature of specific information after the claimant did not object to the defendant’s corresponding assertion.
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC 海牙分部在 Umicore 诉 Elemental Benelux 案中作出程序令。法院确认若双方无异议可认定信息保密性；既有宽泛 NDA 阻止单方缩小诉讼保密圈；驳回自动屏蔽公众访问请求，要求个案平衡；明确程序期限自收到未删减答辩状起算，并允许对分类信息预先授予保密待遇。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Local Division in The Hague issued a procedural order in Umicore v. Elemental Benelux addressing key procedural and confidentiality issues. The Court confirmed that information is classified as confidential if parties agree, noting the claimant’s lack of objection to the defendant’s assertion. Crucially, the Court ruled that a pre-existing broad non-disclosure agreement prevents a party from unilaterally restricting access to a narrower circle in UPC proceedings. The defendants’ request for automatic exclusion of public access under Rule 262.3 was dismissed, requiring individual assessment balancing transparency and confidentiality. Additionally, procedural deadlines under Rule 29(a) commence only upon receipt of the full, unredacted Statement of Defence. The Court also granted ex ante confidential treatment for future submissions, mandating dual filing of redacted and unredacted versions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在 UPC 诉讼中应审慎签署 NDA，避免限制后续举证权利。需关注程序期限起算点，确保及时回应。面对保密申请，应主张个案审查而非自动屏蔽，以维护程序公平。
items.ai_insight_en: Parties engaging in UPC litigation must carefully align pre-litigation NDAs with anticipated procedural needs. A broad existing NDA can secure wider access to evidence during proceedings, preventing opponents from imposing restrictive confidentiality rings. Litigants should verify the completeness of served documents to accurately calculate response deadlines, ensuring procedural rights are preserved. When handling sensitive data, requesting ex ante confidentiality orders streamlines subsequent filings by eliminating repetitive motions. Furthermore, stakeholders should advocate for case-by-case assessments of public access rather than accepting automatic exclusions, thereby maintaining procedural fairness and transparency while protecting legitimate trade secrets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["既有宽泛 NDA 阻止单方在诉讼中缩小保密接触范围", "程序期限自收到完整未删减答辩状及附件后开始计算", "禁止自动屏蔽公众访问，须个案平衡透明度与保密需求"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["A pre-existing broad NDA prevents a party from unilaterally narrowing the confidentiality circle in UPC litigation.", "Procedural deadlines under Rule 29(a) begin only upon receipt of the complete, unredacted Statement of Defence and exhibits.", "Public access requests require individual balancing; automatic blanket exclusion of confidential information is prohibited."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["签署 NDA 时需考量未来诉讼保密圈扩展可能性", "利用规则争取完整证据获取以保障程序期限利益"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Align pre-litigation NDAs with litigation strategy to prevent unilateral restriction of evidence access circles.", "Practice note: Verify receipt of complete, unredacted defence documents to accurately trigger procedural response deadlines.", "What to monitor: Individual judicial balancing of public access requests versus confidentiality claims under Rule 262.3."]
core_points_en: A pre-existing broad NDA prevents a party from unilaterally narrowing the confidentiality circle in UPC litigation.
Procedural deadlines under Rule 29(a) begin only upon receipt of the complete, unredacted Statement of Defence and exhibits.
Public access requests require individual balancing; automatic blanket exclusion of confidential information is prohibited.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Align pre-litigation NDAs with litigation strategy to prevent unilateral restriction of evidence access circles.
Practice note: Verify receipt of complete, unredacted defence documents to accurately trigger procedural response deadlines.
What to monitor: Individual judicial balancing of public access requests versus confidentiality claims under Rule 262.3.

items.title: LD Düsseldorf, April 16, 2026, Decision, UPC_CFI_779/2024
items.title_zh: UPC 杜塞尔多夫分院关于双组件产品间接侵权的判决要点
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf: Indirect Infringement and Remanufacturing in Two-Component Patent Claims
items.summary: Where a patent claim protects a two-component product, the objective elements of indirect infringement are satisfied if the accused component is designed to cooperate with a second component...
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC 杜塞尔多夫分院在 Brita 诉 Wessper 案中裁定，认定双组件专利间接侵权时，无需被控组件与第二组件同时存在，仅需前者设计用于配合后者即可。法院强调权利要求所有特征均至关重要，并指出若替换件体现关键技术效果，其更换构成非法“重造”而非合法维修。此外，UPCA 适用于持续侵权行为的全过程。
items.ai_summary_en: In Brita SE v. Wessper, the UPC Court of First Instance in Düsseldorf clarified indirect infringement standards for two-component products under Article 26 UPCA. The court ruled that the objective elements are satisfied if the accused component is designed to cooperate with a second component configured per the invention, regardless of whether the second component currently exists. Existence of the second component relates only to subjective requirements. All claim features are essential, irrespective of their placement in preamble or characterizing portion. Regarding exhaustion under Article 29 UPCA, replacing a wear part embodying the key technical effect constitutes impermissible remaking, not permissible repair. This assessment depends on the specific asserted claim combination. Furthermore, the UPCA applies to ongoing infringement acts committed before its entry into force, though parties may invoke more favorable national law for pre-UPCA conduct.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出口企业需警惕组件单独出口引发的间接侵权风险，避免产品设计刻意配合欧洲专利。对于耗材与替换件业务，须重新评估是否触及“重造”红线，防止因小部件替换导致整体产品侵权指控。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision significantly impacts supply chains involving multi-component systems and consumables. Manufacturers exporting individual components to Europe must assess whether their design facilitates cooperation with patented combinations, as this alone may satisfy indirect infringement criteria. For businesses dealing in replacement parts or refills, distinguishing between permissible repair and illegal remaking is critical. If a replaced part embodies the core technical effect, it risks being classified as remaking. Companies should review claim constructions carefully, noting that functional definitions in claims control scope over descriptive examples. Legal teams must also evaluate historical infringement periods under both UPCA and relevant national laws to determine applicable liability standards.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["间接侵权客观要件不需双组件同时存在，仅需设计配合即可", "替换件若体现关键技术效果，更换行为构成非法重造而非维修", "UPCA 适用于持续侵权全程，包括生效前行为但可援引更有利国法"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Indirect infringement requires only that the accused component is designed to cooperate with the patented second component; actual existence of the second component is irrelevant…", "Replacing a wear part that embodies the key technical effect of the asserted claim constitutes impermissible remaking rather than permissible repair under Article 29 UPCA.", "The UPCA applies to the entire duration of ongoing infringement, including acts before June 1, 2023, though defendants may argue for more favorable prior national law."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["组件出口需排查间接侵权风险，避免设计专门配合他人专利", "替换件业务应审慎评估技术效果，防范被认定为非法重造"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Suppliers of single components for multi-part systems face heightened indirect infringement risk if designs target patented combinations.", "Practice note: Evaluate replacement parts against the key technical effect of asserted claims to avoid classification as impermissible remaking.", "What to monitor: Jurisdictional shifts from withdrawn opt-outs do not alter substantive law application for pre-UPCA infringement acts."]
core_points_en: Indirect infringement requires only that the accused component is designed to cooperate with the patented second component; actual existence of the second component is irrelevant…
Replacing a wear part that embodies the key technical effect of the asserted claim constitutes impermissible remaking rather than permissible repair under Article 29 UPCA.
The UPCA applies to the entire duration of ongoing infringement, including acts before June 1, 2023, though defendants may argue for more favorable prior national law.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Suppliers of single components for multi-part systems face heightened indirect infringement risk if designs target patented combinations.
Practice note: Evaluate replacement parts against the key technical effect of asserted claims to avoid classification as impermissible remaking.
What to monitor: Jurisdictional shifts from withdrawn opt-outs do not alter substantive law application for pre-UPCA infringement acts.

items.title: LD Mannheim, April 16, 2026, Infringement Action And Counterclaim For Revocation, UPC_CFI_819/2024 And UPC_CFI_414/2025
items.title_zh: UPC 曼海姆法院就无碱定义及控股公司责任作出判决
items.title_en: UPC Mannheim: Alkali-Free Threshold, Past Infringement Presumption, and Holding Company Liability
items.summary: The term “alkali-free” was interpreted not as a complete absence but as a concentration below a specific threshold defined in the patent itself.
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC 曼海姆地方法院在侵权与无效反诉案中裁定：专利术语“无碱”指低于特定阈值而非绝对零含量；非故意残留不视为使用禁用元素。法院认定过去侵权事实成立并颁发禁令，但驳回对控股公司的连带责任主张，且损害赔偿适用授权后宽限期。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Local Division in Mannheim issued a judgment in combined infringement and revocation proceedings. The Court construed the term “alkali-free” as denoting a concentration below a specific patent-defined threshold rather than absolute absence, ruling that unintentional residual contamination does not constitute prohibited use of arsenic or antimony. The Court found past infringement established under Rule 171.2 RoP because the defendant failed to specifically contest substantiated allegations, merely stating current non-infringement. An injunction was granted based on the risk of repetition. However, liability against the parent holding company was dismissed, as mere shareholder status is insufficient without proof of operational control or accomplice liability. The Court also rejected a Chinese invalidation decision, noting material differences in inventive step standards compared to the EPC.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应重视 UPC 诉讼中事实抗辩的具体化，避免因沉默被推定侵权。同时需注意控股架构隔离风险，以及中国无效判决在欧效力有限，须依欧洲标准单独应对。
items.ai_insight_en: Litigants before the UPC must ensure factual defenses are explicitly detailed, particularly regarding past infringement allegations, to avoid adverse presumptions under procedural rules. Reliance on foreign judgments, such as Chinese invalidity decisions, is unlikely to succeed due to divergent legal standards for inventiveness under the EPC. Parties should prepare independent validity arguments aligned with European jurisprudence. Furthermore, corporate structures require careful management; parent entities cannot rely solely on separate legal personality to shield against infringement claims if they exercise decisive control over subsidiary operations involved in the disputed activities.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["专利术语依内部定义解释，非故意残留不构成侵权使用", "被告未具体抗辩过往侵权事实，法院直接推定其成立", "仅凭股东身份无法追究控股公司侵权责任，需证明实际控制"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Patent terms are interpreted via internal definitions; unintentional residual contamination does not constitute infringing use of banned elements.", "Past infringement facts are presumed true if the defendant fails to specifically contest substantiated allegations under Rule 171.2 RoP.", "Holding companies avoid infringement liability absent proof of decisive operational control or accomplice conduct beyond mere shareholding."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC 诉讼中必须具体否认过往侵权指控，否则面临事实推定风险", "中国专利无效判决在欧无直接效力，需依据 EPC 标准独立抗辩"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure specific, detailed rebuttals to past infringement allegations to prevent procedural presumptions of fact.", "Practice note: Do not rely on foreign invalidity judgments; mount independent inventive step defenses per EPC standards.", "What to monitor: Judicial interpretation of 'operational control' for holding company liability in multi-entity infringement cases."]
core_points_en: Patent terms are interpreted via internal definitions; unintentional residual contamination does not constitute infringing use of banned elements.
Past infringement facts are presumed true if the defendant fails to specifically contest substantiated allegations under Rule 171.2 RoP.
Holding companies avoid infringement liability absent proof of decisive operational control or accomplice conduct beyond mere shareholding.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure specific, detailed rebuttals to past infringement allegations to prevent procedural presumptions of fact.
Practice note: Do not rely on foreign invalidity judgments; mount independent inventive step defenses per EPC standards.
What to monitor: Judicial interpretation of 'operational control' for holding company liability in multi-entity infringement cases.
