items.title: Software and utility models in Spain: criteria shift requires protection strategies rethink
items.title_zh: 西班牙软件与实用新型：标准变更需重思保护策略
items.title_en: Spain Clarifies Software Exclusion from Utility Models, Prompting Strategy Shift
items.summary: New guidelines reinforce the interpretation that CIIs are explicitly excluded from utility model protection under Spanish Law 24/2015
items.ai_summary_zh: 西班牙发布新指南，强化了对 2015 年第 24 号法律的解释，明确指出计算机实施发明（CIIs）被排除在实用新型保护范围之外。这一政策转变要求申请人重新评估其在西班牙的软件相关技术保护策略，避免依赖实用新型途径，转而寻求发明专利或其他形式的知识产权保护，以应对日益严格的审查标准。
items.ai_summary_en: New guidelines issued by Spanish authorities reinforce the interpretation that computer-implemented inventions are explicitly excluded from utility model protection under Law 24/2015. This policy shift clarifies that software-related technical solutions cannot be protected via the utility model route, which traditionally offered faster and cheaper registration than patents. Consequently, applicants must reassess their intellectual property strategies in Spain. Relying on utility models for software innovations now carries a high risk of rejection due to subject matter ineligibility. To ensure effective protection, rights holders should pivot towards filing standard patent applications or exploring other suitable IP forms. This adjustment is critical to navigate increasingly strict examination standards and secure robust legal rights for software technologies within the Spanish jurisdiction.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在西班牙布局软件技术时，应摒弃实用新型申请路径，避免因客体不适格被驳回。建议提前进行专利性评估，聚焦具备技术贡献的发明点，通过发明专利程序获取更稳固的权利基础，降低维权风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders seeking protection for software technologies in Spain should abandon utility model filings to avoid immediate rejection based on subject matter eligibility. Instead, focus resources on preparing strong patent applications that clearly articulate the technical contribution of the invention beyond mere code execution. Emphasizing technical effects and solving specific technical problems will enhance the likelihood of grant under stricter examination criteria. This approach mitigates enforcement risks associated with invalid titles. Monitoring future administrative practices regarding the definition of technical character in software cases remains essential for maintaining a resilient portfolio and adapting to evolving judicial interpretations in the European context.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["新指南明确计算机实施发明无法获得西班牙实用新型保护", "依据 2015 年第 24 号法律，软件类技术方案被正式排除", "企业需调整策略，转向发明专利以确保技术成果获有效保护"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["New guidelines confirm that computer-implemented inventions are ineligible for utility model protection in Spain.", "Law 24/2015 formally excludes software-related technical solutions from the scope of utility model rights.", "Companies must adjust strategies by pursuing patent applications to ensure valid protection for software innovations."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["放弃西班牙实用新型路径，转投发明专利以规避客体风险", "强化技术方案的技术贡献论证，提升发明专利授权概率"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid utility model filings for software in Spain to prevent rejection; prioritize patent applications for stronger enforceability.", "Practice note: Strengthen patent specifications by highlighting technical contributions and effects to satisfy eligibility requirements.", "What to monitor: Administrative guidance on technical character definitions for computer-implemented inventions in Spain."]
core_points_en: New guidelines confirm that computer-implemented inventions are ineligible for utility model protection in Spain.
Law 24/2015 formally excludes software-related technical solutions from the scope of utility model rights.
Companies must adjust strategies by pursuing patent applications to ensure valid protection for software innovations.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid utility model filings for software in Spain to prevent rejection; prioritize patent applications for stronger enforceability.
Practice note: Strengthen patent specifications by highlighting technical contributions and effects to satisfy eligibility requirements.
What to monitor: Administrative guidance on technical character definitions for computer-implemented inventions in Spain.

items.title: Sharp, three others beef up Access Advance’s video distribution pool
items.title_zh: 夏普等三家企业加入 Access Advance 视频分发专利池
items.title_en: Sharp and Three Others Join Access Advance Video Pool Amid FRAND White Paper Release
items.summary: Access Advance also shares white paper claiming that its pool for streamers offers FRAND rates, in likely effort to boost licensee interest in the year-old programme
items.ai_summary_zh: Access Advance 宣布夏普等三家新成员加入其视频分发专利池，旨在增强该成立一年的项目吸引力。同时，该组织发布白皮书，主张其针对流媒体服务的专利池提供符合公平、合理和非歧视（FRAND）原则的费率，试图以此提升潜在被许可人的参与意愿，缓解市场对该专利池定价合规性的疑虑。
items.ai_summary_en: Access Advance has announced the addition of Sharp and three other companies to its video distribution patent pool, a strategic move designed to enhance the appeal of the program established one year ago. Concurrently, the organization released a white paper asserting that its licensing rates for streaming services comply with Fair, Reasonable, and Non-Discriminatory (FRAND) principles. This publication aims to address market concerns regarding pricing compliance and stimulate interest among potential licensees. By reinforcing its commitment to fair licensing terms, Access Advance seeks to mitigate skepticism and encourage broader participation from rights holders and implementers alike in the evolving landscape of video technology standards.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国流媒体及终端企业应关注该专利池动态，评估其 FRAND 主张实质。建议提前研判入池必要性，利用白皮书内容作为谈判参考，防范潜在的许可费堆叠风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor the expansion of the Access Advance pool and evaluate the substantive validity of its FRAND claims. The newly released white paper may serve as a critical reference point during licensing negotiations, offering arguments against excessive royalty demands. Companies should assess the necessity of joining the pool versus seeking individual licenses, while remaining vigilant about potential royalty stacking risks. Understanding the practical impact of this consolidation on overall licensing costs is essential for effective intellectual property strategy and budget planning in the video distribution sector.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["夏普等三家企业正式加入 Access Advance 视频专利池", "官方发布白皮书重申其流媒体费率符合 FRAND 原则", "此举意在通过强化合规叙事以提升被许可人兴趣"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Sharp and three additional entities have officially joined the Access Advance video distribution patent pool.", "The organization published a white paper reaffirming that its streaming service rates adhere to FRAND principles.", "This initiative aims to strengthen compliance narratives and increase licensee interest in the program."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注专利池扩容对许可费率的实际影响", "利用 FRAND 白皮书作为许可谈判的抗辩依据"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess the actual impact of pool expansion on aggregate licensing fees and operational costs.", "Practice note: Utilize the FRAND white paper as a defensive basis in licensing negotiations to challenge unreasonable rate proposals.", "What to monitor: Track further membership changes and any regulatory scrutiny regarding the pool's compliance with competition laws."]
core_points_en: Sharp and three additional entities have officially joined the Access Advance video distribution patent pool.
The organization published a white paper reaffirming that its streaming service rates adhere to FRAND principles.
This initiative aims to strengthen compliance narratives and increase licensee interest in the program.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess the actual impact of pool expansion on aggregate licensing fees and operational costs.
Practice note: Utilize the FRAND white paper as a defensive basis in licensing negotiations to challenge unreasonable rate proposals.

items.title: Nike scores $11m ‘dupe’ win against social media influencer
items.title_zh: 耐克诉网红售假案胜诉获赔 1100 万美元
items.title_en: Nike Secures $11 Million Judgment Against Social Media Influencer for Promoting Counterfeit Goods
items.summary: Decision is turning point in curbing online fakes culture, underlining that individuals who promote and monetise replica products can be liable even if they are not directly manufacturing the goods.
items.ai_summary_zh: 耐克在一起针对社交媒体网红的诉讼中获得 1100 万美元赔偿。该判决被视为遏制在线假冒文化的转折点，明确即使个人未直接制造商品，只要推广并从中获利，亦需对销售复制品承担法律责任。此裁决强化了品牌方在数字时代打击侵权的法律效力。
items.ai_summary_en: Nike has successfully obtained an $11 million judgment in a significant legal action against a social media influencer. This ruling represents a critical development in the enforcement of intellectual property rights within the digital landscape, specifically targeting the culture of online counterfeiting. The court established that individuals who actively promote and monetize replica products bear legal liability for trademark infringement, even if they are not directly involved in the manufacturing or physical distribution of the goods. This decision underscores the expanding scope of responsibility for digital content creators and marketers. It signals to brand owners that legal avenues are increasingly effective in holding third-party promoters accountable. For market participants, it highlights the severe financial consequences associated with facilitating the sale of counterfeit items through social media channels, regardless of direct involvement in production.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海企业及营销机构需警惕，在欧推广时若涉及仿品或“平替”宣传，即便不直接生产也可能面临高额索赔。建议严格审查合作网红内容，建立合规风控机制，避免卷入商标侵权纠纷。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment significantly expands the potential liability exposure for digital marketing ecosystems. Brands and agencies must recognize that promotional activities involving 'dupes' or replicas can trigger severe financial penalties, even without direct manufacturing involvement. Legal teams should prioritize rigorous vetting of influencer partnerships and marketing content to ensure compliance with trademark laws. Establishing robust contractual indemnities and monitoring mechanisms is essential to mitigate the risk of joint liability. Furthermore, companies operating across jurisdictions should remain vigilant regarding evolving judicial interpretations of secondary liability in digital commerce, as courts increasingly focus on the commercial impact of promotional conduct rather than just physical supply chain roles.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["网红推广并变现仿品行为被认定需承担法律责任", "法院判决耐克获赔 1100 万美元以震慑侵权行为", "确立非制造商亦可因促进假货销售而受罚的先例"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Social media influencers promoting and monetizing replica products are held legally liable for trademark infringement.", "The court awarded Nike $11 million in damages, establishing a strong deterrent against the promotion of counterfeit goods online.", "This precedent confirms that non-manufacturers can face substantial penalties for facilitating the sale of fake products."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["非制造商推广仿品在欧洲同样面临高额赔偿风险", "企业应加强海外营销合规审查以规避连带侵权责任"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Non-manufacturing entities promoting replicas face high compensation risks similar to manufacturers in European jurisdictions.", "Practice note: Strengthen overseas marketing compliance reviews and influencer contracts to prevent joint infringement liability.", "What to monitor: Judicial trends regarding secondary liability for digital promoters and the definition of commercial exploitation of fakes."]
core_points_en: Social media influencers promoting and monetizing replica products are held legally liable for trademark infringement.
The court awarded Nike $11 million in damages, establishing a strong deterrent against the promotion of counterfeit goods online.
This precedent confirms that non-manufacturers can face substantial penalties for facilitating the sale of fake products.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Non-manufacturing entities promoting replicas face high compensation risks similar to manufacturers in European jurisdictions.
Practice note: Strengthen overseas marketing compliance reviews and influencer contracts to prevent joint infringement liability.
What to monitor: Judicial trends regarding secondary liability for digital promoters and the definition of commercial exploitation of fakes.

items.title: Brussels LD, March 18, 2026, Procedural Order (R. 158 RoP)(II), UPC_CFI_1357/2025, UPC_CFI_629/2026
items.title_zh: UPC 布鲁塞尔分部裁定哥斯达黎加执行非过度负担并责令担保
items.title_en: Brussels LD Orders Security for Costs Despite Rejecting Undue Burden Claim
items.summary: The Brussels Local Division did not consider enforcement of a UPC costs order in Costa Rica to be unduly burdensome on the evidence presented...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院布鲁塞尔地方分部作出程序令，驳回被告关于在哥斯达黎加执行费用命令构成“过度负担”的主张，因被告未提供充分基准。但鉴于原告自身财务状况存在风险，法院依据规则第 158 条责令其提供 60 万欧元诉讼费用担保，明确评估对象为原告而非其关联公司。
items.ai_summary_en: The Brussels Local Division issued a procedural order rejecting the defendants' argument that enforcing a UPC costs order in Costa Rica would be unduly burdensome. The Court found insufficient evidence to prove excessive duration in recognition proceedings. However, citing legitimate concerns regarding the claimant's independent financial position, the Court ordered security for costs under Rule 158 of the Rules of Procedure. The amount was set at EUR 600,000, representing 50% of the provisional recoverable costs ceiling. The judgment clarifies that assessments focus on the claimant entity itself, excluding non-party group companies, and may include likely costs of defensive counterclaims for revocation.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在 UPC 应诉时，若主张境外执行困难需提交详实对比数据。同时应关注对方财务独立性，利用其资金弱点申请费用担保，以降低维权成本风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Parties seeking to resist security for costs orders must provide concrete comparative data demonstrating that cross-border enforcement is genuinely prohibitive. Mere assertions of difficulty are insufficient without detailed benchmarks regarding foreign judicial timelines. Conversely, applicants should rigorously analyze the opposing party's standalone financial health rather than relying on broader corporate group stability. Demonstrating specific liquidity risks within the litigant entity can successfully justify security requests. This approach helps mitigate the risk of unrecoverable legal fees, particularly when facing opponents with complex international structures but potentially fragile individual balance sheets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["被告未能证明哥斯达黎加执行程序构成法律意义上的过度负担", "法院基于原告自身财务状况而非集团情况裁定需要费用担保", "担保金额定为可收回费用上限的 50%，即 60 万欧元"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Defendants failed to provide sufficient benchmarks proving that enforcement proceedings in Costa Rica were unduly burdensome under UPC case law.", "Security for costs was ordered based on the claimant's specific financial risks, explicitly excluding the financial status of related group companies.", "The Court set security at EUR 600,000, exercising discretion to award fifty percent of the EUR 1.2 million provisional recoverable costs ceiling."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["主张境外执行负担重需提供充分基准证据", "善用对方财务漏洞申请诉讼费用担保"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Substantiate claims of undue enforcement burden with detailed comparative data on foreign judicial timelines.", "Practice note: Focus security applications on the litigant's standalone financial weakness, disregarding parent or subsidiary solvency.", "What to monitor: How courts weigh defensive counterclaim costs when calculating the quantum of required security for costs."]
core_points_en: Defendants failed to provide sufficient benchmarks proving that enforcement proceedings in Costa Rica were unduly burdensome under UPC case law.
Security for costs was ordered based on the claimant's specific financial risks, explicitly excluding the financial status of related group companies.
The Court set security at EUR 600,000, exercising discretion to award fifty percent of the EUR 1.2 million provisional recoverable costs ceiling.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Substantiate claims of undue enforcement burden with detailed comparative data on foreign judicial timelines.
Practice note: Focus security applications on the litigant's standalone financial weakness, disregarding parent or subsidiary solvency.
What to monitor: How courts weigh defensive counterclaim costs when calculating the quantum of required security for costs.

items.title: CoA, March 13, 2026, Order On Preliminary Objections Concerning International Jurisdiction, UPC_CoA_922/2025, UPC_CoA_923/2025, UPC_CoA_924/2025, UPC_CoA_925/2025
items.title_zh: UPC 上诉院裁定：起诉状须详述管辖事实且不得延伸至非成员国
items.title_en: UPC CoA: Statement of Claim Must Establish Jurisdiction; No Extension to Non-Member States
items.summary: The Court of Appeal makes clear that, as a rule, the claimant must already set out in the Statement of claim the facts and legal arguments necessary to establish...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉院作出命令，明确原告必须在起诉状中详尽阐述确立 UPC 国际管辖权的事实与法律依据，后续提交证据无法弥补诉状缺陷。法院裁定，依据《布鲁塞尔条例 Ia》第 7(2) 条的管辖权仅限于 UPC 成员国境内的损害，不能延伸至瑞士、英国等非成员国。此外，法院拒绝适用法国国内法规则来扩大 UPC 作为共同法院的管辖范围，撤销了一审关于非成员国部分的管辖权认定。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal clarified that claimants must comprehensively set out facts and legal arguments establishing international jurisdiction within the initial Statement of Claim. Subsequent evidence cannot cure deficiencies in these initial pleadings. The Court held that jurisdiction under Article 7(2) of the Brussels Ia Regulation is strictly limited to damage occurring within UPC Member States, rejecting its application to alleged infringements in non-member states such as Switzerland and the UK. Furthermore, the Court ruled that the UPC, as a common court, cannot rely on national provisions like Article 14 of the French Civil Code to expand its jurisdiction. Consequently, the first-instance decision granting jurisdiction over non-UPC territories was set aside due to insufficient pleading of facts required under Article 71b(3) Brussels Ia.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧发起或应对专利诉讼时，须在起诉阶段即严谨论证管辖权基础，避免依赖后续补救。针对涉及非欧盟国家（如英国、瑞士）的侵权指控，需警惕 UPC 管辖权的地理局限，合理规划多国诉讼策略，防止因管辖异议导致部分诉求被驳回。
items.ai_insight_en: Litigants before the UPC must rigorously substantiate jurisdictional bases at the outset of proceedings, avoiding reliance on post-filing corrections. Claims involving non-EU jurisdictions, such as the UK or Switzerland, face significant hurdles under current interpretations of the Brussels Ia Regulation. Parties should anticipate strict scrutiny regarding cross-border infringement allegations and consider separate litigation strategies for non-UPC territories. Failure to adequately plead specific facts supporting extended jurisdiction may result in partial dismissal of claims, necessitating careful strategic planning for multi-jurisdictional patent enforcement actions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["起诉状必须初始即完备阐述管辖权事实，后期补正无效", "基于损害地的管辖权严格限定于 UPC 成员国领土范围内", "UPC 作为共同法院不得援引单一成员国国内法扩张国际管辖权"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Jurisdictional facts and legal arguments must be fully detailed in the initial Statement of Claim; later filings cannot remedy omissions.", "Article 7(2) Brussels Ia jurisdiction is geographically restricted to damage within UPC Member States, excluding non-members.", "The UPC cannot invoke single Member State domestic laws, such as French Civil Code Article 14, to broaden its international jurisdiction."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["起诉阶段务必完备管辖权论证，杜绝依赖事后补充证据", "涉非欧盟国侵权需另案处理，不可盲目依赖 UPC 统一管辖"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure initial pleadings comprehensively address jurisdictional requirements to prevent partial dismissal of cross-border claims.", "Practice note: Develop separate litigation strategies for non-UPC territories, as UPC jurisdiction does not automatically extend to non-member states.", "What to monitor: Future applications of Article 71b(3) Brussels Ia and potential shifts in how the UPC interprets 'sufficient value' of assets within its territory."]
core_points_en: Jurisdictional facts and legal arguments must be fully detailed in the initial Statement of Claim; later filings cannot remedy omissions.
Article 7(2) Brussels Ia jurisdiction is geographically restricted to damage within UPC Member States, excluding non-members.
The UPC cannot invoke single Member State domestic laws, such as French Civil Code Article 14, to broaden its international jurisdiction.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure initial pleadings comprehensively address jurisdictional requirements to prevent partial dismissal of cross-border claims.
Practice note: Develop separate litigation strategies for non-UPC territories, as UPC jurisdiction does not automatically extend to non-member states.
What to monitor: Future applications of Article 71b(3) Brussels Ia and potential shifts in how the UPC interprets 'sufficient value' of assets within its territory.

items.title: CoA, March 16, 2026, Order Concerning Review Of An Ex Parte Order For Inspection, UPC_CoA_3/2026
items.title_zh: UPC 上诉法院裁定单方检查令申请须披露所有重大事实
items.title_en: UPC CoA Revokes Ex Parte Inspection Order Due to Incomplete Disclosure of Material Facts
items.summary: In ex parte inspection proceedings, the applicant must disclose all material facts relevant to the request, including those that may weigh against it...
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC 上诉法院在 Ecovacs 诉 Roborock 案中裁定，单方检查令申请人必须披露所有相关重大事实，包括不利事实。因一审申请遗漏证据可用性及产品商业存在等关键信息，法院撤销原检查令。强调合法性仅基于原始申请评估，后续无法补正缺陷。
items.ai_summary_en: In Ecovacs v. Roborock, the UPC Court of Appeal ruled that applicants for ex parte inspection orders must disclose all material facts, including those unfavorable to their request. The Court upheld the revocation of a first-instance inspection order because the original application was ambiguous and incomplete regarding key issues such as evidence availability and the commercial presence of products in Europe. The judgment emphasizes that the legality of an ex parte measure is assessed strictly based on the application as originally filed. Defects involving missing or misleading information cannot be cured during review proceedings or by relying on materials from parallel cases. This decision reinforces the strict duty of candor required when seeking urgent measures without prior hearing of the opposing party.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在 UPC 提起单方行动时，务必全面披露正反事实，避免因隐瞒或遗漏导致命令被撤销并损害信誉。建议建立严格的内部合规审查机制，确保申请文件完整性，防范程序性败诉风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling highlights the critical importance of comprehensive factual disclosure in ex parte applications before the Unified Patent Court. Practitioners must ensure that initial filings address both supporting and adverse facts, particularly concerning necessity and proportionality. Relying on subsequent corrections or parallel case materials to fix initial deficiencies is not permitted. Companies should implement rigorous internal compliance reviews for patent enforcement strategies to prevent procedural failures. Failure to meet this high standard of transparency risks immediate revocation of protective measures and may damage credibility in future litigation.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["单方程序申请人负有严格披露义务，须包含不利事实", "原始申请的重大遗漏可导致检查令被撤销且不可补正", "法院审查必要性时仅依据最初提交的文件，不含平行程序材料"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Applicants in ex parte proceedings face a strict duty to disclose all material facts, including those weighing against the requested measure.", "Material omissions in the original application justify revoking the inspection order, and such defects cannot be repaired later.", "Legality is assessed solely on the originally filed documents, excluding any supplementary materials from parallel proceedings."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["单方申请需全面披露不利事实，严禁选择性陈述", "原始文件缺陷不可事后补正，务必强化事前合规审查"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ex parte applicants must provide full transparency on all material facts, including adverse ones, to avoid order revocation.", "Practice note: Strengthen pre-filing compliance checks to ensure original applications are complete and free of material omissions.", "What to monitor: Judicial scrutiny regarding the completeness of initial disclosures and the prohibition on curing defects via parallel proceedings."]
core_points_en: Applicants in ex parte proceedings face a strict duty to disclose all material facts, including those weighing against the requested measure.
Material omissions in the original application justify revoking the inspection order, and such defects cannot be repaired later.
Legality is assessed solely on the originally filed documents, excluding any supplementary materials from parallel proceedings.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ex parte applicants must provide full transparency on all material facts, including adverse ones, to avoid order revocation.
Practice note: Strengthen pre-filing compliance checks to ensure original applications are complete and free of material omissions.
What to monitor: Judicial scrutiny regarding the completeness of initial disclosures and the prohibition on curing defects via parallel proceedings.

items.title: Local Division Munich, March 11, 2026, Decision, UPC_CFI_63/2024, UPC_CFI_449/2024
items.title_zh: UPC 慕尼黑分部判决：侵权举证责任转移与禁止超范围修改
items.title_en: UPC Munich: Sample-Based Infringement Presumptions and Added Matter Revocation
items.summary: The claimant can exemplify infringement on a sample product. The burden then shifts to the defendant to specifically dispute why other listed products do not fall under this definition.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院慕尼黑分部就 Network System Technologies 诉高通案作出判决。法院裁定原告可通过样品证明侵权，举证责任随后转移至被告；专利登记推定起诉资格。同时，因权利要求暗示原申请未披露的物理结构及功能主体变更，认定构成不可允许扩展而撤销专利。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Local Division in Munich issued a significant judgment in Network System Technologies LLC v. Qualcomm Incorporated regarding European patent EP 1 552 669. The court established that claimants may exemplify infringement using a sample product, shifting the burden to defendants to specifically dispute why other listed products fall outside this definition. Registration in the patent register creates a rebuttable presumption of standing to sue, which mere disputes over purchase agreements cannot overturn. However, the patent was revoked for inadmissible extension under Article 123(2) EPC. The claims implied physical structures and functional attributions not unambiguously disclosed in the original application. Additionally, an order to produce evidence under Rule 190 RoP was rejected as the initial evidence was implausible, preventing fishing expeditions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应诉需警惕样品举证的扩散效应，应针对性反驳非涉案产品。申请阶段需严格核对权利要求与支持文件，避免因措辞暗示未披露特征导致专利被无效。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision clarifies procedural burdens and substantive validity standards at the UPC. For rights holders, registering patents remains crucial for establishing standing, but sample-based infringement strategies require robust initial plausibility to avoid rejection of evidence orders. For alleged infringers, merely challenging title is ineffective without compelling facts; specific technical rebuttals against sample extrapolation are essential. Furthermore, the strict application of added matter rules highlights drafting risks. Practitioners must ensure claim language aligns precisely with original disclosures to prevent invalidity based on implied physical separations or functional shifts not explicitly supported by the application text.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["原告以样品 exemplify 侵权后，被告须具体反驳其他产品为何不侵权", "专利登记产生起诉资格的可反驳推定，仅质疑购买协议不足推翻", "权利要求暗示未披露物理结构或功能主体变更构成禁止超范围修改"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Claimants may exemplify infringement via a sample product, shifting the burden to defendants to specifically dispute non-infringement of other listed items.", "Patent register registration creates a rebuttable presumption of standing; disputing purchase agreement validity alone is insufficient to rebut this.", "Claims implying undisclosed physical structures or changing functional entities constitute inadmissible extensions, leading to patent revocation."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["应对样品举证策略，须具体论证列明产品为何不在侵权定义范围内", "撰写时避免使用暗示未披露结构或功能的词汇，防范无效风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Defendants must provide specific technical arguments to counter sample-based infringement presumptions rather than relying solely on procedural challenges.", "Practice note: Drafters should avoid claim terms implying physical structures or functional attributions not explicitly supported by the original disclosure to prevent Art. 123(2)…", "What to monitor: Courts' evolving interpretation of 'plausible evidence' for Rule 190 production orders to distinguish substantiated claims from fishing expeditions."]
core_points_en: Claimants may exemplify infringement via a sample product, shifting the burden to defendants to specifically dispute non-infringement of other listed items.
Patent register registration creates a rebuttable presumption of standing; disputing purchase agreement validity alone is insufficient to rebut this.
Claims implying undisclosed physical structures or changing functional entities constitute inadmissible extensions, leading to patent revocation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Defendants must provide specific technical arguments to counter sample-based infringement presumptions rather than relying solely on procedural challenges.
Practice note: Drafters should avoid claim terms implying physical structures or functional attributions not explicitly supported by the original disclosure to prevent Art. 123(2)…
What to monitor: Courts' evolving interpretation of 'plausible evidence' for Rule 190 production orders to distinguish substantiated claims from fishing expeditions.

items.title: CD Milan, March 13, 2026, Order And Decision On Costs, UPC_CFI_722/2025
items.title_zh: UPC 米兰分部裁定 EPO 撤销专利后诉讼无意义并判令高额费用
items.title_en: UPC Milan: EPO Final Revocation Terminates Action; High Costs Awarded Despite No Merits Decision
items.summary: The Milan Central Division held that, once the Opposition Division had revoked the patent in its entirety and that decision had become final because no appeal was filed...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）米兰中央分部裁定，若欧洲专利局（EPO）异议部已最终撤销涉案专利，则在 UPC 提起的撤销诉讼因失去目的而须依程序规则终止。尽管未实体审理，原告仍被视为胜诉方。法院驳回被告关于公平原则的抗辩，鉴于书面程序已深入，判令被告承担高达 80% 的费用上限，即 48 万欧元律师费及部分诉讼费。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Central Division in Milan ruled that a revocation action becomes devoid of purpose once the European Patent Office Opposition Division finally revokes the patent. Consequently, the action must be disposed of under Rule 360 of the Rules of Procedure. The Court determined that the claimant remains the successful party for costs purposes, even without a substantive decision on validity by the UPC. It rejected the defendant's equity arguments regarding stays and additional evidence. Given the advanced stage of written proceedings, the Court awarded 80% of the cost ceiling, totaling EUR 480,000 in representation costs plus partial court fees, emphasizing the front-loaded nature of UPC litigation expenses.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对欧洲专利纠纷时，若 EPO 已撤销涉案专利，应迅速向 UPC 申请终止诉讼以锁定胜诉地位并追偿费用。同时需注意，即使案件未走完全流程，只要书面阶段深入，仍可能面临巨额费用赔偿，需提前评估风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Parties should monitor EPO opposition outcomes closely, as a final revocation there automatically terminates parallel UPC actions. Claimants can leverage this to secure a 'successful party' status and recover significant legal costs, even without a UPC judgment on validity. Conversely, defendants face high financial exposure because UPC costs are heavily front-loaded. Even if a case ends early due to EPO decisions, parties may still bear substantial costs if the written phase progressed. Strategic coordination between EPO and UPC proceedings is essential to manage litigation risks and cost liabilities effectively across both forums.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EPO 最终撤销专利后，UPC 撤销诉讼因无实际意义须依法终止", "即便诉讼终止，原告仍被认定为胜诉方，被告需承担主要诉讼费用", "法院基于程序前端负载特性，在终止案件中仍可判赔高额比例费用"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["A UPC revocation action is terminated under Rule 360 RoP when the patent is finally revoked by the EPO Opposition Division.", "The claimant is deemed fully successful for costs allocation, despite the case being disposed of without a merits decision.", "Substantial costs up to 80% of the ceiling are recoverable if the written procedure was significantly advanced before disposal."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用 EPO 撤销结果快速终结 UPC 诉讼，确立胜诉方地位以追偿", "警惕 UPC 费用前端加载机制，即便案件终止也可能承担高额赔偿"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage final EPO revocations to terminate UPC actions and secure cost recovery as the prevailing party.", "Practice note: Assess cost risks early, as advanced written procedures trigger high fee awards even upon early case disposal.", "What to monitor: Interplay between EPO opposition timelines and UPC procedural stages to optimize settlement or termination strategies."]
core_points_en: A UPC revocation action is terminated under Rule 360 RoP when the patent is finally revoked by the EPO Opposition Division.
The claimant is deemed fully successful for costs allocation, despite the case being disposed of without a merits decision.
Substantial costs up to 80% of the ceiling are recoverable if the written procedure was significantly advanced before disposal.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage final EPO revocations to terminate UPC actions and secure cost recovery as the prevailing party.
Practice note: Assess cost risks early, as advanced written procedures trigger high fee awards even upon early case disposal.
What to monitor: Interplay between EPO opposition timelines and UPC procedural stages to optimize settlement or termination strategies.

items.title: Demand for European patents in 2025 exceeded 200 000 for first time
items.title_zh: 2025 年欧洲专利申请量首超 20 万件
items.title_en: EPO Reports Record 2025 Patent Filings Exceeding 200,000 with Strong Digital Growth
items.summary: EPO Technology Dashboard shows strong growth in demand for patents for digital and energy technologies. The US and Germany remain top countries of origin, while China enters top three for first time.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局发布数据显示，2025 年专利申请量达 201,974 件，创历史新高。数字与能源技术领域增长强劲，计算机技术居首，AI 与量子技术增速显著。美国与德国保持领先，中国首次跻身来源国前三。统一专利制度 uptake 较高，尤其受欧洲中小企业青睐。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office (EPO) reported a record 201,974 patent applications in 2025, marking the first time annual filings surpassed the 200,000 threshold. This represents a 1.4% increase from the previous year, driven by robust demand in digital and energy technologies. Computer technology remained the leading field, significantly boosted by artificial intelligence and quantum innovations. The United States and Germany retained their positions as top countries of origin, while China entered the top three for the first time. Notably, uptake of the Unitary Patent system was high, particularly among European small and medium-sized enterprises seeking streamlined protection across participating states.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应关注欧洲在 AI、半导体及绿色能源领域的专利布局热点。利用统一专利制度降低维权成本，同时警惕来自日韩欧美的激烈竞争，加强高价值专利组合构建以应对技术主权趋势。
items.ai_insight_en: The surge in filings within strategic sectors like AI, semiconductors, and green energy highlights Europe’s focus on tech sovereignty. Stakeholders should leverage the Unitary Patent system to optimize protection costs and enforcement efficiency across multiple jurisdictions. However, increased competition from Asian and US applicants necessitates robust high-value patent portfolios. Companies must align filing strategies with policy priorities to secure market position amidst evolving regulatory landscapes and intensifying global technological rivalry.

items.title: Judgment of the Court in Case C-767/23 [Remling]
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院：终审法院拒绝初步裁决必须详述理由
items.title_en: CJEU: National Courts of Last Instance Must Reason Refusals to Refer
items.summary: A national court or tribunal of last instance must always give reasons for its refusal to refer questions to the Court of Justice for a preliminary ruling
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院在 Remling 案中裁定，成员国终审法院若拒绝向欧盟法院提请初步裁决，必须具体说明理由。即使国内法允许简易驳回上诉，法院仍需详细阐述为何符合无需裁决的三种例外情形（问题无关、已有判例或显而易见）。本案源于荷兰国务委员会处理摩洛哥公民居留许可案时未充分说明拒绝理由，法院强调此举对维护欧盟法律解释统一性至关重要。
items.ai_summary_en: In Case C-767/23 Remling, the Court of Justice ruled that national courts of last instance must always provide specific reasons when refusing to refer questions for a preliminary ruling. Even where domestic law permits summary dismissal of appeals, such courts must concretely explain why an exception to the obligation to refer applies: irrelevance, existing case law, or acte clair. The judgment addresses Dutch legislation allowing supreme courts to dismiss appeals with brief reasoning, emphasizing that procedural efficiency cannot override the fundamental duty to ensure uniform EU law interpretation. Lower court grounds may be adopted only if they clearly justify the refusal under these strict exceptions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment reinforces procedural safeguards for parties seeking clarification of EU law in final instances. It limits the ability of supreme courts to bypass referrals through summary decisions, ensuring transparency in the application of CILFIT criteria. For litigants, this provides a clearer basis to challenge refusals to refer, particularly in complex areas like immigration or trade where EU law interpretation is critical for consistent application across member states.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["终审法院拒绝初步裁决时必须具体说明适用例外的理由", "即便国内法允许简易裁判，也不能免除详述拒绝裁决理由的义务", "下级法院的理由可被采纳，但前提是其已清晰论证符合例外情形"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Courts of last instance must specifically state reasons for refusing preliminary references, regardless of domestic summary procedure rules.", "Refusals are valid only if the question is irrelevant, previously interpreted, or so obvious as to leave no reasonable doubt.", "Lower court reasoning may be adopted by supreme courts only if it explicitly justifies the refusal under established exceptions."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用新规要求终审法院详述理由，增加案件转询欧盟法院几率", "在涉欧诉讼中强化程序抗辩，确保欧盟法律解释的统一适用"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Parties in final appeal stages can demand concrete justification for non-referral, enhancing procedural fairness and legal certainty.", "Practice note: Counsel should scrutinize lower court judgments for adequate CILFIT analysis, as supreme courts may adopt these grounds for refusal.", "What to monitor: National implementations of this ruling may affect how supreme courts draft summary dismissals in future EU law disputes."]
core_points_en: Courts of last instance must specifically state reasons for refusing preliminary references, regardless of domestic summary procedure rules.
Refusals are valid only if the question is irrelevant, previously interpreted, or so obvious as to leave no reasonable doubt.
Lower court reasoning may be adopted by supreme courts only if it explicitly justifies the refusal under established exceptions.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Parties in final appeal stages can demand concrete justification for non-referral, enhancing procedural fairness and legal certainty.
Practice note: Counsel should scrutinize lower court judgments for adequate CILFIT analysis, as supreme courts may adopt these grounds for refusal.
What to monitor: National implementations of this ruling may affect how supreme courts draft summary dismissals in future EU law disputes.

items.title: Judgment of the Court in Case C-521/21 Rzecznik Praw Obywatelskich (Recusal of a judge of an ordinary court)
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院裁定：法官任命瑕疵本身不足以认定其缺乏独立性
items.title_en: CJEU: Judicial Appointment Irregularities Alone Do Not Establish Lack of Independence
items.summary: Rule of law: an irregularity committed during the appointment of a judge is not in itself sufficient to find that that judge is not independent, since an overall assessment of all of the circumstances attending the appointment of that judge is necessary
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院在 C-521/21 号案件中裁定，法官任命过程中的不规则性本身不足以认定该法官缺乏独立性。法院强调需对任命背景进行整体评估，仅当瑕疵性质严重且共同导致外部干涉风险时，才构成回避理由。波兰司法改革引发的任命争议及救济缺失，单独或结合均不自动导致法官回避。
items.ai_summary_en: In Case C-521/21, the Court of Justice ruled that irregularities in a judge's appointment do not automatically render the judge non-independent. An overall assessment of all circumstances is required. The participation of Poland's reformed National Council of the Judiciary (KRS) and the lack of remedies for unsuccessful candidates are insufficient grounds for recusal alone. Recusal requires irregularities capable of creating a real risk of state interference or reasonable doubt regarding impartiality. National courts must review the lawfulness of appointments but cannot rely solely on structural reforms to disqualify judges without evidence of specific risks to independence.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中企涉诉时，利用波兰法官任命瑕疵申请回避的策略受限。需聚焦具体案件中的实质干涉证据，而非单纯依赖程序瑕疵，建议加强个案法律风险评估。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment clarifies the threshold for challenging judicial independence in EU member states, particularly regarding systemic reforms. For litigants, it establishes that procedural defects in appointment mechanisms, such as those involving Poland's KRS, are insufficient for recusal without demonstrating specific risks of external interference. Parties seeking disqualification must focus on concrete evidence of compromised impartiality rather than relying solely on general arguments about institutional independence.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["任命瑕疵需整体评估，非自动导致法官缺乏独立性", "仅严重瑕疵致外部干涉风险时，方质疑法庭合法性", "波兰司法改革背景下的任命参与不足以免除法官履职"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Irregularities in judicial appointment procedures require an overall assessment of circumstances rather than automatic findings of non-independence.", "Participation of the reformed Polish KRS and lack of remedies for candidates do not individually justify judge recusal under EU law.", "Recusal requires irregularities creating a real risk of state interference or reasonable doubt regarding the judge's impartiality."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["申请法官回避需证明实质干涉风险，非仅凭程序瑕疵", "在波诉讼应强化个案独立性证据收集与法律论证"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Litigants in Poland and similar jurisdictions must provide specific evidence of interference risks to successfully challenge judicial independence.", "Practice note: Legal arguments for recusal should focus on the gravity and nature of specific irregularities affecting the individual judge's impartiality.", "What to monitor: Future CJEU applications of this 'overall assessment' standard to other member states undergoing judicial system reforms."]
core_points_en: Irregularities in judicial appointment procedures require an overall assessment of circumstances rather than automatic findings of non-independence.
Participation of the reformed Polish KRS and lack of remedies for candidates do not individually justify judge recusal under EU law.
Recusal requires irregularities creating a real risk of state interference or reasonable doubt regarding the judge's impartiality.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Litigants in Poland and similar jurisdictions must provide specific evidence of interference risks to successfully challenge judicial independence.
Practice note: Legal arguments for recusal should focus on the gravity and nature of specific irregularities affecting the individual judge's impartiality.
What to monitor: Future CJEU applications of this 'overall assessment' standard to other member states undergoing judicial system reforms.

items.title: DOJ antitrust investigation targets automakers in licensing negotiation group
items.title_zh: 美司法部反垄断调查瞄准汽车专利许可谈判集团
items.title_en: DOJ Antitrust Probe Targets Automakers Over Joint SEP Licensing Negotiation Group
items.summary: IAM confirmed the Department of Justice sent investigative demand letters to BMW, VW, Mercedes and thyssenkrupp about whether their Automotive Licensing Negotiation Group was potentially collusive
items.ai_summary_zh: 美国司法部向宝马、大众、梅赛德斯 - 奔驰及蒂森克虏伯发出调查函，审查其组建的“汽车许可谈判集团”是否涉嫌违反反垄断法。该集团旨在联合进行标准必要专利许可谈判，此举引发监管机构对车企协同行为可能导致市场垄断的担忧，标志着跨国专利许可策略面临更严格的竞争法审视。
items.ai_summary_en: The US Department of Justice has issued investigative demand letters to BMW, Volkswagen, Mercedes-Benz, and thyssenkrupp. The inquiry examines whether their Automotive Licensing Negotiation Group constitutes illegal collusion under antitrust laws. This group was established to conduct joint negotiations for standard-essential patent licenses. Regulators are concerned that such coordinated behavior among major automakers could lead to market monopolization or anti-competitive outcomes. This development signals a stricter regulatory scrutiny of cross-border patent licensing strategies. It highlights the growing tension between collective industry approaches to IP management and global competition law requirements. Companies must now carefully evaluate the legal risks associated with collaborative licensing frameworks to ensure compliance with both US and international antitrust standards.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国车企及通信企业在欧开展 SEP 许可时，应避免组建或参与可能被认定为垄断的联合谈判组织。需独立评估许可策略，强化反垄断合规审查，防范因集体行动引发的跨境执法风险，确保商业合作符合竞争法要求。
items.ai_insight_en: Industry participants engaging in standard-essential patent licensing should exercise caution when forming or joining collective negotiation bodies. Such arrangements may attract scrutiny from competition authorities globally, including in the EU and US. Companies must independently assess their licensing strategies to ensure they do not violate antitrust regulations. Strengthening internal compliance reviews is essential to mitigate cross-border enforcement risks. Collaborative efforts must be structured to align with competition law requirements, avoiding any appearance of market manipulation or unfair coordination. Proactive legal evaluation helps safeguard commercial operations against potential regulatory penalties and reputational damage in international markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["美司法部启动对四大欧企专利谈判集团的反垄断调查", "调查焦点在于联合许可谈判是否构成非法协同行为", "欧洲车企集体专利许可模式面临严峻法律合规挑战"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["US DOJ launched an antitrust investigation into four European automakers regarding their joint patent negotiation group.", "The primary focus is determining if collective licensing negotiations constitute illegal collusive behavior under antitrust laws.", "European automakers face significant legal compliance challenges regarding their collective standard-essential patent licensing models."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕联合谈判模式引发的跨境反垄断执法风险", "建立独立的专利许可合规体系以应对监管审查"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Be alert to cross-border antitrust enforcement risks triggered by joint negotiation models in SEP licensing.", "Practice note: Establish independent patent licensing compliance systems to effectively manage and respond to regulatory scrutiny.", "What to monitor: Evolving antitrust enforcement trends regarding collective actions and collaborative IP licensing strategies globally."]
core_points_en: US DOJ launched an antitrust investigation into four European automakers regarding their joint patent negotiation group.
The primary focus is determining if collective licensing negotiations constitute illegal collusive behavior under antitrust laws.
European automakers face significant legal compliance challenges regarding their collective standard-essential patent licensing models.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Be alert to cross-border antitrust enforcement risks triggered by joint negotiation models in SEP licensing.
Practice note: Establish independent patent licensing compliance systems to effectively manage and respond to regulatory scrutiny.
What to monitor: Evolving antitrust enforcement trends regarding collective actions and collaborative IP licensing strategies globally.

items.title: Classement 2025 des demandes de brevets à l’OEB
items.title_zh: 2025 年欧洲专利局专利申请排名发布
items.title_en: Classement 2025 des demandes de brevets à l’OEB
items.summary: Classement 2025 des demandes de brevets à l’OEB sgurtler@inpi.fr 23 mars 2026 L’essentiel à retenir : La France reste le 2ᵉ pays d’origine en Europe pour les demandes de brevet européen, avec 10 932 en 2025 et se classe au 6ᵉ rang mondial ; Les transports demeurent le principal domaine d’innovation en France, devant l’informatique et les machines, appareils et énergie électriques ; Principaux demandeurs français : Valeo, Safran Group et le CEA Les innovateurs français recourent davantage au brev
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国国家工业产权局发布 2025 年欧专局申请数据。法国以 10932 件申请居欧洲第二、全球第六，交通领域创新领先。法企加速采用单一专利制度，占比达三分之一。美、德申请量微跌，中、韩显著增长，显示全球专利布局竞争加剧。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应关注欧洲交通与能源技术竞争态势，评估在法布局策略。鉴于法企高频使用单一专利，中方需优化欧专局与单一专利组合申请，以应对日益激烈的区域技术保护竞争。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法国稳居欧洲第二大专利申请国，交通领域创新最强", "瓦雷奥、赛峰等法企主导申请，单一专利使用率提升", "中韩申请量大幅增长，欧美传统强国呈现小幅下滑态势"]

items.title: Merz and Uexküll prevail as Federal Court of Justice upholds fampridine patents
items.title_zh: 德联邦最高法院维持默克法姆吡啶专利有效
items.title_en: German Federal Court of Justice Upholds Merz Fampridine Patents for Multiple Sclerosis Treatment
items.summary: Merz is owner of EP 2 377 536 and EP 1 732 548. Both cover “methods of using sustained release aminopyridine compositions” and relate to a dosing regimen for fampridine. This forms the basis of Fampyra, a drug which aims to improve walking ability in multiple sclerosis patients. Acorda originally filed both patents. Merz Therapeutics […] The post Merz and Uexküll prevail as Federal Court of Justice upholds fampridine patents appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 德国联邦最高法院（BGH）作出判决，维持默克公司（Merz）持有的两项关于法姆吡啶缓释组合物使用方法及给药方案的欧洲专利有效。该专利是多发性硬化症药物 Fampyra 的基础，原由 Acorda 申请。法院驳回了无效挑战，确认了默克及其合作伙伴 Uexküll 在该关键药物上的专利权稳定性，对相关市场格局产生重要影响。
items.ai_summary_en: The German Federal Court of Justice (BGH) has issued a judgment upholding the validity of two European patents held by Merz, covering methods for using sustained-release aminopyridine compositions and specific dosing regimens for fampridine. These patents form the legal basis for Fampyra, a medication designed to improve walking ability in patients with multiple sclerosis. Although originally filed by Acorda, the rights are now owned by Merz Therapeutics. The court rejected challenges seeking invalidation, thereby confirming the stability of Merz’s patent rights and those of its partner Uexküll. This decision significantly impacts the market landscape for this key therapeutic agent, reinforcing the exclusivity surrounding its commercialization in Germany.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企在欧布局仿制药或改良型新药时，需高度警惕此类方法专利的壁垒。建议提前开展自由实施分析（FTO），评估绕过给药方案专利的可能性，或考虑通过许可谈判降低侵权风险，避免进入德国市场后遭遇禁令。
items.ai_insight_en: For pharmaceutical companies operating in Europe, particularly those developing generics or improved new drugs, method-of-use patents representing dosing regimens pose significant barriers to entry. It is essential to conduct thorough freedom-to-operate analyses early in development to assess infringement risks associated with such patents. Companies should evaluate potential design-around strategies or consider licensing negotiations to mitigate litigation exposure. Failure to address these intellectual property hurdles may result in injunctions upon market entry, especially in jurisdictions like Germany where patent enforcement remains robust and protective of established therapeutic monopolies.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["德国最高院裁定默克持有的法姆吡啶用途专利继续有效", "涉案专利覆盖多发性硬化症药物 Fampyra 的核心给药方案", "法院驳回无效请求，巩固了权利人在该治疗领域的垄断地位"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The German Federal Court of Justice ruled that Merz's fampridine use patents remain valid and enforceable against challengers.", "The disputed patents cover the core dosing regimen for Fampyra, a critical treatment for improving mobility in multiple sclerosis patients.", "By dismissing invalidity requests, the court solidified the patent holder's exclusive position within this specific therapeutic market segment."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["赴欧药企应重点排查给药方案类专利的侵权风险与规避路径", "面对核心专利维持有效，需预先制定许可谈判或产品调整策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Generics and biosimilar developers must rigorously screen for dosing regimen patents to avoid infringement liabilities in key European markets.", "Practice note: Conduct comprehensive freedom-to-operate analyses focusing on method-of-use claims and explore non-infringing alternatives or licensing options proactively.", "What to monitor: Monitor enforcement trends regarding second medical use patents in Germany, as courts continue to uphold strong protections for established therapeutic regimens."]
core_points_en: The German Federal Court of Justice ruled that Merz's fampridine use patents remain valid and enforceable against challengers.
The disputed patents cover the core dosing regimen for Fampyra, a critical treatment for improving mobility in multiple sclerosis patients.
By dismissing invalidity requests, the court solidified the patent holder's exclusive position within this specific therapeutic market segment.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Generics and biosimilar developers must rigorously screen for dosing regimen patents to avoid infringement liabilities in key European markets.
Practice note: Conduct comprehensive freedom-to-operate analyses focusing on method-of-use claims and explore non-infringing alternatives or licensing options proactively.
What to monitor: Monitor enforcement trends regarding second medical use patents in Germany, as courts continue to uphold strong protections for established therapeutic regimens.

items.title: Selective Presentation Of Content Of Varying Complexity: Non-technical
items.title_zh: 基于消费速率调整内容复杂度被认定为非技术特征
items.title_en: EPO Board Rules Content Complexity Adaptation Non-Technical Under Comvik Approach
items.summary: The application underlying the discussed decision concerns a method for selecting online content to present to users based on their content consumption behavior on a social media platform.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局上诉委员会在 T 1207/22 号案件中裁定，根据用户内容消费速率动态调整展示内容复杂度的方法属于非技术目标。尽管消费速率可客观测量，但该逻辑本身可由非技术人员构想，不具备技术性。不过，案件因涉及临时内容与静态内容处理的技术实现细节被发回重审。
items.ai_summary_en: In decision T 1207/22, EPO Technical Board of Appeal 3.5.01 assessed a method for selecting online content based on user consumption behavior. The Board held that adapting content complexity according to a user's content consumption rate constitutes a non-technical aim under the Comvik approach, as the underlying concept could be conceived by a non-technical person. Although the consumption rate is objectively measured via user inputs and elapsed time, this does not automatically confer technical character. However, the Board remitted the case for further examination regarding features distinguishing ephemeral from static content for rate calculation, noting these may involve non-obvious technical implementation considerations requiring further search and assessment.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧申请社交算法类专利时，应避免仅强调商业逻辑或用户体验优化。需深入挖掘底层数据处理、缓存机制等具体技术实现细节，以证明存在超越常规编程的技术贡献，规避被驳回风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision reinforces the strict application of the Comvik two-piece test to social media algorithms and user interface adaptations. Rights holders must distinguish between business-driven user experience optimizations and genuine technical solutions. While measuring user interaction speed is technical in execution, using that data to drive content selection logic is often viewed as administrative or psychological. Claims focusing solely on the adaptive presentation strategy face high rejection risks unless supported by specific, non-obvious technical processing steps.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["依据 Comvik 原则，适配内容复杂度属非技术目的", "即使基于客观数据，核心逻辑仍缺乏技术贡献", "临时内容处理的技术实现需进一步审查与检索"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Adapting content complexity based on consumption rate is a non-technical aim under Comvik, even with objective measurement.", "The core logic of adjusting presentation difficulty can be conceived by a non-technical person without technical contribution.", "Distinguishing ephemeral from static content for rate calculation involves potential technical implementation details warranting further examination."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["算法专利需聚焦底层技术实现而非业务逻辑", "区分临时与静态内容的处理可作为技术突破口"]

items.title: SACEPO Working Party on Rules reviews digitalisation, legal changes and accessibility measures
items.title_zh: SACEPO 规则工作组审议数字化、法律变更及无障碍措施
items.title_en: EPO SACEPO Rules Working Party Reviews Digitalisation, Legal Changes and Accessibility
items.summary: The EPO hosted a lively SACEPO Working Party on Rules meeting on 12 March 2026. Key topics were digitalisation and simplification initiatives, the paperless PGP project, convergence of practice and upcoming fee adjustments.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026 年 3 月，EPO 召开 SACEPO 规则工作组会议，重点讨论专利授予流程数字化与简化。核心议题包括 2027 年实现无纸化 PGP 的法律修订、引入 DOCX 文件格式、AI 辅助权利要求翻译，以及在视频口头程序中增加实时字幕以提升无障碍性。此外，会议还通报了实践趋同计划第三周期的启动情况。
items.ai_summary_en: On 12 March 2026, the European Patent Office hosted the first biannual meeting of the SACEPO Working Party on Rules. Thirteen user group representatives discussed digital transformation initiatives, including legal amendments required to achieve a paperless patent granting process by 1 April 2027. The EPO presented plans to introduce DOCX as a new filing format in mid-2026 and proposed AI-supported claim translations at grant. Discussions also covered the third basket of legal changes aimed at improving user-friendliness, such as substantiating auxiliary requests. Additionally, the Office announced live captioning for videoconference oral proceedings to enhance accessibility for participants with hearing impairments. Members exchanged views on implementation challenges and the convergence of practice programme.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需关注 EPO 无纸化及 DOCX 格式切换，提前升级内部系统以适应新标准。同时应评估 AI 翻译在确权中的风险，并利用新增的无障碍功能优化跨国远程应诉策略，降低程序成本。
items.ai_insight_en: Stakeholders should prepare internal systems for the transition to paperless procedures and the adoption of DOCX filing formats by mid-2026. While AI-supported translations offer efficiency, firms must assess potential risks regarding accuracy during the grant phase. Furthermore, the introduction of live captioning in remote hearings provides an opportunity to optimize cross-border litigation strategies, potentially reducing travel costs while ensuring compliance with new procedural requirements for substantiating auxiliary requests.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["推进无纸化进程，计划 2027 年 4 月前实现专利授予流程全数字化", "拟引入 DOCX 格式及 AI 翻译，优化申请体验但引发部分讨论", "在反对程序视频会议中增加实时字幕，提升听障人士参与度"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Legal amendments are being finalized to enable a fully paperless patent granting process by 1 April 2027, reflecting user feedback on digital-only communication principles.", "The EPO plans to introduce DOCX as a new filing format by the second quarter of 2026 and is exploring AI-supported claim translations at the grant stage.", "Live captioning will be introduced for opposition oral proceedings via videoconference to improve digital accessibility and inclusivity for hearing-impaired participants."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["提前布局系统升级，适应 2026-2027 年 EPO 无纸化及新格式要求", "审慎利用 AI 翻译工具，关注辅助请求实质化举证的新规挑战"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Prepare IT infrastructure for DOCX filing adoption and paperless workflows scheduled for 2026-2027 implementation.", "Practice note: Evaluate AI translation tools for reliability and ensure robust substantiation of auxiliary requests under new legal standards.", "What to monitor: Final details on exceptions to mandatory online filing and technical specifications for the new digital submission formats."]
core_points_en: Legal amendments are being finalized to enable a fully paperless patent granting process by 1 April 2027, reflecting user feedback on digital-only communication principles.
The EPO plans to introduce DOCX as a new filing format by the second quarter of 2026 and is exploring AI-supported claim translations at the grant stage.
Live captioning will be introduced for opposition oral proceedings via videoconference to improve digital accessibility and inclusivity for hearing-impaired participants.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Prepare IT infrastructure for DOCX filing adoption and paperless workflows scheduled for 2026-2027 implementation.
Practice note: Evaluate AI translation tools for reliability and ensure robust substantiation of auxiliary requests under new legal standards.
What to monitor: Final details on exceptions to mandatory online filing and technical specifications for the new digital submission formats.

items.title_zh: 从公开网络到暗网：EUIPO培训法官和检察官掌握新技术
items.title_en: From the open web to the dark web: EUIPO trains judges and prosecutors on new technologies
items.source_name: 欧盟知识产权局商标资讯 (EUIPO)
items.summary: From the open web to the dark web: EUIPO trains judges and prosecutors on new technologies
items.ai_summary_zh: 标题显示，欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）正在为法官和检察官提供关于新技术的培训。该培训旨在帮助司法人员应对从公开网络到暗网的知识产权侵权挑战，提升其在数字环境下的执法能力。原文仅提供了标题及链接，未包含更多正文细节。

items.title: CJEU rules that derivative works enjoy copyright protection if they are … original
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院裁定：具备独创性的衍生作品受版权保护
items.title_en: CJEU Confirms Derivative Works Receive Copyright Protection in EU If They Are Original
items.summary: Article 2(3) of the Berne Convention mandates the copyright protection of derivative works like “translations, adaptations, arrangements of music and other alterations of a literary or artistic work” upon them being sufficiently original. It follows that, under international law, a derivative work enjoys copyright protection if it is sufficiently original.Does the same apply in EU copyright law?
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院（CJEU）针对衍生作品在欧盟法下的保护地位作出明确裁决。依据《伯尔尼公约》原则，法院确认翻译、改编等衍生作品若具备充分独创性，即在欧盟法律框架下享有独立版权保护。该判决澄清了国际公约与欧盟内部法律适用的一致性，确立了以“独创性”为核心的统一判断标准，消除了成员国间可能存在的法律分歧。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the European Union has issued a definitive ruling regarding the copyright status of derivative works under EU law. Aligning with the principles established in Article 2(3) of the Berne Convention, the Court confirmed that translations, adaptations, and other alterations of literary or artistic works enjoy independent copyright protection within the EU legal framework, provided they meet the threshold of sufficient originality. This judgment clarifies the consistency between international treaty obligations and internal EU copyright legislation. By establishing originality as the central criterion for protection, the decision eliminates potential legal divergences among Member States. It ensures that creators of derivative works receive uniform recognition and protection across the Union, reinforcing the harmonized application of copyright standards for adapted content.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧进行内容本地化或二次创作时，应确保产出具备独创性以获保护。同时需警惕侵犯原作品权利，避免因未获授权而面临侵权诉讼风险，建议加强合规审查。
items.ai_insight_en: Entities operating within the EU must ensure that any localized content or secondary creations demonstrate sufficient originality to secure independent copyright protection. While securing these rights, it is critical to avoid infringing upon the underlying original works. Unauthorized adaptations may lead to significant litigation risks and liability. Therefore, comprehensive compliance reviews are essential before launching derivative projects. Stakeholders should verify that their creative inputs transform the source material sufficiently to meet the originality standard while simultaneously obtaining necessary licenses from original rightsholders to mitigate infringement exposure.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟法院确认衍生作品受保护前提是具备充分独创性", "裁决实现伯尔尼公约原则与欧盟版权法的适用统一", "明确翻译与改编等作品在满足条件时享有独立权利"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The CJEU confirms that derivative works are protected under EU law only if they possess sufficient originality.", "The ruling aligns EU copyright application with Berne Convention principles, ensuring consistent legal interpretation.", "Translations and adaptations qualify for independent copyright rights when they satisfy the originality requirement."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["在欧衍生创作须确保独创性以获取独立版权保护", "开展改编业务前务必取得原作品著作权人授权"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure derivative creations exhibit sufficient originality to qualify for independent copyright protection in the EU market.", "Practice note: Obtain explicit authorization from original copyright holders before commencing adaptation or localization activities.", "What to monitor: Monitor national implementations of this ruling to identify any residual discrepancies in how originality is assessed locally."]
core_points_en: The CJEU confirms that derivative works are protected under EU law only if they possess sufficient originality.
The ruling aligns EU copyright application with Berne Convention principles, ensuring consistent legal interpretation.
Translations and adaptations qualify for independent copyright rights when they satisfy the originality requirement.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure derivative creations exhibit sufficient originality to qualify for independent copyright protection in the EU market.
Practice note: Obtain explicit authorization from original copyright holders before commencing adaptation or localization activities.
What to monitor: Monitor national implementations of this ruling to identify any residual discrepancies in how originality is assessed locally.

items.title: Failure to seek UPC confidentiality order strips EOflow’s trade secrets of protection
items.title_zh: 未申请保密令致 EOflow 在 UPC 诉讼中丧失商业秘密保护
items.title_en: UPC Ruling: Failure to Seek Confidentiality Order Strips Trade Secrets of Protection
items.summary: Judges rule that information shared without obtaining Rule 262.2 RoP protection loses its confidential character in spat with rival insulin-delivery device maker Insulet
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）法官裁定，EOflow 在与竞争对手 Insulet 的胰岛素输送设备纠纷中，因未按程序规则第 262.2 条申请保密令，导致其共享信息失去机密性特征。该判决明确了当事人在 UPC 诉讼中主动寻求程序保护的义务，否则将面临商业秘密泄露且无法获得救济的风险，确立了严格的程序合规标准。
items.ai_summary_en: Judges at the Unified Patent Court have ruled that information shared without obtaining protection under Rule 262.2 of the Rules of Procedure loses its confidential character. This decision arises from a dispute between EOflow and rival insulin-delivery device maker Insulet. The court emphasized that parties must proactively seek procedural safeguards to maintain secrecy. By failing to apply for a confidentiality order, EOflow exposed sensitive data, thereby forfeiting trade secret protection. This judgment establishes strict procedural compliance standards, highlighting that mere assertion of confidentiality is insufficient. Parties must actively utilize available legal mechanisms to secure their proprietary information during litigation, or risk irreversible disclosure and loss of legal remedies.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在参与 UPC 诉讼时，务必重视程序规则的主动适用。涉及技术秘密交换时，应第一时间依据规则 262.2 申请保密令，避免因程序疏漏导致核心数据公开，造成不可逆的商业损失。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling highlights critical procedural risks in UPC litigation. Parties must conduct upfront assessments to identify sensitive technical data requiring protection. It is essential to immediately apply for confidentiality orders under Rule 262.2 before exchanging any such information. Procedural compliance is as vital as substantive legal arguments; neglecting these steps can lead to irreversible commercial damage through public disclosure. Legal teams should integrate strict confidentiality protocols into their litigation strategy from the outset to prevent inadvertent waiver of trade secret rights.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 裁定未依规则 262.2 申请保密令将导致信息丧失机密性", "EOflow 因程序疏忽在与 Insulet 纠纷中失去商业秘密保护", "判决强调当事人须主动利用程序规则维护敏感信息安全"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UPC judges ruled that sharing information without securing Rule 262.2 protection causes it to lose its confidential legal status.", "EOflow lost trade secret protection in its dispute with Insulet due to procedural negligence in failing to request a confidentiality order.", "The judgment underscores the mandatory obligation for parties to actively use procedural rules to safeguard sensitive information."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["涉欧诉讼需前置评估并立即申请 UPC 保密令以锁定机密性", "程序合规与实体抗辩同等重要，疏忽将致商业秘密裸奔"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Litigants must prioritize proactive procedural measures to preserve trade secret confidentiality during UPC proceedings.", "Practice note: Secure Rule 262.2 confidentiality orders immediately before sharing any sensitive technical or business information.", "What to monitor: Strict adherence to procedural compliance is required to avoid waiving protection and exposing core data to public view."]
core_points_en: UPC judges ruled that sharing information without securing Rule 262.2 protection causes it to lose its confidential legal status.
EOflow lost trade secret protection in its dispute with Insulet due to procedural negligence in failing to request a confidentiality order.
The judgment underscores the mandatory obligation for parties to actively use procedural rules to safeguard sensitive information.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Litigants must prioritize proactive procedural measures to preserve trade secret confidentiality during UPC proceedings.
Practice note: Secure Rule 262.2 confidentiality orders immediately before sharing any sensitive technical or business information.
What to monitor: Strict adherence to procedural compliance is required to avoid waiving protection and exposing core data to public view.

items.title: BREAKING: Geely sued by IP Bridge in Brazil as Avanci licensors seek next Chinese licensee
items.title_zh: 突发：吉利在巴西遭 IP Bridge 起诉，Avanci 寻求下一家中国被许可人
items.title_en: IP Bridge Sues Geely in Brazil as Avanci Licensors Target Next Chinese Licensee
items.summary: At least four Chinese carmakers have taken a pool licence to date, and several bilateral deals have also been signed
items.ai_summary_zh: 日本 IP Bridge 代表 Avanci 专利池许可方在巴西起诉吉利汽车，指控其未获得标准必要专利许可。此前已有至少四家中国车企签署池许可或多边协议。此案标志着专利主张实体将诉讼战场扩展至南美，旨在迫使剩余中国主流车企接受全球许可条款，加剧了跨国知识产权博弈。
items.ai_summary_en: IP Bridge, representing Avanci patent pool licensors, has initiated legal proceedings against Geely in Brazil. The complaint alleges that the automotive manufacturer failed to secure licenses for standard-essential patents. This action follows agreements reached with at least four other Chinese carmakers through pool licensing or bilateral deals. The case signals a strategic expansion of patent enforcement into South America by non-practicing entities. The objective is to compel remaining major Chinese manufacturers to accept global licensing terms. This development intensifies cross-border intellectual property disputes and highlights the increasing pressure on automotive companies operating internationally to resolve SEP licensing obligations comprehensively.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国车企出海需警惕非欧盟法域的 SEP 诉讼风险，避免单一市场应对。建议提前评估全球专利组合，主动参与 FRAND 谈判或构建防御性专利联盟，防止被各个击破。
items.ai_insight_en: Automotive companies expanding globally must assess standard-essential patent risks beyond traditional European and US markets. Relying solely on local defenses is insufficient against coordinated multi-jurisdictional enforcement strategies. Rights holders are increasingly targeting emerging markets to create leverage for global settlements. Companies should proactively evaluate their FRAND compliance status and consider defensive patent alliances. Early engagement in negotiations prevents fragmented litigation exposure. A unified global IP response mechanism is essential to mitigate the risk of injunctions or excessive damages across different legal systems.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["IP Bridge 在巴西发起诉讼，意在施压吉利达成全球专利许可协议", "Avanci 专利池已获多家中国车企授权，但未覆盖所有主要制造商", "诉讼地选择巴西显示权利人多辖区围堵策略，增加企业应诉成本"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["IP Bridge filed suit in Brazil to pressure Geely into concluding a comprehensive global patent licensing agreement.", "While several Chinese automakers joined the Avanci pool, key manufacturers remain unlicensed, prompting further enforcement actions.", "Selecting Brazil as the litigation venue demonstrates a multi-jurisdictional strategy designed to increase defendants' compliance costs."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕权利人多法域连环诉讼，建立全球统一的专利应对机制", "主动评估 FRAND 合规性，避免因消极应对导致高额赔偿或禁售"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Automotive exporters face escalating SEP litigation risks in non-traditional jurisdictions like Brazil, requiring holistic global licensing strategies rather tha…", "Practice note: Establish a centralized patent defense framework to manage multi-jurisdictional claims efficiently, ensuring consistent FRAND negotiation positions and reducing cum…", "What to monitor: Track enforcement trends by NPEs in South American and Asian markets, as these regions are becoming critical battlegrounds for securing global licensing commitmen…"]

While several Chinese automakers joined the Avanci pool, key manufacturers remain unlicensed, prompting further enforcement actions.
Selecting Brazil as the litigation venue demonstrates a multi-jurisdictional strategy designed to increase defendants' compliance costs.

items.title: WIPO launches initiative to evolve IP infrastructure in age of AI
items.title_zh: WIPO 启动倡议以演进人工智能时代的知识产权基础设施
items.title_en: WIPO Launches Initiative to Evolve IP Infrastructure in the Age of Artificial Intelligence
items.summary: Rights holders, AI developers, technical experts and other stakeholders will come together within the Artificial Intelligence Infrastructure Interchange.
items.ai_summary_zh: 世界知识产权组织（WIPO）正式推出“人工智能基础设施交换”倡议，旨在汇聚权利人、AI 开发者及技术专家等多方利益相关者。该举措致力于在人工智能快速发展的背景下，共同探索并优化全球知识产权基础设施的演进路径，以应对新技术带来的挑战与机遇，确保 IP 体系适应数字化时代需求。
items.ai_summary_en: The World Intellectual Property Organization has officially launched the Artificial Intelligence Infrastructure Interchange initiative. This platform is designed to convene rights holders, AI developers, technical experts, and other key stakeholders. The primary objective is to collaboratively explore and optimize the evolution of global intellectual property infrastructure amidst rapid artificial intelligence advancements. By bringing together diverse perspectives, the initiative aims to address both the challenges and opportunities presented by new technologies. It seeks to ensure that existing IP systems adapt effectively to the demands of the digital era, fostering a more resilient and responsive framework for innovation protection and management in an increasingly automated landscape.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国企业需密切关注此倡议对数据训练及算法专利审查标准的影响。建议提前布局合规策略，积极参与国际规则对话，利用新机制保护自身创新成果，规避潜在侵权风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Organizations operating globally should closely monitor how this initiative influences data training protocols and patent examination standards for algorithms. Proactive engagement in international rule-making dialogues is recommended to safeguard overseas interests and ensure compliance with emerging norms. Companies are advised to leverage these new mechanisms to protect their own innovative outputs while mitigating potential infringement risks. Staying informed about infrastructure changes will help legal teams adjust strategies accordingly, ensuring that intellectual property assets remain secure and enforceable as regulatory frameworks evolve to accommodate artificial intelligence developments.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["WIPO 建立多方协作平台以应对 AI 技术冲击", "聚焦知识产权基础设施在智能时代的演进策略", "汇集权利人与开发者共同构建适应性 IP 生态"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["WIPO establishes a multi-stakeholder collaborative platform to address the disruptive impact of artificial intelligence technologies on existing systems.", "The initiative focuses on strategic evolution of intellectual property infrastructure to meet the specific requirements of the intelligent technology era.", "It brings together rights holders and developers to jointly construct an adaptive ecosystem capable of supporting future digital innovations."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注 AI 基础设施变革对专利审查标准影响", "主动参与国际规则制定以维护企业海外权益"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor shifts in patent examination standards for AI-driven inventions and data processing methods resulting from infrastructure reforms.", "Practice note: Actively participate in international policy discussions to influence rule development and protect corporate intellectual property rights abroad.", "What to monitor: Changes in global IP infrastructure that may affect data usage rights, algorithmic transparency, and cross-border enforcement mechanisms."]
core_points_en: WIPO establishes a multi-stakeholder collaborative platform to address the disruptive impact of artificial intelligence technologies on existing systems.
The initiative focuses on strategic evolution of intellectual property infrastructure to meet the specific requirements of the intelligent technology era.
It brings together rights holders and developers to jointly construct an adaptive ecosystem capable of supporting future digital innovations.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor shifts in patent examination standards for AI-driven inventions and data processing methods resulting from infrastructure reforms.
Practice note: Actively participate in international policy discussions to influence rule development and protect corporate intellectual property rights abroad.
What to monitor: Changes in global IP infrastructure that may affect data usage rights, algorithmic transparency, and cross-border enforcement mechanisms.
