items.title: LD The Hague, Infringement Action, February 24, 2026, UPC_CFI_619/2025
items.title_zh: UPC 海牙分院判决：广泛禁令覆盖未来变体及辅助请求可行性
items.title_en: UPC Hague Ruling: Broad Injunctions Cover Future Variants and Auxiliary Request Feasibility
items.summary: Following the CoA's Abbott/Sibio ruling (UPC_CoA_328/2024), a claimant requesting general injunctive relief covering patent claims...
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC 海牙地方法院在 GSK 诉 Mode 案中作出重要裁定。依据上诉法院先例，确认广泛措辞的禁令请求可覆盖未来产品变体而无需修改诉请；若 EMA 批准晚于起诉，允许增加新产品为被告。法院质疑 14 个月内审理大量辅助请求的可行性，但未设硬性上限，要求原告提供表格概览。同时认定前端加载程序下，原告回复中提交新证据不属逾期，保障听审权。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Local Division in The Hague issued a significant ruling in the GSK v. Mode case, aligning with the Court of Appeal’s Abbott/Sibio precedent. The court confirmed that broadly drafted requests for general injunctive relief can encompass future product variants without requiring claim amendments. Furthermore, adding a new product to the infringement action is admissible if EMA approval occurs after the Statement of Claim is filed, as parties are not obligated to anticipate regulatory timelines in other jurisdictions like the US. While the court expressed serious concerns regarding the feasibility of handling numerous auxiliary requests within the strict fourteen-month procedural timeline, it declined to impose a hard numerical cap. Instead, the claimant was ordered to provide a comprehensive tabular overview clarifying features and invalidity attacks. The decision also affirmed that submitting new evidence in reply does not violate front-loaded proceedings, thereby safeguarding the right to be heard.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企出口欧洲需警惕 UPC 广泛禁令风险，提前布局专利规避设计。面对海量辅助请求，应强化技术比对与无效抗辩准备，利用程序规则争取回应时间，避免被动应对突发变体侵权指控。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment clarifies strategic boundaries for patent enforcement and defense at the UPC. Rights holders can leverage broad injunctive language to capture evolving product lines, reducing the need for iterative claim amendments when regulatory approvals shift during litigation. For defendants, the ruling highlights the importance of robust technical comparison and invalidity preparations early in proceedings. While courts remain wary of excessive auxiliary requests, they prioritize substantive fairness over rigid numerical limits. Parties should focus on structured presentations of arguments to navigate the expedited fourteen-month timeline effectively, ensuring that procedural efficiency does not compromise the right to present a full defense against alleged infringements involving variant products.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["广泛禁令请求可涵盖未来产品变体，无需修正诉请", "EMA 批准后新增产品入案合法，不强制预判美欧审批差异", "大量辅助请求虽受质疑但未设上限，需提交清晰特征对照表"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Broadly worded injunction requests cover future product variants without necessitating formal claim amendments under Rule 263 RoP.", "Adding products approved by the EMA post-filing is admissible, as prior FDA approval does not mandate anticipating European market entry.", "High volumes of auxiliary requests face feasibility scrutiny but lack a strict cap, requiring clear tabular overviews of features and defenses."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["预警广泛禁令覆盖未来变体，需前置专利自由实施分析", "应对海量辅助请求，应建立快速技术比对与无效证据库"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Broad injunctions may capture future product iterations, necessitating proactive freedom-to-operate analyses for evolving portfolios.", "Practice note: Prepare detailed tabular overviews for auxiliary requests to address judicial concerns about procedural feasibility and clarity.", "What to monitor: How courts balance the volume of auxiliary requests against the mandatory fourteen-month decision deadline in future cases."]
core_points_en: Broadly worded injunction requests cover future product variants without necessitating formal claim amendments under Rule 263 RoP.
Adding products approved by the EMA post-filing is admissible, as prior FDA approval does not mandate anticipating European market entry.
High volumes of auxiliary requests face feasibility scrutiny but lack a strict cap, requiring clear tabular overviews of features and defenses.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Broad injunctions may capture future product iterations, necessitating proactive freedom-to-operate analyses for evolving portfolios.
Practice note: Prepare detailed tabular overviews for auxiliary requests to address judicial concerns about procedural feasibility and clarity.
What to monitor: How courts balance the volume of auxiliary requests against the mandatory fourteen-month decision deadline in future cases.

items.title: EPO likely to continue to require adaptation of description: preliminary opinion in G 1/25
items.title_zh: EPO 或将继续要求调整说明书：G 1/25 案初步意见
items.title_en: EPO Likely to Maintain Description Adaptation Requirement: Preliminary Opinion in G 1/25
items.summary: The preliminary opinion of the Enlarged Board in G 1/25 can be found
items.ai_summary_zh: EPO 扩大上诉委员会在 G 1/25 案中发布初步意见，倾向于维持现行实践，即在审查和异议程序中要求申请人调整说明书以匹配权利要求。委员会指出 T 56/21 号决定中关于“支持”一词普通含义的解释与 G 1/24 号决定不一致，强调权利要求清晰性及法律确定性优于保留未主张主题的描述，尽管尚未详细阐述具体理由。
items.ai_summary_en: The Enlarged Board of Appeal has issued a preliminary opinion in case G 1/25, indicating it will likely uphold the European Patent Office’s current practice. This practice mandates that applicants adapt the patent description to align with the claims during both examination and opposition proceedings. The Board suggests that the interpretation of Article 84 EPC in decision T 56/21, which argued against such adaptation, conflicts with the earlier ruling in G 1/24. While detailed reasoning is not yet fully elaborated, the Board emphasizes that claim clarity and legal certainty are paramount. Consequently, retaining unclaimed subject matter in the description is viewed as inconsistent with established EPO jurisprudence, reinforcing the requirement for strict alignment between the description and the scope of protection sought.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧申请专利时，需预判说明书将被严格审查并可能被迫删减未主张内容。建议在撰写阶段即确保说明书与权利要求高度一致，避免因后续调整导致技术方案披露不充分或影响侵权判定范围。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should anticipate rigorous scrutiny of the description during EPO proceedings, with a high probability of being required to delete unclaimed embodiments. To mitigate risks, drafting strategies must ensure tight correspondence between the specification and the intended claim scope from the outset. Failure to align these elements may lead to forced amendments that could inadvertently narrow technical disclosures or create ambiguities relevant to post-grant infringement assessments. Stakeholders should carefully evaluate how mandatory description adaptations might impact the evidentiary value of the specification in national courts, particularly concerning the interpretation of claim terms and the determination of the protected technical contribution.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["扩大上诉委员会拟维持 EPO 要求调整说明书的现行做法", "认定 T 56/21 号决定对第 84 条的解释与 G 1/24 号决定相冲突", "强调权利要求的清晰界定比说明书保留未主张内容更重要"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Enlarged Board intends to maintain the EPO's existing requirement for adapting descriptions to match claims in prosecution and opposition.", "The Board finds that T 56/21's interpretation of Article 84 EPC regarding description support is inconsistent with the precedent set by G 1/24.", "Clarity of claims and legal certainty are prioritized over preserving unclaimed subject matter within the patent description."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["撰写时应确保说明书内容与权利要求范围严格对应", "需评估说明书调整对后续侵权诉讼及无效程序的潜在风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure strict alignment between the description and claim scope during initial drafting to avoid forced deletions of unclaimed content later.", "Practice note: Assess potential risks where mandatory description adaptations might reduce technical disclosure sufficiency or affect future infringement determinations.", "What to monitor: Watch for the final reasoned decision in G 1/25 to understand the full scope of the obligation to adapt descriptions under Article 84 EPC."]
core_points_en: The Enlarged Board intends to maintain the EPO's existing requirement for adapting descriptions to match claims in prosecution and opposition.
The Board finds that T 56/21's interpretation of Article 84 EPC regarding description support is inconsistent with the precedent set by G 1/24.
Clarity of claims and legal certainty are prioritized over preserving unclaimed subject matter within the patent description.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure strict alignment between the description and claim scope during initial drafting to avoid forced deletions of unclaimed content later.
Practice note: Assess potential risks where mandatory description adaptations might reduce technical disclosure sufficiency or affect future infringement determinations.
What to monitor: Watch for the final reasoned decision in G 1/25 to understand the full scope of the obligation to adapt descriptions under Article 84 EPC.

items.title: Fake toner, real trouble: protect your printers from counterfeits
items.title_zh: UKIPO警示：假冒碳粉危害大，需强化防伪保护
items.title_en: UKIPO Warns of Risks from Counterfeit Ink and Toner Cartridges
items.summary: Counterfeit ink and toner cartridges can be poor quality and can cause serious problems, especially for small businesses.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局（UKIPO）发布博客文章，警示假冒墨盒和碳粉对中小企业造成的严重损害。文章指出全球假冒贸易规模巨大且常关联有组织犯罪，电商平台第三方卖家易成售假渠道。UKIPO联合ICCE等机构开展执法与情报协调，强调造假者不承担赔偿责任。建议企业建立严格采购体系，通过比对正品特征识别假货，以规避法律与经济损失风险。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office highlights the significant risks posed by counterfeit ink and toner cartridges, particularly for small businesses. Citing an OECD/EUIPO report estimating global counterfeiting trade at USD 467 billion, the agency notes strong links to organized crime. The blog emphasizes that online marketplaces facilitate the sale of fake goods by third-party sellers, often at lower prices. Unlike genuine manufacturers, counterfeiters do not accept liability for damage caused to printers or other equipment. The IPO collaborates with industry groups like the Imaging Consumables Coalition of Europe (ICCE) to coordinate enforcement and raise awareness about identifying fake products through quality checks.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国打印耗材出口企业应警惕被卷入假冒供应链指控。需建立严格的知识产权合规与溯源体系，主动配合品牌方打假。同时，进口商应加强供应商尽职调查，避免采购来源不明的低价耗材，以防因销售假冒产品面临UKIPO执法行动及民事索赔风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This update underscores the operational and financial exposure businesses face when procuring unverified printing supplies. For in-house counsel and procurement teams, the absence of liability coverage from counterfeiters represents a distinct risk profile compared to standard supply chain disputes. It reinforces the need for strict vendor due diligence and authentication protocols within corporate purchasing policies to mitigate hardware damage and potential association with illicit trade networks.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UKIPO警示假冒耗材致损严重，造假者免责且不赔偿", "全球假冒贸易达4670亿美元，常关联有组织犯罪网络", "建议企业完善采购系统，比对正品特征以识别假冒商品"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Counterfeit print consumables cause serious operational issues and lack manufacturer liability for resulting damages.", "Global counterfeit trade is valued at approximately USD 467 billion with established links to organized crime networks.", "Online third-party sellers frequently offer counterfeit goods, requiring robust procurement systems to prevent infiltration."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["出口企业需建立合规溯源体系，防范假冒供应链指控", "进口商应加强供应商尽调，避免采购不明来源低价耗材"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Businesses should review procurement contracts to ensure supplier warranties cover equipment damage from defective consumables.", "Practice note: Implement visual and functional verification steps for high-volume consumable purchases to distinguish genuine from fake items.", "What to monitor: Ongoing collaboration between IP offices and industry coalitions regarding enforcement actions against online marketplace sellers."]
core_points_en: Counterfeit print consumables cause serious operational issues and lack manufacturer liability for resulting damages.
Global counterfeit trade is valued at approximately USD 467 billion with established links to organized crime networks.
Online third-party sellers frequently offer counterfeit goods, requiring robust procurement systems to prevent infiltration.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Businesses should review procurement contracts to ensure supplier warranties cover equipment damage from defective consumables.
Practice note: Implement visual and functional verification steps for high-volume consumable purchases to distinguish genuine from fake items.
What to monitor: Ongoing collaboration between IP offices and industry coalitions regarding enforcement actions against online marketplace sellers.

items.title: A revolution in Patents on Software Developments? UK Supreme Court Aligns with Europe
items.title_zh: 软件专利革命？英国最高法院判决与欧洲接轨
items.title_en: UK Supreme Court Abolishes Aerotel Test, Aligning Software Patentability with European Practice
items.summary: Recently, the world of intellectual property has witnessed one of the most momentous decisions of the last decade in the United Kingdom. The Supreme Court’s judgment in the case of Emotional Perception AI Limited v. Comptroller General of Patents has marked the end of an era, abandoning the Aerotel test for the patentability of computer-implemented ... Read more La entrada A revolution in Patents on Software Developments? UK Supreme Court Aligns with Europe se publicó primero en PONS IP .
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国最高法院在 Emotional Perception AI 诉专利局长案中作出里程碑式判决，正式废除长期适用的 Aerotel 测试标准。该裁决标志着英国在计算机实施发明（含 AI）的可专利性审查上结束旧时代，转而寻求与欧洲专利局实践保持一致，为软件及人工智能技术的专利保护开辟新路径。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Supreme Court has delivered a landmark judgment in Emotional Perception AI Limited v. Comptroller General of Patents, formally abandoning the long-standing Aerotel test for assessing the patentability of computer-implemented inventions. This decision marks a significant shift in UK intellectual property law, ending an era of divergent national practice. The ruling explicitly seeks to harmonize UK examination standards with those of the European Patent Office, particularly regarding software and artificial intelligence technologies. By aligning with established EPO case law, the judgment provides greater legal certainty and opens new pathways for securing patent protection for AI-driven innovations and software developments within the United Kingdom.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在英布局 AI 及软件专利时，需关注审查标准变化带来的授权机遇。建议重新评估被拒申请，利用新标准争取权利；同时注意英欧标准趋同后的统一应对策略，优化在欧洲市场的知识产权资产配置。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should reassess previously refused software or AI patent applications in light of the new eligibility standards, as the alignment with EPO practice may offer renewed opportunities for grant. It is crucial to monitor how the UKIPO implements these changes to ensure consistent application across cases. Furthermore, companies operating in both jurisdictions should develop unified strategies for their European IP portfolios, leveraging the convergence of UK and European standards to optimize asset allocation and streamline prosecution efforts across the market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国最高法院废除 Aerotel 测试，重塑软件专利审查标准", "判决旨在使英国专利实践与欧洲整体标准重新趋于一致", "此案为人工智能及计算机实施发明的可专利性确立新方向"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UK Supreme Court abolishes the Aerotel test, fundamentally reshaping the criteria for software patent eligibility.", "The judgment aims to realign UK patent examination practices with broader European standards and EPO jurisprudence.", "This ruling establishes a new direction for determining the patentability of AI and computer-implemented inventions."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用新标准重新评估在英国被驳回的软件或 AI 专利申请", "关注英欧标准趋同，制定统一的欧洲市场知识产权策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Reassess previously refused UK software or AI patent applications under the new eligibility standards to identify potential grant opportunities.", "Practice note: Develop unified European IP strategies that leverage the convergence of UK and EPO standards for streamlined portfolio management.", "What to monitor: Track UKIPO implementation guidelines to ensure consistent application of the new software patentability criteria."]
core_points_en: The UK Supreme Court abolishes the Aerotel test, fundamentally reshaping the criteria for software patent eligibility.
The judgment aims to realign UK patent examination practices with broader European standards and EPO jurisprudence.
This ruling establishes a new direction for determining the patentability of AI and computer-implemented inventions.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Reassess previously refused UK software or AI patent applications under the new eligibility standards to identify potential grant opportunities.
Practice note: Develop unified European IP strategies that leverage the convergence of UK and EPO standards for streamlined portfolio management.
What to monitor: Track UKIPO implementation guidelines to ensure consistent application of the new software patentability criteria.

items.title: A twist too functional: General Court rejects hand-grip corkscrew trade mark
items.title_zh: 握把过于功能化：普通法院驳回手形开瓶器商标
items.title_en: General Court Rejects Hand-Grip Corkscrew Mark as Purely Functional Shape
items.summary: Can a corkscrew handle shaped like a human hand grip be registered as an EU trade mark, or is such a form purely functional? The General Court's judgment in Empreinte v EUIPO (T-437/25) offers a cautionary tale for designers hoping to protect innovative but technically driven product shapes.   Background In March 2024 the French company Empreinte filed an application for registration of the
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国公司 Empreinte 申请注册手形握把开瓶器为欧盟商标被拒。欧盟普通法院在 T-437/25 号案件中维持原判，认定该形状主要由技术功能决定，缺乏显著性且具功能性特征。此案警示设计师，纯技术驱动的产品形状难以获得商标保护，即便其具有创新性。
items.ai_summary_en: The EU General Court has upheld the refusal to register a corkscrew handle shaped like a human hand grip as an EU trade mark. In case T-437/25, Empreinte v EUIPO, the Court determined that the contested shape is dictated exclusively by the technical function of gripping and twisting. Consequently, the form lacks inherent distinctiveness and falls under the absolute ground for refusal concerning functional shapes. This judgment reinforces the principle that product features necessary to achieve a technical result cannot be monopolized through trade mark law. The decision serves as a significant reminder for designers and rights holders that innovative but technically driven product configurations face high barriers to trade mark registration in the EU, regardless of their visual appeal or market recognition.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧申请产品形状商标时，应避免纯粹功能性设计。建议结合非功能美学元素提升显著性，或转而寻求外观设计专利保护，以规避因功能性被驳回的风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders seeking to protect product shapes in the EU should carefully assess whether design elements are purely functional or possess sufficient aesthetic independence. Relying solely on trade mark registration for technically driven forms carries a high risk of refusal based on functionality grounds. To mitigate this, applicants should consider integrating non-functional decorative features to enhance distinctiveness. Alternatively, pursuing registered Community design protection may offer a more robust and appropriate legal framework for safeguarding innovative product appearances. Strategic portfolio planning should align the type of IP right with the specific nature of the product feature to ensure effective enforcement and avoid costly litigation over invalid registrations.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["普通法院认定手形握把形状主要受技术功能支配", "purely functional 形状无法注册为欧盟商标", "设计师需注意技术创新与商标保护的界限"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The General Court ruled the hand-grip shape is primarily dictated by technical functions required for operating a corkscrew.", "Purely functional shapes are excluded from EU trade mark registration to prevent unfair competition and monopoly over technical solutions.", "Designers must distinguish between technical innovation suitable for patent protection and distinctive signs eligible for trade marks."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["避免纯功能性形状申请商标，以防被驳回", "结合美学设计或转申外观专利以强化保护"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid filing trade mark applications for shapes where every element serves a technical purpose to prevent rejection.", "Practice note: Combine functional designs with distinctive aesthetic elements or seek registered design protection instead.", "What to monitor: CJEU interpretations of 'substantial value' and 'technical result' exclusions in future shape mark disputes."]
core_points_en: The General Court ruled the hand-grip shape is primarily dictated by technical functions required for operating a corkscrew.
Purely functional shapes are excluded from EU trade mark registration to prevent unfair competition and monopoly over technical solutions.
Designers must distinguish between technical innovation suitable for patent protection and distinctive signs eligible for trade marks.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid filing trade mark applications for shapes where every element serves a technical purpose to prevent rejection.
Practice note: Combine functional designs with distinctive aesthetic elements or seek registered design protection instead.
What to monitor: CJEU interpretations of 'substantial value' and 'technical result' exclusions in future shape mark disputes.

items.title: Judgment of the Court in Case C-84/24 EM System
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院：受制裁人控股公司资产可被冻结
items.title_en: CJEU Rules Assets of Non-Listed Companies Controlled by Sanctioned Persons May Be Frozen
items.summary: The assets of a company not included on the list of persons subject to sanctions may be frozen if it is controlled by a person included on that list
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院就 C-84/24 案作出判决，裁定即便公司未列入制裁名单，若其由受制裁自然人控制（如持股 50%），其资产亦可被冻结。法院强调“控制”概念需广义解释，涵盖直接与间接影响，并确立 50% 持股即推定控制的规则，以确保制裁措施的有效性与突发性，防止规避行为。
items.ai_summary_en: In Case C-84/24 EM System, the Court of Justice of the European Union ruled that the assets of a company not explicitly listed in EU sanctions may still be frozen if it is controlled by a designated person. The case originated when Lithuanian banks froze the funds of EM SYSTEM because a sanctioned Belarusian national held a 50% shareholding. The Court affirmed that Regulation No 765/2006 covers funds owned, held, or controlled by listed persons to prevent sanction circumvention. It established a legal presumption that 50% shareholding constitutes control. Furthermore, the concepts of 'held' and 'controlled' must be interpreted broadly to include both direct and indirect influence over economic resources. This interpretation ensures the effectiveness, speed, and surprise element of restrictive measures, requiring clear criteria for internal decision-making structures to identify controlled entities accurately.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中企需严格审查股东结构，避免因关联受制裁主体导致资产被冻。建议建立实时合规筛查机制，评估间接持股风险，确保不被推定为受控实体，保障资金链安全。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating within the EU jurisdiction must conduct rigorous due diligence on shareholder structures to assess exposure to sanctions risks. Even if an entity is not directly named on a sanctions list, indirect ownership or control by a designated individual can trigger asset freezes under current CJEU interpretations. Companies should implement dynamic compliance monitoring systems to evaluate indirect shareholding risks continuously. Establishing robust internal controls to demonstrate independence from sanctioned parties is critical. Failure to address these presumptions of control may result in severe financial disruptions and legal challenges regarding the unfreezing of assets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["未列名公司若由受制裁人控制，其资产同样适用冻结措施", "持有公司 50% 股份即被法律推定为拥有控制权及资源支配力", "控制概念须广义解释，包含直接及间接影响资金使用的各种方式"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Assets of non-listed companies are subject to freezing if controlled by a person included on the EU sanctions list.", "A 50% shareholding in a company creates a legal presumption of control over its funds and economic resources.", "The concept of control requires broad interpretation, encompassing both direct and indirect influence on resource usage."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["严查股权穿透，避免因关联方受制裁而触发资产冻结风险", "建立动态合规监测，防范因持股比例达标被推定受控的后果"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Entities must verify that no sanctioned individuals hold significant stakes or exert control through indirect channels.", "Practice note: Implement real-time screening mechanisms to detect changes in ownership that might trigger control presumptions under EU law.", "What to monitor: Judicial developments regarding the definition of 'control' and the scope of indirect influence in sanctions enforcement."]
core_points_en: Assets of non-listed companies are subject to freezing if controlled by a person included on the EU sanctions list.
A 50% shareholding in a company creates a legal presumption of control over its funds and economic resources.
The concept of control requires broad interpretation, encompassing both direct and indirect influence on resource usage.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Entities must verify that no sanctioned individuals hold significant stakes or exert control through indirect channels.
Practice note: Implement real-time screening mechanisms to detect changes in ownership that might trigger control presumptions under EU law.
What to monitor: Judicial developments regarding the definition of 'control' and the scope of indirect influence in sanctions enforcement.

items.title: Dispute between ParTec and Nvidia threatens to escalate after dismissal
items.title_zh: ParTec 诉英伟达案被驳回后争端或升级
items.title_en: UPC Dismisses ParTec’s Infringement Claim Against Nvidia, Escalation Risks Rise
items.summary: Today’s decision concerns one of three lawsuits filed by ParTec against the US chip giant at the Unified Patent Court. ParTec is backed by former IPCom founder Bernhard Frohwitter. ParTec claims infringement of EP 3 743 812 and sought an injunction requiring Nvidia to refrain from distributing essential products of its GPU product portfolio in […] The post Dispute between ParTec and Nvidia threatens to escalate after dismissal appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）驳回了 ParTec 针对英伟达提起的三项诉讼之一。ParTec 由前 IPCom 创始人支持，指控英伟达 GPU 产品组合侵犯其 EP3743812 号专利并寻求禁令。此次败诉可能导致双方争端进一步升级，凸显 UPC 在芯片领域专利执法中的关键角色及诉讼风险。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court has dismissed one of three infringement lawsuits filed by ParTec against US chipmaker Nvidia. Backed by former IPCom founder Bernhard Frohwitter, ParTec alleged that Nvidia violated European patent EP 3 743 812 and sought an injunction to halt the distribution of key products within Nvidia’s GPU portfolio. Although this specific claim was rejected, the outcome signals potential for further legal escalation between the parties. This development underscores the critical role of the UPC in enforcing patent rights within the semiconductor sector. It also highlights the significant litigation risks facing major technology companies operating in Europe, where patent enforcement actions can directly impact core product distributions and market access strategies.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国芯片及硬件出口企业应高度关注 UPC 判例走向，评估自身产品在欧洲的侵权风险。建议在进入欧洲市场前进行严格的 FTO 分析，并针对高价值专利提前布局防御性策略或许可谈判，避免陷入高额禁令风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants in the semiconductor and hardware sectors should closely monitor evolving UPC case law to assess potential infringement exposures across European jurisdictions. Conducting rigorous freedom-to-operate analyses prior to market entry is essential to mitigate legal uncertainties. Companies holding high-value patents or facing enforcement actions should proactively develop defensive patent portfolios and prepare licensing negotiation frameworks. These measures help avoid costly injunctions and ensure business continuity. Strategic preparation allows firms to navigate complex cross-border IP disputes effectively while maintaining stable operations in the European market.

items.title: Qualcomm and Bardehle win semiconductor dispute with NST
items.title_zh: 高通与 Bardehle 赢得与 NST 的半导体专利纠纷
items.title_en: Qualcomm and Bardehle Prevail in Semiconductor Patent Dispute Against NST
items.summary: The judgments relate to three patents. In March 2024, US company Network System Technologies, which focuses on licensing, sued Qualcomm for infringement of EP 1 552 669 as well as EP 1 875 683 and EP 1 552 399. All patents relate to chip technology and cover methods for the transmission of signals in semiconductors. […] The post Qualcomm and Bardehle win semiconductor dispute with NST appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 美国许可公司 NST 于 2024 年 3 月起诉高通，指控其侵犯三项涉及半导体信号传输方法的欧洲专利。最新判决显示，高通及其代理律所 Bardehle 在这场关于芯片技术的专利侵权纠纷中胜诉，成功驳回了 NST 的侵权主张。
items.ai_summary_en: In March 2024, US-based licensing entity Network System Technologies (NST) initiated infringement proceedings against Qualcomm regarding three European patents: EP 1 552 669, EP 1 875 683, and EP 1 552 399. These patents specifically cover methods for signal transmission within semiconductor chip technologies. Recent judicial decisions have concluded that Qualcomm, represented by the law firm Bardehle, successfully defended against these claims. The court rulings effectively dismissed NST’s allegations of patent infringement, marking a significant victory for Qualcomm in this high-stakes dispute concerning advanced chip technology and intellectual property rights enforcement in Europe.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国半导体企业应关注此类 NPE 诉讼动态，强化自有专利储备。在欧出口时需提前进行 FTO 分析，防范类似许可实体发起的侵权狙击，必要时寻求专业律所支持。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should remain vigilant regarding non-practicing entities leveraging European patents to initiate infringement actions. Proactive freedom-to-operate analyses are essential before exporting products to Europe to mitigate risks associated with aggressive licensing demands. Strengthening internal patent portfolios provides strategic leverage against such entities. Companies must integrate robust IP risk management into their global expansion strategies, ensuring comprehensive due diligence is conducted to prevent potential litigation traps posed by specialized licensing firms targeting specific technology sectors.

items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor NPE litigation trends to anticipate potential infringement risks in European markets.", "Practice note: Conduct thorough freedom-to-operate investigations and strengthen patent layouts prior to market entry.", "What to monitor: Track enforcement activities by licensing-focused entities targeting semiconductor technologies."]

Practice note: Conduct thorough freedom-to-operate investigations and strengthen patent layouts prior to market entry.
What to monitor: Track enforcement activities by licensing-focused entities targeting semiconductor technologies.

items.title: Action en nullité d’une marque de l’Union : l’arrêt GEOGRAPHICAL NORWAY EXPEDITION du 11 mars 2026
items.title_zh: 欧盟商标无效诉讼：地理挪威探险案判决
items.title_en: EU General Court Ruling on GEOGRAPHICAL NORWAY EXPEDITION: Methodological Approach to Bad Faith and Invalidity
items.summary: Un contentieux emblématique Le 11 mars 2026, le Tribunal de l’Union européenne a rendu un arrêt méthodologiquement remarquable dans le cadre d’un long contentieux opposant une société française et une société italienne autour de la marque GEOGRAPHICAL NORWAY EXPEDITION. Cette décision s’inscrit dans une série de procédures devant l’EUIPO et le Tribunal, illustrant la complexité… Lire la suite Action en nullité d’une marque de l’Union : l’arrêt GEOGRAPHICAL NORWAY EXPEDITION du 11 mars 2026
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026 年 3 月 11 日，欧盟普通法院就法国与意大利公司间关于'GEOGRAPHICAL NORWAY EXPEDITION'商标的长期纠纷作出判决。该案历经 EUIPO 及法院多轮程序，法院在判决中展示了严谨的方法论，针对欧盟商标无效申请中的关键争议点进行了裁决，凸显了此类跨国商标确权案件的复杂性。
items.ai_summary_en: On March 11, 2026, the General Court of the European Union delivered a significant judgment in a long-standing dispute between French and Italian companies concerning the EU trademark 'GEOGRAPHICAL NORWAY EXPEDITION'. This decision is part of an extensive series of proceedings before the EUIPO and the Courts, highlighting the procedural and substantive complexities inherent in cross-border trademark litigation. The Court’s ruling provides a methodologically rigorous analysis of key legal issues surrounding invalidity actions, particularly regarding bad faith and distinctiveness. It serves as a critical reference point for understanding how EU institutions adjudicate conflicts involving geographical terms and establishes important precedents for future challenges to registered trademarks based on similar grounds.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局含地理名称商标时，需警惕因缺乏显著性或误导公众被提无效。应提前评估地名使用风险，避免陷入类似法意企业的长期诉讼泥潭，确保品牌稳定性。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders seeking protection for marks containing geographical indications must conduct thorough due diligence regarding distinctiveness and potential misleading effects. Applications relying heavily on place names face heightened scrutiny for bad faith and lack of distinctive character. To mitigate risks of costly invalidity actions, applicants should secure robust evidence of acquired distinctiveness and legitimate commercial intent prior to registration. Proactive assessment of geographical term usage helps avoid prolonged litigation and ensures greater brand stability across the EU market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟普通法院作出方法论严谨的商标无效判决", "解决法意两国企业间长期商标权属纠纷", "阐释欧盟商标无效诉讼程序的复杂性与要点"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The General Court issued a methodologically rigorous judgment addressing complex legal arguments in a high-profile EU trademark invalidity case.", "The ruling resolves a protracted ownership dispute between French and Italian entities, clarifying standards for assessing bad faith filings.", "The decision elucidates the intricate procedural requirements and substantive criteria governing EU trademark invalidity litigation."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["含地理名称商标需严格评估显著性与误导性风险", "防范跨国商标纠纷，提前布局证据以应对无效挑战"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Marks incorporating geographical names require strict evaluation of distinctiveness and risk of misleading the public to withstand invalidity challenges.", "Practice note: Applicants should gather comprehensive evidence of use and good faith early to defend against claims of bad faith or descriptiveness.", "What to monitor: Evolving CJEU and General Court jurisprudence on the intersection of geographical terms, bad faith, and trademark validity."]
core_points_en: The General Court issued a methodologically rigorous judgment addressing complex legal arguments in a high-profile EU trademark invalidity case.
The ruling resolves a protracted ownership dispute between French and Italian entities, clarifying standards for assessing bad faith filings.
The decision elucidates the intricate procedural requirements and substantive criteria governing EU trademark invalidity litigation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Marks incorporating geographical names require strict evaluation of distinctiveness and risk of misleading the public to withstand invalidity challenges.
Practice note: Applicants should gather comprehensive evidence of use and good faith early to defend against claims of bad faith or descriptiveness.
What to monitor: Evolving CJEU and General Court jurisprudence on the intersection of geographical terms, bad faith, and trademark validity.

items.title: EPO adopts cutting-edge AI solution developed in Europe
items.title_zh: EPO 采用欧洲研发的尖端 AI 解决方案
items.title_en: EPO Adopts Mistral AI OCR Solution to Enhance Patent Processing and European Digital Sovereignty
items.summary: EPO experts and Mistral AI’s technical teams have created a new solution, which integrates state-of-the-art optical character recognition (OCR) developed by Mistral AI into the EPO’s workflows.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局（EPO）宣布与 Mistral AI 达成战略合作，共同开发并部署集成先进光学字符识别（OCR）技术的解决方案。该举措旨在解决专利文件中公式、化学结构等复杂元素的提取难题，提升多语言数据处理精度。此举不仅强化了欧洲数字主权，确保敏感数据符合欧盟法律与伦理框架，也标志着 EPO 在 2028 战略计划下加速数字化转型的决心。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office (EPO) has announced a strategic collaboration with Mistral AI to deploy a specialized optical character recognition (OCR) solution within its workflows. This joint initiative addresses the complex challenge of processing multilingual patent documents containing formulas, chemical structures, and sequence listings. By integrating advanced AI technologies developed in Europe, the EPO significantly improves data extraction accuracy and content structuring for the patent granting process. The project underscores a commitment to European digital sovereignty, ensuring that sensitive patent information is handled in strict compliance with EU legal, operational, and ethical frameworks. This deployment aligns with the EPO’s AI Policy and Strategic Plan 2028, marking a decisive step toward robust digital transformation and enhanced service efficiency for global users.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧专利申请将受益于更高效的审查流程，但需关注 EPO 对 AI 生成内容的合规要求。建议企业提前优化申请文件格式以适应新 OCR 系统，并密切关注 EPO 关于 AI 辅助审查的最新指南，确保申请策略符合欧洲数据主权趋势。
items.ai_insight_en: Applicants should anticipate more efficient processing of complex patent documents as the EPO integrates advanced OCR capabilities. Rights holders are advised to optimize application formatting to enhance machine readability, potentially accelerating examination timelines. Furthermore, stakeholders must remain vigilant regarding evolving EPO guidelines on AI-generated content and data sovereignty. Ensuring compliance with European ethical and legal standards when utilizing AI tools for patent preparation is increasingly critical. Monitoring these developments will help applicants align their strategies with the Office’s digital transformation trajectory and maintain robust compliance in the European jurisdiction.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EPO 携手 Mistral AI 定制开发专用 OCR 模型，显著提升复杂专利文档的…", "项目坚持欧洲本土研发与部署，确保敏感专利信息处理符合欧盟法律及伦理监管框架", "该合作是 EPO 落实人工智能政策及 2028 战略规划的关键步骤，旨在构建稳健的数字…"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["EPO and Mistral AI co-developed a fine-tuned OCR model tailored to handle complex elements like chemical structures and formulas in patent applications.", "The solution is developed and deployed in Europe, ensuring sensitive data processing adheres to EU legal standards and ethical regulatory frameworks.", "This initiative supports the EPO’s Strategic Plan 2028 by leveraging AI to accelerate digital transformation and improve examination efficiency."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用 EPO 新 OCR 技术红利，优化申请文件排版以提升机器读取效率，加速审查进程", "警惕欧洲数据主权趋势，确保在使用 AI 工具准备申请材料时符合欧盟合规与伦理标准"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Optimizing document formatting can leverage new OCR capabilities to improve machine reading accuracy and potentially speed up examination.", "Practice note: Ensure all AI-assisted patent preparation processes comply with EU data sovereignty laws and emerging ethical guidelines for AI usage.", "What to monitor: Watch for updated EPO guidance on AI-generated content and specific technical requirements for digital patent submissions."]
core_points_en: EPO and Mistral AI co-developed a fine-tuned OCR model tailored to handle complex elements like chemical structures and formulas in patent applications.
The solution is developed and deployed in Europe, ensuring sensitive data processing adheres to EU legal standards and ethical regulatory frameworks.
This initiative supports the EPO’s Strategic Plan 2028 by leveraging AI to accelerate digital transformation and improve examination efficiency.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Optimizing document formatting can leverage new OCR capabilities to improve machine reading accuracy and potentially speed up examination.
Practice note: Ensure all AI-assisted patent preparation processes comply with EU data sovereignty laws and emerging ethical guidelines for AI usage.
What to monitor: Watch for updated EPO guidance on AI-generated content and specific technical requirements for digital patent submissions.

items.title: UKIPO eyes IP crime unit expansion, confirms continued funding
items.title_zh: 英国知识产权局拟扩编 IP 犯罪部门并确认持续资助
items.title_en: UKIPO Confirms IP Crime Unit Funding Until 2029 and Plans Expansion
items.summary: Practitioners have welcomed extended funding of the specialist police unit until 2029, while the UKIPO says it is exploring increased scale
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局（UKIPO）确认将把专门的知识产权警察单位资金支持延长至 2029 年，业界对此表示欢迎。同时，UKIPO 正探索进一步扩大该部门的规模，以应对日益复杂的知识产权犯罪挑战。此举标志着英国在脱欧后继续强化本土知识产权刑事执法力度的政策导向，旨在通过增加资源投入提升打击侵权效率。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO) has confirmed that funding for the specialist police unit dedicated to combating intellectual property crime will be extended through 2029. This decision has been widely welcomed by legal practitioners and industry stakeholders who view sustained financial support as critical for effective enforcement. Concurrently, the UKIPO is actively exploring options to expand the scale and capacity of this specialized law enforcement body. This strategic move aims to address the increasingly complex and sophisticated nature of modern IP infringement challenges. The extension and potential expansion signal a continued policy commitment to strengthening domestic criminal enforcement mechanisms post-Brexit, ensuring that resources are available to maintain high efficiency in tackling counterfeiting and other IP-related offenses.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在英中企需警惕升级的刑事执法风险，合规审查应覆盖供应链全环节。建议利用此窗口期完善内部风控，避免因侵权面临更严厉的刑事追责，同时可积极与该专项单位合作维权。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating within the United Kingdom should anticipate a more rigorous environment for intellectual property criminal enforcement. The extended funding and planned expansion of the specialist police unit indicate a heightened focus on investigating and prosecuting IP violations. Companies must ensure their compliance frameworks cover all aspects of their supply chains to mitigate the risk of severe criminal liability. Rights holders may find this strengthened enforcement landscape advantageous for proactive brand protection strategies. It is advisable to review internal risk controls during this period and consider engaging with the specialized unit to leverage improved investigative capabilities for enforcing intellectual property rights effectively against infringers.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国确认 IP 警察单位获资至 2029 年", "UKIPO 正计划进一步扩大执法部门规模", "业界对加强刑事执法资源表示广泛欢迎"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UK government has confirmed that funding for the specialist IP crime police unit will continue until 2029.", "The UKIPO is currently evaluating plans to significantly increase the operational scale of the IP crime enforcement department.", "Legal practitioners and rights holders have broadly welcomed the enhanced resources dedicated to criminal IP enforcement."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["在英运营企业需严防知识产权刑事风险", "借势强化执法环境主动开展品牌维权行动"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Companies operating in the UK must strictly manage IP criminal risks due to intensified enforcement efforts and expanded police resources.", "Practice note: Leverage the strengthened enforcement environment to proactively initiate brand protection actions and collaborate with the specialized IP crime unit.", "What to monitor: Future announcements regarding the specific scale of the unit's expansion and any new procedural guidelines for criminal IP investigations."]
core_points_en: The UK government has confirmed that funding for the specialist IP crime police unit will continue until 2029.
The UKIPO is currently evaluating plans to significantly increase the operational scale of the IP crime enforcement department.
Legal practitioners and rights holders have broadly welcomed the enhanced resources dedicated to criminal IP enforcement.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Companies operating in the UK must strictly manage IP criminal risks due to intensified enforcement efforts and expanded police resources.
Practice note: Leverage the strengthened enforcement environment to proactively initiate brand protection actions and collaborate with the specialized IP crime unit.
What to monitor: Future announcements regarding the specific scale of the unit's expansion and any new procedural guidelines for criminal IP investigations.

items.title: What’s changing – paper forms
items.title_zh: UKIPO简化专利纸质表格并逐步取消邮件提交
items.title_en: UKIPO Consolidates Patent Paper Forms and Phases Out Email Submissions
items.summary: We’re continuing our work to modernise and simplify the way customers interact with the IPO. As part of this, we’re making a number of changes to our patent paper forms.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局（UKIPO）为现代化服务，将于2026年4月1日合并Form 1、7、9A及10为单一专利申请表，仅填写相关部分即可。同时，为配合数字化战略，将逐步取消通过电子邮件提交表格的方式，鼓励使用在线账户或邮寄。低量表格需下载PDF上传，其他表格可选在线或邮寄。纸质表格因无障碍和立法原因保留，但不再作为标准提交渠道。此变更仅涉及专利，商标和设计申请暂不受影响。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office is modernizing its services by introducing a single combined patent form to replace four existing paper forms, effective 1 April 2026. This new form integrates functions for applying, declaring inventorship, and requesting search and examination, allowing users to complete only relevant sections. Concurrently, the IPO is phasing out email submissions for patent forms to align with its digital-first strategy. While paper forms remain available for accessibility and legislative compliance, standard submission will shift to online accounts or post. Low-volume actions require downloading PDFs for upload via user accounts. These changes apply exclusively to patents; trademark and design filings continue via email. The update coincides with fee changes, aiming to streamline processes and improve clarity for all market participants interacting with the UK patent system.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在英申请专利的中国企业需提前适应新表格结构，避免沿用旧表单导致延误。应优先建立UKIPO在线账户，熟悉数字化提交流程以获取即时确认及更快处理。鉴于邮件提交将被取消，建议内部流程及时更新，确保合规并降低沟通成本。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders filing in the UK should prepare for the consolidation of patent forms and the discontinuation of email submissions. Transitioning to the One IPO account ensures access to immediate confirmation and faster processing times. Internal filing protocols must be updated to avoid reliance on obsolete forms or email channels, which may lead to delays. Familiarity with the new combined form structure is essential for efficient application management. Monitoring official guidance updates on GOV.UK will help ensure compliance with the new digital-first requirements and maintain seamless communication with the UKIPO during this transition period.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["合并四种现有专利纸质表格为单一综合表格，提升清晰度与数字服务一致性", "逐步淘汰电子邮件作为表格提交的标准方式，引导用户转向在线数字渠道", "变更定于2026年4月1日生效，仅针对专利领域，商标与设计申请维持现状"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Four existing patent paper forms are merged into a single combined form to enhance clarity and align with digital service structures.", "Email submissions for patent forms are being phased out, directing users toward online accounts or postal mail as primary channels.", "These procedural changes take effect on 1 April 2026 and apply solely to patent applications, leaving trademark and design processes unchanged."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["提前熟悉新合并表格结构，避免因使用旧表单导致申请延误或驳回", "加速转向UKIPO在线账户提交，利用数字化优势提升处理效率与透明度"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Update internal filing workflows to prioritize the new combined patent form and digital submission routes to prevent processing delays.", "Practice note: Establish or verify One IPO accounts to leverage immediate confirmations and streamlined handling of patent applications.", "What to monitor: Official GOV.UK guidance updates regarding the specific timeline for ending email acceptance and detailed instructions for low-volume forms."]
core_points_en: Four existing patent paper forms are merged into a single combined form to enhance clarity and align with digital service structures.
Email submissions for patent forms are being phased out, directing users toward online accounts or postal mail as primary channels.
These procedural changes take effect on 1 April 2026 and apply solely to patent applications, leaving trademark and design processes unchanged.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Update internal filing workflows to prioritize the new combined patent form and digital submission routes to prevent processing delays.
Practice note: Establish or verify One IPO accounts to leverage immediate confirmations and streamlined handling of patent applications.
What to monitor: Official GOV.UK guidance updates regarding the specific timeline for ending email acceptance and detailed instructions for low-volume forms.

items.title: Via video licensors kept in the dark about pool sale; Access Advance seeking support for merged offering
items.title_zh: 视频许可人对专利池出售不知情，Access Advance 寻求支持合并方案
items.title_en: Access Advance Seeks Licensors' Support for Merged Pool Amid Transparency Concerns
items.summary: EXCLUSIVE: Access Advance CEO Peter Moller tells IAM he is now “actively engaging with licensors” to persuade them of the benefits of a combined pool
items.ai_summary_zh: Access Advance 首席执行官透露正积极游说许可人支持其合并许可池方案。此前，部分视频技术许可人对另一专利池的出售交易毫不知情，引发透明度担忧。该动态反映了欧洲 SEP 许可市场的整合趋势及潜在的反垄断合规风险，旨在通过合并提升许可效率并应对复杂的全球许可环境。
items.ai_summary_en: Access Advance CEO Peter Moller confirmed that the organization is actively engaging with video technology licensors to secure support for a proposed merger of patent pools. This strategic push follows revelations that certain licensors were unaware of prior pool sale transactions, raising significant concerns regarding transparency and due process within the standard-essential patent licensing ecosystem. The development highlights an ongoing consolidation trend in the European SEP market, driven by efforts to streamline licensing efficiency and navigate complex global regulatory environments. However, this integration also introduces potential antitrust compliance risks. Stakeholders are closely watching how these structural changes impact market dynamics, particularly concerning the balance between operational efficiency and the preservation of competitive fairness and information symmetry in negotiations.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国科技企业应密切关注专利池合并对许可费率及 FRAND 合规的影响。建议企业评估新许可架构风险，积极参与许可人沟通，避免因信息不对称陷入被动，同时预留反垄断抗辩空间以维护自身合法权益。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor the progress of patent pool mergers to assess their specific impact on future royalty rate structures and overall FRAND compliance costs. Proactive engagement in licensor communication channels is essential to prevent weakened negotiating positions caused by information asymmetry. Companies must evaluate the risks associated with new licensing architectures and maintain sufficient space for potential antitrust defenses to protect their legitimate interests. Ensuring transparency in these consolidated offerings is critical for maintaining fair competition and avoiding passive disadvantage in subsequent licensing negotiations.

items.title: DPMA-Jahresstatistik 2025: Zahl der Erfindungen deutlich gestiegen
items.title_zh: 德国专利局 2025 年报：发明数量显著增长
items.title_en: DPMA 2025 Report: Patent Filings Rise as Trademark Demand Surges Driven by Digital and EV Sectors
items.summary: DPMA-Jahresstatistik 2025: Zahl der Erfindungen deutlich gestiegen - Innovationstätigkeit in Digitaltechnologien und bei Elektromobilität legt massiv zu – Automobilindustrie weiter anmeldestärkste Branche – Nachfrage nach Markenschutz boomt unter anderem durch chinesische Anmelder und Online-Plattformen – DPMA-Präsidentin: Gutes Zeichen, dass der Schutz geistigen Eigentums für die Unternehmen weiter hohe Priorität hat - Im Video: Deutlich mehr Patent- und Markenanmeldungen - Innovationstreiber:
items.ai_summary_zh: 德国专利商标局（DPMA）发布 2025 年统计数据，显示专利申请量增长 4.7%，达 62,050 件，其中本土申请人贡献突出。商标注册需求激增 19.8%，部分源于中国申请者及电商平台。汽车业仍是最大申请行业，数字技术与电动_mobility 成为主要创新驱动力，彰显知识产权在经济困难时期的战略优先级。
items.ai_summary_en: The German Patent and Trade Mark Office (DPMA) released its 2025 annual statistics, revealing a significant increase in intellectual property filings. Patent applications rose by 4.7 percent to 62,050, with domestic applicants contributing substantially to this growth. Trademark registrations surged by 19.8 percent to 96,328, driven notably by Chinese applicants and online platforms. The automotive industry remained the leading sector for patent filings, while digital technologies and electromobility emerged as primary innovation drivers. Design applications increased by 5.9 percent, and utility model filings grew by 19.3 percent. DPMA President Eva Schewior highlighted that these figures demonstrate the continued strategic priority companies place on protecting innovations, even during economically challenging times. The office also reported improved efficiency in examination and registration procedures across most categories.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应关注德国在电动出行与数字领域的专利布局机会，同时利用商标保护应对电商扩张。鉴于本土申请活跃，需强化差异化创新以避免直接竞争，并重视 DPMA 审查效率提升带来的快速确权红利。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should monitor the rapid expansion of patent activity in Germany’s digital technology and electromobility sectors, which present strategic opportunities for portfolio optimization. The substantial rise in trademark filings, particularly from international entities and platform operators, indicates intensifying brand competition in the German market. Rights holders are advised to prioritize early brand protection strategies to secure market position against emerging competitors. Furthermore, the DPMA’s demonstrated capacity for handling increased volumes efficiently offers a favorable environment for expedited rights confirmation. Companies should leverage these procedural efficiencies to accelerate their IP enforcement capabilities while ensuring their innovation strategies remain distinct to mitigate direct competitive pressures in saturated technological fields.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["年德国专利申请量增 4.7%，本土企业增长强劲", "商标注册量飙升近两成，中国申请者与平台助推增长", "机械工程为首要领域，电动出行与数字技术增速最快"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["German patent applications increased by 4.7 percent to 62,050 in 2025, with strong contributions from domestic enterprises and research institutions.", "Trademark demand surged by 19.8 percent to 96,328 registrations, significantly boosted by Chinese applicants and major online e-commerce platforms.", "The automotive industry led patent filings, while digital technologies and electromobility recorded the fastest growth rates among innovation sectors."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["借力德国电动与数字技术热点，优化海外专利组合策略", "针对商标激增态势，提前布局品牌保护以应对平台竞争"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strategic alignment of patent portfolios with high-growth sectors like electromobility and digital technologies in Germany.", "Practice note: Prioritize early trademark registration to counteract intensified brand competition from international applicants and online platforms.", "What to monitor: DPMA procedural efficiencies and processing times for accelerated rights confirmation amidst rising application volumes."]
core_points_en: German patent applications increased by 4.7 percent to 62,050 in 2025, with strong contributions from domestic enterprises and research institutions.
Trademark demand surged by 19.8 percent to 96,328 registrations, significantly boosted by Chinese applicants and major online e-commerce platforms.
The automotive industry led patent filings, while digital technologies and electromobility recorded the fastest growth rates among innovation sectors.

Practice note: Prioritize early trademark registration to counteract intensified brand competition from international applicants and online platforms.
What to monitor: DPMA procedural efficiencies and processing times for accelerated rights confirmation amidst rising application volumes.

items.title: UKIPO extends funding for specialist IP crime unit as counterfeit risks grow
items.title_zh: 英国知识产权局延长 IP 犯罪专项组资金以应对假冒风险
items.title_en: UKIPO Extends Funding for Specialist IP Crime Unit Amid Rising Counterfeit Risks
items.summary: UKIPO extends funding for specialist IP crime unit as counterfeit risks grow
items.ai_summary_zh: 随着不安全假冒商品风险上升，英国政府宣布将资助伦敦-based 知识产权犯罪专项组至 2029 年。此举旨在加强执法能力，并与欧盟知识产权局及欧洲当局日益严格的反假冒行动保持协同，共同打击跨境侵权活动，保障市场安全。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK government has confirmed the extension of funding for the London-based Intellectual Property Crime Unit (IPCU) through to 2029. This strategic decision aims to bolster enforcement capabilities against the growing threat of unsafe counterfeit goods entering the market. The move aligns with intensified efforts by the European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) and other European authorities to combat cross-border intellectual property infringement. By sustaining support for this specialized unit, UK officials seek to enhance collaboration with continental counterparts, ensuring a coordinated approach to tackling illicit trade. This development underscores the increasing priority placed on consumer safety and the protection of legitimate businesses from the reputational and economic harms associated with counterfeit products in both the UK and broader European jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中企需警惕英国及欧盟联合加强的打假力度，尤其关注涉及人身安全的产品。建议企业主动配合当地执法，完善供应链合规审查，利用专项组机制维权，避免因假冒关联遭受连带法律风险或声誉损失。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should anticipate heightened enforcement intensity across the UK and EU regarding counterfeit activities, particularly involving products posing safety risks. Rights holders are advised to proactively engage with local law enforcement mechanisms, such as the IPCU, to protect their interests. Strengthening supply chain compliance reviews is critical to mitigate the risk of inadvertent association with illicit goods. Companies must also remain vigilant regarding joint enforcement trends between British and European agencies. Proactive measures, including robust monitoring and cooperation with authorities, can help prevent legal liabilities and safeguard brand reputation against the damaging effects of counterfeiting operations.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国政府确认资助 IP 犯罪专项组直至 2029 年", "重点应对不断增长的不安全假冒商品风险", "强化与 EUIPO 及欧洲各国当局的执法协作"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UK government confirms continued financial support for the specialist IP crime unit until 2029.", "Focus shifts toward addressing escalating risks posed by unsafe counterfeit goods in the market.", "Enhanced enforcement collaboration established with EUIPO and European authorities to tackle cross-border infringement."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注英欧联合执法趋势，排查产品假冒风险", "利用专项组机制主动维权，强化供应链合规"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Anticipate stricter joint UK-EU enforcement against counterfeits, especially for safety-critical products.", "Practice note: Leverage specialized units like the IPCU for rights protection and strengthen supply chain compliance audits.", "What to monitor: Evolving collaborative enforcement strategies between UKIPO, EUIPO, and national authorities."]

Focus shifts toward addressing escalating risks posed by unsafe counterfeit goods in the market.

items.title: Court of Appeal refuses additional written round
items.title_zh: 上诉庭拒绝额外书面轮次：一审辅助请求自动纳入上诉
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Refuses Additional Written Round: First-Instance Auxiliary Requests Automatically Included in Appeal
items.summary: Sibio Technology v Abbott Diabetes Care – Court of Appeal, 27 February 2026 (UPC_CoA_884/2025)
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉庭在 Sibio 诉 Abbott 案中裁定，驳回上诉人要求增加书面答辩轮次的请求。法院明确，一审已受理的辅助请求自动构成上诉案件一部分，无需重新提交；被上诉人仅维持原判结果不构成交叉上诉，故无权触发额外书面程序。
items.ai_summary_en: In Sibio Technology v Abbott Diabetes Care, the UPC Court of Appeal dismissed the appellant’s request for an additional exchange of written pleadings. The Court clarified that auxiliary requests admitted during first-instance proceedings automatically form part of the appeal record and do not require re-filing. Since the respondent merely maintained these existing requests without challenging the first-instance outcome, no conditional cross-appeal existed. Consequently, there was no procedural basis under the Rules of Procedure to grant further written submissions. The decision reinforces that parties cannot trigger extra procedural rounds solely because the opponent relies on previously filed claims, ensuring efficient case management and preventing unnecessary delays in appellate proceedings.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在 UPC 诉讼中应重视一审阶段的请求布局，所有一审辅助请求将自动带入上诉。若作为上诉人，需预判对方可能援引的旧请求并提前准备；作为被上诉人，可利用此规则避免程序拖延，稳固胜诉成果。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling clarifies the continuity of claim sets between UPC instances, impacting litigation strategy significantly. Parties must ensure all potential auxiliary requests are rigorously prepared and admitted during first-instance proceedings, as they will persist into any appeal. Appellants should anticipate arguments based on these surviving requests rather than seeking procedural extensions. Respondents can leverage this principle to resist attempts to delay proceedings through new written rounds. Efficient preparation at the trial stage is critical, as the appellate phase focuses on reviewing the established record rather than reopening factual or claim-based debates unnecessarily.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["一审已受理的辅助请求自动纳入上诉程序，无需当事人重新提交", "被上诉人仅维持原判结果不构成交叉上诉，不触发额外书面轮次", "仅因对方依赖既有请求而要求增补书面意见，不符合程序规则"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Auxiliary requests admitted at first instance automatically remain part of the appeal proceedings without requiring re-submission by the parties.", "A respondent maintaining existing requests without challenging the lower court's decision does not constitute a conditional cross-appeal.", "Reliance on previously filed auxiliary requests alone does not justify granting an additional round of written pleadings under procedural rules."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["一审提交的辅助请求会自动延续至上诉，需在初审阶段严谨布局", "利用规则防止对手以程序为由拖延，确保上诉审理高效推进"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure comprehensive auxiliary request strategies are finalized during first-instance trials to avoid limitations in subsequent appeals.", "Practice note: Resist opposing motions for additional written rounds when they rely solely on previously admitted claims to maintain procedural efficiency.", "What to monitor: How the Court applies Rule 222 RoP regarding the automatic inclusion of prior filings in future multi-instance disputes."]
core_points_en: Auxiliary requests admitted at first instance automatically remain part of the appeal proceedings without requiring re-submission by the parties.
A respondent maintaining existing requests without challenging the lower court's decision does not constitute a conditional cross-appeal.
Reliance on previously filed auxiliary requests alone does not justify granting an additional round of written pleadings under procedural rules.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure comprehensive auxiliary request strategies are finalized during first-instance trials to avoid limitations in subsequent appeals.
Practice note: Resist opposing motions for additional written rounds when they rely solely on previously admitted claims to maintain procedural efficiency.
What to monitor: How the Court applies Rule 222 RoP regarding the automatic inclusion of prior filings in future multi-instance disputes.

items.title: Axel Voss’ Good Intention – The European Parliament adopts report to recalibrate EU copyright for Generative AI
items.title_zh: 欧洲议会采纳报告拟重构生成式 AI 版权规则
items.title_en: European Parliament Adopts Report to Recalibrate EU Copyright Rules for Generative AI
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲议会法律事务委员会通过报告，呼吁欧盟委员会立法应对生成式 AI 挑战。报告强调在发展中尊重知识产权，主张通过自愿许可机制解决训练数据授权问题，同时为科研教育保留例外。特别指出新闻出版业需更强排他性控制，以防聚合信息损害民主结构，并建议扩展邻接权覆盖推理及检索增强生成等新型使用场景。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Parliament’s Committee on Legal Affairs adopted a report urging the European Commission to legislate on generative AI copyright challenges. The document emphasizes respecting intellectual property rights during AI development and proposes voluntary licensing mechanisms to address training data authorization issues. It preserves exceptions for scientific research, education, and non-commercial innovation. Notably, the press and news media sector receives specific protection due to its democratic importance. The report suggests expanding neighboring rights to cover new AI usage scenarios, such as reasoning and retrieval-augmented generation, ensuring stronger exclusive control for publishers against potential harms from aggregated information services.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国 AI 企业及内容出口商需警惕欧盟许可合规门槛提升。建议提前布局版权清算机制，关注新闻出版领域特殊规则，避免侵权风险；同时利用科研例外条款优化研发策略。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants deploying generative AI models should anticipate higher compliance costs regarding training data authorization within the EU. The proposed shift towards voluntary licensing and enhanced transparency requirements signals a stricter regulatory environment for content ingestion. Rights holders in the news and publishing sectors may gain stronger leverage through expanded neighboring rights. Companies should proactively assess their data sourcing strategies, considering both the preserved exceptions for research and the anticipated rigorous consent requirements for commercial use of protected works.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["呼吁欧盟立法平衡生成式 AI 发展与版权保护", "主张建立自愿许可机制并强化权利人透明度义务", "新闻出版业获豁免且需扩展邻接权覆盖新型 AI 用法"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Calls for EU legislation balancing generative AI technological development with robust copyright protection standards.", "Advocates establishing voluntary licensing frameworks while strengthening transparency obligations for rights holders.", "Grants exemptions to the press sector and proposes extending neighboring rights to cover novel AI application methods."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["预判欧盟将提高 AI 训练数据授权合规成本", "新闻出版内容使用将面临更严格的事前同意要求"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Anticipate increased compliance costs for AI training data authorization under evolving EU rules.", "Practice note: Prepare for stricter prior consent requirements when using press and news content for AI training.", "What to monitor: Developments in neighboring rights extensions covering reasoning and retrieval-augmented generation uses."]
core_points_en: Calls for EU legislation balancing generative AI technological development with robust copyright protection standards.
Advocates establishing voluntary licensing frameworks while strengthening transparency obligations for rights holders.

Practice note: Prepare for stricter prior consent requirements when using press and news content for AI training.
What to monitor: Developments in neighboring rights extensions covering reasoning and retrieval-augmented generation uses.

items.summary: This report looks at how design protection in the UK is being used and makes suggestions for potential reform.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局发布报告，深入评估现行外观设计保护制度的使用现状与效能。报告识别了制度运行中的潜在问题，并针对未来改革提出具体政策建议，旨在优化英国脱欧后的独立设计保护体系，平衡权利人利益与市场创新需求。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office has released a comprehensive report evaluating the current state and effectiveness of the United Kingdom's design protection framework. The study provides an in-depth analysis of how existing design rights are utilized in practice, identifying specific operational challenges and potential inefficiencies within the current system. Furthermore, the document outlines concrete policy recommendations aimed at reforming the legal landscape. These proposals seek to optimize the independent design protection regime established post-Brexit, ensuring it remains robust and fit for purpose. The ultimate goal is to strike a necessary balance between safeguarding the legitimate interests of rights holders and fostering an environment conducive to market innovation and competition.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在英布局需密切关注制度变革，及时调整申请策略以适配新规则。建议重新评估存量权利稳定性，并针对可能的改革方向提前规划，确保持续获得有效保护。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in the UK should closely monitor developments regarding potential design law reforms. It is advisable to dynamically adjust filing strategies to align with evolving regulatory requirements. Rights holders are encouraged to conduct a thorough review of their existing portfolios to assess stability and anticipate how proposed changes might impact current protection scopes. Proactive planning is essential to ensure continuous effective protection under the new independent regime. By staying informed and adapting early, companies can mitigate risks associated with legislative shifts and maintain strong competitive positions in the UK market.

items.title: IPO confirms continued funding for specialist IP crime unit
items.title_zh: 英国知识产权局确认持续资助专业知识产权犯罪部门
items.title_en: IPO confirms continued funding for specialist IP crime unit
items.summary: Three-year funding commitment alongside new industry partnership pilot.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局（UKIPO）正式宣布，将为专业的知识产权犯罪部门提供为期三年的持续资金支持。此举旨在强化对知识产权侵权犯罪的打击力度。同时，该局启动了新的行业合作试点项目，通过深化与权利人的协作机制，提升情报共享效率与执法精准度，确保持续遏制假冒与盗版活动。
items.ai_insight_zh: 在英中企应重视 UKIPO 强化的刑事执法趋势，主动利用新试点机制维权。建议出口企业加强英国市场监控，遇侵权时积极寻求与该专项部门合作，提升打假效率。

items.title: Honor joins Via’s AAC patent pool
items.title_zh: 荣耀加入 Via AAC 专利池以规避诉讼风险
items.title_en: Honor Joins Via Licensing AAC Patent Pool to Secure Global Audio Codec Compliance
items.summary: China’s top-5 smartphone company takes a litigation-free path to Via’s audio codec licence
items.ai_summary_zh: 中国头部智能手机厂商荣耀正式加入 Via Licensing 管理的 AAC 音频编码专利池。此举标志着荣耀选择通过商业许可而非诉讼对抗的方式，解决其在欧洲及全球市场的音频技术知识产权合规问题，体现了行业对无诉讼许可路径的偏好。
items.ai_summary_en: Honor, a leading global smartphone manufacturer, has officially joined the AAC audio codec patent pool administered by Via Licensing. This strategic move allows the company to secure necessary intellectual property rights for its devices through a streamlined commercial licensing framework rather than engaging in protracted litigation. By adopting this collaborative approach, Honor addresses potential IP compliance challenges across European and international markets. The decision highlights a growing industry preference for resolving standard-essential patent disputes via efficient licensing mechanisms. It underscores the importance of proactive IP management for technology firms operating globally, ensuring uninterrupted market access while minimizing legal risks associated with audio codec technologies.
items.ai_insight_zh: 建议中国出海企业优先评估加入国际专利池的性价比，以低成本规避欧洲高额诉讼风险。同时应建立 SEP 预警机制，将许可谈判前置化，确保产品在欧洲市场顺畅流通。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should evaluate the cost-effectiveness of joining international patent pools as a primary strategy to mitigate high litigation risks in Europe. Establishing robust SEP early-warning systems is critical for anticipating licensing obligations. Companies must prioritize intellectual property compliance as a fundamental prerequisite for market entry. Integrating license negotiations into early product development cycles ensures smoother distribution channels. This proactive stance helps avoid disruptive legal conflicts and supports sustainable business operations within complex regulatory environments like the EU.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["荣耀加入 Via 专利池实现 AAC 技术合规授权", "采取无诉讼策略降低海外市场法律不确定性", "反映中国手机厂商在欧洲 SEP 许可上的成熟化"]

items.title: Amazon-InterDigital dispute and landmark CJEU referral put UPC jurisdiction in focus
items.title_zh: 亚马逊与 InterDigital 纠纷及 CJEU 转介聚焦 UPC 管辖权
items.title_en: Amazon-InterDigital Dispute and CJEU Referral Focus Attention on UPC Jurisdiction
items.summary: A recent UK court ruling calls for clearer cross-court communication while the UPC asks Europe’s top court for clarity on the use of long-arm jurisdiction
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国法院近期裁决呼吁加强跨国司法沟通，与此同时，统一专利法院（UPC）就长臂管辖权的适用问题向欧盟法院（CJEU）寻求澄清。该动态源于亚马逊与 InterDigital 的标准必要专利争端，标志着 UPC 管辖范围面临关键法律测试，可能重塑欧洲 SEP 诉讼格局。
items.ai_summary_en: A recent ruling by a UK court has emphasized the urgent need for clearer communication mechanisms between national courts to enhance judicial efficiency in cross-border disputes. Concurrently, the Unified Patent Court (UPC) has submitted a landmark request for a preliminary ruling to the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU). This referral specifically seeks clarification on the application of long-arm jurisdiction within the UPC framework. These developments stem from the ongoing standard essential patent (SEP) litigation between Amazon and InterDigital. This high-profile dispute serves as a critical legal test for the boundaries of the UPC's authority. The outcome is expected to significantly influence the future landscape of SEP enforcement and licensing strategies across Europe, affecting how rights holders and implementers navigate multi-jurisdictional patent conflicts.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧遭遇 SEP 诉讼时，需高度关注 UPC 管辖权界定进展。建议提前评估长臂管辖风险，制定多国应诉策略，并利用 CJEU 裁决窗口期优化全球专利布局与许可谈判筹码。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants involved in European SEP litigation should closely monitor the evolving definition of UPC jurisdiction. It is prudent to assess potential exposure to long-arm jurisdiction claims early in any dispute lifecycle. Stakeholders should develop robust multi-country defense strategies that account for varying judicial interpretations across Europe. Furthermore, the period awaiting the CJEU's final decision offers a strategic window to optimize global patent portfolios and strengthen positions in licensing negotiations. Understanding these jurisdictional nuances is essential for effective risk management and maintaining leverage in complex international intellectual property conflicts.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国法院强调需明确跨国法院间的沟通机制以提升效率", "UPC 就长臂管辖权向 CJEU 提起具有里程碑意义的初步裁决请求", "亚马逊与 InterDigital 的纠纷成为检验 UPC 管辖边界的关键案例"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UK court stressed the necessity of establishing clear communication protocols among transnational courts to improve procedural efficiency.", "The UPC filed a landmark preliminary ruling request with the CJEU regarding the specific scope and application of long-arm jurisdiction.", "The Amazon versus InterDigital case acts as a pivotal test case defining the operational limits of UPC jurisdiction."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["密切关注 CJEU 对 UPC 长臂管辖权的最终解释以规避诉讼风险", "结合英欧司法动态调整标准必要专利的全球许可与应对策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor the CJEU's final interpretation of UPC long-arm jurisdiction to effectively mitigate litigation risks.", "Practice note: Adjust global SEP licensing and response strategies by integrating insights from both UK and EU judicial dynamics.", "What to monitor: Track procedural updates in the Amazon-InterDigital case and related UPC jurisdictional referrals."]
core_points_en: The UK court stressed the necessity of establishing clear communication protocols among transnational courts to improve procedural efficiency.
The UPC filed a landmark preliminary ruling request with the CJEU regarding the specific scope and application of long-arm jurisdiction.
The Amazon versus InterDigital case acts as a pivotal test case defining the operational limits of UPC jurisdiction.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor the CJEU's final interpretation of UPC long-arm jurisdiction to effectively mitigate litigation risks.
Practice note: Adjust global SEP licensing and response strategies by integrating insights from both UK and EU judicial dynamics.
What to monitor: Track procedural updates in the Amazon-InterDigital case and related UPC jurisdictional referrals.

items.title: EPO meets with Novartis
items.title_zh: EPO 与诺华举行会议探讨专利审查时效与统一专利体系
items.title_en: EPO and Novartis Discuss Examination Timeliness, Unitary Patent, and SPC Framework
items.summary: On 4 February, the European Patent Office (EPO) held an online meeting with the Swiss pharmaceutical and biotechnology company Novartis.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026 年 2 月 4 日，欧洲专利局（EPO）与瑞士诺华公司举行线上会议，重点讨论专利审查时效对投资决策的关键影响。双方就统一专利制度及建立集中化补充保护证书（SPC）体系交换意见，诺华支持由 EPO 实施该体系并交由统一专利法院处理无效诉讼。此外，会议还涉及抗体相关专利申请审查指南的修订反馈机制。
items.ai_summary_en: On 4 February 2026, the European Patent Office held an online meeting with Swiss pharmaceutical company Novartis. The discussion focused on how patent examination timeliness critically influences investment decisions for new formulations and medical uses. Participants exchanged views on the Unitary Patent system and advocated for a centralized supplementary protection certificate (SPC) framework. Novartis expressed support for the EPO implementing this system, with invalidity appeals handled by the Unified Patent Court to ensure EU-wide consistency. The dialogue also covered recent revisions to the Guidelines for Examination regarding antibody-related applications. The EPO highlighted its annual public consultation process, allowing users to provide feedback on draft guidelines before their April entry into force, reinforcing ongoing engagement with major applicants.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企应关注欧洲专利审查提速趋势，优化布局策略。同时需重视统一专利法院在 SPC 纠纷中的核心地位，提前构建应对集中化无效挑战的预案，利用指南修订窗口期提升申请质量。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should monitor the trend toward accelerated patent examination at the EPO, as examination speed directly impacts commercialization strategies and investment timing for high-value pharmaceutical assets. With the potential introduction of a centralized SPC system, rights holders must prepare for unified validity challenges before the Unified Patent Court. This shift requires robust litigation readiness and strategic portfolio management. Companies should actively engage during the EPO’s annual guideline consultation periods to influence procedural standards. Proactively adapting application strategies to align with updated examination practices, particularly for complex technologies like antibodies, will help secure stronger patents and mitigate future enforcement risks.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["诺华强调专利审查时效性直接决定新药研发投资决策", "支持建立集中化 SPC 体系，主张由统一专利法院管辖无效上诉", "双方就抗体专利审查指南修订及公众咨询机制进行深入交流"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Novartis emphasized that timely patent examination outcomes are essential for planning investments in new drug formulations and medical uses.", "The company supports a centralized SPC system implemented by the EPO, with invalidity appeals managed by the Unified Patent Court.", "Both parties discussed feedback mechanisms for revised antibody patent examination guidelines and the EPO's annual public consultation process."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["借势审查提速机制，加速高价值医药专利在欧洲的授权落地", "针对集中化 SPC 体系，预演统一专利法院的无效抗辩与诉讼策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Accelerated examination timelines require optimized filing strategies to secure rapid grant for key pharmaceutical assets.", "Practice note: Develop litigation readiness for centralized SPC invalidity proceedings before the Unified Patent Court.", "What to monitor: Annual EPO public consultations on examination guidelines to refine application quality and procedural compliance."]
core_points_en: Novartis emphasized that timely patent examination outcomes are essential for planning investments in new drug formulations and medical uses.
The company supports a centralized SPC system implemented by the EPO, with invalidity appeals managed by the Unified Patent Court.
Both parties discussed feedback mechanisms for revised antibody patent examination guidelines and the EPO's annual public consultation process.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Accelerated examination timelines require optimized filing strategies to secure rapid grant for key pharmaceutical assets.
Practice note: Develop litigation readiness for centralized SPC invalidity proceedings before the Unified Patent Court.
What to monitor: Annual EPO public consultations on examination guidelines to refine application quality and procedural compliance.
