items.title: UPC confirms that the “Malta Problem” bars a Unitary Patent | EIP
items.title_zh: UPC 确认“马耳他问题”阻碍部分欧洲专利获得单一效力
items.title_en: UPC Confirms Malta Problem Bars Unitary Patent Protection for Pre-2007 Applications
items.summary: It was foreseen that the requirements of the Unitary Patent Regulation (1257/2012) might result that old European patent applications (effective filing date before 1 March 2007) could be ineligible for unitary patent protection. The UPC has now confirmed the view of the EPO that this is indeed the case.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）在最新裁决中确认，因马耳他于 2007 年 3 月 1 日才加入《欧洲专利公约》，在此日期前申请的欧洲专利无法指定马耳他，故不满足单一专利法规关于“所有参与成员国权利要求一致”的要求。UPC 维持了欧洲专利局（EPO）驳回相关单一效力请求的决定，明确此类旧案仅能通过国家生效途径保护。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) has confirmed that European patent applications filed before March 1, 2007, are ineligible for unitary effect. This is because Malta joined the European Patent Convention on that date, meaning earlier applications could not designate Malta. Consequently, these patents fail to meet the requirement under Article 3(1) of Regulation 1257/2012 that a European patent be granted with the same set of claims for all participating Member States. The UPC upheld the European Patent Office’s refusal to register unitary effect for such cases. Rights holders must rely on traditional national validation routes for protection in these instances, as the lack of designation for Malta creates an insurmountable legal barrier to unitary status.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局需核查优先权日，若早于 2007 年 3 月且拟获单一专利保护，须警惕资格缺失风险。建议对存量旧案进行合规筛查，必要时转为传统国家生效模式，避免因程序瑕疵丧失核心市场保护。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders managing legacy portfolios should verify the effective filing dates of European patents intended for unitary protection. Applications with priority dates prior to March 1, 2007, face automatic ineligibility due to the historical non-designation of Malta. It is critical to conduct compliance screenings for existing cases to identify those affected by this 'Malta Problem.' For impacted patents, strategies must pivot toward traditional national validations in key markets to ensure continuous protection. Failure to adjust may result in unintended gaps in coverage across participating Member States where unitary effect was anticipated but legally unattainable.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 裁定 2007 年 3 月前申请案因未指定马耳他，不符单一专利要件", "确认 EPO 驳回决定合法，此类案件上诉至 UPC 而非 EPO 上诉委员会", "受影响主要为多代分案申请，未来随时间推移该问题将自然消失"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UPC rules pre-March 2007 applications cannot obtain unitary effect due to inability to designate Malta.", "UPC upholds EPO refusal, confirming appeals against unitary effect refusals fall under UPC jurisdiction.", "Impact is limited mainly to multi-generational divisional applications and will diminish over time."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["排查 2007 年前申请案，评估单一专利资格缺失对国家生效的影响", "针对无法获单一效力的旧案，及时调整策略转为重点国家单独验证"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Verify effective filing dates for legacy patents to assess unitary patent eligibility risks.", "Practice note: Screen old cases and transition to national validation if unitary effect is barred by Malta issues.", "What to monitor: Impact on multi-generational divisional applications derived from pre-2007 parent filings."]
core_points_en: UPC rules pre-March 2007 applications cannot obtain unitary effect due to inability to designate Malta.
UPC upholds EPO refusal, confirming appeals against unitary effect refusals fall under UPC jurisdiction.
Impact is limited mainly to multi-generational divisional applications and will diminish over time.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Verify effective filing dates for legacy patents to assess unitary patent eligibility risks.
Practice note: Screen old cases and transition to national validation if unitary effect is barred by Malta issues.
What to monitor: Impact on multi-generational divisional applications derived from pre-2007 parent filings.

items.title: The CJEU SACD case: Admissibility of a copyright action that does not include all the co-authors
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院裁定：未列明全体共有人不必然驳回版权诉讼
items.title_en: CJEU Ruling: Copyright Actions Need Not Include All Co-Authors If Unlocatable
items.summary: In 2019 the heirs of the famous French film director, author of many Nouvelle Vague (New Wave) films, Claude Chabrol who died in 2010, brought a case against the assignees of 14 films he made between 1967 and 1974.
items.ai_summary_zh: 针对法国电影导演夏布洛尔继承人起诉案，因无法联系所有共同作者导致诉讼受阻，巴黎法院提请欧盟法院释法。欧盟法院裁定，虽然欧盟法不排斥法国要求全体共有人参与诉讼的规定，但应灵活解释：若原告已尽合理努力仍无法定位其他权利人，不应因此驳回诉讼，以保障知识产权有效执行。
items.ai_summary_en: In a case involving the heirs of French director Claude Chabrol, litigation regarding the exploitation of films was stalled because French law required all co-authors to be joined as parties. Despite diligent efforts, the claimants could not locate all rights holders for these older audiovisual works. The Paris Court of First Instance sought guidance from the CJEU on whether EU directives preclude such strict national admissibility rules. The CJEU ruled that while EU law does not prohibit national requirements for joining all co-authors, these rules must be applied flexibly. Specifically, if a plaintiff has made reasonable efforts to locate other rights holders but fails, the action should not be declared inadmissible. This ensures the effective enforcement of copyright while balancing the protection of co-authors' interests.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国影视企业在欧维权时，若遇历史作品共有人缺失难题，可援引此案主张程序灵活性。建议提前梳理权利链条，保留寻找共有人的证据，避免因程序瑕疵被驳回，利用欧盟判例突破成员国僵化程序限制。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling provides critical procedural flexibility for rights holders enforcing copyright in Europe, particularly for historical audiovisual works where identifying all contributors is challenging. It prevents rigid national procedural rules from blocking legitimate claims when locating every co-author is practically impossible. Rights holders should document all attempts to identify and contact potential co-authors thoroughly. Demonstrating due diligence in these searches is essential to invoke this exception and avoid dismissal based on non-joinder. This approach supports effective IP enforcement across member states by aligning national procedures with EU principles of effective judicial protection.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法国法原规定版权诉讼须列明全体共有人，否则不予受理", "欧盟法院认为在无法定位共有人时，应灵活适用该程序规则", "裁决旨在平衡保护共有人权益与确保版权有效执法的需求"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["French law traditionally requires all co-authors to be joined in copyright infringement actions for admissibility.", "The CJEU mandates flexible application of this rule when co-authors cannot be located despite reasonable efforts.", "The judgment balances protecting co-authors' rights with ensuring effective copyright enforcement mechanisms."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["遇共有人缺失时，可依据本案主张灵活适用诉讼资格规则", "务必保留已尽合理努力寻找其他权利人的相关证据材料"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders can pursue infringement claims without joining all co-authors if they demonstrate reasonable efforts to locate them.", "Practice note: Meticulously document all search activities and communications attempted to identify missing co-authors to prove due diligence.", "What to monitor: How national courts interpret 'reasonable efforts' and apply this flexibility in future copyright admissibility disputes."]
core_points_en: French law traditionally requires all co-authors to be joined in copyright infringement actions for admissibility.
The CJEU mandates flexible application of this rule when co-authors cannot be located despite reasonable efforts.
The judgment balances protecting co-authors' rights with ensuring effective copyright enforcement mechanisms.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders can pursue infringement claims without joining all co-authors if they demonstrate reasonable efforts to locate them.
Practice note: Meticulously document all search activities and communications attempted to identify missing co-authors to prove due diligence.
What to monitor: How national courts interpret 'reasonable efforts' and apply this flexibility in future copyright admissibility disputes.

items.title: Trademark News: What you might have missed in February 2026
items.title_zh: 2026 年 2 月商标要闻：欧盟 SME 支持及多国规费调整
items.title_en: February 2026 Trademark Roundup: EU SME Support and Global Fee Adjustments
items.summary: Stay informed with our comprehensive roundup of the past month's headlines, all neatly compiled for you in one convenient post. Catch up on the latest news reported by the Kluwer IP Reporter.Argentina revises trademark examination procedureOn 11 December 2025, Argentina’s National Institute of Industrial Property approved Resolution 583/2025, changing the way trademark applications are examined to shorten timelines and adjust the examination process.Read the full text here
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文汇总 2026 年 2 月全球商标动态。欧洲方面，欧盟知识产权局网络推出新版网站以整合支持中小企业；世界知识产权组织宣布调整芬兰、英国等在马德里体系下的指定规费。此外，阿根廷、哈萨克斯坦及新西兰亦更新了审查程序或冲突商品列表。
items.ai_summary_en: This update compiles key global trademark developments from February 2026. In Europe, the European Union Intellectual Property Network launched a redesigned website to provide integrated intellectual property support for small and medium-sized enterprises across the European Union. The World Intellectual Property Organization notified revised individual fees for Finland, the United Kingdom, and Israel under the Madrid System. Additionally, Argentina revised its trademark examination procedures to shorten timelines, while Kazakhstan enacted amendments to major IP laws. New Zealand also updated its list of conflicting goods. These changes impact international registration strategies, budgeting for renewals, and procedural compliance for rights holders operating in or entering these jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需关注欧盟针对中小企业的服务升级，善用新平台降低维权成本。同时，出口企业应核查马德里体系中英国、芬兰等地的最新规费标准，精准预算国际注册与续展费用，避免因费率变动影响布局。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should leverage the new EUIPN platform to access consolidated resources, potentially reducing administrative burdens and costs associated with trademark enforcement and registration in Europe. For international portfolios managed via the Madrid System, applicants must verify the updated fee structures for Finland, the United Kingdom, and Israel to ensure accurate budgeting for upcoming renewals and new designations. Failure to account for these rate adjustments may lead to unexpected cost overruns or procedural delays. Monitoring these procedural shifts in non-EU jurisdictions like Argentina and Kazakhstan is also essential for maintaining compliant and efficient global brand protection strategies.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟知识产权局网络上线新站，旨在为中小企业提供一体化知识产权支持服务", "WIPO 通知调整芬兰、英国、以色列等国在马德里国际注册中的指定单独规费", "阿根廷修改商标审查流程以缩短时限，哈萨克斯坦通过修正案更新多项知识产权法律"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EUIPN launched a new website offering integrated IP support services specifically designed for small and medium-sized enterprises within the European Union.", "WIPO announced revised individual fees for Finland, the United Kingdom, and Israel regarding designations in international trademark applications and renewals.", "Argentina streamlined trademark examination procedures to reduce timelines, while Kazakhstan implemented significant amendments to its core intellectual property legislation."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用欧盟新平台资源，优化中小企业在欧洲的商标申请与维权策略", "核实马德里体系下英芬等国新规费，及时调整国际商标注册预算与计划"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Utilize the new EUIPN digital resources to optimize trademark application workflows and enforcement strategies for SMEs operating in Europe.", "Practice note: Verify current Madrid System fees for Finland, the UK, and Israel to adjust international registration budgets and prevent payment discrepancies.", "What to monitor: Track procedural updates in Argentina and legislative changes in Kazakhstan to ensure ongoing compliance with local trademark examination requirements."]
core_points_en: The EUIPN launched a new website offering integrated IP support services specifically designed for small and medium-sized enterprises within the European Union.
WIPO announced revised individual fees for Finland, the United Kingdom, and Israel regarding designations in international trademark applications and renewals.
Argentina streamlined trademark examination procedures to reduce timelines, while Kazakhstan implemented significant amendments to its core intellectual property legislation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Utilize the new EUIPN digital resources to optimize trademark application workflows and enforcement strategies for SMEs operating in Europe.
Practice note: Verify current Madrid System fees for Finland, the UK, and Israel to adjust international registration budgets and prevent payment discrepancies.
What to monitor: Track procedural updates in Argentina and legislative changes in Kazakhstan to ensure ongoing compliance with local trademark examination requirements.

items.title: Description amendments can extend protection (T 439/22)
items.title_zh: 说明书修正案可扩大保护范围（T 439/22）
items.title_en: EPO Board T 439/22: Description Amendments May Extend Protection Scope Under G 1/24
items.summary: The comments space for the last IPKat article on claim interpretation/description amendments appears to have maxed out. Sorry everyone, but maybe it's for the best. However, fear not, we now have a new decision to restart the debate! The decision in T 439/22 takes us back to our old friend, the definition of "gathered". Applying G 1/24, the Board of Appeal in the case is the second to find
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局上诉委员会在 T 439/22 号决定中，依据 G 1/24 扩大会议决定，重新审视了“收集”一词的定义。该裁决是第二起应用 G 1/24 原则的案件，确认在特定情形下，对说明书的修正可能被视为扩大了专利保护范围，从而引发关于权利要求解释与说明书修改界限的新辩论。
items.ai_summary_en: In decision T 439/22, the EPO Board of Appeal revisited the definition of the term 'gathered' by applying the principles established in Enlarged Board of Appeal decision G 1/24. This ruling marks only the second instance where G 1/24 has been applied, signaling a developing line of case law regarding claim interpretation and description amendments. The Board confirmed that, under specific circumstances, amendments to the patent description can be interpreted as extending the scope of protection. This outcome reignites critical debates within the European patent community concerning the precise boundaries between permissible descriptive clarifications and substantive changes that alter the legal reach of a patent claim.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧专利申请需警惕说明书修改风险，避免无意扩大保护范围导致无效。建议密切关注 EPO 最新判例，优化撰写策略，确保权利要求与说明书一致性，必要时寻求专业法律意见以规避潜在诉讼风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should exercise caution when amending patent descriptions during opposition or appeal proceedings. As demonstrated in T 439/22, such amendments carry the risk of being interpreted as extending the scope of protection, potentially leading to invalidity findings under Article 123(3) EPC. Applicants must ensure strict logical consistency between claims and the description to avoid unintended broadening. Proactive monitoring of EPO jurisprudence following G 1/24 is essential for refining drafting strategies. Legal counsel should review amendment proposals carefully to mitigate litigation risks associated with scope expansion disputes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["上诉委员会应用 G 1/24 决定重新界定“收集”含义", "确认说明书修改在特定条件下可实质扩大保护范围", "引发业界对权利要求解释与修改界限的新一轮讨论"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Board applied G 1/24 principles to redefine the technical meaning of the term 'gathered' in the context of the disputed patent claims.", "The decision confirms that description amendments may effectively extend protection scope if they alter the technical teaching beyond the original disclosure.", "This ruling triggers renewed industry discussion regarding the legal limits of claim interpretation versus permissible description modifications."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注 EPO 判例动态，防范说明书修改引发的保护范围争议", "优化专利申请文件撰写，确保权利要求与说明书逻辑一致"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor EPO case law developments to prevent description amendments from inadvertently expanding protection scope and triggering invalidity.", "Practice note: Optimize patent application drafting to ensure rigorous logical consistency between claims and the description, minimizing interpretation risks.", "What to monitor: Track subsequent Board of Appeal decisions applying G 1/24 to understand evolving standards for claim interpretation and description limits."]
core_points_en: The Board applied G 1/24 principles to redefine the technical meaning of the term 'gathered' in the context of the disputed patent claims.
The decision confirms that description amendments may effectively extend protection scope if they alter the technical teaching beyond the original disclosure.
This ruling triggers renewed industry discussion regarding the legal limits of claim interpretation versus permissible description modifications.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor EPO case law developments to prevent description amendments from inadvertently expanding protection scope and triggering invalidity.
Practice note: Optimize patent application drafting to ensure rigorous logical consistency between claims and the description, minimizing interpretation risks.
What to monitor: Track subsequent Board of Appeal decisions applying G 1/24 to understand evolving standards for claim interpretation and description limits.

items.title: The validity and value of cell therapy safety protocol inventions (T 1555/23)
items.title_zh: 细胞疗法安全协议发明的有效性与价值评估
items.title_en: Validity and Value of Broad Cell Therapy Safety Protocol Inventions in T 1555/23
items.summary: The patent in T 1555/23 is impressively broad. The claims covered a medical use invention comprising treating the side effects of CAR-T cell therapy using the now industry-standard class of cytokine inhibitor. The claim was not limited to any particular CAR-T cell therapy, target, type of cancer or specific inhibitor. The data supporting the claim, whilst clinical, was also relatively
items.ai_summary_zh: 本案 T 1555/23 涉及一项范围极宽的医药用途专利，主张使用细胞因子抑制剂治疗 CAR-T 细胞疗法的副作用。权利要求未限定具体疗法、靶点或癌症类型。尽管支持数据为临床数据但相对有限，案件核心在于探讨此类宽泛权利要求在缺乏充分实施例支持下的有效性及商业价值。
items.ai_summary_en: Decision T 1555/23 concerns a patent with exceptionally broad claims covering the medical use of cytokine inhibitors to treat side effects of CAR-T cell therapy. The claims were not limited to specific CAR-T therapies, targets, cancer types, or particular inhibitors. Although the supporting data was clinical in nature, it remained relatively limited in scope. The central legal issue addressed whether such expansive medical use assertions could be validly supported without sufficient illustrative examples. This case highlights the tension between broad functional claiming strategies and the requirement for adequate technical disclosure in the biopharmaceutical sector.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企在欧洲布局细胞疗法专利时，应避免过度宽泛的功能性限定，确保实施例数据能充分支撑权利要求范围，以防因公开不充分被无效。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders seeking patent protection for cell therapy safety protocols in Europe must ensure that the volume and quality of experimental data correspond strictly to the breadth of their claims. Overly broad functional definitions risk invalidation due to insufficient disclosure if the provided examples do not plausibly demonstrate efficacy across the entire claimed scope. Applicants should carefully calibrate claim language to reflect the actual technical contribution supported by the evidence, avoiding generic assertions that extend beyond the demonstrated therapeutic effects.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["涉案专利权利要求覆盖范围极宽，未限定具体抑制剂或癌症类型", "争议焦点在于有限的临床数据是否足以支持如此宽泛的医疗用途主张", "该案对生物医药领域功能性限定的专利有效性审查具有重要参考意义"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The patent claims covered a very broad medical use invention without limiting specific inhibitors or cancer types.", "The primary dispute focused on whether limited clinical data sufficiently supported such wide-ranging medical use claims.", "This decision offers significant reference value for examining validity of functionally defined patents in biotechnology."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["生物医药专利申请需确保数据量与权利要求范围相匹配", "警惕欧洲审查实践中对医疗用途发明支持充分性的严格要求"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure data quantity matches claim scope to avoid invalidity risks in European filings.", "Practice note: Be aware of strict EPO requirements regarding sufficiency of support for medical use inventions.", "What to monitor: Evolving standards for validating broad functional claims in advanced biopharmaceutical technologies."]
core_points_en: The patent claims covered a very broad medical use invention without limiting specific inhibitors or cancer types.
The primary dispute focused on whether limited clinical data sufficiently supported such wide-ranging medical use claims.
This decision offers significant reference value for examining validity of functionally defined patents in biotechnology.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure data quantity matches claim scope to avoid invalidity risks in European filings.
Practice note: Be aware of strict EPO requirements regarding sufficiency of support for medical use inventions.
What to monitor: Evolving standards for validating broad functional claims in advanced biopharmaceutical technologies.

items.title: EU escalates global FRAND rate-setting WTO complaint against China
items.title_zh: 欧盟升级针对中国的全球 FRAND 费率设定 WTO 申诉
items.title_en: EU Escalates Global FRAND Rate-Setting WTO Complaint Against China
items.summary: The move follows the European Union’s recent arbitration victory in a parallel SEP trade dispute with China
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟近期在与中国平行的标准必要专利贸易争端仲裁中获胜，随即宣布升级其在世贸组织框架下针对中国全球 FRAND 费率设定的投诉。此举标志着欧中在知识产权定价权上的博弈从双边仲裁扩展至多边贸易体制，旨在通过国际规则施压，确立更有利于欧洲权利人的全球许可费率裁判标准。
items.ai_summary_en: Following a recent arbitration victory in a parallel standard essential patent trade dispute with China, the European Union has announced an escalation of its complaint within the World Trade Organization framework. This strategic move targets China’s judicial practices regarding the setting of global FRAND licensing rates. The action signifies a significant expansion of the intellectual property pricing conflict from bilateral arbitration mechanisms to the broader multilateral trade system. By leveraging international trade rules, the EU aims to exert pressure and establish global adjudication standards for licensing rates that are more favorable to European rights holders. This development highlights the increasing intersection of IP litigation and international trade law, as stakeholders navigate complex jurisdictional challenges in cross-border SEP enforcement.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临更严峻的 SEP 诉讼环境，需警惕欧盟利用 WTO 规则限制中国法院的全球禁令与费率裁定。建议企业提前布局海外许可谈判，评估管辖权风险，并关注欧盟后续可能出台的限制性政策指引。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants involved in SEP licensing should anticipate a more rigorous legal environment in Europe. The EU's utilization of WTO rules may restrict the effectiveness of Chinese court rulings on global injunctions and rate determinations. Companies must proactively assess jurisdictional risks and strengthen compliance frameworks to mitigate potential trade sanctions. Strategic preparation for overseas licensing negotiations is critical to navigating these evolving regulatory dynamics. Stakeholders should closely monitor subsequent policy guidance from the EU, which may introduce further limitations on foreign judicial interventions in global IP disputes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟借仲裁胜诉之势，将 SEP 争端升级至 WTO 层面", "焦点在于挑战中国法院设定全球 FRAND 费率的管辖权与实践", "此举意在通过多边机制重塑全球标准必要专利许可规则"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EU leverages its recent arbitration success to elevate the SEP dispute to the WTO level.", "The core focus challenges Chinese courts' jurisdiction and practices in determining global FRAND rates.", "This initiative seeks to reshape global SEP licensing rules through established multilateral trade mechanisms."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕欧盟借多边机制削弱中国法院全球费率裁定效力", "出口企业应强化 SEP 合规审查，规避潜在贸易制裁风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders face heightened scrutiny as the EU uses multilateral mechanisms to challenge the validity of Chinese global rate rulings.", "Practice note: Export-oriented entities should reinforce SEP compliance reviews to avoid exposure to potential trade-related sanctions.", "What to monitor: Future EU policy directives that may further constrain the enforceability of foreign judgments on global FRAND terms."]
core_points_en: The EU leverages its recent arbitration success to elevate the SEP dispute to the WTO level.
The core focus challenges Chinese courts' jurisdiction and practices in determining global FRAND rates.
This initiative seeks to reshape global SEP licensing rules through established multilateral trade mechanisms.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders face heightened scrutiny as the EU uses multilateral mechanisms to challenge the validity of Chinese global rate rulings.
Practice note: Export-oriented entities should reinforce SEP compliance reviews to avoid exposure to potential trade-related sanctions.
What to monitor: Future EU policy directives that may further constrain the enforceability of foreign judgments on global FRAND terms.

items.title: Nokia agrees to UK-set interim and final licences with Paramount, Warner Bros
items.title_zh: 诺基亚同意英国设定的派拉蒙与华纳兄弟临时及最终许可
items.title_en: Nokia Accepts UK-Set SEP Licences for Paramount and Warner Bros While Appealing Acer and Asus Ruling
items.summary: Meanwhile, the Finnish company continues to appeal against the interim licence declaration granted on behalf of Acer and Asus
items.ai_summary_zh: 芬兰诺基亚公司已同意接受英国法院为派拉蒙和华纳兄弟设定的临时及最终标准必要专利许可条款。与此同时，该公司正继续就此前代表宏碁和华硕授予的临时许可声明提起上诉。此案凸显了英国法院在全球 SEP 费率设定中的活跃角色及争议性。
items.ai_summary_en: Nokia has agreed to accept the interim and final standard essential patent licence terms set by the UK High Court on behalf of media giants Paramount Global and Warner Bros Discovery. This development marks a significant step in resolving long-standing licensing disputes between the Finnish telecommunications equipment manufacturer and major Hollywood studios regarding the use of its cellular technology patents. Meanwhile, Nokia continues to pursue an appeal against the earlier interim licence declaration granted in cases involving consumer electronics manufacturers Acer and Asus. This parallel legal action highlights the ongoing complexity and high stakes involved in global FRAND rate determinations. The situation underscores the influential role of the UK judiciary in setting global licensing benchmarks, creating a dynamic environment where rights holders and implementers must navigate concurrent settlements and appeals across different defendant categories.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧运营需高度关注英国法院的 SEP 裁判趋势，其裁决常具全球效力。建议提前评估专利风险，制定灵活的许可谈判策略，并密切关注相关上诉进展以规避禁令风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should closely monitor UK judicial trends in SEP litigation, as these rulings often carry substantial global weight. Given the potential for worldwide effect, companies must proactively assess patent infringement risks and develop flexible licensing negotiation strategies. It is critical to track appellate developments in related cases to effectively manage exposure to injunctions and ensure compliance with evolving FRAND obligations. A coordinated approach to multi-jurisdictional disputes is essential for mitigating legal and financial risks associated with standard essential patent enforcement.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["诺基亚接受英国法院为影视巨头设定的 SEP 许可条款", "公司同时针对宏碁和华硕案的临时许可声明持续上诉", "英国司法辖区在确定全球 FRAND 费率方面影响力显著"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Nokia accepts UK court-determined interim and final SEP licence terms for Paramount and Warner Bros.", "The company continues appealing the interim licence declaration previously granted for Acer and Asus.", "UK courts maintain significant influence in determining global FRAND rates for standard essential patents."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["英国法院 SEP 裁决具全球辐射力，中企需强化风险预警", "面对多重诉讼态势，应统筹谈判策略并关注上诉动态"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: UK SEP rulings have global reach; rights holders and implementers must anticipate cross-border impacts on licensing portfolios.", "Practice note: Develop integrated negotiation strategies that account for concurrent settlements and active appeals in parallel proceedings.", "What to monitor: Track appellate progress in hardware manufacturer cases to gauge shifts in judicial interpretation of FRAND commitments."]
core_points_en: Nokia accepts UK court-determined interim and final SEP licence terms for Paramount and Warner Bros.
The company continues appealing the interim licence declaration previously granted for Acer and Asus.
UK courts maintain significant influence in determining global FRAND rates for standard essential patents.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: UK SEP rulings have global reach; rights holders and implementers must anticipate cross-border impacts on licensing portfolios.
Practice note: Develop integrated negotiation strategies that account for concurrent settlements and active appeals in parallel proceedings.
What to monitor: Track appellate progress in hardware manufacturer cases to gauge shifts in judicial interpretation of FRAND commitments.

items.title: Pro Bono Legal Support for SMEs: Call for Design Rights Experts as demand grows
items.title_zh: EUIPO 呼吁外观设计专家加入 SMEs 公益法律支持项目
items.title_en: EUIPO Seeks Design Experts for SME Pro Bono Programme Amid Record Demand
items.summary: Pro Bono Legal Support for SMEs: Call for Design Rights Experts as demand grows
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）公益项目需求激增，2025 年受理请求创历史新高，超九成为微型企业。尽管商标咨询仍占主导，但 2026 年将战略重心转向外观设计保护，急需相关领域专家加入网络。该项目覆盖全欧盟，旨在为缺乏专业代表的初创企业提供高质量知识产权指导，用户满意度极高。
items.ai_summary_en: The EUIPO Pro Bono Programme has recorded a historic high of 1,375 support requests in 2025, reflecting steady annual growth since 2020. Over 90 percent of applicants are microenterprises lacking professional representation, with participation spanning all EU Member States. While trade mark assistance remains the primary demand, design protection queries are rising significantly. Consequently, the programme will strategically prioritize design rights expertise in 2026 to better support creative industries. The initiative currently engages 289 active IP experts who provide high-quality guidance, resulting in exceptional user satisfaction rates. This expansion underscores the critical need for specialized legal support for early-stage businesses navigating European intellectual property systems without existing counsel.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海中小企业可利用该免费资源降低欧洲确权成本，尤其关注外观设计布局。中国知识产权从业者若具备欧盟外观设计实务经验，可申请加入专家网络，拓展跨境服务渠道并提升国际影响力。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should note that the EUIPO Pro Bono Programme offers accessible, high-quality IP guidance for early-stage entities, potentially reducing initial registration costs. For rights holders focusing on aesthetic innovation, this resource provides valuable strategic advice on design rights before engaging paid counsel. Simultaneously, IP practitioners with substantive experience in EU design law have an opportunity to join the expert network. Participation enhances professional visibility within the European market and facilitates cross-border client acquisition. Monitoring the programme’s evolving focus on design protection allows firms to anticipate shifts in SME needs and align their service offerings accordingly.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["年 SME 支持请求达 1375 件，德西意法荷参与度最高", "九成申请者属微型企业，商标为主但外观设计需求上升", "年将重点招募外观设计专家，强化创意产业支持"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["SME support requests reached 1,375 in 2025, with highest participation from Germany, Spain, Italy, France, and the Netherlands.", "Over ninety percent of applicants are microenterprises; while trademarks dominate, design protection needs are increasingly prominent.", "The 2026 strategy focuses on recruiting design experts to strengthen support for Europe's creative and innovative business sectors."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["中国微型出海企业可申请 EUIPO 公益支持以降低维权成本", "中国资深代理人可加入专家库，拓展欧洲跨境业务网络"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Early-stage companies can leverage free pro bono consultations to optimize initial IP strategies and reduce entry costs.", "Practice note: Practitioners with EU design expertise should consider joining the network to expand cross-border service capabilities.", "What to monitor: The increasing emphasis on design rights indicates growing strategic importance for aesthetic IP in European markets."]

Over ninety percent of applicants are microenterprises; while trademarks dominate, design protection needs are increasingly prominent.
The 2026 strategy focuses on recruiting design experts to strengthen support for Europe's creative and innovative business sectors.

items.title: New EU System for Geographical Indications for Craft and Industrial Products – important reminder for applicants
items.title_zh: 欧盟手工业产品地理标志新体系申请重要提醒
items.title_en: EUIPO Launches New EU System for Craft and Industrial Geographical Indications
items.summary: New EU System for Geographical Indications for Craft and Industrial Products – important reminder for applicants
items.ai_summary_zh: 自 2025 年 12 月 1 日起，EUIPO 正式负责注册手工业和工业产品地理标志（CIGIs）。至 2026 年 12 月，各国原有制度将全面被欧盟新体系取代。多数成员国申请人需经国家阶段审查后转交 EUIPO，而丹麦等七国可直接向 EUIPO 申请。目前已有多国提交现有名称保护申请，EUIPO 推出 GIportal 工具并配套指南以协助过渡。
items.ai_summary_en: As of December 1, 2025, the EUIPO assumes responsibility for registering Geographical Indications for Craft and Industrial Products (CIGIs). By December 2, 2026, existing national protection systems will be fully replaced by this new EU framework. Most applicants must undergo a two-stage process: initial examination by national authorities followed by Union-level review at the EUIPO. However, applicants from Denmark, Finland, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, the Netherlands, and Sweden may file directly with the Office. The system also allows protection for existing CIGIs established before the launch date. To support this transition, the EUIPO introduced GIportal, an intuitive e-filing tool, alongside comprehensive guidance resources. Early implementation has seen significant interest, with dozens of applications already published for sectors such as ceramics, textiles, and minerals.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国涉欧手工艺品及工业品出口企业应关注新体系过渡期，评估现有产品是否符合 CIGI 保护标准。建议利用 EUIPO 新平台提前布局，通过欧盟统一注册降低多国维权成本，提升品牌在欧洲市场的法律保护层级。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants exporting European craft and industrial goods should assess eligibility for CIGI protection during this transitional period. Utilizing the unified EU registration system can streamline legal protection across borders, reducing the complexity and cost associated with maintaining separate national rights. Rights holders are encouraged to leverage the new GIportal platform for efficient filing. Strategic planning should account for the specific procedural requirements of each jurisdiction, particularly regarding opposition timelines and evidence standards. Proactive engagement with the available EUIPO resources ensures smoother navigation of the new regulatory landscape and strengthens brand security within the single market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["年底起 EUIPO 统一受理手工业产品地理标志注册申请", "多数成员国实行国家初审加欧盟复审的双阶段审查程序", "丹麦等七国申请人可直接向 EUIPO 提交申请无需国家阶段"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EUIPO officially manages CIGI registrations starting December 1, 2025, replacing all national systems by late 2026.", "Most member states require a dual-phase review involving national preliminary examination before forwarding applications to the EUIPO.", "Applicants from seven specific member states can bypass national stages and submit CIGI applications directly to the EUIPO."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用双阶段程序特点，提前准备国家层面异议应对策略", "关注直接申请国别名单，优化特定市场注册路径选择"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Evaluate product eligibility for CIGI status to secure unified EU protection and reduce multi-jurisdictional enforcement costs.", "Practice note: Prepare robust evidence packages for national opposition phases in jurisdictions requiring preliminary state-level examination.", "What to monitor: Track the specific filing pathways for the seven direct-access member states to optimize registration strategies."]
core_points_en: The EUIPO officially manages CIGI registrations starting December 1, 2025, replacing all national systems by late 2026.
Most member states require a dual-phase review involving national preliminary examination before forwarding applications to the EUIPO.
Applicants from seven specific member states can bypass national stages and submit CIGI applications directly to the EUIPO.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Evaluate product eligibility for CIGI status to secure unified EU protection and reduce multi-jurisdictional enforcement costs.
Practice note: Prepare robust evidence packages for national opposition phases in jurisdictions requiring preliminary state-level examination.
What to monitor: Track the specific filing pathways for the seven direct-access member states to optimize registration strategies.

items.title: Intellectual Property for all: The critical importance of IP rights to SMEs
items.title_zh: 知识产权惠及全员：IP 权利对中小企业的至关重要性
items.title_en: IP Rights as Critical Strategic Assets: Driving SME Success and Attracting EU Venture Capital
items.summary: Intellectual Property for all: The critical importance of IP rights to SMEs
items.ai_summary_zh: 斯德哥尔摩大学教授撰文指出，知识产权是欧盟中小企业商业成功的关键。EUIPO 与 EPO 联合研究显示，拥有 IP 权利的中小企业人均营收高出 44%，且更易获得风险投资。报告强调商标与专利等无形资产保护能显著提升企业估值、工资水平及退出成功率，是创新密集型产业获取资金的核心信号。
items.ai_summary_en: Professor Eleonora Rosati highlights that intellectual property is fundamental to the commercial success of small and medium-sized enterprises in the European Union. Recent joint studies by the EUIPO and EPO reveal that SMEs holding IP rights generate significantly higher revenue per employee compared to those without protection, with registered rights correlating to a forty-four percent increase. Furthermore, IP-intensive industries attract the vast majority of private equity and venture capital funding, receiving over eighty-eight percent of total investments between 2021 and 2023. The analysis underscores that protecting intangible assets like trademarks and patents not only boosts valuation and wage premiums but also serves as a vital signal of innovativeness, directly influencing access to capital and overall market competitiveness for emerging businesses across the EU single market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国赴欧中小企业及初创公司应将 IP 布局视为融资与生存战略，而非单纯法律合规。建议提前在 EUIPO 注册商标与专利，以数据证明创新能力，从而提升获取欧盟风投几率及并购退出估值，避免无形资产流失。
items.ai_insight_en: For companies operating in Europe, intellectual property strategy should be integrated into core business planning rather than treated solely as legal compliance. Securing trademark and patent registrations early provides tangible data on innovation capacity, which is crucial for attracting venture capital and enhancing exit valuations during mergers or acquisitions. This approach helps prevent the loss of intangible assets while signaling robustness to investors. Market participants should view IP portfolios as essential tools for securing funding and demonstrating competitive advantage, ensuring that creative outputs are legally protected to maximize financial returns and sustain long-term growth within the dynamic European marketplace.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["拥有 IP 权利的中小企业人均营收比无保护者高出 44%", "超八成欧盟风投资金流向知识产权密集型行业的初创企业", "注册专利或商标使初创企业获早期风投的可能性增加十倍"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["SMEs registering IP rights achieve forty-four percent higher revenue per employee than unprotected counterparts.", "Over eighty-eight percent of EU venture capital funding flows to startups operating within IP-intensive industries.", "Registering patents or trademarks increases early-stage venture capital acquisition likelihood by tenfold."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["将 IP 注册作为获取欧盟风险投资的核心敲门砖", "利用 IP 资产组合显著提升企业人均营收与工资溢价"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Treat IP registration as a primary mechanism for securing EU venture capital and improving exit valuations.", "Practice note: Leverage IP asset portfolios to demonstrably increase per-employee revenue and justify wage premiums.", "What to monitor: Track EUIPO and EPO data trends linking IP intensity to investment flows and competitive performance."]
core_points_en: SMEs registering IP rights achieve forty-four percent higher revenue per employee than unprotected counterparts.
Over eighty-eight percent of EU venture capital funding flows to startups operating within IP-intensive industries.
Registering patents or trademarks increases early-stage venture capital acquisition likelihood by tenfold.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Treat IP registration as a primary mechanism for securing EU venture capital and improving exit valuations.
Practice note: Leverage IP asset portfolios to demonstrably increase per-employee revenue and justify wage premiums.
What to monitor: Track EUIPO and EPO data trends linking IP intensity to investment flows and competitive performance.

items.title: Opinion of the Advocate General in Cases C-496/23 P Meta Platforms Ireland v Commission (Facebook Marketplace) and C-497/23 P Meta Platforms Ireland v Commission (Facebook Data)
items.title_zh: 欧盟总法务官建议驳回 Meta 关于数据与市场调查的上诉
items.title_en: AG Rantos Recommends Dismissing Meta Appeals Against EU Commission Data Requests
items.summary: Advocate General Rantos proposes the dismissal of the appeals by Meta Platforms Ireland relating to an investigation into abuse of a dominant position concerning the use of Facebook Data and the Facebook Marketplace service
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院总法务官 Rantos 发表意见，建议驳回 Meta 爱尔兰公司针对欧盟委员会反垄断调查的上诉。该案涉及委员会要求 Meta 提供内部文件以调查其滥用市场支配地位（Facebook 数据及 Marketplace 服务）。总法务官认为普通法院此前裁定委员会的信息请求合理、必要且符合隐私保护，未发现法律错误，确认了委员会在竞争调查中的广泛取证权。
items.ai_summary_en: Advocate General Rantos has issued an Opinion recommending that the Court of Justice dismiss Meta Platforms Ireland’s appeals against General Court judgments. These judgments upheld the European Commission’s decisions requesting internal documents during an abuse of dominance investigation concerning Facebook Data and Marketplace services. The AG found no legal error in the General Court’s assessment that the information requests were necessary, proportionate, and compliant with privacy rights. This Opinion reinforces the Commission’s broad investigative powers under EU competition procedural regulations, confirming that regulators may request extensive internal records without proving the relevance of each individual document, provided adequate safeguards for sensitive personal data are implemented.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国科技企业在欧面临类似反垄断调查时，需重视数据合规与配合义务。应提前建立内部文档分级管理机制，评估跨境数据传输风险，并在诉讼中关注程序性抗辩的边界，避免因阻碍调查引发更严厉处罚。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants subject to EU competition investigations should prioritize robust internal data governance to ensure compliant responses to regulatory document requests. Establishing clear protocols for document classification and cross-border data transfer risks is essential to mitigate penalties for obstructing inquiries. While companies may leverage privacy protections as a procedural defense, they must balance this with cooperation obligations. Legal teams should carefully monitor the boundaries of procedural challenges to avoid escalating enforcement actions, ensuring that claims regarding data sensitivity are substantiated by concrete technical and organizational safeguards.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["总法务官建议维持原判，驳回 Meta 针对欧盟委员会信息请求的上诉", "确认委员会在反垄断调查中拥有广泛权力，无需逐项证明文件相关性", "裁定信息请求程序已充分保障敏感个人数据隐私与正当行政原则"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Advocate General recommends dismissing Meta’s appeals, upholding the General Court’s validation of the Commission’s information requests.", "The Commission possesses broad investigative powers in antitrust probes and is not required to demonstrate the specific relevance of every requested document individually.", "Information requests must comply with privacy principles and good administration, which was satisfied here through established virtual data room safeguards."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["强化内部数据治理，确保能合规响应欧盟监管机构的文件调取需求", "关注竞争调查程序边界，合理利用隐私保护原则进行合法抗辩"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strengthen internal data governance frameworks to ensure compliant and efficient responses to EU regulatory document demands.", "Practice note: Monitor the procedural boundaries of competition investigations to utilize privacy principles for legitimate, targeted defenses.", "What to monitor: Develop internal document classification systems and assess cross-border data transfer risks to prevent obstruction allegations."]
core_points_en: The Advocate General recommends dismissing Meta’s appeals, upholding the General Court’s validation of the Commission’s information requests.
The Commission possesses broad investigative powers in antitrust probes and is not required to demonstrate the specific relevance of every requested document individually.
Information requests must comply with privacy principles and good administration, which was satisfied here through established virtual data room safeguards.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strengthen internal data governance frameworks to ensure compliant and efficient responses to EU regulatory document demands.
Practice note: Monitor the procedural boundaries of competition investigations to utilize privacy principles for legitimate, targeted defenses.
What to monitor: Develop internal document classification systems and assess cross-border data transfer risks to prevent obstruction allegations.

items.title: Judgment of the Court of Justice in Case C-337/22 P EUIPO v Nowhere
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院裁定脱欧后纯英国权利不得支持欧盟商标异议
items.title_en: CJEU Rules UK-Only Rights Cannot Support EU Trademark Oppositions Post-Brexit Transition
items.summary: Brexit and EU trade marks: Earlier rights based solely on UK law can no longer be relied upon in support of an opposition where EUIPO rules after the end of the transition period
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院撤销普通法院判决，确立关键规则：若欧盟知识产权局在过渡期结束后作出决定，仅基于英国法律的在先未注册商标权不得作为异议依据。法院强调，在先权利不仅需在申请日前存在，还必须在局方作出决定时依然有效并能禁止他人使用，否则异议不能成立。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the European Union has set aside the General Court’s judgment in Case C-337/22 P, EUIPO v Nowhere. The Court established that earlier rights based solely on United Kingdom law cannot serve as the basis for an opposition against an EU trademark application if the European Union Intellectual Property Office renders its decision after the expiry of the Brexit transition period. Although the trademark application was filed before the UK’s withdrawal, the CJEU clarified that reliance on unregistered signs requires not only prior acquisition of rights but also their continued validity at the time of the EUIPO’s decision. Since UK-specific rights ceased to confer protection within the EU jurisdiction post-transition, they no longer satisfy the requirement to prohibit the use of a later mark. This ruling upholds the EUIPO’s original rejection of the opposition, reinforcing strict temporal and territorial limits on prior rights in EU proceedings.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局商标时，若遭遇基于纯英国权利的异议，可援引此判例抗辩。反之，若依赖英国权利保护欧盟市场，需意识到其效力已随脱欧终结，应尽快重新评估并补充注册欧盟商标以确保持续保护。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment clarifies critical procedural requirements for opposing EU trademarks using non-EU prior rights. Rights holders relying on pre-Brexit UK unregistered marks must recognize that such rights no longer provide a valid basis for oppositions decided after the transition period ended. For applicants facing such objections, this precedent offers a robust defense against outdated or territorially invalid claims. Market participants should audit existing portfolios to identify gaps where UK-based protections previously covered EU markets. Proactive registration strategies within the EU system are essential to maintain comprehensive coverage, as automatic cross-border effects from UK rights have been definitively severed by this legal interpretation.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["异议依据的在先权利必须在局方作出决定时依然有效存续", "仅基于英国法律的权利在脱欧过渡期结束后无法支持欧盟异议", "欧盟法院推翻普通法院判决，维持欧盟知识产权局原驳回决定"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Prior rights invoked in opposition must remain valid and enforceable at the exact time the EUIPO issues its final decision.", "Rights derived exclusively from UK national law cannot support EU trademark oppositions once the Brexit transition period has expired.", "The CJEU overturned the General Court, confirming that the EUIPO correctly rejected the opposition based on lapsed UK-only protections."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用该判例对抗基于纯英国权利的恶意或过时欧盟商标异议", "脱欧后英国权利不再自动覆盖欧盟，需独立布局欧盟商标体系"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Applicants can challenge oppositions based on UK-only rights if the EUIPO decision occurs post-transition, leveraging this binding precedent.", "Practice note: Opponents must ensure prior rights are valid in an EU member state at the time of the EUIPO decision; UK rights alone are insufficient.", "What to monitor: Future cases defining 'validity' of unregistered rights in other third countries post-withdrawal agreements or similar geopolitical shifts."]
core_points_en: Prior rights invoked in opposition must remain valid and enforceable at the exact time the EUIPO issues its final decision.
Rights derived exclusively from UK national law cannot support EU trademark oppositions once the Brexit transition period has expired.
The CJEU overturned the General Court, confirming that the EUIPO correctly rejected the opposition based on lapsed UK-only protections.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Applicants can challenge oppositions based on UK-only rights if the EUIPO decision occurs post-transition, leveraging this binding precedent.
Practice note: Opponents must ensure prior rights are valid in an EU member state at the time of the EUIPO decision; UK rights alone are insufficient.
What to monitor: Future cases defining 'validity' of unregistered rights in other third countries post-withdrawal agreements or similar geopolitical shifts.

items.title: Advocate General's Opinion in Case C-876/24 Vueling Airlines (Court having jurisdiction in the event of an online contract for national carriage by air)
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院：在线购票地非乘客住所决定航空管辖权
items.title_en: AG Opinion: Passenger Residence Does Not Determine Jurisdiction for Online Air Ticket Contracts
items.summary: Jurisdiction: according to Advocate General Spielmann, in the case of the online purchase of an airline ticket, the passenger's place of residence cannot determine the courts having jurisdiction for the place where the carrier has a place of business through which the air transport contract has been made
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院总法律顾问就 Vueling 航空案发表意见，明确《蒙特利尔公约》适用于欧盟境内航空运输。意见指出，在线购票时乘客住所地不能作为确定承运人营业地法院管辖权的依据。建议将管辖法院定为承运人办理登机及行李托运手续的机场所在地法院，以统一裁判标准。
items.ai_summary_en: Advocate General Spielmann has issued an Opinion in Case C-876/24 concerning jurisdiction for online air transport contracts within the EU. The Opinion clarifies that the Montreal Convention rules apply to domestic carriage between Member States. Crucially, it establishes that a passenger’s place of residence cannot serve as the basis for determining jurisdiction via the carrier’s place of business when tickets are purchased online. Instead, the AG proposes that jurisdiction lies with the courts of the airport where the carrier performs check-in services for passengers and baggage. This interpretation aims to unify legal standards and provide certainty for carriers operating digital sales channels across Europe.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国航司及 OTA 平台在欧运营需关注管辖权新规，避免在乘客住所地被诉。建议在用户协议中明确争议解决地，并优化登机流程记录，以便在行李丢失等纠纷中快速锁定有利管辖法院，降低应诉成本。
items.ai_insight_en: This Opinion significantly impacts airlines and online travel agencies operating in the European market by limiting forum shopping based on consumer residence. For dispute resolution involving baggage loss or service failures, parties should prioritize identifying the specific airport handling check-in procedures. Legal teams should review terms of service to align with this emerging interpretation, ensuring that contractual clauses do not inadvertently expand jurisdictional exposure. Proactive documentation of check-in locations will be essential for defending against claims filed in unfavorable jurisdictions, thereby reducing litigation costs and procedural uncertainty.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["蒙特利尔公约规则适用于欧盟成员国境内的航空运输案件", "乘客在线购票的住所地不构成承运人营业地法院的管辖依据", "管辖权应归属于承运人实际办理乘客及行李登机手续的机场地"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Montreal Convention jurisdiction rules explicitly apply to air transport operations occurring entirely within a single EU Member State.", "A passenger's residence does not establish jurisdiction over the carrier's place of business for contracts concluded via online platforms.", "Jurisdiction is properly vested in the courts located at the airport where passenger and baggage check-in services are performed."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["在线销售不应默认接受乘客住所地法院的潜在管辖风险", "明确登机服务发生地是应对跨境行李索赔的关键抗辩点"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Airlines and OTAs must reassess jurisdictional risks, as passenger residence no longer automatically triggers local court competence for online sales.", "Practice note: Identify and document the specific airport handling check-in services to establish the correct venue for potential baggage or contract disputes.", "What to monitor: The final CJEU judgment in Case C-876/24, which will confirm whether the proposed airport-based jurisdiction standard becomes binding EU law."]
core_points_en: Montreal Convention jurisdiction rules explicitly apply to air transport operations occurring entirely within a single EU Member State.
A passenger's residence does not establish jurisdiction over the carrier's place of business for contracts concluded via online platforms.
Jurisdiction is properly vested in the courts located at the airport where passenger and baggage check-in services are performed.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Airlines and OTAs must reassess jurisdictional risks, as passenger residence no longer automatically triggers local court competence for online sales.
Practice note: Identify and document the specific airport handling check-in services to establish the correct venue for potential baggage or contract disputes.
What to monitor: The final CJEU judgment in Case C-876/24, which will confirm whether the proposed airport-based jurisdiction standard becomes binding EU law.

items.title: What happens to global SEP strategy if Wilus wins US injunction?
items.title_zh: 若 Wilus 获美禁令，全球 SEP 战略将如何演变？
items.title_en: Global SEP Strategy Shifts If Wilus Secures US Injunction and Restores Enforcement Venue
items.summary: It could dramatically reshape global SEP litigation by restoring the US as a top enforcement venue
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文探讨若 Wilus 在美国成功获得标准必要专利（SEP）禁令，将如何重塑全球诉讼格局。分析指出，此举可能恢复美国作为顶级执行地的地位，迫使全球权利人与实施者重新评估其许可谈判策略及管辖地选择，进而影响包括欧洲在内的全球 SEP 生态体系。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the potential transformation of global standard-essential patent litigation should Wilus successfully obtain an injunction in the United States. Such a ruling could significantly restore the status of US courts as premier venues for enforcing SEP rights, altering the current landscape dominated by other jurisdictions. The outcome would likely compel both rights holders and implementers worldwide to reassess their licensing negotiation strategies and jurisdictional choices. Consequently, this shift would impact the broader global SEP ecosystem, including European markets, by changing leverage dynamics in cross-border disputes. The article highlights how a favorable US decision could rebalance enforcement power, influencing where parties choose to litigate and settle complex SEP conflicts globally.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧运营需警惕美国判决的域外溢出效应。建议提前评估在美专利风险，优化全球许可布局，避免因地缘司法变动导致在欧洲市场的谈判筹码受损或面临双重诉讼压力。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating across jurisdictions should anticipate the extraterritorial ripple effects of significant US SEP rulings. A decisive US injunction could alter negotiation leverage in Europe, potentially exposing companies to dual litigation pressures or diminished bargaining power. It is prudent to evaluate patent exposure in the US early within the licensing cycle. Stakeholders should develop defensive strategies that account for divergent judicial approaches in key markets like the US and Europe. Monitoring these developments helps mitigate risks associated with geopolitical judicial shifts and ensures robust positioning in global FRAND negotiations.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Wilus 若获美禁令将显著提升美国法院执行力", "全球 SEP 诉讼重心可能向美国市场发生转移", "权利人需据此调整跨国许可与诉讼组合策略"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["A successful US injunction for Wilus would substantially enhance the enforceability and strategic importance of US courts in SEP disputes.", "The center of gravity for global SEP litigation may shift toward the US market, altering traditional venue preferences for major stakeholders.", "Rights holders must adapt their multinational licensing frameworks and litigation portfolios to align with evolving US judicial enforcement trends."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕美国禁令对全球许可谈判的连锁反应", "需构建兼顾欧美司法特点的防御性专利策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Anticipate how US injunctive relief may diminish negotiation leverage in European SEP discussions.", "Practice note: Develop integrated defense strategies addressing distinct judicial standards in both US and European forums.", "What to monitor: Track the progression of the Wilus case and its immediate impact on global forum selection tactics."]
core_points_en: A successful US injunction for Wilus would substantially enhance the enforceability and strategic importance of US courts in SEP disputes.
The center of gravity for global SEP litigation may shift toward the US market, altering traditional venue preferences for major stakeholders.
Rights holders must adapt their multinational licensing frameworks and litigation portfolios to align with evolving US judicial enforcement trends.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Anticipate how US injunctive relief may diminish negotiation leverage in European SEP discussions.
Practice note: Develop integrated defense strategies addressing distinct judicial standards in both US and European forums.
What to monitor: Track the progression of the Wilus case and its immediate impact on global forum selection tactics.

items.title: The never-ending story of Brexit – The Final Chapter
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院裁定：商标异议中在先权利须存续至决定作出时
items.title_en: CJEU Rules: Earlier Rights Must Exist Until EUIPO Decision in Opposition Proceedings
items.summary: The Court of Justice of the EU (“CJEU”) has rendered its long-awaited decision in EUIPO v Nowhere (C-337/22), disagreeing with the Advocate General’s (“AG”) opinion. In short, the CJEU held that, in opposition proceedings, the earlier right must continue to exist until the time the European Intellectual Property Office (“EUIPO”) or its Board of Appeal (“BoA”) renders the decision.   Background In
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院在 EUIPO v Nowhere 案中作出终审判决，推翻总检察长意见。法院明确，在商标异议程序中，作为异议基础的在先权利必须持续有效，直至欧盟知识产权局或其上诉委员会作出最终决定之时。该裁决厘清了脱欧背景下英国商标在欧盟异议程序中的效力截止点，确立了以“决定作出时”为判断基准的关键规则，对后续类似跨境权利冲突案件具有指导意义。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the EU has issued its final judgment in EUIPO v Nowhere (C-337/22), departing from the Advocate General’s opinion. The Court clarified that, within opposition proceedings, the earlier right serving as the basis for opposition must remain valid and existing until the moment the European Union Intellectual Property Office or its Board of Appeal renders its decision. This ruling resolves significant uncertainty regarding the temporal validity of rights, particularly concerning United Kingdom trademarks following Brexit. By establishing that the critical assessment point is the date of the administrative decision rather than an earlier procedural stage, the CJEU provides a definitive standard for evaluating the continuity of earlier rights. This interpretation ensures legal certainty for all parties involved in cross-border intellectual property disputes within the EU framework.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧进行商标布局或应对异议时，需动态监控英国等第三国在先权利状态。若依赖此类权利提出异议，必须确保其在欧盟官方作出决定前依然有效，避免因权利中途失效导致异议失败，建议提前制定备选维权方案。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders relying on third-country marks, such as those from the UK, to oppose EU applications must ensure these rights remain in force throughout the entire administrative process. Since the validity is assessed at the time of the final decision, any lapse or cancellation before that date will invalidate the opposition grounds. Practitioners should implement robust monitoring systems to track the status of cited earlier rights dynamically. It is advisable to prepare alternative legal strategies or supplementary evidence in case the primary earlier right expires during lengthy proceedings, thereby mitigating the risk of opposition failure due to loss of standing.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟法院裁定在先权利须存续至异议决定作出之时", "判决推翻总检察长意见，确立新的程序性审查标准", "明确脱欧后英国商标在欧盟异议程序中的效力界限"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Earlier rights relied upon in opposition must remain valid until the EUIPO or Board of Appeal issues its final decision.", "The judgment overturns the Advocate General's opinion, establishing a new procedural standard for assessing rights continuity.", "The ruling clarifies the temporal limits of post-Brexit UK trademark validity within EU opposition procedures."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["依赖第三国权利异议需确保持续有效至决定作出", "应建立权利状态动态监控机制以防中途失效风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure third-country earlier rights remain valid until the EUIPO decision date to sustain opposition grounds.", "Practice note: Implement dynamic monitoring of cited rights' status and prepare fallback strategies against mid-process lapses.", "What to monitor: Changes in the legal status of UK and other non-EU earlier rights during pending opposition proceedings."]
core_points_en: Earlier rights relied upon in opposition must remain valid until the EUIPO or Board of Appeal issues its final decision.
The judgment overturns the Advocate General's opinion, establishing a new procedural standard for assessing rights continuity.
The ruling clarifies the temporal limits of post-Brexit UK trademark validity within EU opposition procedures.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure third-country earlier rights remain valid until the EUIPO decision date to sustain opposition grounds.
Practice note: Implement dynamic monitoring of cited rights' status and prepare fallback strategies against mid-process lapses.
What to monitor: Changes in the legal status of UK and other non-EU earlier rights during pending opposition proceedings.

items.title: Presidium announces establishment of a second panel at Düsseldorf Local Division
items.title_zh: UPC 主席团宣布杜塞尔多夫地方分院设立第二审判庭
items.title_en: UPC Presidium Establishes Second Panel at Düsseldorf Local Division Effective March 2026
items.summary: The Presidium of the Unified Patent Court (UPC) has decided the establishment of a second panel at Düsseldorf Local Division (DE) as of 1 March 2026
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）主席团决定自 2026 年 3 月 1 日起，在杜塞尔多夫地方分院设立第二个审判庭。该决定基于 2026 年 1 月 20 日的决议，旨在应对案件增长。Bérénice Thom 法官被任命为第二审判庭审判长，与 Ingo Rinken 法官共同履职；原第一审判庭由 Ronny Thomas 法官继续领导。此举标志着 UPC 在德国关键司法辖区的审理能力显著增强。
items.ai_summary_en: The Presidium of the Unified Patent Court (UPC) has officially decided to establish a second panel at the Düsseldorf Local Division, effective 1 March 2026. This structural expansion, formalized in Decision PR/20012026 dated 20 January 2026, aims to address increasing caseloads and enhance judicial capacity within this key European patent litigation hub. Ms. Bérénice Thom has been appointed as the Presiding Judge of the new Panel 2, serving alongside legally qualified judge Mr. Ingo Rinken. The existing Panel 1 will continue under the presidency of Mr. Ronny Thomas, joined by Ms. Jule Schumacher. This development significantly strengthens the UPC’s operational capabilities in Germany, reflecting the growing importance of the Düsseldorf venue for high-stakes patent disputes and ensuring more efficient case management for rights holders and defendants alike.
items.ai_insight_zh: 杜塞尔多夫是欧洲专利诉讼重镇，增设审判庭意味着案件处理效率提升但诉讼风险亦增。在欧中国企业需重新评估当地诉讼策略，加强专利稳定性分析，并密切关注新审判庭的判例倾向，以防范潜在侵权纠纷。
items.ai_insight_en: The addition of a second panel at the Düsseldorf Local Division signals increased litigation capacity, potentially accelerating case timelines and influencing strategic forum selection for market participants. Rights holders may benefit from faster resolutions, while defendants must prepare for heightened enforcement activity. Stakeholders should closely monitor the procedural preferences and substantive tendencies of the new panel, particularly under Presiding Judge Bérénice Thom. Proactive assessment of patent stability and early case evaluation are advisable to mitigate risks associated with this expanded judicial infrastructure in one of Europe’s most active patent jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 杜塞尔多夫分院将于 2026 年 3 月正式增设第二审判庭", "Bérénice Thom 获任第二审判庭审判长，强化法官资源配置", "此举措反映 UPC 案件量上升及德国作为专利诉讼中心的重要性"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["A second panel is established at the Düsseldorf Local Division effective 1 March 2026 to handle rising case volumes.", "Ms. Bérénice Thom is appointed Presiding Judge of Panel 2, joining Mr. Ingo Rinken on the newly formed bench.", "This expansion underscores Düsseldorf's critical role as a primary center for patent litigation within the UPC system."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["诉讼产能扩充可能加速案件审理，企业需提前做好应诉准备", "关注新任审判长背景，预判杜塞尔多夫分院未来裁判风格走向"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Expanded capacity may accelerate proceedings, requiring earlier preparation for potential infringement actions in Düsseldorf.", "Practice note: Evaluate patent portfolio strength and consider settlement strategies given the increased likelihood of rapid trial scheduling.", "What to monitor: Track initial rulings by Panel 2 to assess any distinct procedural or substantive trends under Presiding Judge Thom."]
core_points_en: A second panel is established at the Düsseldorf Local Division effective 1 March 2026 to handle rising case volumes.
Ms. Bérénice Thom is appointed Presiding Judge of Panel 2, joining Mr. Ingo Rinken on the newly formed bench.
This expansion underscores Düsseldorf's critical role as a primary center for patent litigation within the UPC system.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Expanded capacity may accelerate proceedings, requiring earlier preparation for potential infringement actions in Düsseldorf.
Practice note: Evaluate patent portfolio strength and consider settlement strategies given the increased likelihood of rapid trial scheduling.
What to monitor: Track initial rulings by Panel 2 to assess any distinct procedural or substantive trends under Presiding Judge Thom.

items.title: Article 33.1(b) UPCA: “same infringement” does not require identical accused products
items.title_zh: UPC 裁决：同一侵权指控无需涉案产品完全一致
items.title_en: UPC Ruling: Same Infringement Claim Does Not Require Identical Accused Products
items.summary: Valeo v Bosch – Local Division Paris, 17 February 2026 (UPC_CFI_1963/2025)
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）巴黎地方分部在 Valeo 诉 Bosch 案中裁定，依据《统一专利法院协议》第 33.1(b) 条，针对多被告的诉讼只需涉及“同一专利侵权”，不要求被告涉案产品、分销渠道或具体行为完全一致。法院驳回了博世关于管辖权的异议，强调灵活解释以避免多重诉讼和裁判冲突，确认集团内商业关系足以满足管辖条件。
items.ai_summary_en: In Valeo v Bosch, the Paris Local Division of the Unified Patent Court clarified Article 33.1(b) of the UPC Agreement regarding multi-defendant actions. The court rejected Bosch’s preliminary objection challenging internal competence, which argued that different entities were accused regarding distinct product variants and distribution channels. The judges held that 'same alleged infringement' refers to the violation of the same patent, not identical products or commercial conduct. This flexible interpretation aims to prevent multiple proceedings and irreconcilable decisions. The ruling confirms that a commercial relationship within a corporate group, combined with allegations of infringing the same patent, is sufficient to consolidate cases against multiple defendants, regardless of differences in specific product embodiments or roles in manufacturing and distribution.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临专利诉讼时，即便涉及不同产品 variant，仍可能因同一专利被合并起诉。建议出口企业统筹评估集团内各实体风险，避免因地域分散而误判管辖安全，需提前制定统一的欧洲专利抗辩与合规策略。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants facing patent litigation in Europe should recognize that the threshold for consolidating multi-defendant actions has been lowered. Even if different corporate entities handle distinct product variants or operate in separate regions, they may still be sued together for infringing the same patent. Companies must reassess the effectiveness of their current entity structures and risk isolation measures within Europe. It is crucial to evaluate potential liability across the entire corporate group rather than assuming geographic or product-based separation provides jurisdictional safety. Proactive coordination of defense strategies and compliance assessments across all relevant entities is essential to manage exposure effectively.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["同一侵权指侵犯同一专利，而非产品型号或分销链完全相同", "多被告只需存在商业关系且被控侵犯同一专利即可合并审理", "灵活解释管辖规则旨在避免多重诉讼及判决结果相互矛盾的风险"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Same infringement means violating the same patent, not requiring identical product models or distribution chains.", "Multiple defendants can be joined if they share a commercial relationship and are accused of infringing the same patent.", "Flexible jurisdictional interpretation prevents fragmented litigation and reduces risks of conflicting judicial outcomes."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["多被告合并诉讼门槛降低，集团内不同产品线易被一网打尽", "应重新评估在欧洲的实体布局与专利风险隔离措施的有效性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Lowered consolidation thresholds mean diverse product lines within a corporate group face unified litigation risks.", "Practice note: Re-evaluate European entity layouts and patent risk isolation strategies to ensure they remain effective under new precedents.", "What to monitor: Develop unified European patent defense and compliance frameworks to address cross-entity infringement allegations proactively."]
core_points_en: Same infringement means violating the same patent, not requiring identical product models or distribution chains.
Multiple defendants can be joined if they share a commercial relationship and are accused of infringing the same patent.
Flexible jurisdictional interpretation prevents fragmented litigation and reduces risks of conflicting judicial outcomes.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Lowered consolidation thresholds mean diverse product lines within a corporate group face unified litigation risks.
Practice note: Re-evaluate European entity layouts and patent risk isolation strategies to ensure they remain effective under new precedents.
What to monitor: Develop unified European patent defense and compliance frameworks to address cross-entity infringement allegations proactively.

items.title: UPC Court of Appeal: ATE insurance can eliminate the need for security for costs
items.title_zh: UPC 上诉院：事后保险可消除诉讼费用担保需求
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal: ATE Insurance Can Eliminate Need for Security for Costs
items.summary: On 18 February 2026, the UPC Court of Appeal set aside an order for security for costs of €2 million, holding that the Munich Local Division (Munich LD)...
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC 上诉院撤销慕尼黑地方分院要求原告提供 200 万欧元费用担保的命令。法院认定，下级法院未考量原告持有的高额“事后”（ATE）保险属于行使裁量权错误。该保险含反规避条款且承保方符合欧盟偿付能力标准，足以保障被告权益，故无需额外现金或银行担保。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal set aside a Munich Local Division order requiring a claimant to provide €2 million in security for costs. The appellate court held that the lower division erred by failing to consider the claimant’s existing After The Event (ATE) insurance when assessing the necessity of such security. The ATE policy, issued by an EU-based insurer with a €4 million limit and an anti-avoidance endorsement, was deemed sufficient to protect the defendant’s interests regarding potential costs orders. Consequently, the requirement for additional cash deposits or bank guarantees was removed. This decision clarifies that valid ATE insurance forms part of a claimant’s financial position and can effectively substitute traditional security measures under Rule 158.1 of the Rules of Procedure.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧涉诉时，应积极利用合规 ATE 保险抗辩高额费用担保，降低现金流压力。需确保保单包含反规避条款并选择欧盟持牌机构，以通过 UPC 审查，优化诉讼资金配置。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment significantly impacts litigation strategy before the Unified Patent Court by validating After The Event insurance as a viable alternative to substantial cash or bank guarantees. Rights holders can leverage compliant ATE policies to mitigate liquidity constraints while defending against security for costs applications. To succeed, applicants must ensure their policies include robust anti-avoidance endorsements and are issued by reputable EU-regulated insurers. This approach allows market participants to optimize capital allocation during patent proceedings without compromising the opposing party's ability to recover awarded costs.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["上诉院裁定 ATE 保险构成原告财务状况一部分，必须纳入担保必要性评估", "含反规避条款及欧盟合规承保方的保单，可有效替代传统现金或银行担保", "明确保险人单方解约权可通过等待期机制解决，不影响保单作为担保的效力"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["ATE insurance constitutes part of the claimant's financial position and must be considered when assessing security for costs.", "Policies with anti-avoidance endorsements from EU-compliant insurers can effectively replace traditional cash or bank guarantees.", "Insurer termination rights do not invalidate the policy as security if mechanisms like waiting periods are included."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["采购含反规避条款的欧盟合规 ATE 保险，可作为对抗费用担保的有力工具", "利用保险替代现金担保，显著降低中国企业参与欧洲专利诉讼的资金门槛"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Using compliant ATE insurance reduces upfront cash requirements for security for costs, preserving working capital for companies engaged in UPC litigation.", "Practice note: Ensure ATE policies include anti-avoidance endorsements and are issued by EU-regulated insurers to withstand judicial scrutiny under Rule 158.1 RoP.", "What to monitor: Future UPC decisions refining the criteria for acceptable ATE terms, particularly regarding insurer solvency and unilateral termination clauses."]
core_points_en: ATE insurance constitutes part of the claimant's financial position and must be considered when assessing security for costs.
Policies with anti-avoidance endorsements from EU-compliant insurers can effectively replace traditional cash or bank guarantees.
Insurer termination rights do not invalidate the policy as security if mechanisms like waiting periods are included.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Using compliant ATE insurance reduces upfront cash requirements for security for costs, preserving working capital for companies engaged in UPC litigation.
Practice note: Ensure ATE policies include anti-avoidance endorsements and are issued by EU-regulated insurers to withstand judicial scrutiny under Rule 158.1 RoP.
What to monitor: Future UPC decisions refining the criteria for acceptable ATE terms, particularly regarding insurer solvency and unilateral termination clauses.
