items.title: Exemptions and compensation schemes for commercial users: Private copying levies after the CJEU’s ruling in bluechip v ZPÜ (C-822/24)
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院裁定商业用户私人复制 levy 豁免与补偿条件
items.title_en: CJEU Clarifies Private Copying Levy Exemptions and Compensation Conditions for Commercial Users
items.summary: In bluechip Computer Aktiengesellschaft v Zentralstelle für Überspielungsrechte (ZPÜ), the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) further determines the conditions under which EU Member States can implement the exception or limitation for reproduction of protected works for private uses under art.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院在 bluechip v ZPÜ案中明确，成员国仅当设备被自然人用于非商业性私人复制时方可征收公平补偿金。法院指出，向商业终端用户销售设备可推定存在私人用途，但制造商若能举证反驳该推定则无需付费。此裁决厘清了信息社会指令下补偿金的征收边界与举证责任。
items.ai_summary_en: In Bluechip Computer Aktiengesellschaft v Zentralstelle für Überspielungsrechte (ZPÜ), the Court of Justice of the European Union clarified that Member States may only levy fair compensation under Article 5(2)(b) of the InfoSoc Directive when devices are used by natural persons for non-commercial private copying. The Court addressed whether a rebuttable presumption applies to devices sold to commercial end-users, assuming such devices facilitate private reproduction. It confirmed that while this presumption exists, manufacturers, importers, or traders can avoid liability if they provide evidence proving that the devices were not used for private copying on more than a small scale. This ruling defines the boundaries for collecting levies and establishes the specific burden of proof required from rights holders and device suppliers within the EU copyright framework.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧销售存储设备的中国企业应审查德国等国的 levy 缴纳记录，建立设备最终用户用途的证据留存机制，以应对可能的费用追缴或申请退费，避免双重成本。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants selling storage media in the EU should review their historical levy payment records, particularly in jurisdictions like Germany where presumptions favor collection from commercial sales channels. Establishing robust mechanisms to document final user purposes is critical for challenging these presumptions effectively. Companies should assess past transactions to identify potential overpayments and evaluate eligibility for reimbursement claims. Proactive evidence retention regarding customer usage profiles will help mitigate financial exposure and prevent double costs arising from conflicting national interpretations of private copying exceptions and compensation schemes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["仅限自然人非商业用途复制方可征收补偿金", "售予商业用户的设备存在私人用途的可反驳推定", "制造商需承担证明无需缴费的举证责任"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Fair compensation is strictly limited to reproductions made by natural persons for non-commercial private purposes.", "A rebuttable presumption exists that devices sold to commercial users facilitate private copying subject to levies.", "Manufacturers bear the burden of proof to demonstrate that devices were not used for private copying to avoid fees."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立商业客户用途证明档案以反驳征税推定", "核查历史订单评估潜在补偿金退还可能性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Manufacturers must implement systems to capture final user data to rebut statutory presumptions linking commercial sales to private copying.", "Practice note: Review historical sales and levy payments to identify opportunities for refund claims based on the clarified burden of proof standards.", "What to monitor: National implementations of the CJEU ruling regarding evidentiary thresholds for proving non-private use by commercial entities."]
core_points_en: Fair compensation is strictly limited to reproductions made by natural persons for non-commercial private purposes.
A rebuttable presumption exists that devices sold to commercial users facilitate private copying subject to levies.
Manufacturers bear the burden of proof to demonstrate that devices were not used for private copying to avoid fees.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Manufacturers must implement systems to capture final user data to rebut statutory presumptions linking commercial sales to private copying.
Practice note: Review historical sales and levy payments to identify opportunities for refund claims based on the clarified burden of proof standards.
What to monitor: National implementations of the CJEU ruling regarding evidentiary thresholds for proving non-private use by commercial entities.

items.title: Via adds two major Chinese auto suppliers to Qi patent pool
items.title_zh: Via 无线充电专利池新增两家中国主要汽车供应商
items.title_en: Via Adds Major Chinese Auto Suppliers to Qi SEP Pool as Powermat Expands Independent Licensing
items.summary: The programme gets fresh licensee endorsement while non-pool licensor Powermat steps up own licensing efforts
items.ai_summary_zh: Via Licensing 管理的 Qi 标准必要专利池获得两家中国主要汽车供应商的许可背书，显示该专利池在汽车行业渗透力增强。与此同时，非专利池成员 Powermat 正加强其独立许可计划。这一动态反映了无线充电领域 SEP 许可格局的演变，可能影响进入欧洲市场的中国汽车供应链企业的合规策略与授权成本结构。
items.ai_summary_en: Via Licensing’s Qi standard-essential patent pool has secured license endorsements from two major Chinese automotive suppliers, signaling strengthened penetration within the global automotive supply chain. This development highlights the growing acceptance of pooled licensing models for wireless charging technologies among key industry participants. Concurrently, non-pool licensor Powermat is intensifying its independent licensing efforts, creating a dual-track enforcement environment. These parallel dynamics illustrate the evolving landscape of SEP licensing in the wireless charging sector. For market participants, particularly those supplying European automakers, this shift necessitates careful evaluation of compliance strategies and potential authorization cost structures. The coexistence of pool-based and bilateral licensing approaches suggests a complex negotiation environment where rights holders are actively pursuing diverse monetization paths across different jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国车企及供应商应评估 Via 与 Powermat 双重许可压力，避免重复付费。建议提前开展 FTE 分析，利用 FRAND 原则谈判，并关注欧洲潜在反垄断监管动向以维护自身权益。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should assess exposure to dual licensing pressures from both Via and Powermat to mitigate risks of double-dipping or redundant royalty payments. Conducting thorough freedom-to-operate analyses early in product development cycles allows companies to leverage FRAND principles effectively during negotiations. Additionally, monitoring potential antitrust scrutiny by European regulators regarding cumulative royalty rates and licensing practices is crucial. Rights holders and implementers alike must navigate these overlapping claims carefully, utilizing EU competition law tools where appropriate to optimize licensing terms and ensure compliant market access without excessive financial burden.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Via 专利池获两家中国头部汽配商加入，扩大行业覆盖", "非池内权利人 Powermat 同步强化独立许可攻势", "汽车领域无线充电 SEP 许可竞争态势日趋复杂"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Via Licensing expands Qi patent pool coverage by adding two leading Chinese automotive component suppliers as new licensees.", "Non-pool licensor Powermat simultaneously strengthens its independent licensing program, increasing competitive pressure on implementers.", "The wireless charging SEP licensing landscape becomes increasingly complex with concurrent pool and bilateral enforcement strategies."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需警惕多重许可主张，建立内部 SEP 风险评估机制", "善用 FRAND 抗辩与欧盟竞争法工具优化许可条款"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Evaluate exposure to dual licensing demands from Via and Powermat to prevent duplicate payments and manage budget forecasts.", "Practice note: Implement internal SEP risk assessment mechanisms and utilize FRAND defenses alongside EU competition law arguments to negotiate favorable terms.", "What to monitor: Track regulatory developments concerning cumulative royalties and antitrust investigations into wireless charging SEP licensing practices in Europe."]
core_points_en: Via Licensing expands Qi patent pool coverage by adding two leading Chinese automotive component suppliers as new licensees.
Non-pool licensor Powermat simultaneously strengthens its independent licensing program, increasing competitive pressure on implementers.
The wireless charging SEP licensing landscape becomes increasingly complex with concurrent pool and bilateral enforcement strategies.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Evaluate exposure to dual licensing demands from Via and Powermat to prevent duplicate payments and manage budget forecasts.
Practice note: Implement internal SEP risk assessment mechanisms and utilize FRAND defenses alongside EU competition law arguments to negotiate favorable terms.
What to monitor: Track regulatory developments concerning cumulative royalties and antitrust investigations into wireless charging SEP licensing practices in Europe.

items.title: Amplifying the fight against organised crime: EMPACT Communicators Network at EUIPO
items.title_zh: 强化打击有组织犯罪：EMPACT 传播者网络会议在欧盟知识产权局召开
items.title_en: EUIPO Hosts EMPACT Communicators Network to Strengthen Fight Against Organised Crime
items.summary: Amplifying the fight against organised crime: EMPACT Communicators Network at EUIPO
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局在阿利坎特主办 EMPACT 传播者网络会议，汇聚欧盟各国沟通专家，旨在加强针对严重有组织犯罪的联合行动宣传。会议聚焦提升 EMPACT 作为欧盟执法合作基石的可见度，涵盖打击贩运、欺诈及知识产权犯罪等领域，以巩固公众信心并支持 2026-2029 新运营周期。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) hosted the EMPACT Communicators Network meeting in Alicante, gathering communication experts from across the EU. The primary objective was to enhance public understanding of joint actions against serious and organised crime as EMPACT enters its new operational cycle for 2026–2029. Participants emphasized the critical role of effective communication in sustaining political momentum, supporting operational excellence, and reinforcing public confidence. A key focus was increasing the visibility of EMPACT as a cornerstone of EU law enforcement cooperation. The network discussed strategies to align messages and share best practices regarding coordinated investigations targeting trafficking, fraud, cybercrime, and intellectual property crime. By clarifying how EU cooperation translates into concrete results, the initiative aims to strengthen trust among institutions and citizens while ensuring operational achievements are widely understood beyond specialist audiences.
items.ai_insight_zh: 欧盟正升级跨机构执法协作与舆论引导，知识产权犯罪打击力度将增强。在欧中企需高度重视合规风险，避免卷入有组织犯罪链条；同时应关注欧盟执法新动向，配合官方行动以维护自身品牌权益与市场安全。
items.ai_insight_en: The EU is intensifying cross-agency law enforcement collaboration and strategic communication, signaling heightened scrutiny and transparency in combating organized crime, including intellectual property violations. For rights holders and market participants operating in Europe, this shift underscores the importance of robust compliance frameworks to mitigate risks associated with being inadvertently linked to criminal networks. Companies should proactively monitor evolving enforcement priorities and cooperate with official initiatives to protect brand integrity and market security. Enhanced visibility of enforcement actions suggests that authorities will more aggressively publicize successes, potentially increasing pressure on supply chains to demonstrate due diligence against IP infringement and related illicit activities.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟知识产权局主办 EMPACT 网络会议，强化打击有组织犯罪沟通策略", "会议旨在提升欧盟执法合作可见度，涵盖知识产权犯罪等关键领域", "通过协调信息与分享最佳实践，支持 EMPACT2026 至 2029 新运营周期"]

items.title: BREAKING: Ericsson sues Verifone at UPC in first IoT SEP enforcement actions
items.title_zh: 爱立信在 UPC 起诉 Verifone，启动首例物联网 SEP 执法
items.title_en: Ericsson Sues Verifone at UPC in First IoT SEP Enforcement Action
items.summary: SEP owner has asked Mannheim Local Division to determine whether its offer is FRAND
items.ai_summary_zh: 标准必要专利持有人爱立信已向统一专利法院（UPC）曼海姆地方分院提起诉讼，指控 Verifone 侵权。此案标志着 UPC 受理的首批物联网领域 SEP 执法行动。核心诉求在于请求法院裁定爱立信提出的许可要约是否符合公平、合理及无歧视（FRAND）原则，这将测试新法院在处理复杂许可纠纷上的效能。
items.ai_summary_en: Standard essential patent holder Ericsson has initiated infringement proceedings against Verifone before the Unified Patent Court. Filed at the Mannheim Local Division, this case represents the first enforcement action concerning Internet of Things standard essential patents within the new court system. The core legal request asks the tribunal to determine whether Ericsson’s licensing offer complies with fair, reasonable, and non-discriminatory principles. This litigation serves as a critical test for the UPC’s capacity to handle complex global licensing disputes. It highlights the court's emerging role as a central venue for resolving high-stakes intellectual property conflicts across Europe, setting important precedents for future technology sector litigation involving connectivity standards.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国 IoT 企业需警惕 UPC 成为 SEP 诉讼新高地，其裁决具泛欧效力。建议提前评估专利风险，制定符合 FRAND 原则的谈判策略，避免在德国等地遭遇禁令威胁，并密切关注曼海姆分院的裁判风向。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in the connected device sector should recognize the UPC as an increasingly influential forum for SEP disputes, given its pan-European injunctive relief capabilities. Rights holders and implementers must ensure their licensing negotiations strictly adhere to FRAND obligations to mitigate litigation risks. Companies should proactively assess their patent exposure and refine negotiation protocols to withstand judicial scrutiny, particularly in jurisdictions like Germany known for rigorous enforcement. Monitoring early procedural developments and substantive rulings from the Mannheim Local Division is essential for adapting corporate IP strategies to this new legal landscape effectively.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["爱立信发起 UPC 首例物联网 SEP 诉讼，被告为 Verifone", "案件由曼海姆地方分院审理，聚焦许可要约的 FRAND 合规性", "此举确立 UPC 作为欧洲 SEP 争议解决新核心平台的地位"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Ericsson filed the first IoT SEP lawsuit at the UPC against Verifone, marking a significant milestone in European patent enforcement history.", "The Mannheim Local Division will adjudicate the FRAND compliance of Ericsson's licensing offer, focusing on equitable negotiation standards.", "This action establishes the UPC as a primary platform for resolving SEP disputes, influencing future European intellectual property litigation strategies."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC 首案确立新范式，中国出口企业面临泛欧禁令风险", "需优化 FRAND 谈判合规性，以应对德国分院的严格审查"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: The UPC offers pan-European injunctive relief, significantly increasing leverage for SEP owners and risk for implementers.", "Practice note: Parties must rigorously document FRAND-compliant negotiation steps to defend against potential injunctions in strict jurisdictions.", "What to monitor: Early case management decisions and substantive rulings from the Mannheim Local Division regarding SEP licensing norms."]
core_points_en: Ericsson filed the first IoT SEP lawsuit at the UPC against Verifone, marking a significant milestone in European patent enforcement history.
The Mannheim Local Division will adjudicate the FRAND compliance of Ericsson's licensing offer, focusing on equitable negotiation standards.
This action establishes the UPC as a primary platform for resolving SEP disputes, influencing future European intellectual property litigation strategies.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: The UPC offers pan-European injunctive relief, significantly increasing leverage for SEP owners and risk for implementers.
Practice note: Parties must rigorously document FRAND-compliant negotiation steps to defend against potential injunctions in strict jurisdictions.
What to monitor: Early case management decisions and substantive rulings from the Mannheim Local Division regarding SEP licensing norms.

items.title: Working worse is no defence: a recent UPC order reinforces feature-based claim scope (UPC_CFI_630/2025)
items.title_zh: 功能实现不足非抗辩理由：UPC 重申基于特征的权利要求范围
items.title_en: Inferior Functionality No Defense: UPC Reinforces Feature-Based Claim Scope in Preliminary Injunctions
items.summary: Court docket: LD Düsseldorf
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC 杜塞尔多夫分院在临时禁令程序中裁定，若被控产品具备权利要求所有结构特征，即使未完全实现专利所述技术优势，仍构成侵权。法院强调权利要求解释需结合说明书，且被告在临时程序中承担专利无效的详细举证责任，若翻译争议未充分论证将采信原告版本。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Düsseldorf Local Division ruled in preliminary injunction proceedings that a device claim’s scope covers embodiments possessing all claimed structural features, even if they do not fully realize the technical advantages described in the patent specification. The Court emphasized that claim construction must be interpreted in light of the description and drawings, rather than isolating claim features. Furthermore, the decision clarifies procedural burdens: applicants must promptly respond to contested facts with sufficient certainty for provisional measures, as summary proceedings allow limited fact-finding. Defendants bear the burden of proving invalidity and must provide detailed reasoning for any disputed prior art translations; failure to do so may result in the Court adopting the applicant’s translation version.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应对 UPC 临时禁令时，仅主张产品功能差异难获支持，必须聚焦结构特征比对。挑战专利有效性时需准备高质量的现有技术翻译及详细论证，避免因举证不足导致不利推定。
items.ai_insight_en: In UPC preliminary injunction proceedings, relying solely on arguments regarding inferior product functionality is unlikely to succeed against a patentee asserting structural infringement. Rights holders should focus litigation strategies on demonstrating the presence of all claimed structural features, supported by the specification. Conversely, alleged infringers challenging validity must prepare high-quality translations of prior art documents accompanied by rigorous legal and technical arguments. Insufficient substantiation of translation disputes or invalidity grounds carries significant risk of unfavorable presumptions, given the limited scope for further evidence gathering in expedited procedures.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["装置权利要求涵盖具备结构特征但未完全实现功能的劣等实施例", "临时禁令程序中事实澄清空间有限，申请人须及时回应被告异议", "被告对专利无效负有详尽举证责任，翻译争议需提供具体理由"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Device claims cover inferior embodiments with all structural features present, regardless of incomplete functional realization or technical advantage.", "Summary proceedings limit factual clarification; applicants must immediately substantiate responses to defendant contestations with required certainty.", "Defendants bear the burden of proving invalidity and must provide detailed justification for disputed prior art translations to avoid adverse findings."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["抗辩策略应从功能差异转向结构特征缺失或现有技术抗辩", "无效举证须包含精准翻译与详尽理由，否则面临败诉风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Shift defense strategies from functional differences to challenging structural feature presence or raising robust prior art defenses.", "Practice note: Ensure invalidity challenges include precise prior art translations and comprehensive reasoning to meet the strict burden of proof.", "What to monitor: How the UPC applies these claim construction principles across different Local Divisions in future expedited infringement cases."]
core_points_en: Device claims cover inferior embodiments with all structural features present, regardless of incomplete functional realization or technical advantage.
Summary proceedings limit factual clarification; applicants must immediately substantiate responses to defendant contestations with required certainty.
Defendants bear the burden of proving invalidity and must provide detailed justification for disputed prior art translations to avoid adverse findings.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Shift defense strategies from functional differences to challenging structural feature presence or raising robust prior art defenses.
Practice note: Ensure invalidity challenges include precise prior art translations and comprehensive reasoning to meet the strict burden of proof.
What to monitor: How the UPC applies these claim construction principles across different Local Divisions in future expedited infringement cases.

items.title: World Intellectual Property Report: Pace of Innovation Diffusion Reaches Historical Heights, But Access, Usage and Capability Gaps Remain
items.title_zh: WIPO 报告：技术扩散速度创历史新高，但应用差距仍存
items.title_en: WIPO Report: Innovation Diffusion Hits Record Pace, Yet Access and Capability Gaps Persist
items.summary: New technologies are spreading across borders at unprecedented pace and gaps are narrowing in how intensively countries are using the innovations, the latest World Intellectual Property Report (WIPR) 2026, Technology on the Move finds.
items.ai_summary_zh: 世界知识产权组织发布《2026 年世界知识产权报告》，指出新技术跨境传播速度达到历史峰值，各国在创新使用强度上的差距正在缩小。然而，报告同时警示，不同国家在获取技术、实际 usage 及创新能力建设方面仍存在显著鸿沟，需政策干预以促进均衡。
items.ai_summary_en: The World Intellectual Property Organization has released the 2026 World Intellectual Property Report, titled Technology on the Move. The report indicates that new technologies are spreading across international borders at an unprecedented historical pace. Concurrently, disparities regarding the intensity with which different nations utilize these innovations are narrowing significantly. However, the analysis also issues a cautionary note, highlighting that substantial gaps remain concerning technology access, practical application, and the development of innovation capabilities across various countries. These persistent structural divides suggest that policy interventions are necessary to promote more balanced global technological adoption and ensure equitable participation in the modern innovation ecosystem.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应利用技术扩散加速窗口期，优化欧洲专利布局。同时需关注目标市场的能力建设缺口，通过技术合作或本地化运营弥补应用差距，避免单纯依赖出口模式。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should leverage the current window of accelerated technology diffusion to optimize their European patent portfolios strategically. It is critical to address capability gaps in target markets by fostering local technical collaborations rather than relying solely on export models. Companies must assess local application strengths to bridge usage divides effectively. This approach ensures robust IP protection while enhancing market penetration through localized operational strategies and cooperative frameworks that align with regional innovation ecosystems.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["新技术跨境扩散速度达到前所未有的历史高点", "各国间创新使用强度的差距呈现收窄趋势", "技术获取渠道与应用能力差距依然是主要挑战"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Cross-border diffusion of new technologies has reached an unprecedented historical peak in speed and reach.", "Disparities in the intensity of innovation usage among different countries are showing a clear narrowing trend.", "Significant challenges persist regarding technology access channels and overall application capabilities."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["把握技术快速扩散机遇，加速高价值专利海外布局", "针对应用能力短板，深化在欧洲的本地化技术合作"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Accelerate overseas deployment of high-value patents to capitalize on rapid technology diffusion opportunities.", "Practice note: Deepen localized technical cooperation in Europe to address specific application capability shortcomings.", "What to monitor: Evolving policy interventions aimed at reducing global technology access and usage disparities."]
core_points_en: Cross-border diffusion of new technologies has reached an unprecedented historical peak in speed and reach.
Disparities in the intensity of innovation usage among different countries are showing a clear narrowing trend.
Significant challenges persist regarding technology access channels and overall application capabilities.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Accelerate overseas deployment of high-value patents to capitalize on rapid technology diffusion opportunities.
Practice note: Deepen localized technical cooperation in Europe to address specific application capability shortcomings.
What to monitor: Evolving policy interventions aimed at reducing global technology access and usage disparities.

items.title: Unitary effect requires designation of all participating Member States
items.title_zh: UPC 裁定：单一专利效力须涵盖所有参与成员国
items.title_en: UPC Confirms Unitary Effect Requires Designation of All Participating Member States
items.summary: Papst Licensing v EPO – Court of Appeal, 9 February 2026, UPC_CoA_8/2026
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC 上诉院驳回 Papst Licensing 上诉，维持 EPO 拒绝 EP3327608 单一专利效力的决定。法院指出，因申请时马耳他未加入 EPC 导致授权文本缺失该国，不符合“所有参与国权利要求一致”的法定要件。裁决强调单一专利不可领土分割，并提示黑山未来入欧可能引发类似合规风险。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal dismissed Papst Licensing’s appeal, upholding the EPO’s refusal to register unitary effect for EP 3 327 608. The patent originated from a 2005 application filed before Malta joined the EPC, resulting in a granted text that excluded Malta. By the time of grant in 2025, Malta was a participating Member State. The Court ruled that Article 3(1) of Regulation 1257/2012 strictly requires the same claims for all participating states, prohibiting territorial carve-outs. Arguments based on proportionality or fundamental rights were rejected as contra legem interpretations. This decision clarifies that historical designation gaps prevent unitary effect, even if unintentional. While currently rare due to Malta’s early EPC accession, similar compliance risks may arise if Montenegro joins the EU and UPCA, given its later EPC entry in 2022.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局长龄专利家族时，需核查早期申请是否遗漏现参与国。若存在领土缺失，将无法转化为单一专利，建议提前评估转为传统欧洲专利束或调整诉讼策略以规避效力瑕疵。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders managing legacy patent portfolios must verify whether early applications predate the EPC membership of current unitary participating states. If a participating state was not designated due to historical non-membership, the patent cannot obtain unitary effect. Stakeholders should assess such assets for conversion into traditional European patent bundles to ensure enforceability. Proactive audits of long-standing patent families are essential to identify territorial coverage gaps. For affected patents, alternative litigation strategies or licensing structures should be developed to mitigate validity risks associated with the lack of unitary status.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["单一专利生效前提必须是授权文本涵盖所有参与成员国", "即使因历史原因缺失某国，也不允许通过解释变通获得部分效力", "此类“马耳他情形”目前罕见，但未来黑山入欧后或重现类似障碍"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Unitary effect mandates that the European patent be granted with identical claims covering all participating Member States without exception.", "Historical inability to designate a state at filing does not justify partial unitary effect or territorial carve-outs under current regulations.", "Future EU accession by EPC members like Montenegro could recreate this eligibility barrier for older patent families lacking prior designations."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["核查旧案申请日与各国加入 EPC 时间，识别潜在领土覆盖缺口", "针对无法获单一专利的存量资产，制定差异化维权或许可方案"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Verify if legacy patent applications predate the EPC accession of current unitary participating states to avoid registration refusals.", "Practice note: Convert affected patents to traditional European patent bundles and adjust enforcement strategies to address territorial coverage gaps.", "What to monitor: Potential future EU and UPCA accession by EPC members like Montenegro, which may trigger similar designation issues for newer filings."]
core_points_en: Unitary effect mandates that the European patent be granted with identical claims covering all participating Member States without exception.
Historical inability to designate a state at filing does not justify partial unitary effect or territorial carve-outs under current regulations.
Future EU accession by EPC members like Montenegro could recreate this eligibility barrier for older patent families lacking prior designations.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Verify if legacy patent applications predate the EPC accession of current unitary participating states to avoid registration refusals.
Practice note: Convert affected patents to traditional European patent bundles and adjust enforcement strategies to address territorial coverage gaps.
What to monitor: Potential future EU and UPCA accession by EPC members like Montenegro, which may trigger similar designation issues for newer filings.

items.title: Oath taking of newly appointed legally qualified judges
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院五名新任法律法官举行就职宣誓
items.title_en: Oath taking of newly appointed legally qualified judges
items.summary: Oath taking of 5 newly appointed legally qualified judges will take place at the Local division Munich (DE), Local division The Hague (NL), the Nordic-Baltic Regional division, Local division Düsseldorf (DE) and Central division Munich (DE)
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）宣布五名新任法律合格法官将于各分部举行公开宣誓就职仪式。依据行政委员会2025年11月4日的决定，Ina Schnurr、Robert van Peursem等五人分别被任命至慕尼黑、海牙、北欧-波罗的海地区及杜塞尔多夫等地方法庭或中央法庭。根据《UPC规约》第6条，他们在正式履职前须在公开法庭宣誓，标志着UPC司法团队进一步扩充，旨在保障欧洲统一专利诉讼体系的顺利运行与案件审理能力。
items.ai_insight_zh: UPC司法团队的持续扩充表明其审理能力正在稳步提升，未来专利诉讼效率可能提高。中国出口企业应密切关注新任法官所在分部的管辖倾向，特别是慕尼黑和海牙等活跃法庭的司法实践。建议提前完善在欧洲的专利FTO分析，并针对UPC程序特点优化应诉策略，以应对潜在的高强度专利纠纷。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC五名新任法律法官将在德荷等地方法庭及中央法庭举行公开宣誓", "任命依据行政委员会2025年11月4日决定，涵盖慕尼黑、海牙等多个关键司法管辖区", "法官需依《UPC规约》第6条在公开法庭宣誓后方可正式履行司法职责"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC司法资源扩充预示诉讼效率提升，企业需警惕案件处理周期缩短", "关注新任法官所在地法庭的裁判倾向，针对性优化在欧洲的专利合规布局"]

items.title: Ericsson reveals market-by-market IoT approach, new healthcare/agriculture deals
items.title_zh: 爱立信披露分市场 IoT 策略及新医疗农业协议
items.title_en: Ericsson Unveils Market-Specific IoT Licensing Strategy and New Healthcare, Agriculture Deals
items.summary: New insights from Swedish company helps build picture of how leading licensors are tackling tricky and fragmented IoT markets
items.ai_summary_zh: 瑞典爱立信公司披露其针对碎片化物联网市场的逐国许可策略，并宣布在医疗与农业领域达成新交易。该动态揭示了主要 SEP 权利人如何应对复杂市场，为观察欧洲物联网领域的标准必要专利许可实践提供了关键视角。
items.ai_summary_en: Swedish technology giant Ericsson has disclosed its market-by-market approach to licensing Internet of Things (IoT) patents, alongside announcements of new agreements in the healthcare and agricultural sectors. This strategic shift provides critical insights into how leading standard-essential patent (SEP) holders are navigating the complex and highly fragmented IoT landscape. By tailoring licensing efforts to specific industry verticals and geographic markets, Ericsson demonstrates a methodical response to the challenges posed by diverse IoT implementations. These developments offer a significant perspective on current SEP licensing practices within Europe, highlighting how major licensors are adapting their commercial strategies to address the unique regulatory and technical requirements of distinct industrial sectors.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国 IoT 企业应关注爱立信分市场策略，评估出口欧洲的许可成本。建议提前布局医疗农业领域专利交叉许可，防范 SEP 诉讼风险，优化合规架构。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely analyze Ericsson’s segmented licensing model to anticipate potential fluctuations in European royalty rates and compliance costs. Companies operating in connected health or smart agriculture sectors must strengthen their patent reserves to better position themselves during future negotiations with SEP holders. Proactively assessing cross-licensing opportunities in these high-growth verticals can help mitigate litigation risks. Furthermore, firms should review their internal IP compliance architectures to ensure alignment with evolving industry-specific licensing norms, thereby optimizing cost structures and reducing exposure to enforcement actions in key export markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["爱立信采取按市场细分的物联网专利许可策略", "新增医疗健康与农业科技领域的专利授权合作", "揭示头部许可方应对碎片化市场的实际操作路径"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Ericsson employs a segmented, market-specific strategy for licensing its IoT-related patent portfolio across different regions.", "The company has secured new patent licensing collaborations specifically targeting the healthcare and agricultural technology sectors.", "These moves illustrate practical pathways for top-tier licensors to manage and monetize rights in fragmented IoT markets."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注分市场策略以预判欧洲许可成本波动", "加强医疗农业领域专利储备以应对许可谈判"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor segmented licensing strategies to forecast European royalty cost volatility and budget accordingly.", "Practice note: Enhance patent portfolios in healthcare and agriculture to strengthen leverage in upcoming SEP negotiations.", "What to monitor: Track new sector-specific licensing deals to identify emerging compliance benchmarks and litigation trends."]
core_points_en: Ericsson employs a segmented, market-specific strategy for licensing its IoT-related patent portfolio across different regions.
The company has secured new patent licensing collaborations specifically targeting the healthcare and agricultural technology sectors.
These moves illustrate practical pathways for top-tier licensors to manage and monetize rights in fragmented IoT markets.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor segmented licensing strategies to forecast European royalty cost volatility and budget accordingly.
Practice note: Enhance patent portfolios in healthcare and agriculture to strengthen leverage in upcoming SEP negotiations.
What to monitor: Track new sector-specific licensing deals to identify emerging compliance benchmarks and litigation trends.

items.title: WIPO Coordination Committee Nominates Singapore’s Daren Tang for Second Term as Director General
items.title_zh: WIPO 协调委员会提名邓鸿森连任总干事
items.title_en: WIPO Coordination Committee Nominates Daren Tang for Second Term as Director General
items.summary: The Coordination Committee of the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) has nominated Mr. Daren Tang from Singapore for a second six year term to the post of Director General.
items.ai_summary_zh: 世界知识产权组织（WIPO）协调委员会正式提名来自新加坡的邓鸿森先生，续任下一届六年期的总干事职务。该提名标志着国际社会对其首个任期工作的认可，后续需经成员国大会正式任命。作为全球 IP 体系的核心领导者，其连任将确保 WIPO 在推动国际专利、商标及版权合作方面的政策连续性与稳定性。
items.ai_summary_en: The Coordination Committee of the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) has officially nominated Mr. Daren Tang from Singapore for a second six-year term as Director General. This nomination reflects international recognition of his performance during the first term and serves as a precursor to formal appointment by the WIPO Member States Assembly. As the central leader of the global intellectual property system, Mr. Tang’s re-election is expected to ensure policy continuity and stability in promoting international cooperation regarding patents, trademarks, and copyrights. The process underscores the importance of stable leadership in maintaining the effectiveness and predictability of the multilateral IP framework for rights holders and applicants worldwide.
items.ai_insight_zh: 邓鸿森连任有利于维持多边 IP 规则稳定性，降低中国企业出海面临的制度不确定性。建议企业密切关注其在任期内对全球专利审查高速路及数字版权议题的推进，提前布局国际申请策略。
items.ai_insight_en: Leadership continuity at WIPO supports the stability of multilateral IP rules, reducing institutional uncertainty for market participants engaged in cross-border operations. Stable governance facilitates the continuation of existing international cooperation mechanisms, which is beneficial for strategic global IP portfolio management. Rights holders should closely monitor policy developments regarding the Patent Prosecution Highway and digital copyright issues during this term. Proactive adjustment of international filing strategies and attention to WIPO’s policy direction in digitalization and green technology sectors will help organizations navigate the evolving global IP landscape effectively.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["WIPO 协调委员会完成对现任总干事邓鸿森的第二个任期提名", "新任期时长为六年，旨在维持全球知识产权治理体系的稳定", "提名程序完成后，尚需等待成员国大会进行最终正式任命"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The WIPO Coordination Committee has completed the nomination of current Director General Daren Tang for a second term.", "The new six-year term aims to maintain stability within the global intellectual property governance system.", "Following the nomination procedure, final formal appointment remains subject to approval by the Member States Assembly."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["领导层稳定有助于延续现有国际合作机制，利好跨国 IP 布局", "企业应关注 WIPO 未来六年在全球数字化与绿色技术领域的政策导向"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leadership stability supports the continuity of existing international cooperation mechanisms, benefiting multinational IP layout strategies.", "Practice note: Organizations should proactively adjust international application strategies in response to ongoing policy frameworks.", "What to monitor: Monitor WIPO's policy orientation in global digitalization and green technology sectors over the next six years."]
core_points_en: The WIPO Coordination Committee has completed the nomination of current Director General Daren Tang for a second term.
The new six-year term aims to maintain stability within the global intellectual property governance system.
Following the nomination procedure, final formal appointment remains subject to approval by the Member States Assembly.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leadership stability supports the continuity of existing international cooperation mechanisms, benefiting multinational IP layout strategies.
Practice note: Organizations should proactively adjust international application strategies in response to ongoing policy frameworks.
What to monitor: Monitor WIPO's policy orientation in global digitalization and green technology sectors over the next six years.

items.title: BREAKING: Wilus enforces Wi-Fi 6 injunction, asks Asus to remove products from Germany
items.title_zh: 突发：Wilus 执行 Wi-Fi 6 禁令，要求华硕撤出德国市场
items.title_en: Wilus Enforces Wi-Fi 6 Injunction Against Asus in Germany Following Munich Court Ruling
items.summary: Sisvel licensor moves to impose sales ban awarded by Munich Regional Court
items.ai_summary_zh: Sisvel 被许可方 Wilus 依据慕尼黑地区法院此前作出的判决，正式执行针对华硕（Asus）产品的销售禁令。该行动聚焦于 Wi-Fi 6 标准必要专利侵权纠纷，强制要求华硕立即从德国市场移除相关涉案产品。此举标志着德国法院在 SEP 执法层面的强硬立场，展示了专利权人利用当地司法程序迅速实现市场排除的商业策略，对涉及无线通信标准的终端制造商构成直接合规挑战。
items.ai_summary_en: Wilus, a Sisvel licensor, has moved to enforce a sales ban against Asus products in Germany. The action follows an injunction awarded by the Munich Regional Court regarding alleged infringement of Wi-Fi 6 standard essential patents. This development highlights ongoing enforcement activities in the German jurisdiction concerning wireless communication technologies and the immediate commercial implications for device manufacturers facing such judicial orders.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，德国后续是否会围绕这类执法动作给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This case underscores the continued significance of German courts in SEP disputes, particularly regarding Wi-Fi standards. Rights holders are actively utilizing local injunctive relief to secure market exclusivity or leverage licensing negotiations. For stakeholders, this reinforces the need to monitor enforcement trends in key European jurisdictions where patent litigation outcomes can rapidly impact product availability and supply chain stability.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Wilus 依据慕尼黑法院判决，强制执行针对华硕的销售禁令", "涉案技术为 Wi-Fi 6 标准必要专利，要求产品立即撤出德国", "凸显德国作为 SEP 诉讼高地，禁令救济执行力度显著增强"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Wilus is enforcing a sales ban on Asus products in Germany based on a Munich Regional Court injunction.", "The enforcement action relates to alleged infringement of Wi-Fi 6 standard essential patents held by Sisvel."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["德国禁令执行迅速，出口企业须建立 SEP 风险预警机制", "面对 SEP 诉讼，应主动评估许可必要性，规避市场禁入风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Device manufacturers should assess exposure to SEP enforcement actions in Germany, especially for Wi-Fi 6 compliant products.", "Practice note: Monitor the execution phase of Munich Regional Court decisions involving Sisvel licensors to gauge enforcement speed and scope.", "What to monitor: Further developments in Wilus v. Asus proceedings and any related appeals or settlement announcements affecting the German market."]
core_points_en: Wilus is enforcing a sales ban on Asus products in Germany based on a Munich Regional Court injunction.
The enforcement action relates to alleged infringement of Wi-Fi 6 standard essential patents held by Sisvel.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Device manufacturers should assess exposure to SEP enforcement actions in Germany, especially for Wi-Fi 6 compliant products.
Practice note: Monitor the execution phase of Munich Regional Court decisions involving Sisvel licensors to gauge enforcement speed and scope.
What to monitor: Further developments in Wilus v. Asus proceedings and any related appeals or settlement announcements affecting the German market.

items.title_en: EXCLUSIVE: Samsung seeks new arbitration following InterDigital deal
items.summary: Samsung's challenge to InterDigital royalties is revealed to be a fresh arbitration request relating to certain terms of the $1.05-billion patent deal
items.ai_summary_zh: 三星对 InterDigital 专利费提出挑战，实为针对双方此前达成的 10.5 亿美元专利许可协议中特定条款发起的全新仲裁请求。该动态揭示了巨头间在标准必要专利定价上的持续博弈，虽已达成巨额和解，但执行细节仍存争议，需通过仲裁进一步厘清 FRAND 义务边界。

items.title: L’OEB ajuste son barème de taxes à compter du 1er avril 2026
items.title_zh: 欧专局2026年4月起调整专利收费表
items.title_en: EPO Adjusts Fee Schedule Effective April 1, 2026, Raising Search and Designation Costs
items.summary: 1. L’Office européen des brevets (OEB) ajustera son barème de taxes à compter du 1er avril 2026. 2.1 Les hausses concernent notamment les taxes suivantes : La taxe de recherche européenne (de 1520 € à 1595 €) La taxe de désignation (de 685 € à 720 €) Les taxes annuelles La taxe d’examen (de (suite) L’article L’OEB ajuste son barème de taxes à compter du 1er avril 2026 est apparu en premier sur Lavoix - Propriété intellectuelle .
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局（EPO）宣布自2026年4月1日起调整专利收费表。主要涨幅包括：欧洲检索费由1520欧元增至1595欧元，指定费由685欧元增至720欧元。此外，年费及审查费也将相应上调。此举旨在反映运营成本变化，申请人需提前规划预算以应对即将到来的费用增长。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office (EPO) has announced adjustments to its official fee schedule, which will take effect on April 1, 2026. This update reflects necessary changes to cover operational costs and ensures the sustainable funding of the patent granting process. Key increases include the European search fee, rising from EUR 1,520 to EUR 1,595, and the designation fee, increasing from EUR 685 to EUR 720. Additionally, annual renewal fees and examination fees are subject to corresponding upward adjustments. These changes impact all applicants filing or maintaining patents before the EPO. Rights holders should review their current portfolios and financial planning to accommodate these higher costs, ensuring that budget allocations for upcoming fiscal periods account for the revised fee structure across various procedural stages.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应重新评估在欧专利组合维持策略，针对非核心专利考虑提前放弃以控制成本。建议财务部门预留预算应对2026年的费用上涨，并优化申请流程以降低检索与审查阶段的支出压力。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should proactively reassess their European patent portfolios in light of the impending fee increases. Evaluating the commercial value of each asset allows for strategic decisions, such as abandoning non-core or low-value patents to control maintenance costs. Finance and IP teams must align budgets for 2026 to accommodate higher search, examination, and renewal fees. Furthermore, optimizing application strategies during the search and examination phases can help mitigate expenditure pressures. Early planning ensures that cost management measures are integrated into broader IP lifecycle strategies without disrupting critical protection objectives.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧专局将于2026年4月1日正式实施新的专利收费表", "欧洲检索费与指定费均出现明显上涨，具体金额已公布", "年费及审查费等其他常规费用也将同步进行价格调整"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EPO will implement a new fee schedule effective April 1, 2026, impacting various procedural costs for patent applicants and rights holders.", "Specific fee increases include the European search fee rising to EUR 1,595 and the designation fee increasing to EUR 720.", "Annual renewal fees and examination fees will also be adjusted upwards, requiring updated budget planning for ongoing patent maintenance."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重新评估在欧专利组合，剔除低价值专利以控制成本", "预留预算应对2026年费用上涨，优化申请流程降低支出"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Reassess European patent portfolios to identify and abandon low-value assets, thereby controlling maintenance costs amidst rising fees.", "Practice note: Reserve adequate budget for 2026 to cover increased search, designation, examination, and annual renewal fees.", "What to monitor: Optimize application workflows to reduce expenditure pressures during search and examination stages under the new fee regime."]
core_points_en: The EPO will implement a new fee schedule effective April 1, 2026, impacting various procedural costs for patent applicants and rights holders.
Specific fee increases include the European search fee rising to EUR 1,595 and the designation fee increasing to EUR 720.
Annual renewal fees and examination fees will also be adjusted upwards, requiring updated budget planning for ongoing patent maintenance.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Reassess European patent portfolios to identify and abandon low-value assets, thereby controlling maintenance costs amidst rising fees.
Practice note: Reserve adequate budget for 2026 to cover increased search, designation, examination, and annual renewal fees.
What to monitor: Optimize application workflows to reduce expenditure pressures during search and examination stages under the new fee regime.

items.title: EUIPO holds first Plenary Meeting of the Advisory Board for Craft and Industrial Geographical Indications
items.title_zh: EUIPO 召开手工艺与工业地理标志咨询委员会首次全会
items.title_en: EUIPO Convenes First Plenary of Advisory Board for Craft and Industrial Geographical Indications
items.summary: EUIPO holds first Plenary Meeting of the Advisory Board for Craft and Industrial Geographical Indications
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）召开手工艺与工业地理标志（CIGIs）咨询委员会首次全会，标志欧盟该领域保护框架正式实施。会议选举了领导层，明确了委员会在质量评估、声誉认定及防止名称通用化方面的咨询职能，并讨论了实施指南。此举旨在确保新条例在全欧范围内高效、一致地适用，加强成员国与欧盟机构间的协作。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) held the inaugural plenary meeting of the Advisory Board for Craft and Industrial Geographical Indications (CIGIs). This event marks a significant milestone in implementing the new EU regulatory framework protecting these specific geographical indications. The meeting established the Board’s leadership and clarified its mandate to ensure efficient, coherent, and consistent application of the Regulation across all Member States. Key discussions focused on quality criteria assessment, reputation establishment, and preventing genericization of names. The Board will provide expert opinions on applications and facilitate cooperation between national authorities, the Commission, and EUIPO. Initial exchanges regarding implementation guidelines aim to standardize procedures, supporting rights holders and applicants in navigating the new system effectively while maintaining high standards of consumer protection throughout the European market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国涉欧出口企业应关注欧盟手工艺地理标志新规，评估自身产品是否具备申请资格以获取更高溢价。同时需排查出口产品是否无意中使用受保护名称，避免侵权风险。建议提前布局，利用新机制提升品牌在欧洲市场的法律保护层级。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants exporting to Europe should assess whether their traditional crafts or industrial products qualify for registration under the new CIGI framework. Securing such protection can enhance brand value and provide stronger legal recourse against imitation within the EU single market. Concurrently, companies must conduct thorough clearance searches to ensure product names do not infringe upon newly protected geographical indications. Failure to comply may result in enforcement actions or market access barriers. Proactive engagement with the evolving guidelines is essential for strategic IP portfolio management and risk mitigation in cross-border trade operations involving specialized goods.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EUIPO 成立 CIGIs 咨询委员会，标志欧盟手工艺地理标志保护体系落地", "委员会负责提供质量标准评估意见，协助审查申请及解决消费者混淆风险", "会议确立工作流程并讨论实施指南，推动全欧执法标准统一与程序高效"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["EUIPO established the CIGI Advisory Board, marking the operational launch of the new EU craft and industrial geographical indication protection framework.", "The Board provides expert opinions on quality criteria, reputation assessment, and risks of consumer confusion or name genericization.", "Initial meetings defined working methods and discussed implementation guidelines to ensure uniform enforcement and procedural efficiency across Member States."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["评估中国传统手工艺品申请欧盟地理标志保护的可行性与战略价值", "排查出口产品命名合规性，防范因触犯欧盟新地理标志规则而受阻"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Evaluate eligibility for CIGI registration to secure premium positioning and enhanced legal protection for traditional crafts and industrial goods in the EU.", "Practice note: Conduct comprehensive name clearance checks to avoid infringement of new geographical indications, ensuring compliance with EU labeling and marketing regulations.", "What to monitor: Track the publication of final implementation guidelines and Board decisions to anticipate shifts in examination standards and opposition practices."]
core_points_en: EUIPO established the CIGI Advisory Board, marking the operational launch of the new EU craft and industrial geographical indication protection framework.
The Board provides expert opinions on quality criteria, reputation assessment, and risks of consumer confusion or name genericization.
Initial meetings defined working methods and discussed implementation guidelines to ensure uniform enforcement and procedural efficiency across Member States.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Evaluate eligibility for CIGI registration to secure premium positioning and enhanced legal protection for traditional crafts and industrial goods in the EU.
Practice note: Conduct comprehensive name clearance checks to avoid infringement of new geographical indications, ensuring compliance with EU labeling and marketing regulations.
What to monitor: Track the publication of final implementation guidelines and Board decisions to anticipate shifts in examination standards and opposition practices.

items.title: Mannheim on penalties and the limits of enforcing UPC decisions in non UPC territory
items.title_zh: 曼海姆论处罚令及 UPC 裁决在非成员国的执行限制
items.title_en: Mannheim Division Confirms Penalties and Limits UPC Enforcement in Non-Member States
items.summary: Unified Patent Court – Mannheim Local Division, 30 January 2026 (UPC_CFI_365/2023)
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC 曼海姆地方法院在二审执行程序中确认巨额处罚令，并深入探讨 UPC 裁决在非成员国的执行力。法院裁定法官报告人可单独签发处罚令；同时强调，针对非 UPC 领土行为的强制执行力需经当地法院承认，跨境禁令不自动产生域外处罚效力，但可责令披露境外销售信息以量化境内损害赔偿。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court Mannheim Local Division confirmed substantial penalty orders for continued non-compliance with information, recall, and destruction obligations during a second enforcement round. The judgment clarifies that the judge-rapporteur may independently issue such penalties under Rule 354.4 of the Rules of Procedure. Crucially, the Court distinguished between international jurisdiction on the merits and enforceability abroad. It held that coercive enforcement of UPC decisions in non-UPC territories requires prior recognition by local courts, meaning cross-border injunctions do not automatically trigger penalty exposure for foreign-market conduct. However, the Court affirmed that measures within UPC territory remain enforceable even if factually linked to overseas activities, including ordering disclosure of foreign sales data to quantify domestic infringement damages.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应诉需警惕 UPC 处罚令的快速签发机制。虽跨境行为难直接被罚，但境外销售数据可能被强制披露用于定损。建议提前梳理全球供应链数据合规，评估非欧盟市场的法律隔离效果，制定分层应对策略。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders and defendants must recognize that while UPC penalties do not automatically extend to non-contracting states, the Court retains significant leverage over global supply chains. Foreign sales data can be compelled for damage quantification, creating indirect financial exposure. Parties should establish rapid response protocols for penalty proceedings, as the judge-rapporteur can act swiftly without full panel review. Additionally, assessing the legal isolation of non-EU markets is critical. Companies should prepare robust data compliance frameworks to manage potential disclosure orders regarding international operations, ensuring that global commercial activities do not inadvertently exacerbate liability within the UPC system.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法官报告人有权依程序规则单独签发违规处罚令", "UPC 跨境禁令在非成员国无自动强制执行力，需当地承认", "可责令被告披露境外销售数据以辅助计算境内侵权损害"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The judge-rapporteur holds authority to independently issue penalty orders for procedural violations under the Rules of Procedure.", "Cross-border UPC injunctions lack automatic coercive force in non-member states, requiring prior local court recognition for enforcement.", "Courts may order disclosure of overseas sales data within UPC territory to accurately calculate damages for domestic infringement."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕法官报告人快速签发处罚令，需建立即时响应机制", "境外销售数据或将被迫披露，需提前规划数据合规防火墙"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Cross-border injunctions require local recognition for enforcement outside UPC states, limiting immediate penalty risks in non-member jurisdictions.", "Practice note: Establish rapid response mechanisms for penalty proceedings, as the judge-rapporteur can issue orders independently under current procedural rules.", "What to monitor: Potential orders disclosing foreign sales data to quantify domestic damages, necessitating prepared global supply chain data compliance strategies."]
core_points_en: The judge-rapporteur holds authority to independently issue penalty orders for procedural violations under the Rules of Procedure.
Cross-border UPC injunctions lack automatic coercive force in non-member states, requiring prior local court recognition for enforcement.
Courts may order disclosure of overseas sales data within UPC territory to accurately calculate damages for domestic infringement.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Cross-border injunctions require local recognition for enforcement outside UPC states, limiting immediate penalty risks in non-member jurisdictions.
Practice note: Establish rapid response mechanisms for penalty proceedings, as the judge-rapporteur can issue orders independently under current procedural rules.
What to monitor: Potential orders disclosing foreign sales data to quantify domestic damages, necessitating prepared global supply chain data compliance strategies.

items.title: Prior use infringement defence at the LD Munich: the UPC puts its German hat on (UPC_CFI_114/2024; UPC_CFI_448/2024)
items.title_zh: UPC 慕尼黑裁定先用权抗辩依成员国国内法逐案认定
items.title_en: UPC Munich: Prior Use Defence Determined by National Law, Not Unitary Concept
items.summary: Court docket: LD
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）慕尼黑地方分院在 Heraeus 诉 Vibrantz 案中裁定，先用权抗辩非统一概念，须依据涉案成员国国内法（如德国专利法第 12 条）逐国认定。被告虽未意识到技术参数但产品固有满足且已准备使用，成功主张德国先用权。因原告未证明其他缔约国侵权事实，法院最终驳回 enforceable infringement 主张。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Munich Local Division ruled in Heraeus v Vibrantz that the prior use defence under Article 28 UPCA is not a unitary concept but depends on applicable national law. The court applied German principles, finding that the defendant successfully established prior use rights despite lacking specific awareness of technical parameters, provided substantial preparation and manufacturing occurred before the priority date. While direct infringement was found for distribution in Germany, the claim for enforceable infringement across other Contracting Member States failed due to insufficient evidence of infringing acts there. This decision highlights the territorial nature of prior use defences and the UPC’s broad approach to direct infringement.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临专利诉讼时，应重视目标市场国的先用权证据保全。即便产品参数非刻意设计，只要证明在先制造或准备即可抗辩。同时需警惕 UPC 对直接侵权的宽泛认定，提前布局多国合规防线。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders operating in Europe should prioritize robust evidence preservation for prior use activities in key markets like Germany. Even if product specifications were not deliberately designed to meet patent claims, demonstrating consistent pre-priority manufacturing can secure a valid defence. Additionally, market participants must remain vigilant regarding the UPC’s potentially expansive interpretation of direct infringement. Comprehensive compliance strategies should address both substantive defences and rigorous evidentiary requirements across all relevant jurisdictions to mitigate litigation risks effectively.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 确认先用权抗辩需通过 UPCA 第 28 条援引各国国内法界定", "德国先用权成立不需被告知晓具体技术参数，重在实质准备与效果", "原告未能举证其他缔约国侵权行为导致跨域侵权主张无法获得支持"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Article 28 UPCA refers to national law for defining prior use scope, making it a country-by-country defence rather than a unified standard.", "German prior use requires proof of manufacturing or essential preparations before the priority date, regardless of defendant's technical intent.", "Plaintiffs must prove infringing acts in each specific state; failure to do so prevents cross-border enforcement of infringement claims."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["强化在德等关键市场的在先使用证据链以应对潜在专利纠纷", "关注 UPC 侵权认定新趋势，避免因地域举证不足导致全面败诉"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strengthen documentation of pre-priority manufacturing and sales in Germany to support prior use defences.", "Practice note: Ensure distinct evidence of infringing acts exists for each targeted Contracting Member State to avoid dismissal.", "What to monitor: Track UPC jurisprudence on direct infringement breadth and national variations in prior use application."]
core_points_en: Article 28 UPCA refers to national law for defining prior use scope, making it a country-by-country defence rather than a unified standard.
German prior use requires proof of manufacturing or essential preparations before the priority date, regardless of defendant's technical intent.
Plaintiffs must prove infringing acts in each specific state; failure to do so prevents cross-border enforcement of infringement claims.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strengthen documentation of pre-priority manufacturing and sales in Germany to support prior use defences.
Practice note: Ensure distinct evidence of infringing acts exists for each targeted Contracting Member State to avoid dismissal.
What to monitor: Track UPC jurisprudence on direct infringement breadth and national variations in prior use application.

items.title: Samsung and ZTE deliver final UK FRAND arguments as Chongqing decision looms
items.title_zh: 三星与中兴提交英国 FRAND 终辩，重庆判决在即
items.title_en: Samsung and ZTE Conclude UK FRAND Final Arguments as Chongqing Judgment Approaches
items.summary: The relative timings of English and Chinese rate-setting judgments could play an important role in how dispute is resolved, while UPC and German hearings are also pending
items.ai_summary_zh: 三星与中兴已在英国法院完成 FRAND 费率最终辩论，案件走向将受中英两国费率判决时序影响。与此同时，统一专利法院（UPC）及德国相关听证会亦 pending，多国司法程序交织可能重塑全球 SEP 许可格局。
items.ai_summary_en: Samsung and ZTE have completed their final oral arguments regarding FRAND royalty rates in the English High Court. The ultimate resolution of this dispute is heavily influenced by the relative timing of the upcoming judgments from both the English courts and the Chongqing Intermediate People's Court in China. Concurrently, parallel proceedings are advancing at the Unified Patent Court (UPC) and within the German judicial system. These overlapping international legal processes create a complex multi-jurisdictional landscape. The interplay between these distinct forums suggests that the sequence of rate-setting decisions will significantly shape the global standard-essential patent licensing framework, potentially establishing critical precedents for future cross-border intellectual property disputes involving major technology firms.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临多法域诉讼风险，需统筹中英德及 UPC 策略。建议密切跟踪重庆判决对全球费率锚定效应，提前布局跨国应诉与许可谈判方案。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders and implementers must navigate an increasingly fragmented global SEP enforcement landscape where multiple jurisdictions compete for influence over global licensing terms. The strategic coordination of litigation efforts across the UK, China, Germany, and the UPC is essential to optimize outcomes. Stakeholders should closely monitor how the Chongqing court's decision interacts with Western rulings, as this may establish new benchmarks for global royalty anchors. Proactive management of parallel proceedings allows parties to leverage favorable timings and mitigate risks associated with conflicting injunctions or rate determinations across different legal systems.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英中两国费率判决时序将决定纠纷解决路径", "UPC 与德国听证会同步进行增加变量", "三星与中兴已完成英国阶段最终口头辩论"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The chronological sequence of English and Chinese rate-setting judgments will critically determine the strategic path for resolving the ongoing dispute.", "Simultaneous hearings at the Unified Patent Court and in Germany introduce additional procedural variables to the already complex litigation environment.", "Samsung and ZTE have successfully concluded the final stage of oral arguments within the United Kingdom jurisdiction."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注中英判决时序对全球许可定价影响", "统筹 UPC 与成员国诉讼以优化应对策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor the interaction between English and Chinese rate-setting judgments to assess impacts on global licensing pricing strategies.", "Practice note: Coordinate litigation tactics across UPC, German, and national courts to optimize defense and negotiation positions.", "What to monitor: Track the Chongqing judgment's potential effect as a global rate anchor and its influence on subsequent international settlements."]
core_points_en: The chronological sequence of English and Chinese rate-setting judgments will critically determine the strategic path for resolving the ongoing dispute.
Simultaneous hearings at the Unified Patent Court and in Germany introduce additional procedural variables to the already complex litigation environment.
Samsung and ZTE have successfully concluded the final stage of oral arguments within the United Kingdom jurisdiction.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor the interaction between English and Chinese rate-setting judgments to assess impacts on global licensing pricing strategies.
Practice note: Coordinate litigation tactics across UPC, German, and national courts to optimize defense and negotiation positions.
What to monitor: Track the Chongqing judgment's potential effect as a global rate anchor and its influence on subsequent international settlements.

items.title: EPO PACE reform: How to assess the impact on your patent strategy
items.title_zh: 欧洲专利局 PACE 计划改革：如何评估对专利策略的影响
items.title_en: EPO PACE Reform: Impact of Limiting Accelerated Prosecution to Examination Phase
items.summary: As of 1 February 2026, new rules came into effect for patent applicants using the EPO PACE programme. Discover what has changed.
items.ai_summary_zh: 自 2026 年 2 月 1 日起，欧洲专利局（EPO）实施 PACE 计划新规。鉴于搜索报告出具时间已显著缩短且可预测，EPO 决定将加速程序仅限于实质审查阶段。申请人此后无法再通过 PACE 单独请求加速搜索，官方表格仅支持加速审查请求，旨在提升服务时效性与可预测性。
items.ai_summary_en: Effective 1 February 2026, the European Patent Office (EPO) has revised the rules governing the Programme for Accelerated Prosecution of European Patent Applications (PACE). Citing significant improvements in processing efficiency, the EPO determined that search reports are now issued within short, predictable timeframes. Consequently, the PACE programme is restricted exclusively to the substantive examination phase. Applicants can no longer submit separate requests to accelerate the European search phase using form EPO 1005. This strategic adjustment aligns with the EPO’s Strategic Plan 2028, aiming to enhance service quality and predictability. Rights holders must adapt their filing strategies to focus acceleration efforts solely on examination, leveraging faster official actions to potentially secure grants more rapidly under the updated procedural framework.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧申请需调整策略，无需再为搜索阶段提交加速请求。应聚焦于审查阶段的 PACE 申请，利用缩短的审查周期加快获权，同时注意新规则生效日期，避免无效申报。
items.ai_insight_en: With the EPO restricting PACE to the examination phase, rights holders should adjust their prosecution strategies to avoid unnecessary procedural steps during the search stage. Since search reports are now issued predictably, resources previously allocated to accelerating searches can be redirected toward managing the examination phase efficiently. Applicants should prioritize rapid responses to examination reports to fully leverage the accelerated timeline. Monitoring the effective date of 1 February 2026 is critical to ensure compliance with the new form requirements and prevent invalid submissions that could delay proceedings or incur unnecessary costs.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["年 2 月起 PACE 计划不再支持单独加速欧洲专利搜索阶段", "因搜索时限已优化，新规将加速范围限定于实质审查阶段", "更新后的官方表格仅允许提交加速审查请求以缩短授权周期"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["As of 1 February 2026, the PACE programme no longer supports separate requests to accelerate the European search phase for patent applications.", "Due to optimized search timelines, the EPO restricts accelerated prosecution strictly to the substantive examination phase of the application process.", "Updated official forms only permit requests for accelerated examination, aiming to shorten the overall timeline for granting European patents."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["停止针对搜索阶段的 PACE 申请，避免资源浪费与程序混淆", "善用审查阶段加速机制，配合快速响应以缩短整体获权时间"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Discontinue separate PACE requests for the search phase to prevent resource waste and procedural confusion under the new EPO rules.", "Practice note: Utilize the accelerated examination mechanism effectively by preparing rapid responses to office actions to shorten the overall grant timeline.", "What to monitor: Ensure all PACE requests filed after 1 February 2026 strictly target the examination phase using the updated official forms."]
core_points_en: As of 1 February 2026, the PACE programme no longer supports separate requests to accelerate the European search phase for patent applications.
Due to optimized search timelines, the EPO restricts accelerated prosecution strictly to the substantive examination phase of the application process.
Updated official forms only permit requests for accelerated examination, aiming to shorten the overall timeline for granting European patents.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Discontinue separate PACE requests for the search phase to prevent resource waste and procedural confusion under the new EPO rules.
Practice note: Utilize the accelerated examination mechanism effectively by preparing rapid responses to office actions to shorten the overall grant timeline.
What to monitor: Ensure all PACE requests filed after 1 February 2026 strictly target the examination phase using the updated official forms.

items.title: DesignView becomes the world’s largest design image search engine
items.title_zh: DesignView 成为全球最大外观设计图像搜索引擎
items.title_en: DesignView Becomes World's Largest Industrial Design Image Search Engine
items.summary: DesignView becomes the world’s largest design image search engine As of 9 February 2026, the DesignView search functionality for industrial design images has been implemented for all connected intellectual property (IP) offices, making it the largest search engine for design images in the world. Wit
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局宣布，自 2026 年 2 月 9 日起，DesignView 平台已完成对所有连接知识产权局的外观设计图像搜索功能部署。此举标志着该平台正式成为全球规模最大的工业设计图像搜索引擎，旨在通过整合全球数据资源，大幅提升外观设计的检索效率与覆盖面，为申请人及审查员提供强有力的工具支持。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office announced that as of February 9, 2026, the DesignView platform has fully implemented image search functionality for industrial designs across all connected intellectual property offices. This milestone establishes DesignView as the largest global search engine for design images. By integrating comprehensive data resources from participating jurisdictions, the platform significantly enhances the efficiency and scope of design searches. This development provides robust support for applicants and examiners worldwide, facilitating more thorough prior art checks and improving the overall quality of design registration processes through unified access to a vast, consolidated database of industrial design representations.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧申请外观设计时，应充分利用 DesignView 进行更详尽的在先设计检索，以规避侵权风险并提高注册成功率。建议 IP 从业者更新检索策略，适应图像搜索新标准，确保产品出海合规。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders and legal practitioners should leverage the expanded DesignView capabilities to conduct comprehensive prior design searches. Utilizing this extensive global repository helps mitigate infringement risks and improves the likelihood of successful design registrations by ensuring thorough clearance checks. Teams are advised to update their existing search workflows to align with these new image-based search standards. Adapting to this larger, integrated database ensures better compliance for products entering international markets and supports higher quality in securing design rights across multiple jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["DesignView 于 2026 年 2 月 9 日实现全连接局图像搜索覆盖", "该平台现已确立为全球最大的工业设计图像搜索引擎", "功能升级旨在优化全球外观设计检索效率与数据整合"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["DesignView achieved full image search coverage across all connected IP offices on February 9, 2026.", "The platform is now recognized as the largest industrial design image search engine globally.", "The upgrade aims to optimize global design search efficiency and enhance data integration capabilities."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用新版图像搜索强化在先设计排查，降低侵权隐患", "调整检索工作流以适应全球最大图库，提升确权质量"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage enhanced image search to strengthen prior design clearance and reduce infringement liabilities.", "Practice note: Adjust search workflows to utilize the world's largest design library for improved rights acquisition quality.", "What to monitor: Track updates in cross-border design data integration and evolving image search standards."]
core_points_en: DesignView achieved full image search coverage across all connected IP offices on February 9, 2026.
The platform is now recognized as the largest industrial design image search engine globally.
The upgrade aims to optimize global design search efficiency and enhance data integration capabilities.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage enhanced image search to strengthen prior design clearance and reduce infringement liabilities.
Practice note: Adjust search workflows to utilize the world's largest design library for improved rights acquisition quality.
What to monitor: Track updates in cross-border design data integration and evolving image search standards.

items.title: Standard essential patents (SEPs): a new board for the global geostrategic game
items.title_zh: 标准必要专利：全球地缘战略博弈的新棋盘
items.title_en: Standard Essential Patents: A New Board for the Global Geostrategic Game
items.summary: Standard essential patents (SEPs) are patents that protect technologies that are indispensable for implementing a technical standard.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文剖析标准必要专利（SEP）作为数字经济基石的地缘战略意义，指出其已从电信业扩展至汽车等互联领域。文章对比了美、亚、欧三地动态：美国成立工作组并推出加速计划以扶持创新；日本法院作出首例 SEP 禁令；欧盟则面临平衡 FRAND 承诺与防止市场滥用的监管挑战，凸显全球规则博弈加剧。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the geostrategic significance of Standard Essential Patents (SEPs) as foundational elements of the digital economy, highlighting their expansion beyond telecommunications into connected sectors such as automotive and industrial manufacturing. The article contrasts recent regulatory and judicial developments across three key jurisdictions. In the United States, authorities have established a dedicated working group and launched the SPARK pilot program to support innovators against perceived ecosystem hostility. Japanese courts have issued their first SEP injunctions, establishing strict negotiation frameworks. Meanwhile, the European Union faces complex regulatory challenges in balancing FRAND commitments with the prevention of market abuse. These diverging approaches underscore an intensifying global contest over intellectual property rules, supply chain control, and the future governance of interoperable technologies.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国车企及电子企业出海需警惕欧美日 SEP 规则分化。建议提前构建 FRAND 合规谈判流程，避免被认定为恶意拖延；同时关注美国加速审查红利，主动参与标准制定以获取战略优势。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating globally must navigate the increasing divergence in SEP enforcement regimes across Europe, the US, and Japan. Rights holders and implementers should proactively establish robust FRAND compliance frameworks to mitigate risks of being characterized as unwilling licensees or hold-out actors. Companies should strategically leverage US acceleration incentives by actively contributing to standard-setting organizations. Simultaneously, firms must prepare for stricter judicial scrutiny regarding negotiation conduct, particularly in jurisdictions like Japan where injunctions are becoming more accessible. Aligning internal data disclosure protocols and negotiation strategies with evolving local precedents is essential for maintaining operational continuity and competitive advantage in interconnected markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["SEP 成为连接汽车与工业的关键，引发全球监管与诉讼激增", "美国通过设立工作组及 SPARK 计划，旨在扭转对创新者 hostile 的生态", "日本法院确立两阶段谈判机制，因实施者不配合而发出销售禁令", "欧盟需在防止权利人劫持与实施者拖延之间寻找新的监管平衡点"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["SEPs have become critical connectors for automotive and industrial sectors, triggering a significant surge in global regulatory actions and litigation disputes.", "The USPTO created a new working group and the SPARK initiative to reverse an ecosystem perceived as hostile toward innovators through accelerated review incentives.", "Japanese courts established a two-stage negotiation mechanism, issuing sales bans against implementers deemed uncooperative during licensing discussions."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕日本式禁令风险，完善诉讼应对与数据披露合规机制", "利用美国 SPARK 计划激励，加大在标准组织中的技术贡献力度"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor the risk of injunctions similar to Japanese precedents and strengthen litigation response capabilities alongside data disclosure compliance mechanisms.", "Practice note: Leverage US SPARK program incentives to increase technical contributions within standard-setting organizations for strategic positioning.", "What to monitor: Diverging SEP rules in Europe, the US, and Japan, focusing on FRAND negotiation standards and potential hold-out determinations."]
core_points_en: SEPs have become critical connectors for automotive and industrial sectors, triggering a significant surge in global regulatory actions and litigation disputes.
The USPTO created a new working group and the SPARK initiative to reverse an ecosystem perceived as hostile toward innovators through accelerated review incentives.
Japanese courts established a two-stage negotiation mechanism, issuing sales bans against implementers deemed uncooperative during licensing discussions.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor the risk of injunctions similar to Japanese precedents and strengthen litigation response capabilities alongside data disclosure compliance mechanisms.
Practice note: Leverage US SPARK program incentives to increase technical contributions within standard-setting organizations for strategic positioning.
What to monitor: Diverging SEP rules in Europe, the US, and Japan, focusing on FRAND negotiation standards and potential hold-out determinations.

items.title: UPC Munich Local Division rejects GXD-Bio’s claim against Myriad; EP 3 346 403 revoked
items.title_zh: UPC 慕尼黑驳回 GXD-Bio 诉请并撤销 Myriad 涉案专利
items.title_en: UPC Munich Dismisses GXD-Bio Claim and Revokes Myriad Patent for Added Matter
items.summary: Summary/Background The Local Division (LD) Munich dismissed GDX-Bio’s infringement action against My
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC 慕尼黑地方法院驳回 GXD-Bio 对 Myriad 的侵权诉讼，并以“新增事项”为由撤销 EP3346403 号专利。法院适用“黄金标准”，认定原申请未明确将乳腺癌 FFPE 组织样本纳入发明范畴，实验数据仅示通用性而非特定归属，故辅助请求亦被拒。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Munich Local Division dismissed GXD-Bio’s infringement action against Myriad Genetics and revoked European Patent EP 3 346 403. The court found the patent invalid due to added matter, specifically regarding claims directed at quantifying gene expression in formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded (FFPE) breast cancer tissue using the OAZ1 reference gene. The division applied the established 'gold standard' for assessing added matter, determining that the original application did not directly and unambiguously disclose this specific tissue type as part of the invention. Auxiliary requests filed by GXD-Bio were similarly refused. The court reasoned that while experimental data showed general applicability to FFPE samples, it did not establish a specific link to breast cancer tissue required to support the amended claims.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧申请专利时，需确保权利要求特征在原说明书中有直接明确依据，避免依赖实施例推导特定应用场景。面对 UPC 诉讼，应重视新增事项抗辩策略，提前排查文件披露充分性风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision highlights the strict application of the added matter prohibition under the UPC framework. Rights holders must ensure that specific technical features or application scenarios claimed in litigation are explicitly supported by the original disclosure, rather than inferred from general examples. When facing revocation actions, parties should proactively assess the sufficiency of disclosure for any proposed amendments. Relying on broad experimental data to narrow claims to specific embodiments carries significant risk if the original text does not clearly associate those embodiments with the core inventive concept.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 慕尼黑以新增事项为由撤销涉案专利并驳回侵权诉请", "法院适用黄金标准，强调特征须直接明确归属于原发明", "实验数据披露通用性不足以证明特定组织样本属于发明范围"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UPC Munich revoked EP 3 346 403 for added matter and dismissed the associated infringement claim against Myriad.", "The court applied the gold standard, requiring features to be directly and unambiguously disclosed in the original application.", "General experimental data on FFPE stability was insufficient to prove specific breast cancer tissue belonged to the invention."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["专利申请须确保特定技术特征在原文件中直接明确披露", "应对 UPC 诉讼时需重点评估新增事项风险与证据链完整性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure specific claim limitations have explicit basis in the original specification to avoid added matter objections during UPC proceedings.", "Practice note: Evaluate the robustness of auxiliary requests against the gold standard before filing, focusing on direct textual support for narrowed scopes.", "What to monitor: How other UPC divisions interpret the threshold for linking general experimental examples to specific claimed embodiments in future cases."]
core_points_en: UPC Munich revoked EP 3 346 403 for added matter and dismissed the associated infringement claim against Myriad.
The court applied the gold standard, requiring features to be directly and unambiguously disclosed in the original application.
General experimental data on FFPE stability was insufficient to prove specific breast cancer tissue belonged to the invention.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure specific claim limitations have explicit basis in the original specification to avoid added matter objections during UPC proceedings.
Practice note: Evaluate the robustness of auxiliary requests against the gold standard before filing, focusing on direct textual support for narrowed scopes.
What to monitor: How other UPC divisions interpret the threshold for linking general experimental examples to specific claimed embodiments in future cases.
