items.title: InterDigital posts record smartphone revenue in 2025
items.title_zh: InterDigital 获德巴禁令后营收创新高
items.title_en: InterDigital Reports Record Smartphone Revenue Following German Injunction Against Disney
items.summary: The company sees video streaming licensing as a major growth driver, and CEO Liren Chen “could not be happier” after winning German and Brazilian injunctions against Disney
items.ai_summary_zh: InterDigital 宣布 2025 年智能手机许可收入创纪录，视频流媒体授权成为新增长引擎。CEO 陈立人表示，公司在德国和巴西成功获得针对迪士尼的禁令后倍感振奋。该动态凸显了标准必要专利持有人在欧洲主要法域通过诉讼获取禁令救济、进而推动全球许可谈判的战略有效性。
items.ai_summary_en: InterDigital has announced record smartphone licensing revenue for 2025, identifying video streaming licensing as a primary driver of future business growth. The company’s CEO expressed strong satisfaction after securing injunctions against Disney in both Germany and Brazil. This development underscores the strategic effectiveness of standard essential patent holders leveraging litigation to obtain injunctive relief in key European jurisdictions. By successfully enforcing these rights, rights holders are significantly strengthening their position in global licensing negotiations. The outcome highlights how judicial victories in major markets like Germany can directly influence commercial outcomes and accelerate settlement discussions with implementers across various technology sectors.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临更严峻的 SEP 禁令风险，尤其在流媒体等新兴领域。建议提前评估产品侵权风险，制定 FRAND 抗辩策略，并关注德国法院最新裁判倾向，避免遭遇类似迪士尼的禁令打击。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe face increasing risks associated with SEP injunctions, particularly within emerging sectors such as video streaming. Companies should proactively assess potential infringement liabilities and develop robust FRAND defense strategies to mitigate exposure. It is critical to closely monitor recent judicial trends in German courts, which have demonstrated a willingness to grant injunctive relief that can disrupt supply chains. Understanding these dynamics helps implementers avoid severe legal setbacks similar to those experienced by Disney, ensuring better preparedness for complex global licensing disputes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["InterDigital 智能手机许可收入在 2025 年达到历史新高水平", "视频流媒体领域被确立为公司未来业务增长的核心驱动力", "在德国赢得针对迪士尼的禁令极大增强了公司谈判筹码"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["InterDigital achieved record-high smartphone licensing revenue levels in 2025, reflecting strong market performance.", "Video streaming licensing has been established as a core growth engine for the company's future business expansion.", "Securing a German injunction against Disney substantially enhanced InterDigital's leverage in ongoing negotiations."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕德国法院禁令对全球供应链的连锁阻断效应", "新兴流媒体领域或成下一轮 SEP 许可费争夺焦点"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Be aware of the cascading supply chain disruption effects caused by German court injunctions on global operations.", "Practice note: Emerging streaming sectors may become the next focal point for intense SEP royalty disputes and enforcement actions.", "What to monitor: Track evolving judicial attitudes in Germany regarding injunctive relief for standard essential patents."]
core_points_en: InterDigital achieved record-high smartphone licensing revenue levels in 2025, reflecting strong market performance.
Video streaming licensing has been established as a core growth engine for the company's future business expansion.
Securing a German injunction against Disney substantially enhanced InterDigital's leverage in ongoing negotiations.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Be aware of the cascading supply chain disruption effects caused by German court injunctions on global operations.
Practice note: Emerging streaming sectors may become the next focal point for intense SEP royalty disputes and enforcement actions.
What to monitor: Track evolving judicial attitudes in Germany regarding injunctive relief for standard essential patents.

items.title: Avanci Video signs first licensee, announces royalty rates
items.title_zh: Avanci Video 签约首位被许可人并公布费率
items.title_en: Avanci Video Signs First Licensee and Announces Standard Essential Patent Royalty Rates
items.summary: Newly unveiled royalty discounts provide powerful incentives for streamers to sign up early
items.ai_summary_zh: Avanci Video 专利池正式签署首位被许可人，并同步公布其标准必要专利许可费率。该方案特别推出早期签约折扣机制，旨在通过经济激励促使流媒体服务商尽快达成许可协议。此举标志着视频编码技术 SEP 许可模式在欧洲市场的进一步落地与商业化推进。
items.ai_summary_en: The Avanci Video patent pool has officially signed its first licensee, marking a significant commercial milestone in the licensing of video coding standard essential patents. Concurrently, the organization announced its standardized royalty rates, establishing a transparent pricing benchmark for the industry. A key feature of this new framework is the introduction of early-signing discounts, designed to provide strong economic incentives for streaming service providers to conclude licensing agreements promptly. This development signifies the further implementation and commercial advancement of video SEP licensing models within the European market. By securing an initial agreement and clarifying cost structures, Avanci aims to streamline negotiations and encourage broader adoption among technology companies operating in the digital media sector.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国流媒体及终端企业应评估 Avanci 新费率对欧业务成本影响。建议密切关注早期优惠窗口期，结合 FRAND 原则审慎谈判，避免因延迟签约导致合规风险或成本上升，优化欧洲市场知识产权布局。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should assess the potential impact of Avanci’s new fee structure on operational costs for businesses exporting to or operating in Europe. It is advisable to closely monitor the window for early-signing discounts to minimize long-term patent licensing expenditures. Companies are encouraged to engage in prudent negotiations aligned with FRAND principles, ensuring compliance while optimizing their intellectual property strategies. Delaying participation may result in increased financial burdens and heightened regulatory risks. Therefore, evaluating the cost implications and leveraging available incentives can help rights holders and implementers achieve a more efficient and compliant IP landscape in the European market.

items.title: RELIEF - CJEU confirms EUIPO practice contrary to AG Cápeta opinion in APE TEES
items.title_zh: CJEU 确认 EUIPO 实践：在先权利须在裁决时依然有效
items.title_en: CJEU Confirms EUIPO Practice: Earlier Rights Must Remain Valid at Decision Date in APE TEES
items.summary: We reported on Advocate General (AG) Cápeta’s opinion in the APE TEES case (C-337/22 P, aka Nowhere, the name of the applicant before the GC) here expressing the hope – in fact expectation – that the CJEU would not follow that opinion. We are very relieved to report that this expectation has not been disappointed.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院在 APE TEES 案中作出判决，驳回总检察长意见，明确支持 EUIPO 长期实践：商标异议或无效程序中，在先权利不仅需在申请日有效，还必须在 EUIPO 作出实质裁决时依然存续。若期间因脱欧、撤销或未续展等原因失效，则冲突消失，后续商标可获注册。该原则统一适用于异议与无效程序，并约束成员国局。
items.ai_summary_en: In the APE TEES case, the Court of Justice of the European Union rejected the Advocate General’s opinion and firmly upheld the European Union Intellectual Property Office’s long-standing practice. The ruling clarifies that for an opposition or invalidity action to succeed, the earlier right must not only have been valid on the application or priority date but must also remain in force when the EUIPO issues its substantive decision. If the earlier right ceases to exist during proceedings due to reasons such as Brexit, non-use cancellation, surrender, or expiry, the legal conflict disappears. Consequently, there is no longer a ground for refusing registration or declaring invalidity of the later mark. This principle applies uniformly to both opposition and invalidity proceedings and binds national offices, ensuring that formalism does not override commercial logic and equity in EU trademark disputes.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧维权需动态监控对手商标状态，若对方权利在审理期失效可直接胜诉。反之，若自身为在先权利人，务必维持权利有效性至终局裁决，避免因疏忽导致维权失败。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment has significant implications for trademark strategy in Europe. Rights holders must actively maintain the validity of their earlier marks throughout the entire duration of opposition or invalidity proceedings. Failure to renew or use a mark can lead to loss of rights mid-case, causing the action to fail regardless of initial merits. Conversely, applicants facing opposition can monitor the status of the opposing mark; if it expires or is cancelled during proceedings, the obstacle is removed. This dynamic requires continuous monitoring and proactive management of trademark portfolios to ensure rights persist until final adjudication, preventing unnecessary losses due to procedural lapses or strategic oversights by counterparties.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["CJEU 确认在先权利须持续有效至异议或无效裁决作出之日", "权利中途失效导致冲突消除，不再构成驳回或宣告无效理由", "判决推翻总检察长意见，统一欧盟及各成员国审查实践标准"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The CJEU confirms that earlier rights must remain valid and enforceable until the date the EUIPO rules on the opposition or invalidity action.", "If an earlier right loses validity during proceedings, the conflict between rights ceases, removing grounds for refusal or invalidation of the later mark.", "The judgment overturns the Advocate General’s opinion, establishing a unified standard for examining prior rights across the EU and member states."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用对手权利审理期中途失效策略，可低成本清除注册障碍", "作为在先权利人，必须确保持续使用并按时续展直至案件终结"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor opponent trademark status during proceedings; if rights lapse mid-case, the conflict resolves without further cost.", "Practice note: As an earlier rights holder, ensure continuous use and timely renewal to maintain validity until the final decision is issued.", "What to monitor: Track the legal status and renewal dates of cited earlier marks in ongoing opposition or invalidity cases."]
core_points_en: The CJEU confirms that earlier rights must remain valid and enforceable until the date the EUIPO rules on the opposition or invalidity action.
If an earlier right loses validity during proceedings, the conflict between rights ceases, removing grounds for refusal or invalidation of the later mark.
The judgment overturns the Advocate General’s opinion, establishing a unified standard for examining prior rights across the EU and member states.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor opponent trademark status during proceedings; if rights lapse mid-case, the conflict resolves without further cost.
Practice note: As an earlier rights holder, ensure continuous use and timely renewal to maintain validity until the final decision is issued.
What to monitor: Track the legal status and renewal dates of cited earlier marks in ongoing opposition or invalidity cases.

items.title: European SEP developments spur calls to add UK, Germany and UPC to US patent watch list
items.title_zh: 欧洲 SEP 动态引发将英德及 UPC 列入美国专利观察名单呼声
items.title_en: Stakeholders Urge USTR to Add UK, Germany, and UPC to Patent Watch List Amid SEP Disputes
items.summary: As standard essential patent policy debates escalate, some stakeholders advocate for adding European jurisdictions on the USTR watch list
items.ai_summary_zh: 随着标准必要专利（SEP）政策辩论升级，部分利益相关方呼吁美国贸易代表办公室（USTR）将英国、德国及统一专利法院（UPC）列入特别 301 报告观察名单。此举旨在施压欧洲司法辖区调整 FRAND 许可规则，反映跨大西洋在 SEP 执法与定价机制上的深层分歧，可能重塑全球专利许可格局。
items.ai_summary_en: Amid escalating debates over standard essential patent (SEP) policy, certain stakeholders are advocating for the inclusion of specific European jurisdictions on the United States Trade Representative's Special 301 Report watch list. This initiative specifically targets the United Kingdom, Germany, and the Unified Patent Court. The primary objective is to exert diplomatic pressure on these European judicial bodies to adjust their approaches to FRAND licensing rules. This development highlights deep-seated transatlantic divergences regarding SEP enforcement mechanisms and pricing determinations. Such geopolitical friction threatens to reshape the global landscape of patent licensing, creating a more complex regulatory environment for rights holders and implementers alike as they navigate conflicting legal standards across major markets.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临更复杂的地缘政治化专利环境。需警惕美欧规则冲突导致的合规风险，建议提前评估英德诉讼策略，建立多元化 FRAND 抗辩预案，避免成为大国博弈牺牲品。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating across the Atlantic face an increasingly complex and geopolitically charged patent environment. The growing divergence between US and European regulatory stances on SEPs introduces substantial compliance risks that require proactive management. Companies should carefully evaluate litigation strategies within key jurisdictions like the UK and Germany, anticipating potential shifts in judicial interpretation. Establishing diversified FRAND defense frameworks is essential to mitigate exposure to cross-border enforcement actions. Stakeholders must remain agile, ensuring their licensing negotiations and legal postures are robust enough to withstand the pressures of this evolving international IP landscape without becoming collateral damage in broader trade disputes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["利益方呼吁 USTR 将英、德及 UPC 纳入专利观察名单", "争议焦点在于欧洲 SEP 禁令救济与 FRAND 费率裁定机制", "此举标志欧美在标准必要专利政策协调上出现显著裂痕"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Stakeholders formally request the USTR to include the UK, Germany, and the UPC on the patent watch list.", "The central dispute focuses on European courts' handling of SEP injunctions and FRAND rate-setting methodologies.", "This move signals a significant rift in US-EU coordination regarding standard essential patent policies."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注美欧政策分歧对在英德诉讼策略的潜在冲击", "构建应对地缘政治化专利纠纷的多元化合规预案"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor how US-EU policy divergences may impact ongoing and future litigation strategies in the UK and Germany.", "Practice note: Develop comprehensive, multi-jurisdictional compliance plans to address the rising trend of geopolitically influenced patent disputes.", "What to monitor: Track updates from the USTR regarding the Special 301 Report and subsequent reactions from European judicial authorities."]
core_points_en: Stakeholders formally request the USTR to include the UK, Germany, and the UPC on the patent watch list.
The central dispute focuses on European courts' handling of SEP injunctions and FRAND rate-setting methodologies.
This move signals a significant rift in US-EU coordination regarding standard essential patent policies.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor how US-EU policy divergences may impact ongoing and future litigation strategies in the UK and Germany.
Practice note: Develop comprehensive, multi-jurisdictional compliance plans to address the rising trend of geopolitically influenced patent disputes.
What to monitor: Track updates from the USTR regarding the Special 301 Report and subsequent reactions from European judicial authorities.

items.title: EU steps up fight against IP crime to protect innovation and competitiveness
items.title_zh: 欧盟加强打击知识产权犯罪以保护创新
items.title_en: EUIPO and Eurojust Strengthen EU Collaboration to Combat Intellectual Property Crime
items.summary: EU steps up fight against IP crime to protect innovation and competitiveness Reinforcing the EU´s commitment to stronger collaboration, the 10th High-Level Meeting on Intellectual Property (IP) Crime, organised by EUIPO and hosted by Eurojust in The Hague, brought together representatives from other
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局与欧洲司法合作组织在海牙联合举办第十届知识产权犯罪高层会议。此次会议旨在强化欧盟内部协作机制，汇聚多方代表共同应对日益严峻的知识产权犯罪挑战，通过提升执法效率保护欧盟的创新成果与市场竞争力，彰显了欧盟在知识产权刑事保护领域的坚定承诺。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) and Eurojust jointly hosted the tenth High-Level Meeting on Intellectual Property Crime in The Hague. This significant gathering aimed to reinforce internal collaboration mechanisms within the EU, bringing together diverse representatives to address the increasingly severe challenges posed by intellectual property crime. By enhancing law enforcement efficiency, the initiative seeks to protect the EU's innovation outcomes and market competitiveness. The event underscores the EU's firm commitment to robust criminal protection of intellectual property rights, highlighting a strategic focus on cross-border cooperation to safeguard economic interests and foster a secure environment for innovation across member states.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中企需警惕欧盟升级的刑事执法力度，完善内部合规体系以防侵权风险。出口企业应建立知识产权预警机制，避免因无意侵权面临刑事调查，同时可借助欧盟协作网络维护自身合法权益。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor the EU's escalating criminal enforcement intensity regarding intellectual property violations. Companies must enhance internal compliance frameworks to mitigate infringement risks, particularly within complex supply chains. Establishing proactive intellectual property warning systems is essential to prevent unintentional infringements that could trigger criminal investigations. Furthermore, rights holders are encouraged to leverage the strengthened multilateral collaboration networks facilitated by EUIPO and Eurojust. This approach allows businesses to actively defend their legitimate rights while navigating the evolving regulatory landscape, thereby avoiding legal pitfalls and maintaining operational continuity in the European market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["第十届高层会议聚焦强化欧盟内部执法协作机制", "EUIPO 携手 Eurojust 统筹打击跨国知识产权犯罪", "明确将保护创新竞争力作为核心执法战略目标"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The tenth high-level meeting focused on strengthening internal EU law enforcement collaboration mechanisms to effectively combat intellectual property violations.", "EUIPO partnered with Eurojust to coordinate efforts against transnational intellectual property crime, ensuring unified action across borders.", "Protecting innovation and competitiveness was established as the core strategic objective for future intellectual property law enforcement actions."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注欧盟刑事执法新动向，排查供应链侵权隐患", "利用多边协作机制，主动维权并规避法律风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor new trends in EU criminal IP enforcement and audit supply chains for potential infringement liabilities.", "Practice note: Utilize enhanced multilateral collaboration mechanisms to proactively enforce rights and mitigate legal exposure.", "What to monitor: Developments in cross-border judicial cooperation and EUIPO-led initiatives targeting organized IP crime networks."]
core_points_en: The tenth high-level meeting focused on strengthening internal EU law enforcement collaboration mechanisms to effectively combat intellectual property violations.
EUIPO partnered with Eurojust to coordinate efforts against transnational intellectual property crime, ensuring unified action across borders.
Protecting innovation and competitiveness was established as the core strategic objective for future intellectual property law enforcement actions.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor new trends in EU criminal IP enforcement and audit supply chains for potential infringement liabilities.
Practice note: Utilize enhanced multilateral collaboration mechanisms to proactively enforce rights and mitigate legal exposure.
What to monitor: Developments in cross-border judicial cooperation and EUIPO-led initiatives targeting organized IP crime networks.

items.title: New implementation of the EPO PACE programme starting 1 February 2026
items.title_zh: 欧洲专利局 PACE 加速审查计划新规将于 2026 年实施
items.title_en: EPO Revises PACE Programme: Major Structural Changes Effective 1 February 2026
items.summary: On 1 February 2026, the revision of the PACE programme (Programme for Accelerated Prosecution of European Patent Applications) of the European Patent Office (EPO) entered into force. This update, announced in the Notice from the Official Journal of December 2025 (OJ EPO 2025, A69), introduces a major structural change in the acceleration of the European ... Read more La entrada New implementation of the EPO PACE programme starting 1 February 2026 se publicó primero en PONS IP .
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局宣布对 PACE（欧洲专利申请加速审查计划）进行重大修订，新版本将于 2026 年 2 月 1 日正式生效。该更新已通过 2025 年 12 月官方期刊公告发布，旨在对欧洲专利申请的加速程序进行结构性调整，以优化审查流程效率。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office has announced significant revisions to the Programme for Accelerated Prosecution of European Patent Applications (PACE). These changes, detailed in the Notice published in the Official Journal of December 2025 (OJ EPO 2025, A69), introduce a major structural overhaul designed to optimize the efficiency of the accelerated examination process. The updated framework officially enters into force on 1 February 2026. This reform aims to streamline procedural steps and enhance the overall speed and predictability of patent prosecution for applicants seeking faster grant decisions. Rights holders should note that this update represents a fundamental shift in how acceleration requests are managed and processed within the EPO system.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需提前评估现有加速请求策略，适应新程序结构以避免审查延误。建议密切关注 EPO 后续操作指南，调整高价值专利布局节奏，确保在过渡期内合规利用加速通道。
items.ai_insight_en: Applicants currently utilizing or planning to use the PACE programme must reassess their existing acceleration strategies to align with the new structural requirements. Failure to adapt to these procedural changes may result in unintended delays or non-compliance during the transition period. It is crucial for IP teams to closely monitor subsequent operational guidelines issued by the EPO to ensure full understanding of the new mechanics. Strategic adjustment of high-value patent filing schedules is recommended to leverage the accelerated channel effectively while maintaining compliance with the updated regulatory framework.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EPO 对 PACE 加速审查计划实施重大结构性变革", "新规明确于 2026 年 2 月 1 日正式生效执行", "相关修订细节已刊登于 2025 年 12 月官方期刊"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EPO implements major structural reforms to the PACE programme to enhance the efficiency of accelerated patent prosecution procedures.", "The revised PACE regulations officially enter into force on 1 February 2026, marking a new operational phase for accelerated applications.", "Detailed provisions regarding the structural adjustments were published in the Official Journal of December 2025 under reference OJ EPO 2025, A69."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需重新评估现有专利申请的加速请求策略", "应密切跟踪 EPO 发布的后续操作指南细节"]

The revised PACE regulations officially enter into force on 1 February 2026, marking a new operational phase for accelerated applications.
Detailed provisions regarding the structural adjustments were published in the Official Journal of December 2025 under reference OJ EPO 2025, A69.

Practice note: Adjust high-value patent portfolio timelines to ensure compliance and effective use of the accelerated track.
What to monitor: Track upcoming EPO operational guidelines detailing specific procedural requirements for the revised programme.

items.title: Technologies quantiques – Projet « OQULUS » pour 2030
items.title_zh: 法国2030计划：光子量子计算OQULUS项目优先发展
items.title_en: France 2030 Report Prioritizes OQULUS Photonics Quantum Computer Development
items.summary: Le rapport annuel officiel de France 2030 sur la stratégie [1] dans le domaine quantique donne les priorités de développement pour l’avenir.  Parmi celles-ci, le projet « OQULUS » vise à développer un ordinateur quantique à base de photons selon plusieurs technologies. En particulier, il s’agira de mettre au point un prototype à (suite) L’article Technologies quantiques – Projet « OQULUS » pour 2030 est apparu en premier sur Lavoix - Propriété intellectuelle .
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国发布“2030计划”年度官方报告，明确量子领域未来发展优先级。其中，“OQULUS”项目被列为重点，旨在基于多种技术开发光子量子计算机。该项目核心目标是研制出原型机，以推动法国在量子计算领域的战略自主与技术领先，体现了国家层面对前沿硬科技知识产权储备的重视。
items.ai_summary_en: The official annual report for France’s 2030 investment plan outlines strategic priorities for the future of quantum technologies. A central focus is the OQULUS project, which aims to develop a photonic quantum computer utilizing multiple technological approaches. The primary objective is to create a functional prototype, reinforcing France’s strategic autonomy and technical leadership in this critical sector. This initiative highlights the national commitment to securing intellectual property reserves in advanced hard technologies. By prioritizing photonics-based solutions, the strategy seeks to establish a robust foundation for next-generation computing capabilities, ensuring that European stakeholders remain competitive in the global race for quantum supremacy and related industrial applications.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国科技企业应密切关注法国及欧盟在量子计算等前沿领域的政策动向与专利布局。建议提前进行FTO分析，规避潜在专利风险，并加强自主研发与核心专利储备，以应对未来可能的技术壁垒和市场竞争。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should closely monitor policy shifts and patent landscapes in quantum computing, particularly regarding France's strategic initiatives. Conducting early Freedom to Operate (FTO) analyses is essential to mitigate potential infringement risks associated with emerging photonic technologies. Companies are advised to strengthen independent research and development efforts while building core patent portfolios. Proactive IP management will help navigate future technical barriers and competitive pressures. Aligning R&D strategies with national priorities can also facilitate access to funding and collaborative opportunities within the EU quantum ecosystem.

items.title: EUIPO launches 2026 SME Fund - GJE
items.title_zh: EUIPO 启动 2026 年中小企业知识产权基金
items.title_en: EUIPO Launches 2026 SME Fund Offering Over €7,000 for IP Services
items.summary: EU SMEs can access €7,000+ in IP funding through the EUIPO 2026 SME Fund for patents, trade marks, designs and related services.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局于 2026 年 2 月 2 日启动新版中小企业基金，由欧盟委员会发起。该计划为符合定义的欧盟中小企业提供超 7000 欧元资助，覆盖专利、商标、外观设计及相关服务费用。申请主体需满足员工少于 250 人且营收或资产负债表低于特定阈值的条件，旨在降低企业知识产权保护成本。
items.ai_summary_en: On February 2, 2026, the European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) officially launched the 2026 edition of its SME Fund. This initiative, backed by the European Commission, provides financial support to small and medium-sized enterprises operating within the European Union. Eligible companies can access funding exceeding €7,000 to cover costs associated with patents, trademarks, designs, and related intellectual property services. To qualify, applicants must strictly meet the EU definition of an SME, which requires having fewer than 250 employees and an annual turnover below €50 million or a balance sheet total under €43 million. The program aims to reduce the financial burden of securing IP rights, thereby encouraging innovation and protecting market assets for smaller businesses across the region.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧运营的中国中小企业应积极评估资格，利用该基金降低布局成本。建议提前准备合规证明材料，结合商标与专利策略同步申请，最大化政策红利以增强市场竞争力。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should assess their eligibility for this funding scheme to optimize budget allocation for intellectual property protection. Rights holders are advised to prepare necessary compliance documentation early to ensure smooth processing. Integrating trademark and patent strategies during the application phase allows companies to maximize the available subsidies effectively. This approach not only lowers overall acquisition costs but also strengthens the legal framework surrounding key brand and technology assets. Proactive engagement with the fund enables businesses to enhance their competitive positioning while managing operational expenditures efficiently within the current regulatory environment.

items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["符合条件的中资企业应尽快申请以降低确权成本", "需统筹商标专利布局，充分利用资金支持提升保护层级"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Eligible SMEs should apply promptly to offset costs for trademark and patent filings, reducing overall IP portfolio maintenance expenses.", "Practice note: Coordinate trademark and patent applications simultaneously to fully utilize the funding cap and streamline administrative procedures.", "What to monitor: Verify strict adherence to EU SME definitions regarding employee numbers and financial thresholds to prevent application rejection."]
core_points_en: The EUIPO has opened the application window for the 2026 SME Fund, providing significant financial assistance for various intellectual property registrations and services.
Eligibility is strictly limited to EU-based entities meeting specific SME criteria regarding employee count, annual turnover, and balance sheet totals.
Individual companies may receive over €7,000 in funding, covering expenses for patents, trademarks, designs, and other eligible IP-related professional services.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Eligible SMEs should apply promptly to offset costs for trademark and patent filings, reducing overall IP portfolio maintenance expenses.
Practice note: Coordinate trademark and patent applications simultaneously to fully utilize the funding cap and streamline administrative procedures.
What to monitor: Verify strict adherence to EU SME definitions regarding employee numbers and financial thresholds to prevent application rejection.

items.title: KMU-Fonds 2026: EU-Förderprogramm für kleine und mittlere Unternehmen startet wieder
items.title_zh: 欧盟 2026 年中小企业基金重启：资助知识产权扫描与申请
items.title_en: EUIPO Restarts 2026 SME Fund: Subsidies for IP Scans and European Applications
items.summary: KMU-Fonds 2026: EU-Förderprogramm für kleine und mittlere Unternehmen startet wieder - Individueller IP-Scan, Anmeldung von Patenten, Marken, Designs und Sorten sowie Stand-der-Technik-Recherchen: Kleine und mittelgroße Unternehmen erhalten EU-Förderung, um ihr geistiges Eigentum zu schützen – Laut KMU-Barometer verfügen nur 10 Prozent der kleinen Unternehmen über Schutzrechte – DPMA-Präsidentin: Nur wer geistiges Eigentum schützt, kann dessen volles wirtschaftliches Potenzial ausschöpfen - Das
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）于 2026 年 2 月重启中小企业基金，旨在提升欧洲中小企业知识产权持有率。该计划提供资金用于 IP 扫描、现有技术研究及专利、商标、外观设计和植物新品种的申请费用补贴。德国专利商标局（DPMA）负责德国区协调。数据显示仅 10% 中小企业拥有知识产权，而使用者中 93% 获益显著。企业需先在线申请代金券，激活后开展服务并提交报销。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) has relaunched the 2026 SME Fund on February 2, aiming to increase intellectual property ownership among small and medium-sized enterprises across Europe. The program provides financial support for individual IP scans, prior art searches, and application fees for patents, trademarks, designs, and plant variety rights. In Germany, the German Patent and Trade Mark Office (DPMA) coordinates the initiative. Data indicates that only 10% of SMEs currently hold IP rights, despite 93% of those who do reporting positive business impacts. To access funding, applicants must first secure vouchers online, execute services with certified providers, and subsequently submit invoices to EUIPO for reimbursement. This structured approach ensures targeted support for protecting intangible assets.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国中小企业应充分利用该基金降低合规与布局成本。建议立即评估自身技术品牌资产，通过 IP 扫描明确保护策略，利用补贴完成高价值专利或商标的欧洲注册，提升投资吸引力并防范侵权风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should leverage this funding mechanism to optimize budget allocation for intellectual property protection. By utilizing subsidized IP scans, companies can systematically identify unprotected assets and transition from reactive defense strategies to proactive rights confirmation. Securing European patents and trademarks through this scheme enhances investment attractiveness and mitigates infringement risks without straining operational budgets. Rights holders are advised to assess their current portfolio gaps immediately and initiate the voucher application process to capitalize on these cost-saving opportunities while ensuring robust legal coverage across key EU markets.

items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用补贴大幅降低欧洲专利与商标申请成本，优化海外布局预算", "借 IP 扫描诊断资产漏洞，将被动防御转为主动确权的竞争优势"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Utilize subsidies to significantly reduce costs for European patent and trademark filings, optimizing overseas expansion budgets.", "Practice note: Conduct IP scans to diagnose asset vulnerabilities, converting passive defense into a competitive advantage through active rights confirmation.", "What to monitor: Track voucher availability and specific eligibility criteria for certified service providers to ensure timely reimbursement claims."]
core_points_en: EUIPO relaunches the 2026 SME Fund, covering costs for IP scans and applications for patents, trademarks, designs, and plant varieties.
Addressing low IP adoption rates, the program offers comprehensive financial support from initial prior art searches through to final registration.
Standardized application requires obtaining digital vouchers before service execution, followed by invoice submission for EUIPO reimbursement.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Utilize subsidies to significantly reduce costs for European patent and trademark filings, optimizing overseas expansion budgets.
Practice note: Conduct IP scans to diagnose asset vulnerabilities, converting passive defense into a competitive advantage through active rights confirmation.
What to monitor: Track voucher availability and specific eligibility criteria for certified service providers to ensure timely reimbursement claims.

items.title: Trademark News: What you might have missed in January 2025
items.title_zh: 2025 年 1 月商标要闻：尼斯分类更新与丹麦费用调整
items.title_en: January 2025 Trademark Update: Nice Classification Changes and Denmark Fee Adjustments
items.summary: Stay informed with our comprehensive roundup of the past month's headlines, all neatly compiled for you in one convenient post. Catch up on the latest news reported by the Kluwer IP Reporter.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文汇总 2025 年 1 月全球商标动态，核心涉欧内容包括：WIPO 发布 2026 版尼斯分类并于年初生效；丹麦作为马德里体系成员，其单独规费将于 2026 年分两阶段调整。此外涵盖阿根廷、中国、格林纳达及新西兰等地程序变更，为跨国布局提供最新情报参考。
items.ai_summary_en: This update compiles key global trademark developments from January 2025, with significant implications for European rights holders. The World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) released the 2026 version of the Nice Classification, introducing substantial changes to common product categories effective 1 January 2026. Concurrently, revised individual fees for designating Denmark in Madrid System applications will enter into force in two stages during 2026. The summary also covers procedural updates from Argentina, China, Grenada, and New Zealand. Grenada’s accession to the Madrid Protocol expands the system’s reach to 132 countries. These developments provide essential intelligence for managing international trademark portfolios, ensuring compliance with updated classification standards, and optimizing budget planning for designated territories within the Madrid System.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需关注新版尼斯分类对商品描述的影响，及时修正申请策略。针对丹麦市场，应核算 2026 年费用上涨成本，利用过渡期完成续展或新申请，优化欧洲商标布局预算。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should review existing trademark portfolios against the new 2026 Nice Classification standards to ensure accurate identification of goods and services. Misalignment may affect protection scope or require corrective filings. For strategies involving Denmark, applicants must account for the scheduled fee increases in 2026. Evaluating these cost implications allows for better budget forecasting and potential optimization of renewal timelines. Additionally, monitoring procedural shifts in other jurisdictions like Argentina and China helps maintain a cohesive global enforcement strategy. Proactive adaptation to these regulatory and financial changes ensures continued robust protection and efficient resource allocation across international markets.

items.ai_core_points_en: ["The 2026 Nice Classification entered into force on 1 January 2026, introducing significant adjustments to descriptions of common goods and services.", "Denmark's individual fees under the Madrid System will be adjusted in two stages, effective 1 January 2026 and 1 July 2026 respectively.", "Grenada acceded to the Madrid Protocol, increasing the total number of member countries in the international registration system to 132."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["核查商品服务项目是否符合 2026 版尼斯分类新标准", "评估丹麦规费调整对马德里延伸申请的成本影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Verify that current goods and service specifications comply with the 2026 Nice Classification to maintain adequate protection scope.", "Practice note: Assess the financial impact of Denmark's revised Madrid fees on upcoming renewals and new designations to optimize budget planning.", "What to monitor: Track further procedural amendments in Argentina, China, and other key jurisdictions to adapt international filing strategies accordingly."]
core_points_en: The 2026 Nice Classification entered into force on 1 January 2026, introducing significant adjustments to descriptions of common goods and services.
Denmark's individual fees under the Madrid System will be adjusted in two stages, effective 1 January 2026 and 1 July 2026 respectively.
Grenada acceded to the Madrid Protocol, increasing the total number of member countries in the international registration system to 132.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Verify that current goods and service specifications comply with the 2026 Nice Classification to maintain adequate protection scope.
Practice note: Assess the financial impact of Denmark's revised Madrid fees on upcoming renewals and new designations to optimize budget planning.
What to monitor: Track further procedural amendments in Argentina, China, and other key jurisdictions to adapt international filing strategies accordingly.
